EDCS-08700085.translation: To Publius Septimius Geta, most noble Caesar, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, pacifier of the world, and brother of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, given by the public community of Tuccita by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700085.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by the community of Tuccita to Caesar Geta, son of Septimius Severus and brother of Caracalla, by decree of the local council, dating to Geta's co-rule with his father and brother (around 200-211 CE).

EDCS-08700086.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Getae Severo Augusto divi Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi pacatoris orbis filio et Marci Aurelii Antonini imperatoris fratri res publica Tuccitanorum datum decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08700086.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Geta Severus Augustus, son of the Divine Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, pacifier of the world, and brother of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, given by the public community of Tuccita by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700086.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by the community of Tuccita to Emperor Geta Severus Augustus, son of Septimius Severus and brother of Caracalla, by decree of the local council, dating to Geta's reign (209-211 CE).

EDCS-08700087.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Septimii Severi Pii Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Britannici maximi filio divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Augusto Parthico maximo Britannico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XV imperatori bis consuli IIII patri patriae pacatori orbis res publica Tuccitanorum decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08700087.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 15th time, imperator twice, consul four times, father of the country, pacifier of the world, by decree of the town council of the people of Tuccita.
EDCS-08700087.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus) and was erected by the town council of Tuccita.

EDCS-08700088.text_edition: \n Iuliae Augustae matri castrorum res publica Tuccitanorum decreto decurionum posuit\n
EDCS-08700088.translation: The people of Tuccita set this up by decree of the town council to Julia Augusta, mother of the camp.
EDCS-08700088.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the people of Tuccita to Julia Domna, mother of Caracalla and wife of Septimius Severus, likely honoring her title 'mother of the camp'.

EDCS-08700089.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Claudio Pio Felici invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III consuli res publica Tuccitanorum decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08700089.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, Marcus Aurelius Claudius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul, by decree of the town council of the people of Tuccita.
EDCS-08700089.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to Emperor Claudius Gothicus by the people of Tuccita.

EDCS-08700090.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Probo Pio Felici invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis VI consuli IV patri patriae res publica Tuccitanorum devota numini maiestatique eius curatore Iulio Claudio VE CONSS decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08700090.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, Marcus Aurelius Probus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 6th time, consul four times, father of the country, devoted to his divine majesty, by curator Julius Claudius, during the consulship of VE CONSS, by decree of the town council of the people of Tuccita.
EDCS-08700090.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Probus and was erected by the people of Tuccita under the supervision of Julius Claudius.

EDCS-08700091.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Ancarius Quinti filius Sergia Navus locus pedum XII missicius Lucius Ancarius Quinti filius Sergia Pollio hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700091.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Ancarius, son of Quintus, of the Sergia tribe, Navus, a freedman, [has allotted] a space of 12 feet. Lucius Ancarius, son of Quintus, of the Sergia tribe, Pollio, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700091.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial plot of Lucius Ancarius and the allotment of space by Quintus Ancarius, likely from the Roman Republican era due to tribal and gentilic names.

EDCS-08700092.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Luci filio Sergia Scaenae decurioni equitum centurioni hastato primo legionis IIII IIviro Laeta filia\n
EDCS-08700092.translation: To Gaius Julius Scaena, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, a decurion, cavalryman, first centurion of the Fourth Legion, duumvir; Laeta, his daughter.
EDCS-08700092.summary: This inscription commemorates Gaius Julius Scaena, a local official (decurion, duumvir) and a military officer, possibly from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08700093.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaio Urbanio Firmino militi legionis X Iulius Ingenuus miles legionis X \n
EDCS-08700093.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Urbanus Firmus, a soldier of the Tenth Legion. Iulius Ingenuus, a soldier of the Tenth Legion.
EDCS-08700093.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring two soldiers, Gaius Urbanus Firmus and Iulius Ingenuus, both serving in the Tenth Legion.

EDCS-08700094.summary: This very brief inscription records the name and title of Quintus Antonius, who held the rank of prefect.

EDCS-08700095.text_edition: \n Cornelio Luci filio Savoni aedili IIviro pontifici decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08700095.translation: To Cornelius Savonius, son of Lucius, aedile, duumvir, pontifex, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-08700095.summary: This inscription records the civic and religious offices held by Cornelius Savonius, who served as aedile, duumvir, and pontifex, honored by the town council.

EDCS-08700096.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Cornelius Firmus annorum L aedilis duumvir in colonia Augusta Gemella hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700096.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Cornelius Firmus, aged 50, aedile, duumvir in the colony Augusta Gemella, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700096.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Publius Cornelius Firmus, who served as aedile and duumvir in the colony of Augusta Gemella.

EDCS-08700097.text_edition: \n Quintus Iulius Quinti filius Titi nepos Sergia Celsus aedilis IIvir bis de suo dedit\n
EDCS-08700097.translation: Quintus Julius Celsus, son of Quintus, grandson of Titus, of the Sergia tribe, twice aedile and duovir, gave this from his own funds.
EDCS-08700097.summary: This inscription records a dedication of funds by Quintus Julius Celsus, an official, for public works or events, likely in the 1st century CE given the typical career progression.

EDCS-08700098.text_edition: \n Lucio Iulio Luci filio Sergia Culleoni IIviro colonia Augusta Gemella decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08700098.translation: To Lucius Julius Culleo, son of Lucius, duovir of the colony Augusta Gemella, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700098.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Julius Culleo, a duovir of the colony Augusta Gemella, commissioned by the local town council (decurions), likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700099.text_edition: \n Iuliae Cai filiae Laetae flaminicae domus Augustae Lucius Maecius Nativos consobrinae piissumae sic erga se\n
EDCS-08700099.translation: To Livia Laeta, daughter of Caius, priestess of the Imperial Cult, dearest cousin to Lucius Maecius Nativos, for his devotion to her.
EDCS-08700099.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Lucius Maecius Nativos to his cousin Livia Laeta, a priestess of the imperial cult, indicating a relationship of familial affection and likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700100.translation: To Lucius Licinius, prefect, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700100.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Licinius, who held the office of prefect, honored by the local council of decurions, probably from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700101.text_edition: \n Lucio Mummio filio Sergia Rufo IIviro pontifici decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08700101.translation: To Lucius Mummius Rufus, son of Lucius, duovir and pontiff, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700101.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Mummius Rufus, a duovir and pontiff, honored by the local council of decurions in a Roman colony, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-08700103.text_edition: \n Marcus Valerius Marci filius Quirina Marcellus vir municipii Aurgitani accepto loco a re publica horologium omni circensibus et ludis scaenicis \n
EDCS-08700103.translation: Marcus Valerius Marcellus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, a notable of the municipality of Aurgitano, having received from the state a public clock, for all the circuses and theatrical performances.
EDCS-08700103.summary: This inscription records Marcus Valerius Marcellus, a local official in Aurgitano, receiving a public clock for use during public games and performances, suggesting a period of Roman municipal administration.

EDCS-08700104.text_edition: \n Pompeius Epaphroditus Augustalis hic situs est in fronte latum pedes XXIIII\n
EDCS-08700104.translation: Pompeius Epaphroditus Augustalis is buried here; the front is twenty-four feet wide.
EDCS-08700104.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Pompeius Epaphroditus Augustalis, providing the dimensions of his tomb, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700105.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to provide a definitive summary but mentions a woman named Aelianae, possibly part of a dedication or funerary context from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700106.text_edition: \n Aniciae Sexti filiae Postumae Etrilii Afri colonia Augusta Gemella decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08700106.translation: To Postuma Anicia, daughter of Sextus, wife of Etrilius Rufus, of the colony Augusta Gemella, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700106.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Postuma Anicia, wife of Etrilius Rufus and resident of the colony Augusta Gemella, honored by the local council of decurions, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700108.text_edition: \n Manliae Publi filiae Paetinae Valeria Paetina matri\n
EDCS-08700108.translation: To Valeria Paetina, daughter of Publius, in honor of her mother Manlia, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-08700108.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Valeria Paetina to her mother, Manlia, indicating familial devotion and likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700109.text_edition: \n Quinti filio Sergia Vicano ob liberalitatem et eiius sic \n
EDCS-08700109.translation: To Vicanus, son of Quintus, from the tribe Sergia, for his generosity and...
EDCS-08700109.summary: This fragment likely records a dedication to a man named Vicanus, possibly for his generosity, dating uncertain.

EDCS-08700110.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Aviena Calliope to someone named Italicilla, likely a patron or deceased individual.

EDCS-08700111.text_edition: \n Vindici nostro Crescens et Eulalus dant\n
EDCS-08700111.translation: Crescens and Eulalus give this to our Vindicus.
EDCS-08700111.summary: This inscription records a gift from Crescens and Eulalus to Vindicus, possibly a dedication or a marker of some transaction.

EDCS-08700112.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelia Leucothoe Patriciensis nnorum XXVII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis locus pedum XIIII XII\n
EDCS-08700112.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aurelia Leucothoe of Patriciensis, aged 27, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Plot of 14 by 12 feet.
EDCS-08700112.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Leucothoe, who died at 27, detailing her burial plot and a standard wish for the deceased, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700113.text_edition: \n Crespina Crespi filia nnorum X Accitana hic sita est sit tibi terra levis in fronte latum pedes XIX in agro pedes XI\n
EDCS-08700113.translation: Crespina, daughter of Crespius, aged 10, from Acci, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. 19 feet wide at the front, 11 feet in the field.
EDCS-08700113.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Crespina, daughter of Crespius, who died at 10, specifying plot dimensions and a standard epitaph formula, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700114.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Valeria Hispaniani Tuccitana annorum XX- \n
EDCS-08700114.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Valeria of Hispania, from Tuccis, aged 20...
EDCS-08700114.summary: This is a funerary epitaph fragment for Valeria, from Tuccis, who died at 20, beginning with the standard invocation to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-08700115.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph, beginning with the standard invocation to the Divine Manes, for an individual named -amilia.

EDCS-08700116.text_edition: \n In fronte latum pedes XII Amia Marci filia Anulla Sextus Valerius Sexti filius Voltinia Clemens sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700116.translation: 12 feet wide at the front. Amia, daughter of Marcus Anulla, and Sextus Valerius, son of Sextus, of the Voltinia tribe, Clemens. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700116.summary: This inscription seems to mark a plot of land with dimensions and names two individuals, Amia and Sextus Valerius, potentially related to the plot's ownership or burial, and includes a wish for the deceased.

EDCS-08700118.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Calpurnia Nicotyche annorum XXXVIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700118.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Calpurnia Nicotyche, aged 38, pious, is buried here on her own property. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700118.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Calpurnia Nicotyche, aged 38, who is described as pious and buried on her own property, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700120.text_edition: \n Caio Cornelio Luci filio Galeria Romano \n
EDCS-08700120.translation: To Gaius Cornelius Romanus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-08700120.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription for Gaius Cornelius Romanus, son of Lucius, possibly for public service or as a tombstone.

EDCS-08700121.text_edition: \n Delphus Iuliae Lalemae servus annorum XL hic situs est in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-08700121.translation: Delphus, slave of Julia Lalema, aged 40, is buried here; 12 feet in front, 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-08700121.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Delphus, a slave, indicating the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-08700122.summary: This fragment likely records Fadia, a freedwoman of Lucius Haterius, possibly on a funerary monument.

EDCS-08700123.text_edition: \n Quinti libertus Fausthoc monumentum et saepta sibi et o patrono suo et VCVXVIII et Iuliae ITEXXIII fecit\n
EDCS-08700123.translation: Fausthoc, freedman of Quintus, made this monument and enclosure for himself, his patron, and for VCVXVIII and Julia ITEXXIII.
EDCS-08700123.summary: This epitaph records the construction of a tomb and enclosure by Fausthoc, a freedman, for himself, his patron, and two other individuals.

EDCS-08700124.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fortunata nnorum XXV pia in suos hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700124.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunata, aged 25, devoted to her family, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700124.summary: This is a tombstone for Fortunata, recording her age and devotion to her family, with a common Latin funerary formula.

EDCS-08700125.text_edition: \n Caius Gallius |(mulieris) libertus Agathemer Gallia Cai liberta Syra Caius Gallius Cai libertus Syriacus\n
EDCS-08700125.translation: Agathemer, freedman of Gaius Gallius, freedman of a woman; Syra, freedwoman of Gallia, freedwoman of Gaius; Syriacus, freedman of Gaius Gallius, freedman of Syra.
EDCS-08700125.summary: This inscription lists several freedmen and freedwomen connected to the family of Gaius Gallius and possibly Agathemer and Syra, likely on a shared funerary monument.

EDCS-08700127.translation: Eutactus, freedman of Quintus Iulius Philadelphus.
EDCS-08700127.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Eutactus, a freedman of Quintus Iulius Philadelphus.

EDCS-08700129.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Augustina annorum XX pia in suos hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700129.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Augustina, aged 20, devoted to her family, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700129.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulia Augustina, recording her age and devotion to her family, with a common Latin funerary formula.

EDCS-08700130.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Festiva nnorum XXIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700130.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Festiva, aged 23, devoted to her family, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700130.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulia Festiva, recording her age and devotion to her family, with a common Latin funerary formula.

EDCS-08700131.text_edition: \n In fronte pedes X Iulia Quinti liberta Macaria hic sita est sit tibi <t>(erra) <l>(evis)\n
EDCS-08700131.translation: 10 feet in front. Iulia Macaria, freedwoman of Quintus, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700131.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Iulia Macaria, a freedwoman of Quintus, and specifies the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-08700133.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Lucretius...
EDCS-08700133.summary: This incomplete inscription begins a funerary dedication to Lucius Lucretius, honoring the departed spirits.

EDCS-08700134.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Marcius Septuminus sic annorum XXVI\n
EDCS-08700134.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Marcius Septuminus, aged 26.
EDCS-08700134.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Lucius Marcius Septuminus at the age of 26, dedicated to the Divine Spirits.

EDCS-08700135.text_edition: \n Quintus Maxumus annorum Volumnia Quinti liberta Primigenia annorum XXVII hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis in fronte pedes XIIII\n
EDCS-08700135.translation: Quintus Maxumus and Volumnia, daughter of Quintus Primigenia, aged 27, are buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. 14 feet in front.
EDCS-08700135.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Quintus Maxumus and Volumnia Primigenia, with dimensions for the burial plot.

EDCS-08700136.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gaius Memmius Hilarus annorum XLIII D S I hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700136.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gaius Memmius Hilarus, aged 43, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700136.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Gaius Memmius Hilarus, aged 43, dedicated to the Divine Spirits.

EDCS-08700137.text_edition: \n Mussidia Scitae liberta Graphe locus pedum X\n
EDCS-08700137.translation: Graphe, freedwoman of Mussidia Scita, a plot of 10 feet.
EDCS-08700137.summary: A tombstone marking a burial plot of 10 feet for Graphe, a freedwoman.

EDCS-08700138.text_edition: \n Pompeius Felix pedes VIII semis pedes XIII semis\n
EDCS-08700138.translation: Pompeius Felix, 8 feet and a half, 13 feet and a half.
EDCS-08700138.summary: A tombstone indicating plot dimensions of 8.5 feet and 13.5 feet, likely for Pompeius Felix.

EDCS-08700139.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gaius R Sextus annorum plus minus LXV pius in suos fecit Aelia Firma marito bene merenti sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700139.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gaius R. Sextus, about 65 years old, pious towards his family, made this for his deserving husband. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700139.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the Divine Spirits, commemorating Gaius R. Sextus, about 65, and made by Aelia Firma for her deserving husband.

EDCS-08700140.text_edition: \n Rufilla viro fecit Aulo Pompeio Auli filio Sergia Basso\n
EDCS-08700140.translation: Rufilla made this for her husband Aulus Pompeius Bassus, son of Aulus.
EDCS-08700140.summary: A tombstone recording that Rufilla dedicated this monument to her husband, Aulus Pompeius Bassus.

EDCS-08700142.text_edition: \n Locus in fronte pedum X Valeria Postuma \n
EDCS-08700142.translation: A plot of 10 feet in front for Valeria Postuma.
EDCS-08700142.summary: A tombstone marking a burial plot of 10 feet in front for Valeria Postuma.

EDCS-08700143.text_edition: \n In fronte latum pedes XII Quintus Voconius Ponderosus \n
EDCS-08700143.translation: 12 feet wide in front for Quintus Voconius Ponderosus.
EDCS-08700143.summary: A tombstone marking a plot 12 feet wide for Quintus Voconius Ponderosus.

EDCS-08700157.summary: A fragmentary inscription with traces of names and patronymic, likely from a tombstone or public monument.

EDCS-08700167.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Mentesana flaminica perpetua annorum LXVIII lius annorum LX\n
EDCS-08700167.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Mentesana, perpetual priestess, aged 68. Lius, aged 60.
EDCS-08700167.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Mentesana, a perpetual priestess, and Lius, likely her husband or relative.

EDCS-08700168.text_edition: \n Cassiae Auli filiae Montanillae colonia Augusta Gemella decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08700168.translation: To Cassia Montanilla, daughter of Aulus, by decree of the town council of Colonia Augusta Gemella.
EDCS-08700168.summary: This is a funerary monument for Cassia Montanilla, honored by the town council of Colonia Augusta Gemella.

EDCS-08700169.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Aelius Epaphroditus Andurensis nnum XXII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700169.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aelius Epaphroditus of Andura, aged 22, pious to his own people, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700169.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aelius Epaphroditus, a young man from Andura, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700171.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Magnius Martialis annorum XX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700171.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Magnius Martialis, aged 20, pious to his own people, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700171.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Magnius Martialis, a young man who died at age 20, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700173.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Perperna Gallicanus nnum L hic situs est sit tibi terra levis huic merenti filius et nepos fecerunt\n
EDCS-08700173.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Perperna Gallicanus, aged 50, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. His son and grandson made this for this deserving man.
EDCS-08700173.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Perperna Gallicanus, aged 50, and was erected by his son and grandson.

EDCS-08700202.text_edition: \n Caesia Titi filia Celsa annorum LXV hic sita est quod voto petiere suis plerumque parentes cuncta tibi dignae Caesia contigerant lanifici praeclara fides pietatis alumna priscae praecipue fama pudicitiae te rogo praeteriens dicas sit tibi terra levis quo quo versum locus pedum XII\n
EDCS-08700202.translation: Caesia Celsa, daughter of Titus, aged 65, is buried here. All the things which parents usually sought by vow had been granted to you, Caesia, outstanding in your diligence and a follower of ancient piety, especially in reputation for chastity. I ask you, as you pass by, to say, 'May the earth lie lightly upon you.' The plot is 12 feet in width.
EDCS-08700202.summary: This is a tombstone for Caesia Celsa, praising her virtues as a spinner, pious, and chaste woman, and specifying the dimensions of her burial plot.

EDCS-08700203.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Fortunata vixit annis XXXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700203.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Fortunata lived for 35 years, pious to her own people, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700203.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Fortunata, who died at age 35, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700206.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius |(mulieris) libertus Superstes annorum L pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis locus pedum in fronte XII in agro pedum VIII Cornelia |(mulieris) liberta Campana hic sita est\n
EDCS-08700206.translation: Lucius Cornelius Superstes, freedman of a woman, aged 50, pious to his own people, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. The plot is 12 feet in front and 8 feet in depth. Cornelia Campana, freedwoman of a woman, is buried here.
EDCS-08700206.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Cornelius Superstes, a freedman aged 50, and Cornelia Campana, a freedwoman, along with the dimensions of their burial plot.

EDCS-08700208.text_edition: \n Lucius Iulius Luci libertus Latro Arabianus Clodia Luci liberta Elaphe uxor hic siti sunt in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-08700208.translation: Lucius Iulius Latro Arabianus, freedman of Lucius, and Clodia Elaphe, freedwoman of Lucius, his wife, are buried here. 12 feet in front and 10 feet in depth.
EDCS-08700208.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Lucius Iulius Latro Arabianus and his wife Clodia Elaphe, specifying the dimensions of their shared plot.

EDCS-08700210.text_edition: \n Marco Aelio Gallo Aelia Senilla soror Marcus Fabius Senicio nepos avonculo sic\n
EDCS-08700210.translation: To Marcus Aelius Gallus. Aelia Senilla, sister, Marcus Fabius Senicio, nephew, for his uncle.
EDCS-08700210.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcus Aelius Gallus, made by his sister Aelia Senilla and nephew Marcus Fabius Senicio.

EDCS-08700211.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum C Celerina nnrum XXXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700211.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. C. Celerina, aged 35 years, lies here peacefully among her own. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700211.summary: A tombstone recording the death of C. Celerina, aged 35, and includes a common funerary formula.

EDCS-08700219.translation: To the Unconquered Hercules, Lucius Cornelius Ianuarius [dedicated this].
EDCS-08700219.summary: A dedication to the god Hercules by Lucius Cornelius Ianuarius, likely on an altar or shrine.

EDCS-08700221.text_edition: \n ZA servus annorum XXX- pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700221.translation: ZA, a slave, aged 30 years, lies here peacefully among his own. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700221.summary: A tombstone recording the death of a slave named ZA, aged 30, and includes a common funerary formula.

EDCS-08700223.summary: An epitaph identifying Satulla as the freedwoman of a man named Avitus.

EDCS-08700224.summary: An epitaph identifying Tertiola as the freedwoman of a man named Avitus.

EDCS-08700225.text_edition: \n Saluti pro salute Aemiliae Sabinae Fructus servus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08700225.translation: For the health of Aemilia Sabina, Fructus, a slave, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08700225.summary: A votive inscription by a slave named Fructus, dedicated for the health of Aemilia Sabina.

EDCS-08700227.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Luci Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti Parthici Adiabenici \n
EDCS-08700227.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Parthicus Adiabenicus.
EDCS-08700227.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of Septimius Severus, likely part of a public monument.

EDCS-08700229.text_edition: \n D M S ORDO MVNICIPII POLCONENSE P IVNIO ABITO IIVIR DVMIENSES EX DECRETO DECVRIONVM P IVNIO IIVIR LVNENSES EX DRECRETO DECVRIO OB MERITA P IVNI POLCONESI D D\n
EDCS-08700229.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. The council of the municipality of Polla. P. Junius Abitus, duovir of Dumia, by decree of the decurions. P. Junius, duovir of Lunia, by decree of the decurions, for the merits of P. Junius of Polla, gave and dedicated.
EDCS-08700229.summary: A dedication by municipal officials of Polla and Lunia honouring P. Junius of Polla for his merits, likely on a public building or statue.

EDCS-08700231.summary: An epitaph identifying Annia by her filiation, Lucius Annius' daughter.

EDCS-08700233.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Inicia, aged 21.

EDCS-08700234.text_edition: \n Locus pedum XV Marcus Marius Ruga hic situs est <s>(it) tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700234.translation: Marcus Marius Ruga, who lies here, occupied fifteen feet. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700234.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the burial of Marcus Marius Ruga and the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-08700235.text_edition: \n ------] Repentinus Quadrati et Serenae libertus annorum LX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700235.translation: Repentinus, freedman of Quadratus and Serena, sixty years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700235.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Repentinus, a freedman, and his age.

EDCS-08700239.text_edition: \n Publius Malius Celsus annorum L hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700239.translation: Publius Malius Celsus, fifty years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700239.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Publius Malius Celsus and his age.

EDCS-08700241.text_edition: \n Lunae sacrum Servilia Crocale votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08700241.translation: Servilia Crocale willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Luna.
EDCS-08700241.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Servilia Crocale to the goddess Luna.

EDCS-08700242.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Venus by Caelia.

EDCS-08700243.text_edition: \n Quintus Valerius Optatus Augustalis perpetuus huic ordo municipii Flavi Sosontigitanorum cenas publicas decrevit et locum in quo statuas sibi uxori liberisque poneret loco adsignato ponendas curavit\n
EDCS-08700243.translation: Quintus Valerius Optatus, perpetual Augustalis, for whom the town council of Flavia Sosontigitanorum decreed public banquets, took care to place statues for himself, his wife, and his children in the assigned location.
EDCS-08700243.summary: This inscription records honors bestowed by the municipal council of Flavia Sosontigitanorum upon Quintus Valerius Optatus, a perpetual Augustalis, including public banquets and permission to erect statues.

EDCS-08700244.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Caecilius Cai filius Sergia Severus Tuccitanus nnum XXX- pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis locus in fronte pedum XX in agro pedum\n
EDCS-08700244.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Deities. Lucius Caecilius Sergia Severus Tuccitanus, son of Caius, thirty years old, a dutiful son in his own home, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. The plot is twenty feet in front and [space] feet in depth.
EDCS-08700244.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Lucius Caecilius Sergia Severus Tuccitanus, his age, and the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-08700245.text_edition: \n Publius Cincius Publi filius Sergia Patriciensis Anilia Publi liberta Firma posuit Lucius Cincius Publi filius locus pedum CXXX\n
EDCS-08700245.translation: Publius Cincius, son of Publius, of the Sergia tribe, from Patriciensis. Anilia Firma, freedwoman of Publius, placed [this]. Lucius Cincius, son of Publius. The plot is 130 feet.
EDCS-08700245.summary: This inscription records the placement of a tomb by Publius Cincius and Anilia Firma, likely for themselves and Lucius Cincius, along with the plot's dimensions.

EDCS-08700246.translation: Gaius Cornelius Messalla, son of Gaius, of the Ucc tribe.
EDCS-08700246.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary dedication or a record of ancestry, mentioning Gaius Cornelius Messalla.

EDCS-08700247.text_edition: \n Fabia Luci filia Albana Aiungitana nnnorum XXXV pia in suos hic sita est sit tibi terra hic locus sepulturae habet in fronte pedes L in agro lateribus quattuor quoquo versus pedes L hunc locum violandum qui putaverit rei publicae Aiungitanorum solvet HS XX (viginti milium) n(ummum)\n
EDCS-08700247.translation: Fabia Albana, daughter of Lucius, of the Aiungitani tribe, thirty-five years old, a dutiful wife in her own home, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. This burial plot has a frontage of 50 feet and a depth of 50 feet in every direction; whoever considers violating this plot will pay 20,000 sesterces to the public treasury of the Aiungitani.
EDCS-08700247.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Fabia Albana, her age, the dimensions of her plot, and a penalty for its violation, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period in Aiungitania.

EDCS-08700248.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and parentage of Minicia Moderata, likely part of a tomb.

EDCS-08700249.text_edition: \n Locus pedum XII Sextilia Cai liberta Anus hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700249.translation: A space of 12 feet. Sextilia Anus, freedwoman of Caius, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700249.summary: This funerary inscription marks a burial plot allocated to Sextilia Anus, a freedwoman, and includes a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-08700252.text_edition: \n Lucius Marius Marullinus Quinti filius Lunensis pius in suis annorum LXXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700252.translation: Lucius Marius Marullinus, son of Quintus, from Lunensis, dutiful to his family, aged 80, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700252.summary: This funerary inscription records the name, parentage, origin, familial piety, and age of Lucius Marius Marullinus.

EDCS-08700253.text_edition: \n Lucius Cincius Saturninus annorum L hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700253.translation: Lucius Cincius Saturninus, aged 50, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700253.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age of Lucius Cincius Saturninus, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-08700254.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Octavius Faustinus annorum LX Annia Philusa annorum L pii in suis hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-08700254.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Octavius Faustinus, aged 60, and Annia Philusa, aged 50, dutiful to their families, are placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you all.
EDCS-08700254.summary: This funerary inscription records two individuals, Gaius Octavius Faustinus and Annia Philusa, their ages, their familial devotion, and a wish for their peaceful rest.

EDCS-08700255.text_edition: \n Venustus nnorum X pius in suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700255.translation: Venustus, aged 10, dutiful to his family, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700255.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and young age of Venustus, highlighting his familial devotion.

EDCS-08700256.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Acilia Quinti liberta Felicula Ipolcobulculensis nnorum XXXXV pia in suos hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700256.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Acilia Felicula, freedwoman of Quintus, from Ipolcobulculensis, aged 45, dutiful to her family, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700256.summary: This funerary inscription records the name, freed status, origin, age, and familial piety of Acilia Felicula.

EDCS-08700257.text_edition: \n Locus quoquo versus pedum diis Manibus Petronia Luci filia Sosontigitana annorum hic sita est sit \n
EDCS-08700257.translation: A space in all directions of [4] feet to the Spirits of the Dead. Petronia Sosontigitana, daughter of Lucius, aged [?], is placed here; may...
EDCS-08700257.summary: This funerary inscription marks a burial space and names Petronia Sosontigitana, daughter of Lucius, whose age and the full text are incomplete.

EDCS-08700262.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti \n
EDCS-08700262.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the divine Nerva...
EDCS-08700262.summary: This is the beginning of an honorific inscription, likely a dedication or title for an emperor, possibly Trajan or Hadrian.

EDCS-08700264.text_edition: \n Porcio Luci filio Galeria Materno Iliturgicolensi IIviro Porcius Protogenes Porcius Patroclus Porcius Eugnetus liberti dono dederunt\n
EDCS-08700264.translation: Porcius Protogenes, Porcius Patroclus, and Porcius Eugnetus, freedmen, dedicated this to their son Porcius Maternus, son of Lucius, Galerian, twice civic official.
EDCS-08700264.summary: This inscription records a dedication by freedmen to their son, Porcius Maternus, who held the office of IIvir in the Galerian tribe.

EDCS-08700266.text_edition: \n Marcia Marci filia Procula Patriciensis annorum III semis Marcus Marcius Galeria Proculus Patriciensis domo Sucaeloni IIvir colonorum coloniae Patriciae\n
EDCS-08700266.translation: Marcia Procula, daughter of Marcus, of the Patricia tribe, aged 3 and a half. Marcus Marcius Proculus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, from Sucaelo, former duumvir of the colony of Patricia.
EDCS-08700266.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Marcia Procula and Marcus Marcius Proculus, a former duumvir of the colony of Patricia, likely dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-08700267.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Postumius Philadelphus Agilensis nnorum LXXII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700267.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Postumius Philadelphus of Agila, aged 72, dutiful to his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700267.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Postumius Philadelphus, recording his name, origin, age, and a common epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700269.text_edition: \n Lucius Iunius mino filius Galeria Sil Rusticae uxori dono dedit locum\n
EDCS-08700269.translation: Lucius Iunius gave this place as a gift to his wife, Sil Rustica, daughter of Mino.
EDCS-08700269.summary: This inscription records Lucius Iunius gifting a plot of land to his wife, Sil Rustica, likely a private dedication from the Roman period.

EDCS-08700270.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Porcius Athenodo<ru=N>s nnorum LXXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08700270.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Porcius Athenodorus, aged 85, dutiful to his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700270.summary: This is a tombstone for Porcius Athenodorus, recording his name, age, and a common epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700271.text_edition: \n Caius Publicius Nedumus nnorum L hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700271.translation: Caius Publicius Nedumus, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700271.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Publicius Nedumus, recording his name, age, and a common epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700276.text_edition: \n Statuae sacrae Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii et Marci Aureli Veri Caesaris et Luci Aeli Commodi Augusti filiorum Augusti nepotum Caius Annius Prasius Ipolcobulculensis Apueaclesis incola ob honorem seviratus et gratuitum aquae usum quem saepe amisimus redditum \n
EDCS-08700276.translation: Sacred statues of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius and Marcus Aurelius Verus Caesar and Lucius Aelius Commodus Augustus, sons of the Augustus, grandsons of the Augustus. Caius Annius Prasius of Ipolcobulcula, resident of Apueacla, [dedicated these] for the honor of the office of sevir and for the free use of water, which we had often lost and was restored.
EDCS-08700276.summary: This inscription records the dedication of sacred statues by Caius Annius Prasius, a resident, to honor the emperor Antoninus Pius, Marcus Aurelius, and Lucius Verus, and to commemorate the restoration of water supply, likely from the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-08700280.summary: This inscription is a simple name inscription for Catillo Rufinus, likely a tombstone, from the Roman period.

EDCS-08700281.text_edition: \n Sextus Caecilius Optatus Ilurconensis hic situs est annorum XXV sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700281.translation: Sextus Caecilius Optatus of Ilurco, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700281.summary: This is a tombstone for Sextus Caecilius Optatus, recording his name, origin, age, and a common epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700282.text_edition: \n Fabia Quinti filia Fabiana Estedunensis nnorum XXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700282.translation: Fabia Fabiana, daughter of Quintus, of Estedunum, aged 25, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700282.summary: This is a tombstone for Fabia Fabiana, recording her name, parentage, origin, age, and a common epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700283.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Romula nnorum LXXIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700283.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Romula, aged 73, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700283.summary: This is a tombstone for Romula, recording her name, age, and a common epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700285.text_edition: \n Basis Fortunae ex testamento Luci Flavi Proculi relicta per curatorem operis Lucium Iunium facta ex HS VI (sex milibus) secundum sententiam Cai Messi Rufini Patriciensis arbitri operis et rum Patriciensem arbitrum doni totius huic dono XX ab herede deducta non est\n
EDCS-08700285.translation: A shrine to Fortuna, bequeathed by the will of Lucius Flavius Proculus, was constructed by the curator of the work, Lucius Iunius, at a cost of 6,000 sesterces, according to the decision of Gaius Messius Rufus, arbitrator for the work of Patricienses, and it was not deducted from the total gift of 20 [units] by the heir.
EDCS-08700285.summary: This inscription records the construction of a shrine to Fortuna, funded by a bequest and overseen by various officials, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700286.text_edition: \n AVSLLA Marci Porci Nigri serva dominae Veneri aram posuit\n
EDCS-08700286.translation: Aysulla, slave of Marcus Porcius Niger, set up this altar to Venus.
EDCS-08700286.summary: This inscription records a slave setting up an altar to Venus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700287.text_edition: \n Pomponia Gemuniana Iponubensis dominae Veneri votum solvit dono dedit\n
EDCS-08700287.translation: Pomponia Gemuniana from Iponuba fulfilled her vow and gave this gift to Venus.
EDCS-08700287.summary: This inscription records a woman fulfilling a vow and giving a gift to Venus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700288.text_edition: \n Lucius Porcius Quirina Quietus IIvir pontifex solo suo templum et signum et forum suo et Titi Porci Quirina Quieti fili sui nomine de sua pecunia facienda curavit\n
EDCS-08700288.translation: Lucius Porcius Quietus, twice a year official and priest, undertook the construction of a temple and statue and forum on his own land, with his own funds, in the name of himself and his son, Titus Porcius Quietus.
EDCS-08700288.summary: This inscription records a local magistrate funding and overseeing the construction of a temple, statue, and forum, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700289.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fortunatus municipum municipii Ipolcobulculensium servus nnum XXXXIII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700289.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Fortunatus, slave of the citizens of the municipality of Ipolcobulculenses, aged 44, pious in his own [burial place], is laid here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-08700289.summary: This is a tombstone for Fortunatus, a slave, who died at age 44, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700291.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Licinia Modestina Ipolcobulcolensis Licini Soprhonis liberta nnorum LXXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700291.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Licinia Modestina of Ipolcobulcol, freedwoman of Licinius Soprhon, aged 75, pious in her own [burial place], is laid here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-08700291.summary: This is a tombstone for Licinia Modestina, a freedwoman, who died at age 75, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700292.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Postumia Luci liberta Faustina Agulensis annorum LV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700292.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Postumia Faustina, freedwoman of Lucius, from Agulum, aged 55, pious in her own [burial place], is laid here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-08700292.summary: This is a tombstone for Postumia Faustina, a freedwoman, who died at age 55, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700293.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Urbicius Faventinus Ipolcobulclensis nnum XXXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700293.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Urbicius Faventinus of Ipolcobulclensis, aged 45, pious in his own [burial place], is laid here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-08700293.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Urbicius Faventinus, who died at age 45, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700294.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Urbicius Rusticus Ipolcobulculensis annorum XXXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700294.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Urbicius Rusticus of Ipolcobulculensis, aged 40, is laid here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-08700294.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Urbicius Rusticus, who died at age 40, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700298.text_edition: \n Lucio Pompeio Galeria Prisco Baebia Lychnis mater dono dedit\n
EDCS-08700298.translation: Baebia Lychnis gave this as a gift to Lucius Pompeius Priscus of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-08700298.summary: This inscription records a woman giving a gift to a man, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700303.text_edition: \n Veneris Victricis municipio Flavio Cisimbrensi beneficio Imperatoris Caesaris Augusti Domitiani consulis civitatem Romanam consicutus sic per honorem IIviratus Quintus Annius Quirina Niger de sua pecunia dono dedit\n
EDCS-08700303.translation: Quintus Annius Niger, of the Quirina tribe, having obtained Roman citizenship for the Flavius Cisimbrensis municipality through the favor of Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus, consul, dedicated this monument from his own funds in his capacity as IIvir.
EDCS-08700303.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Quintus Annius Niger, an official of the Flavius Cisimbrensis municipality, likely in the late 1st century CE, to celebrate receiving Roman citizenship.

EDCS-08700304.text_edition: \n municipio F(lavio) Cisimbrensi beneficio Imperatoris Caesaris Augusti Vespasiani VIII Titi Caesaris Augusti fili VI consulum civitatem Romanam consecutus cum uxore per honorem IIviratus Valerius Cai filius Quirina Rufus de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700304.translation: Valerius Rufus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, having obtained Roman citizenship for the Flavius Cisimbrensis municipality through the favor of Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus and Emperor Caesar Titus Augustus, consuls, dedicated and consecrated this monument with his wife from his own funds in his capacity as IIvir.
EDCS-08700304.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Valerius Rufus and his wife to the Flavius Cisimbrensis municipality, likely during the Flavian dynasty (69-96 CE), commemorating their acquisition of Roman citizenship.

EDCS-08700305.text_edition: \n Aper libertus pro salute patroni sui votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08700305.translation: Aper, a freedman, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the safety of his patron.
EDCS-08700305.summary: This is a votive inscription by a freedman named Aper, expressing gratitude for his patron's well-being, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700306.text_edition: \n Caius Valerius Cai filius Galeria Valerianus Cisimbrensis IIvir pontifex perpetuus forum aedes quinque signa deorum quinque statuas suas sua impensa dedit donavit Flavia Valeriana neptis heres epulo dato dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700306.translation: Caius Valerius Valerianus, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, from Cisimbra, IIvir and perpetual pontiff, dedicated and donated the forum, a temple, five statues of gods, and five of his own statues at his own expense. Flavia Valeriana, his granddaughter and heir, consecrated it with a feast.
EDCS-08700306.summary: This inscription records a substantial public works project by Caius Valerius Valerianus, a local magistrate, and its consecration by his heir, Flavia Valeriana, in Cisimbra, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700307.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano optimo Augusto Germanico Dacico Parthico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVIII imperatori VII consuli VI patri patriae municipes\n
EDCS-08700307.translation: The inhabitants of the municipality [dedicate this] to Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus, son of the deified Nerva, the best, Germanicus, Dacicus, Parthicus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 18th time, imperator for the 7th time, consul for the 6th time, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-08700307.summary: This is a dedication by the citizens of a municipality to Emperor Trajan, listing his numerous titles and honors, suggesting a date around 114 CE.

EDCS-08700308.text_edition: \n Valeriae Actes ordo municipum municipii Cisimbrensis ob merita eius statuam decrevit Valeria Acte honore accepto impensam remisit Flavia Valeriana neptis faciundam curavit\n
EDCS-08700308.translation: The council and the citizens of the municipality of Cisimbra decreed a statue to Valeria Acte for her merits. Valeria Acte, having received the honor, remitted the expense. Flavia Valeriana, her granddaughter, saw to its construction.
EDCS-08700308.summary: This inscription records the decree of a statue for Valeria Acte by the municipality of Cisimbra, who then waived the cost, with the statue's construction overseen by her granddaughter Flavia Valeriana, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700310.text_edition: \n Dis Manibis sic Severinus annoro sic XXVIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700310.translation: To the departed spirits. Severinus, aged 28, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700310.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Severinus, who died at the age of 28, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700314.text_edition: \n Imperatore Domitiano Caesare Augusto Augusti filio X consule terminus Augustalis municipi Flavi Cisimbrensis ex decreto Luci Antisti Rustici proconsulis\n
EDCS-08700314.translation: During the consulship of Emperor Domitian Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, the Augustan boundary of the Flavius Cisimbrensis municipality, by decree of Lucius Antistius Rusticus, proconsul.
EDCS-08700314.summary: This inscription marks a boundary stone for the Flavius Cisimbrensis municipality, set during the consulship of Emperor Domitian, indicating a date in the late 1st century CE.

EDCS-08700315.text_edition: \n CSTVRGI GATP annorum LXIII pi- in suis hic est sit tibi terra levis Dis Manibus sacrum\n
EDCS-08700315.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. He is buried here in his own [tomb?], aged 63. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700315.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual aged 63, likely from the Roman Imperial period, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-08700316.text_edition: \n VLA HONORMIVRO ANNI ANO F C R C D S P D V PMMO\n
EDCS-08700316.translation: [Dedication?]. VI [years?]. Honors. For the man. Year. Son of A. C. R. D. S. P. D. V. P. M. O.
EDCS-08700316.summary: This inscription is highly fragmentary and likely records a dedication or honorific text, possibly related to an official or magistrate, with an uncertain date and provenance due to the missing text.

EDCS-08700317.text_edition: \n Lucius Postumius Luci filius Galeria Superstes IIvir pontifex colonorum coloniae Patriciae nnorum LXXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis huic ordo colonorum coloniae Patriciae decrevit locum sepulturae funeris inpensam laudationem statuam\n
EDCS-08700317.translation: Lucius Postumius Superstes, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, twice a judge and priest, of the colonists of Colonia Patricia, aged 85, pious in his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. The order of the colonists of Colonia Patricia decreed to him a burial place, funeral expenses, an oration, and a statue.
EDCS-08700317.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Postumius Superstes, a local magistrate and priest in Colonia Patricia, who died at the age of 85, with the local council providing for his burial and commemoration.

EDCS-08700318.text_edition: \n Potitus Ucc Publius D NItiana Marcus Se tati Sever \n
EDCS-08700318.translation: Potitus, son of Ucc, Publius D[...], Marcus Se[...], Sever[...]
EDCS-08700318.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragmentary list of names, possibly associated with an official or a group, with only partial identification of individuals like Potitus and Publius.

EDCS-08700319.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Luci filius Galeria Sodalis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700319.translation: Lucius Valerius Sodalis, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700319.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the burial of Lucius Valerius Sodalis, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe.

EDCS-08700321.text_edition: \n Apollini Augusto municipii Igabrensis beneficio Imperatoris Caesaris Augusti Vespasiani civitatem Romanam consecutus cum suis per honorem Vespasiano VI consule Marcus Aelius Marci filius Niger aedilis dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700321.translation: Marcus Aelius Niger, son of Marcus, an aedile, gave and dedicated this to Apollo Augustus of the municipality of Igabrum, having obtained Roman citizenship with his family through the favor of the Emperor Caesar Augustus Vespasian, when Vespasian was consul for the sixth time.
EDCS-08700321.summary: This is a dedication to Apollo Augustus by Marcus Aelius Niger, an aedile in Igabrum, commemorating the municipality's conferral of Roman citizenship granted under Emperor Vespasian.

EDCS-08700322.text_edition: \n Dominae Daevae Valeria Compse animo lybens sic votum solvit\n
EDCS-08700322.translation: Valeria Compse willingly paid this vow to the Lady Daeva.
EDCS-08700322.summary: This is a votive inscription where Valeria Compse dedicates a vow to the goddess Daeva.

EDCS-08700323.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, likely containing a name (Fabia Pol) and a place name (Igabr), with much of the original text missing.

EDCS-08700324.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-08700324.text_edition: \n Pietati Augustae Flaminia Pale Isiaca Igabrensis huic ordo municipum municipii Igabrensium ob merita statuam decrevit quae honore accepto impensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700324.translation: Flaminia Pale Isiaca of Igabrum, [dedicated this] to Pietas Augusta; the order of citizens of the municipality of Igabrum decreed a statue to her for her merits, and upon receiving the honor, she returned the expense.
EDCS-08700324.summary: This inscription records the decree of a statue to Flaminia Pale Isiaca by the citizens of Igabrum for her merits, noting that she refunded the cost after accepting the honor.

EDCS-08700329.text_edition: \n Aquam Augustam Marcus Cornelius Auli filius Novatus Baebius Balbus praefectus fabrum tribunus militum legionis VI victricis piae fide<l=C>is flamen provinciae Baeticae perducendam de sua pecunia curavit \n
EDCS-08700329.translation: Marcus Cornelius Novatus Baebius Balbus, prefect of engineers, military tribune of the VI Victrix legion, pious and faithful, and priest of the province of Baetica, took care to bring the August water through from his own funds.
EDCS-08700329.summary: This is a record of a public works project, likely an aqueduct, funded by Marcus Cornelius Novatus Baebius Balbus, a prominent local official and priest in the province of Baetica.

EDCS-08700330.text_edition: \n Antoniae Marci filiae Rufinae Ipscensi nnorum XXXX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis sacrum Dis Manibus Antoniae Marci filiae Rufinae\n
EDCS-08700330.translation: Antonia Rufina, daughter of Marcus, of Ipscens, aged 40, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Sacred to the Divine Shades, to Antonia Rufina, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-08700330.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Rufina, daughter of Marcus, who died at the age of 40, with a dedication to the spirits of the departed.

EDCS-08700331.text_edition: \n nius Togatus Igabrensis tanus Mani libertus Igabrensis an(norum) ---XXIIII hic siti sunt sit tibi terra levis Sabina \n
EDCS-08700331.translation: [...]nius and Togatus, both of Igabrum, and Mani, freedman of Igabrum, aged [...]24, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Sabina [dedicated this].
EDCS-08700331.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of at least two individuals, one named Togatus and another freedman named Mani, with uncertain ages, dedicated by Sabina.

EDCS-08700333.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Antoniae Festae Antonius Marcus filiae maerentissimae sic V\n
EDCS-08700333.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Antonia Festa, to her most grieving daughter, from Antonius Marcus.
EDCS-08700333.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a father to his daughter, Antonia Festa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700334.text_edition: \n Marcus Fabius Marci libertus Ausua sibei sic matri fratribus Fabia mater Marcus Fabius Decumus Marcus Fabius Marci libertus Balbinus Marcus Fabius Antiatis libertus Medianus Marcus Fabius Antiatis libertus Seneca\n
EDCS-08700334.translation: Marcus Fabius Ausua, freedman of Marcus, to himself, his mother, brothers: Fabia the mother, Marcus Fabius Decumus, Marcus Fabius Balbinus, freedman of Marcus, Marcus Fabius Medianus, freedman of Antiatis, Marcus Fabius Seneca, freedman of Antiatis.
EDCS-08700334.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Fabius Ausua, who dedicated it to himself and his family, including his mother and freedmen, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700335.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iuliae Valentinae quae vixit nnos XL coniugis sanctissimae Mundicius Caecilianus l posuit\n
EDCS-08700335.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Mundicius Caecilianus, her freedman, placed this for Julia Valentina, who lived 40 years, his most devoted wife.
EDCS-08700335.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a freedman for his devoted wife, Julia Valentina, who lived 40 years, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700336.summary: A dedication to Quintus Luclenus Septumus, possibly marking a tomb or a public monument, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700337.text_edition: \n In fronte pedes XVIII in agro pedes L Titus Minucius Titi libertus Meleager hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700337.translation: In front 18 feet, in the field 50 feet. Titus Minucius Meleager, freedman of Titus, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-08700337.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the dimensions of a plot of land and the burial of Titus Minucius Meleager, a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700341.summary: A brief inscription indicating that Valerius was responsible for the completion of some work, possibly an architectural project or artwork, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700345.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning 'Carisius' twice, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700346.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Perpernas Marci filius Tuscinus Igabrensis nnorum LXXII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700346.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Perpernas Tuscinus, son of Marcus, from Igabrum, aged 72 years, pious towards his own, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-08700346.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Perpernas Tuscinus, an individual from Igabrum who died at 72, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700347.text_edition: \n Vixit Flavius Salutius annis LXIII mensibus VI recessit -- Martias in pace\n
EDCS-08700347.translation: Flavius Salutius lived 63 years and 6 months. He departed on the Ides of March, in peace.
EDCS-08700347.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and date of death of Flavius Salutius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700348.text_edition: \n Flavius Salutius vixit mensibus ? diebus XX baptidiatus sic \n
EDCS-08700348.translation: Flavius Salutius lived approximately ? months and 20 days, baptized.
EDCS-08700348.summary: A funerary epitaph noting the lifespan and baptism of Flavius Salutius, possibly indicating a Christian burial, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700352.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Secunda annorum XXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700352.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Secunda, aged 25, rests here with her family; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700352.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Secunda, who died at age 25, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700353.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Victor collegio illychiniariorum prati novi dono dat\n
EDCS-08700353.translation: Titus Flavius Victor gives this as a gift to the guild of Illyrian boatmen of the new meadow.
EDCS-08700353.summary: This inscription records a donation by Titus Flavius Victor to a guild of Illyrian boatmen, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700356.translation: Traveler, take the public road to the right.
EDCS-08700356.summary: This is a directional marker indicating a public road, possibly dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700359.text_edition: \n Tertul<l=I>a annorum XVI hic sita est \n
EDCS-08700359.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tertulla, who died at age 16, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700366.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fabia Cai filia Secunda vixit nnos LV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700366.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Fabia Secunda, daughter of Caius, lived 55 years and rests here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700366.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia Secunda, who died at age 55, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700367.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum S Compse annorum XXXX pia hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700367.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. S. Compse, aged 40, a devoted woman, rests here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700367.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for S. Compse, who died at age 40, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700369.text_edition: \n Locus pedum CXX Quintus Iulius Publi filius Galeria Rufus agri mensor Siccaenas\n
EDCS-08700369.translation: A plot of 120 feet. Quintus Iulius Rufus, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, land surveyor of Siccaenae.
EDCS-08700369.summary: This inscription identifies a land plot and its owner, Quintus Iulius Rufus, a land surveyor from Siccaenae, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700370.translation: Gaius Avillius, son of Gaius, of the Sergia tribe.
EDCS-08700370.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Gaius Avillius, son of Gaius, of the Sergia tribe, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700371.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aelia Hilarina nnum XLVII pia in suis hic sita est sit terra levis\n
EDCS-08700371.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aelia Hilarina, aged 47, a devoted woman, rests here with her family; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700371.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Hilarina, who died at age 47, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700372.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Numisius SI Stabilis PRAET TVTEIAMVLIISACI ANMNAPLEBIM BONVS RYNI\n
EDCS-08700372.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Numisius Stabilis, good man, [text illegible].
EDCS-08700372.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Numisius Stabilis, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700379.text_edition: \n Maurus Crocini libertus Igabrensis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700379.translation: Maurus, freedman of Crocinus, from Igabrum, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700379.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maurus, a freedman from Igabrum, dated likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700380.translation: [For the sake of] his years, L. Coen[ius?]
EDCS-08700380.summary: A fragmentary inscription, possibly recording the name and age of an individual, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700385.translation: To the Genius of the municipium of the Flavian Iponobenses.
EDCS-08700385.summary: A dedication to the Genius of the municipality of Iponoba, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700386.text_edition: \n Signum Victoriae Augustae Marcus Fabius Livianus pontifex mu<n=M>icipi Sosontigitani ex \n
EDCS-08700386.translation: Statue of Victory Augusta. Marcus Fabius Livianus, pontifex of the municipality of Sosontigitania, from...
EDCS-08700386.summary: The beginning of a dedication for a statue of Victory Augusta, commissioned by Marcus Fabius Livianus, a local priest, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700387.text_edition: \n Cornelius Fidi libertus Saturninus Augustalis Iponobensis dedit dedicativ\n
EDCS-08700387.translation: Cornelius Saturninus, freedman of Fidus, Augustalis from Iponoba, gave [this] as a dedication.
EDCS-08700387.summary: A dedication by Cornelius Saturninus, a freedman and local official, for a dedicatory object, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700388.summary: A dedication to Emperor Caesar Augustus, likely dating to his reign (27 BCE - 14 CE).

EDCS-08700389.text_edition: \n Titus Annius Firmus Iponobensis annorum C Vibia Crocale Patriciensis uxor annorum LXXXXIX\n
EDCS-08700389.translation: Titus Annius Firmus of Iponoba, 105 years old. Vibia Crocale of Patriciensis, his wife, 99 years old.
EDCS-08700389.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the names and remarkably long ages of Titus Annius Firmus and his wife Vibia Crocale, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700390.text_edition: \n Locus pedum quoquo versus Fulvia Pica Ipscensis Coensis pia in suis hic sita sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700390.translation: A plot of land, in all directions. Fulvia Pica of Ipscens, from Coensis, pious, placed here on her own property. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700390.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fulvia Pica, indicating her burial on her own land, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700396.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, possibly a farewell or good wish, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700406.text_edition: \n Liciniae Quinti filiae Rufinae sacerdoti perpetuae in colonia Claritate Iulia et in municipio Contributensi Ipscensi et in municipio Florentino Iliberritano amantissimae civium suorum plebs Contributensis Ipscensis ob merita eius statuam aere conlato dono dedit -Licinia Quinti filia Rufina honore accepto impensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700406.translation: The citizens of Contributensis Ipscens gave the statue in bronze collected from contributions to Licinia Rufina, daughter of Quintus, perpetual priestess in the colony Claritas Iulia and in the municipality Contributensis Ipscens and in the municipality Florentinus Iliberitanus, most beloved by her fellow citizens, for her merits. Licinia Rufina, daughter of Quintus, after receiving the honor, returned the expense.
EDCS-08700406.summary: A complex inscription recording the erection of a bronze statue for Licinia Rufina, a priestess honored in multiple communities, who then returned the cost of the statue, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700407.text_edition: \n Caio Livio Marci filio Quirina Severino decreto decurionum municipum municipii Ipscensis Marcus Livius Gracchus pater honorem accepit impensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700407.translation: By decree of the council of decurions of the municipality of Ipscens, Marcus Livius Gracchus, father, accepted the honour for his son Gaius Livius Severinus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, and remitted the expense.
EDCS-08700407.summary: This inscription records the posthumous honour granted to Gaius Livius Severinus by the local council of Ipscens, with his father bearing the cost.

EDCS-08700408.text_edition: \n Quinto Manlio Quinti liberto Paridi pleps Contributensis Ipscensis ob merita eius aere conlato statuam dono dedit huic ordo locum decrevit\n
EDCS-08700408.translation: The Contributensian plebs of Ipscens gave a statue as a gift to their freedman Quintus Manlius Paris, son of Quintus, for his services, funded by a collection; the council decreed a place for it.
EDCS-08700408.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to Quintus Manlius Paris by the local plebs of Ipscens, funded by public collection.

EDCS-08700409.text_edition: \n sacerdoti CAE Montanae ISPPCVAN SVAM dono dedit huic ordo locum decrevit\n
EDCS-08700409.translation: To the priestess of CAE Montana, she gave her SVAM as a gift; the council decreed a place for it.
EDCS-08700409.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an unspecified object ('SVAM') to a priestess named Montana by her community.

EDCS-08700410.text_edition: \n Caio Sempronio Cai filio Quirina Lucretio Salviano IIviro patrono et praestantissimo civi plebs Contributensis Ipscensis ob merita eius ex pecunia sua statuam dono dedit\n
EDCS-08700410.translation: The Contributensian plebs of Ipscens gave a statue as a gift to their patron and most outstanding citizen, Gaius Sempronius Lucretius Salvianus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, twice aedile, from their own funds, for his services.
EDCS-08700410.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to Gaius Sempronius Lucretius Salvianus, a prominent local official and patron, by the plebs of Ipscens.

EDCS-08700411.text_edition: \n Locus quoquo versus pedum XXV Avitus Serani filius annorum XX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700411.translation: A space of 25 feet in every direction. Avitus, son of Seranus, aged 20 years, is buried here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-08700411.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Avitus, aged 20, specifying the burial plot size.

EDCS-08700413.text_edition: \n Herculi invicto Aulus Licinius Glaucus D I S M\n
EDCS-08700413.translation: To the invincible Hercules. Aulus Licinius Glaucus, by the command of the gods, [dedicated this].
EDCS-08700413.summary: This is a dedication to the god Hercules by Aulus Licinius Glaucus.

EDCS-08700415.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Caesare Augusto Germanico pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate V imperatore X patre patriae consule designato IIII Optatus Reburri libertus imaginem Caesaris Augusti patris patriae imperatoris primus de sua pecunia dedit eamque cum Optato et Reburro filiis dedicavit senatus decrevit perpetuo bonis publicis interesse\n
EDCS-08700415.translation: During the pontificate of Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, holding tribunician power for the 10th time, as emperor for the 10th time, father of the country, and consul designate for the 4th time, Optatus, freedman of Reburrus, was the first to dedicate an image of Caesar Augustus, father of the country, emperor, from his own funds, and dedicated it with his sons Optatus and Reburrus; the senate decreed that it should always be displayed in public.
EDCS-08700415.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an image of Emperor Augustus by Optatus, a freedman, during the reign of Claudius, with a senatorial decree for its perpetual display.

EDCS-08700417.text_edition: \n Lucius Annius Luci Quinti libertus Philotimus Annia Luci liberta Hebene Annia Luci liberta Peregrina Lucius Annius Luci libertus Clarus Annia Luci liberta Optata hic sita est\n
EDCS-08700417.translation: Lucius Annius Philotimus, freedman of Lucius Annius, Lucius Annius Clarus, freedman of Lucius Annius, and freedwomen Annia Hebene, freedwoman of Lucius Annius, and Annia Peregrina, freedwoman of Lucius Annius, and Annia Optata, freedwoman of Lucius Annius, are buried here.
EDCS-08700417.summary: This is a collective tombstone inscription for several freedmen and freedwomen, all associated with Lucius Annius.

EDCS-08700418.text_edition: \n Fulcinia Campana nnorum XXII Caius Fulcinius Viator pater\n
EDCS-08700418.translation: Fulcinia Campana, aged 22 years. Her father, Caius Fulcinius Viator, [erected this].
EDCS-08700418.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Fulcinia Campana, erected by her father.

EDCS-08700419.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aticius Mascellio annorum VI mensium VII dierum V pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700419.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aticius Mascellio, aged 6 years, 7 months, and 5 days, a dutiful son, is buried here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-08700419.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a young boy, Aticius Mascellio, who died at the age of 6.

EDCS-08700420.text_edition: \n Te lapis optestor leviter super ossa residas ne nostro doleat conditus officio Graeca serva hic sita est vale Iacchus vir et de suo dat\n
EDCS-08700420.translation: Stone, I implore you, rest lightly upon her bones so that he who is buried here does not grieve because of our duty. Graeca is buried here. Farewell. Iacchus gives this from his own means.
EDCS-08700420.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Graeca, erected by Iacchus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700421.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription for Julia, daughter of Caius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700425.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription possibly for Flavius Felix, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700426.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Flavius Leontius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700427.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a divine command from the goddess Caelestis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700428.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing personal names, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700429.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription possibly a dedication to Lucretianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700430.text_edition: \n Marcus Pompeius Quinti filius Galeria Icstnis IIvir primus de familia Pompeia\n
EDCS-08700430.translation: Marcus Pompeius, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, first duovir from the Pompeian family.
EDCS-08700430.summary: This inscription records Marcus Pompeius, son of Quintus, who held the office of duovir, likely from the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-08700431.text_edition: \n Fabia Marci filia Aninna Marci Pompei Quinti fili\n
EDCS-08700431.translation: Fabia, daughter of Marcus, wife of Marcus Pompeius, son of Quintus.
EDCS-08700431.summary: This inscription identifies Fabia, daughter of Marcus, as the wife of Marcus Pompeius, son of Quintus, likely from the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-08700432.translation: Of Quintus Pompeius Sabinus, son of Quintus.
EDCS-08700432.summary: This inscription records Quintus Pompeius Sabinus, son of Quintus, likely a funerary or commemorative text from the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-08700433.translation: To Nanna, daughter of Quintus Pompeius.
EDCS-08700433.summary: This is a dedication to Nanna, daughter of Quintus Pompeius.

EDCS-08700434.translation: Quintus Pompeius Velaunis, son of Quintus.
EDCS-08700434.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Quintus Pompeius Velaunis.

EDCS-08700435.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Velaunis, son of Ildrons.

EDCS-08700436.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Igalghis, son of Ildrons.

EDCS-08700438.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Iunia Insghana, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-08700439.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Sisean, son of Bahannon.

EDCS-08700441.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Pompeius Cnaei filius Galeria Afer aedilis IIvir\n
EDCS-08700441.translation: Gnaeus Pompeius Afer, son of Gnaeus, of the Galeria tribe, twice duovir, aedile.
EDCS-08700441.summary: This inscription records the career of Gnaeus Pompeius Afer, who served as aedile and twice as duovir.

EDCS-08700442.text_edition: \n Iulia Marci filia Laeta sacerdos Divae Augustae\n
EDCS-08700442.translation: Iulia Laeta, daughter of Marcus, priestess of the Divine Augusta.
EDCS-08700442.summary: This inscription records Iulia Laeta as a priestess of the Divine Augusta.

EDCS-08700443.text_edition: \n Quinto Mummio Luci filio Galeria Gallo IIviro decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08700443.translation: To Quintus Mummius Gallo, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, duovir, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700443.summary: This is a dedication to Quintus Mummius Gallo, who served as duovir by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-08700444.translation: As duovir by decree of the decurions, with public funds.
