EDCS-08700444.summary: This fragment records the office of duovir, appointed by decree of the decurions and funded by public money.

EDCS-08700445.text_edition: \n Marcus Calpurnius - l(ibertus) Vernio Augustalis Calpurnia Marci liberta Thyce sic uxor Calpurnia Marci liberta Nebris filia Marcus Calpurnius Marci libertus Chryseros f(ilius) Calpurnia Marci liberta Phyramis sic filia Calpurnia Marci liberta Vitalis filia Blattia Modesta uxor\n
EDCS-08700445.translation: Marcus Calpurnius Vernio, freedman of Marcus, his freedman son Chryseros; Calpurnia Thyce, freedwoman of Marcus, wife; Calpurnia Nebris, freedwoman of Marcus, daughter; Calpurnia Phyramis, freedwoman of Marcus, daughter; Vitalis, freedwoman of Marcus, daughter; Blattia Modesta, wife.
EDCS-08700445.summary: This inscription lists members of a family, likely for a funerary monument, including freedmen and their children, originating from the household of Marcus Calpurnius.

EDCS-08700447.text_edition: \n us Claritate Iul(ia)sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700447.translation: May the earth lie lightly on Claritas, daughter of Julia.
EDCS-08700447.summary: This is a funerary epitaph wishing peace to the deceased, Claritas, daughter of Julia.

EDCS-08700448.text_edition: \n Facundus G N servus annorum XXV pius in suis sit tibi terra levis hic situs est\n
EDCS-08700448.translation: Facundus, slave of G. N., aged 25, pious to his own, may the earth lie lightly on him; he is buried here.
EDCS-08700448.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Facundus, a 25-year-old slave, who is described as pious.

EDCS-08700449.text_edition: \n Iulia mulieris lib(erta) Pelagia nnum XXXVIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700449.translation: Pelagia, freedwoman of Julia, aged 38, pious to her own, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on her.
EDCS-08700449.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pelagia, a 38-year-old freedwoman of Julia, wishing her peace.

EDCS-08700450.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording a parent-child relationship, possibly for a tombstone.

EDCS-08700452.text_edition: \n E s Nepos VIII et VII hic sit- \n
EDCS-08700452.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning two individuals named Nepos, aged 8 and 7.

EDCS-08700458.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Mallia.
EDCS-08700458.summary: This is the beginning of a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes (the spirits of the dead) by Mallia.

EDCS-08700460.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record a person's name and parentage, likely for a funerary monument.

EDCS-08700461.text_edition: \n Druso Iulio Caesari Claritas Iulia decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08700461.translation: Claritas Iulia to Drusus Julius Caesar, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700461.summary: This inscription records an honor bestowed by Claritas Iulia upon Drusus Julius Caesar, based on a decision by the town council.

EDCS-08700462.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filio divi Antonini Pii nepoti divi Hadriani pronepoti divi Traiani Parthici abnepoti divi Nervae adnepoti Lucio Septimio Severo Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico patri patriae pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate imperatori VII consuli II proconsuli fortissimo maximoque omnium principum ordo colonorum coloniae Claritatis Iuliae dedicavit Valerius Chalcidicus procurator Augusti curator rei publicae colonorum coloniae Claritatis Iuliae\n
EDCS-08700462.translation: The order of the settlers of the colony Claritas Iulia dedicated this to Imperator Caesar, son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, father of the fatherland, chief priest, with tribunician power for the seventh time, consul for the second time, proconsul, the bravest and greatest of all princes; Valerius Chalcidicus, procurator of Augustus, curator of the public affairs of the settlers of the colony Claritas Iulia.
EDCS-08700462.summary: This is a dedication by the colonists of Claritas Iulia to Emperor Septimius Severus, likely celebrating a significant event and drafted by Valerius Chalcidicus, an imperial official, during the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700463.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Severo Alexandr<o=I> invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate ---? co(n)s(uli) --- proconsuli patri patriae optim<o=I> principis nostro res publica Ucubitanorum devota numini eius \n
EDCS-08700463.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, the unconquered Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, consul, proconsul, father of the fatherland, our most excellent prince, the community of Ucubi is devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-08700463.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the community of Ucubi to Emperor Severus Alexander, likely dating to his reign (222-235 AD).

EDCS-08700467.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Lucretius Fronto Fabianus decurio Ucubitanorum nnum XVIIII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700467.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Lucretius Fronto Fabianus, decurion of Ucubi, aged 19, pious to his own, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700467.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Gaius Lucretius Fronto Fabianus, a local official in Ucubi, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700471.translation: To Petilia, daughter of Lucius, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700471.summary: This inscription records a posthumous honor bestowed by the town council upon Petilia, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-08700472.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius P Celerinus Ipolcobulculensis nnorum LXXXI pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700472.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius P. Celerinus, of Ipolcobulcula, aged 81, pious to his own, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700472.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Lucius P. Celerinus from Ipolcobulcula, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700473.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial from Lucius Argentarius to Argentaria.

EDCS-08700474.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a tombstone, mentioning the name Helvia.

EDCS-08700475.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely relating to family names or ownership.

EDCS-08700476.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Valerius Protervus -II AIAES hic situs est V ]\n
EDCS-08700476.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerius Protervus... is buried here.
EDCS-08700476.summary: This is a fragmentary tomb inscription for Valerius Protervus.

EDCS-08700478.text_edition: \n coloniae Claritatis Iuliae H M D P RECLM cum ea usque ad |(denarios) CCCCXV dedit populo nomine suo et Stertiniae Montanae filiae suae frumentum |(denarium) CLcentum quinquaginta milium contulit V mutuum rei publicae suae semen saepius praestitit\n
EDCS-08700478.translation: The colony of Claritas Iulia... gave 415 denarii to the people in his name and that of his daughter Stertinia Montana. He contributed 150,000 denarii for grain and often provided seed as a loan to his community.
EDCS-08700478.summary: This inscription records significant financial contributions by a citizen of the colony of Claritas Iulia to the community, likely during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700492.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Agathonice, likely a tombstone for a young girl.

EDCS-08700517.summary: This fragmentary inscription names Caius Appius of Ategua and likely formed part of a funerary or honorary monument.

EDCS-08700518.text_edition: \n Cacla Amatla duae gemellae annuclae mensium III hic sitae sunt\n
EDCS-08700518.translation: Cacla and Amatla, twin sisters, three months old, are buried here.
EDCS-08700518.summary: This inscription records the burial of two infant twin sisters, Cacla and Amatla, who died at three months old.

EDCS-08700519.text_edition: \n Chruse annorum XXVIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700519.translation: Chruse, 28 years old, is buried here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08700519.summary: This inscription records the burial of Chruse, who died at the age of 28, and includes a common funerary wish.

EDCS-08700520.text_edition: \n Publius Cornelius Primus nnorum XXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700520.translation: Publius Cornelius Primus, 25 years old, dutiful to his family, is buried here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08700520.summary: This inscription records the burial of Publius Cornelius Primus, who died at 25, and describes him as dutiful to his family.

EDCS-08700523.summary: This is a genitive attribution, likely referring to property or a monument belonging to Gnaeus Magnus.

EDCS-08700524.summary: This inscription attributes something to Gnaeus Magnus, referred to as emperor, possibly indicating a dedication or ownership.

EDCS-08700525.summary: This inscription attributes something to Gnaeus Magnus, referred to as emperor, possibly indicating a dedication or ownership.

EDCS-08700529.summary: This inscription simply names Iulia Melaena, identifying her as the freedwoman of a man named Gaius.

EDCS-08700532.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Augustus, acknowledging him as a patron.

EDCS-08700533.translation: To Lucius Caesar, son of Augustus, the patron.
EDCS-08700533.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Caesar, son of Augustus, acknowledging him as a patron.

EDCS-08700534.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Agrippa, who is honored as a patron, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-08700535.translation: To Pupus, son of Marcus Agrippa.
EDCS-08700535.summary: This inscription records the parentage of Pupus, son of Marcus Agrippa, likely identifying a person on a monument or tomb.

EDCS-08700536.summary: This is a dedication to Tiberius Claudius Nero, honored as a patron, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-08700538.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-08700538.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Septimi Severi Pii Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Britannici maximi filio divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi <H>adriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti Marco Aurelio Severo Pio Augusto Parthico maximo Britannico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVI patri patriae consuli III proconsuli consuli designato ob innumeras glorias eius splendidissimus ordo rei publicae Uliensium statuam faciundam dedicandamque editis circensibus censuit dedicante Marco Manio Corneliano curatore anno ET AMER CLODIANOTEPPPRIA PRIMAM\n
EDCS-08700538.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Septimius Severus Pius Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva; Marcus Aurelius Severus Pius Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, chief priest, with the tribunician power for the 16th time, father of the fatherland, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul, consul-designate. For his countless glories, the most distinguished order of the Uliensian republic resolved to have a statue made and dedicated, with games presented; dedicated by Marcus Manius Cornelius, curator, in the year ET AMER CLODIANOTEPPPRIA PRIMAM.
EDCS-08700538.summary: This extensive inscription records the decision by the Uliensian republic to erect and dedicate a statue to Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Augustus) due to his numerous achievements, detailing his extensive imperial titulature and listing the officials involved in the dedication, dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700539.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Severo Alexandro Pio Felici Augusto divi Severi Pii nepoti divi Antonini Magni filio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli patri patriae proconsuli fortissimo maximoque principi res publica Uliensium devota numini eius dedicante Quinto Fabio Fabiano curatore ---ANNIO Luci Aeli Optati et Luci Clodi Trigeti \n
EDCS-08700539.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, grandson of the deified Severus Pius, son of the deified Antoninus Magnus; chief priest, with tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul, the most valiant and greatest prince. The republic of Uliensians, devoted to his divine will, dedicated this, with Quintus Fabius Fabianus, curator, ---annius, son of Lucius Aelius Optatus and Lucius Clodius Trigetus, presiding.
EDCS-08700539.summary: This inscription records the devotion of the Uliensian republic to Emperor Severus Alexander, detailing his titles and mentioning the officials involved in the dedication, dating to his reign (222-235 CE).

EDCS-08700540.text_edition: \n Iuliae Avitae Mammeae Augustae matri domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Luci Aeli Optati et Luci Clodi Trigeti \n
EDCS-08700540.translation: To Julia Avita Mamaea Augusta, mother of our lord the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, by Lucius Aelius Optatus and Lucius Clodius Trigetus.
EDCS-08700540.summary: This inscription honors Julia Avita Mamaea, mother of Emperor Severus Alexander, identifying her with the officials Lucius Aelius Optatus and Lucius Clodius Trigetus, likely on a monument during Severus Alexander's reign (222-235 CE).

EDCS-08700541.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio Publi filio Fabiano patri aedili IIviro praefecto Cai Caesaris praefecto iterum pontifici sacrorum flam<i=E>ni divi Augusti municipes quod annon \n
EDCS-08700541.translation: To Publius Aelius, son of Publius Fabianus, father, aedile twice, duovir praefect under Caesar, prefect again, pontiff of sacred rites, flamen of the deified Augustus, by the municipes, because of the grain supply.
EDCS-08700541.summary: This inscription honors Publius Aelius Fabianus, detailing his various civic and religious offices within the municipality and noting a contribution related to the grain supply, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700542.text_edition: \n Lucio Aemilio Marci filio Marci nepoti Catoni aedili IIviro praefecto municipes et incolae\n
EDCS-08700542.translation: To Lucius Aemilius, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus Cato, aedile, duovir, prefect, by the municipes and inhabitants.
EDCS-08700542.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Aemilius, detailing his lineage and civic roles as aedile, duovir, and prefect, dedicated by the local community, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700543.text_edition: \n Quinto Caesio Marci filio Publi nepoti Publi pronepoti Galeria Hirro Aelio Patruino Fabiano aedili IIviro flamini quinquennali divorum Augustorum pontifici sacrorum perpetuo decreto decurionum municipum municipii Uliensis Quintus Caesius Hirrus Aelius Patruinus Fabianus honore accepto impensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700543.translation: To Quintus Caesius, son of Marcus, grandson of Publius, great-grandson of Publius, Galeria, Hirrus Aelius Patruinus Fabianus, aedile, duovir, quinquennial flamen of the deified Augusti, perpetual pontiff of sacred rites, by decree of the decurions and members of the municipality of Uliensis. Quintus Caesius Hirrus Aelius Patruinus Fabianus, having accepted the honor, waived the expense.
EDCS-08700543.summary: This inscription records the honors bestowed upon Quintus Caesius Hirrus Aelius Patruinus Fabianus by the municipality of Uliensis for his civic and religious service, and his subsequent remission of expenses associated with the honor, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700544.text_edition: \n Lucio Calpurnio Luci filio Galeria Danquino IIviro municipes et incolae\n
EDCS-08700544.translation: To Lucius Calpurnius, son of Lucius, Galeria Danquinus, duovir, by the municipes and inhabitants.
EDCS-08700544.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Calpurnius Danquinus, identifying him as a duovir and indicating the dedication was made by the local citizens, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700545.text_edition: \n Lucio Fabio Luci filio Galeria Tusco IIviro municipes et incolae honoris liberalitatisque ergo\n
EDCS-08700545.translation: To Lucius Fabius Tuscus, son of Lucius, twice a duumvir, from the fellow citizens and residents, in honor of his generosity.
EDCS-08700545.summary: This inscription is a dedication from the townspeople and residents to Lucius Fabius Tuscus, likely in recognition of his generosity as a local official, probably from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08700546.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely dedicating a monument or honors to Emperor Caesar Augustus, probably from the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE.

EDCS-08700547.translation: To Nio, son of Quintus, [his] brother.
EDCS-08700547.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription recording a dedication to a man named Nio, son of Quintus, and identifying him as the recipient's brother, likely a funerary dedication from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08700548.text_edition: \n Publius Annius Publi filius Avitus nnum XVII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis huic mater de suo monimentum fecit Acilia Sexti filia Cordilla pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Marcus Valerius Severus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700548.translation: Publius Annius Avitus, son of Publius, aged 17, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His mother, Acilia Cordilla, daughter of Sextus, pious, had this monument made for her own burial place. Marcus Valerius Severus is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700548.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Publius Annius Avitus, aged 17, with a monument erected by his mother, and also noting the burial of Marcus Valerius Severus, from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08700549.text_edition: \n Lucia Segris nnorum XXXII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Quintus Valerius Quinti libertus Restitutus vir cum potuero veniam nnorum LX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700549.translation: Lucia Segris, aged 32, pious, is buried here in her own plot. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Quintus Valerius Restitutus, freedman of Quintus, his husband, will come when he can, aged 60, pious, is buried here in his own plot. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700549.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucia Segris, aged 32, and Quintus Valerius Restitutus, aged 60, from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08700550.text_edition: \n Philomela nnorum XXXV pia in suis hic sita sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700550.translation: Philomela, aged 35, pious, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700550.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Philomela, aged 35, from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08700551.text_edition: \n Suriaca nnorum XXXXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700551.translation: Suriaca, aged 45, pious, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700551.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Suriaca, aged 45, from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08700558.text_edition: \n patris patriae E Publius Manlius Publi filius Publi nepos Galeria \n
EDCS-08700558.translation: Father of the Fatherland. Publius Manlius, son of Publius, grandson of Publius, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-08700558.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Publius Manlius and his ancestry, including the honorific title 'father of the fatherland', likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-08700559.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription possibly dedicating a monument or shrine to the deified Emperor Augustus, likely from the 1st century CE.

EDCS-08700569.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus G Hermes annorum XXXX pius in <s>(uis) hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700569.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus G Hermes, aged 40, pious, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700569.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording the burial of Quintus G Hermes, aged 40, from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08700571.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Patricia.
EDCS-08700571.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the divine shades by Patricia.

EDCS-08700572.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius Luci filius Galeria Niger IIvir pontufex sic sacrorum in municipio hic situs est Lucius Calpurnius Luci filius Galeria Danquinus aedilis IIvir praefectus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700572.translation: Lucius Cornelius Niger, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, twice-a-year magistrate and pontifex, keeper of sacred rites in the municipality, lies here. Lucius Calpurnius Danquinus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, twice-a-year magistrate, and prefect, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700572.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucius Cornelius Niger and Lucius Calpurnius Danquinus, both local magistrates, with a wish for a light earth.

EDCS-08700573.text_edition: \n Lucio Lentulo Caio Marcello consulibus Quinto Cassio Cai filio Longino tribuno plebis pro praetore Binsnes Vercellonis filius Xvir maxsumus sic Marcus Coranus Acrini filius Alpis aedilis portam faciundam coeraverunt de sua pecunia\n
EDCS-08700573.translation: In the consulship of Lucius Lentulus and Gaius Marcellus, while Quintus Cassius Longinus, son of Gaius, was tribune of the plebs and propraetor, Binsnes, son of Vercellonis, chief decurion, and Marcus Coranus, son of Acrinus Alpis, aedile, saw to the construction of the gate at their own expense.
EDCS-08700573.summary: A public works inscription detailing the construction of a gate by Binsnes and Marcus Coranus during the consulship of Lentulus and Marcellus.

EDCS-08700576.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Octavia nnorum XXXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700576.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Octavia, pious, lies here in her own property, aged 40. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700576.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the divine shades for Octavia, who lies in her own property at the age of 40.

EDCS-08700577.translation: To Lucius Valerius and Valeria Flavia.
EDCS-08700577.summary: A dedication or memorial inscription for Lucius Valerius and Valeria Flavia.

EDCS-08700578.text_edition: \n Sabdaeus vilicus nnum LIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700578.translation: Sabdaeus, the steward, aged 53, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700578.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Sabdaeus, a steward, aged 53.

EDCS-08700579.text_edition: \n ------] Imo SI us Fir vixit annos
EDCS-08700579.summary: A fragmentary inscription, part of a funerary text, recording a person who lived for...

EDCS-08700581.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum <L=I>(ucius) Caesius Aelenus sic annorum XXXVIIII Sabetanus pius in suis hic situs <e=F>(st) sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08700581.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Caesius Aelenus, a pious man in his own property, aged 39, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700581.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the divine shades for Lucius Caesius Aelenus, who died aged 39.

EDCS-08700582.text_edition: \n In fronte pedes XII in agro pedes X Cornelia Faustilla nnorum XXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700582.translation: Frontage 12 feet, depth 10 feet. Cornelia Faustilla, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700582.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Cornelia Faustilla, aged 25, with dimensions of her plot.

EDCS-08700584.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Servilius Felicissimus A XXX \n
EDCS-08700584.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Servilius Felicissimus, aged 30.
EDCS-08700584.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the divine shades for Servilius Felicissimus, aged 30.

EDCS-08700585.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Antonia, daughter of Marcus, likely on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-08700586.text_edition: \n ------] C Sa pius suis sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08700586.translation: [May] the earth lie lightly upon you, C. Sapius, for your own [people].
EDCS-08700586.summary: This is a funerary inscription for C. Sapius, wishing him a light burial.

EDCS-08700588.text_edition: \n MA EQV sto fili Nicisne ne mortal sit tibi terra levis anima bona pater ann(orum) - pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700588.translation: MA EQV sto, son of Nicis, [let] the earth lie lightly upon you. Good soul, father [of the deceased], pious in his own [family], is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700588.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of EQV sto, son of Nicis, and notes his father's piety.

EDCS-08700592.translation: To Caius, son of Gaius Mar[cus].
EDCS-08700592.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caius, son of Gaius Mar[cus].

EDCS-08700593.summary: This is a possessive inscription, likely indicating ownership or a dedication by Publius Manlius Eutychetes.

EDCS-08700601.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum NECAN VS IC N C nnorum XXV pius in suis H S hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700601.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. NECAN VS IC N, 25 years old, pious in his own [family], is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700601.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of NECAN VS IC N at 25 years old and is dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-08700602.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Of Quintus Publius.
EDCS-08700602.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Divine Manes and records the name Quintus Publius.

EDCS-08700603.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Valeria da annorum \n
EDCS-08700603.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Valeria [died], [aged] [...] years.
EDCS-08700603.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the Divine Manes and records the name Valeria and her age.

EDCS-08700624.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Firmanus annorum XXXXVIIII pius in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700624.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Firmanus, 49 years old, pious in his own [family], may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700624.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the Divine Manes for Firmanus, who died at 49 years old.

EDCS-08700625.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Phile annorum XXIIII mensium II dierum XX pia in suis hic sita sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700625.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Phile, 24 years, 2 months, and 20 days old, pious in her own [family], may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700625.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the Divine Manes for Phile, detailing her age at death as 24 years, 2 months, and 20 days.

EDCS-08700626.summary: This is a fragment of a tombstone or epitaph, likely recording the name Valentius and a brief statement about his life.

EDCS-08700627.text_edition: \n fratri piissimo cuius iuventu satis FECI\n
EDCS-08700627.translation: To my most devoted brother, whose youth I have satisfied [with this monument]
EDCS-08700627.summary: This is an epitaph, possibly on a tomb, dedicated by an individual to their beloved brother.

EDCS-08700628.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a genitive inscription indicating ownership or dedication, belonging to Gaius Valerius Pollio.

EDCS-08700629.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Felix anima bona pius in suis \n
EDCS-08700629.translation: Sacred to the Departed Spirits. Felix, a good soul, devoted to her own people.
EDCS-08700629.summary: This is an epitaph dedicated to Felix, described as a devoted and good soul, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700632.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gaius Accius Balbinus pius in suis annorum XVIII mensium IV dierum XXII H S hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700632.translation: Sacred to the Departed Spirits. Gaius Accius Balbinus, devoted to his own people, aged 18 years, 4 months, and 22 days. Here he lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700632.summary: This is a complete epitaph for Gaius Accius Balbinus, detailing his age and expressing a wish for his peaceful rest, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700633.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum s Parfus pius hic sit- est\n
EDCS-08700633.translation: Sacred to the Departed Spirits. S. Parfus, devoted, lies here.
EDCS-08700633.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph for S. Parfus, indicating he lies here and was devoted, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700635.summary: This is a votive inscription or dedication made for the well-being of someone named Domitian.

EDCS-08700637.summary: This is a religious dedication to the goddess Panthea Isis.

EDCS-08700638.translation: Porcia Sia, devoted to her own people.
EDCS-08700638.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph, likely for Porcia Sia, describing her as devoted to her family.

EDCS-08700645.text_edition: \n Marti Augusto Marcus Valerius Herennianus Ipagrensis VIvir Augustalis K M \n
EDCS-08700645.translation: To Mars Augustus. Marcus Valerius Herennianus of Ipagre, VIvir Augustalis.
EDCS-08700645.summary: This is a dedication to Mars Augustus by Marcus Valerius Herennianus, a local magistrate (VIvir Augustalis) from Ipagre, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700646.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio D<rusi> filio Caesari Augusto Germanico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate imperatori patri patriae censori Lucius Cornelius Luci filius D D S\n
EDCS-08700646.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of Drusus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, imperator, father of the country, censor. Lucius Cornelius, son of Lucius, dedicated this by decree of the council.
EDCS-08700646.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Claudius, likely on a public monument, dating to his reign (41-54 AD).

EDCS-08700647.text_edition: \n Lucio Cornelio Gallo ordo Ipagrensis statuam fieri iussit Lucius Cornelius Lentulus honore accepto impensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700647.translation: The council of Ipagra ordered a statue to be made for Lucius Cornelius Gallus. Lucius Cornelius Lentulus, upon receiving the honor, remitted the expense.
EDCS-08700647.summary: This records a decision by the council of Ipagra to erect a statue for Lucius Cornelius Gallus, with a donation from Lucius Cornelius Lentulus.

EDCS-08700648.text_edition: \n In fronte pedes XI in agro pedes VIII AVG Sisenae fili- annorum XIIII hic sit- est\n
EDCS-08700648.translation: In front 11 feet, in the field 8 feet. AVG Sisena's son, aged 14 years, is buried here.
EDCS-08700648.summary: This is a funerary marker indicating the dimensions of a burial plot and identifying the deceased as the 14-year-old son of AVG Sisena.

EDCS-08700649.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelia BRITAOCC PAT ann LXXIII \n
EDCS-08700649.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Cornelia Brita, aged 73 years.
EDCS-08700649.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Cornelia Brita, who died at the age of 73.

EDCS-08700650.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Cornelius Gallus pater nnrum LIII <et=IF> Sempronia Luci filia Semproniana nnum XXXXIIII Ipagrenses pii in suis hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis \n
EDCS-08700650.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Cornelius Gallus, father, aged 53 years, and Sempronia, daughter of Lucius, Semproniana, aged 44 years, residents of Ipagra, pious towards their own, are buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08700650.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a married couple, Lucius Cornelius Gallus and Sempronia Semproniana, residents of Ipagra, with their ages.

EDCS-08700652.summary: This is a brief dedication or ownership marking, possibly for a tomb or other object, belonging to Gebrilius Iulianus.

EDCS-08700654.text_edition: \n Numini Tiberi Caesaris Augusti FLACVS Fidentinus\n
EDCS-08700654.translation: To the divine power of Tiberius Caesar Augustus. Flaccus Fidentinus [dedicated this].
EDCS-08700654.summary: This is a dedication to the divine power of Emperor Tiberius by Flaccus Fidentinus.

EDCS-08700655.text_edition: \n Germanico Caesari Tiberi filio divi Augusti nepoti Divi Iuli pronepoti auguri flamini Augustali consuli iterum imperatori iterum \n
EDCS-08700655.translation: To Germanicus Caesar, son of Tiberius, grandson of the deified Augustus, great-grandson of the deified Julius, augur, flamen Augustalis, consul for the second time, imperator for the second time.
EDCS-08700655.summary: This is an honorific inscription listing the titles and lineage of Germanicus Caesar, likely part of a larger monument.

EDCS-08700657.text_edition: \n Marcus Fulvius Icarus Pontuficiensis medicus ocularius sibi et suis fecit\n
EDCS-08700657.translation: Marcus Fulvius Icarus of Pontuficia, an eye doctor, made this for himself and his family.
EDCS-08700657.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Fulvius Icarus, an eye doctor from Pontuficia, for himself and his family.

EDCS-08700658.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph stating that Iucundus is buried in this location.

EDCS-08700659.text_edition: \n Aecia Quinti liberta Nice locus pedum XV\n
EDCS-08700659.translation: Aucta, freedwoman of Quintus Aecius, plot of 15 feet.
EDCS-08700659.summary: This inscription marks a plot of land measuring 15 feet, allocated by Aucta, a freedwoman of Quintus Aecius.

EDCS-08700660.text_edition: \n Aecia Quinti liberta Nice locus pedum XV\n
EDCS-08700660.translation: Aucta, freedwoman of Quintus Aecius, plot of 15 feet.
EDCS-08700660.summary: This inscription marks a plot of land measuring 15 feet, allocated by Aucta, a freedwoman of Quintus Aecius.

EDCS-08700661.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Allius Veteranus nnorum LX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi ter<r=P>a levis \n
EDCS-08700661.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Allius Veteranus, 60 years old, pious in his own [burial place], lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700661.summary: This is a tombstone epitaph for Allius Veteranus, who died at the age of 60, with a standard plea for the earth to be light on his remains.

EDCS-08700662.text_edition: \n Sum Cerbio em fili Gerio vale subduc te pater ai sic misera decollata som sic uxor Mastale et tu ere suma sic em importuna timio sic ne vectem frangam selvam sic grave \n
EDCS-08700662.translation: I am here, Cerbius, son of Gerius, farewell. Pull yourself together, father. O, miserable beheaded one, farewell. And you, wife, take him. Farewell, importunate Timio. Farewell, so that I may not break the beam of the wild [place?]. Farewell, it is heavy.
EDCS-08700662.summary: This inscription appears to be a lament or epitaph, possibly related to a violent death, featuring a plea and farewells from a deceased individual to family members and acquaintances.

EDCS-08700664.text_edition: \n Arrena Erotis liberta Aucta et Petronia Luci liberta Philematium hic sitae sunt\n
EDCS-08700664.translation: Aucta, freedwoman of Erotis, and Philematium, freedwoman of Lucius Petronius, lie here.
EDCS-08700664.summary: This inscription records the burial of two women, Aucta and Philematium, both freedwomen belonging to different patrons.

EDCS-08700665.text_edition: \n Cerialis hic situs est pius in suis nnorum XXV sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700665.translation: Cerialis lies here, pious in his own [burial place], 25 years old. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700665.summary: This tombstone epitaph records the burial of Cerialis, who died at the age of 25, with the customary wish for a light grave.

EDCS-08700666.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Felicla serva nni I mensum VIIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700666.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Felicla, a servant, 1 year and 9 months old, pious in her own [burial place], lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700666.summary: This tombstone epitaph commemorates Felicla, a servant who died at the very young age of 1 year and 9 months.

EDCS-08700667.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Sura serva nnrum XXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700667.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Sura, a servant, 30 years old, pious in her own [burial place], lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700667.summary: This tombstone epitaph records the burial of Sura, a servant who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-08700670.text_edition: \n Gavia Procla Luci filia <an=LF>(norum) XVII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08700670.translation: Gavia Procla, daughter of Lucius, 17 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700670.summary: This tombstone epitaph records the burial of Gavia Procla, daughter of Lucius, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-08700671.text_edition: \n Petronia Cai filia Anulla annorum XXX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700671.translation: Petronia Anulla, daughter of Caius, 30 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700671.summary: This tombstone epitaph records the burial of Petronia Anulla, daughter of Caius, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-08700672.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Firmian- annorum XX pi- in suis hic sit- est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700672.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Firmiana, aged 20, has been placed here in her own tomb. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700672.summary: A funerary epitaph for Firmiana, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700675.text_edition: \n Marcus <F=E>uficius Marci libertus <R=E>ufinus <F=E>uficia Marci liberta Copi \n
EDCS-08700675.translation: Marcus Euficius Rufinus, freedman of Marcus, and Fuficia Copia, freedwoman of Marcus.
EDCS-08700675.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the names and familial relationships of two freedpersons, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700680.text_edition: \n In fronte pedes XVIII in agro pedes XXXVI Marcus Fuficius Marci libertus Quietus Augustalis coloniae Augustae Firmae annorum LXI Marcus Fuficius Marci libertus Lybicus Augustalis coloniae Augustae Firmae Fuficia Marci liberta Maurilla uxor Fuficia CINMA locus quoquo versus pedes XXXV\n
EDCS-08700680.translation: In front 18 feet, in the field 36 feet. Marcus Fuficius Quietus, freedman of Marcus, Augustalis of the colony of Augusta Firma, aged 61. Marcus Fuficius Lybicus, freedman of Marcus, Augustalis of the colony of Augusta Firma. Fuficia Maurilla, wife, freedwoman of Marcus. Fuficia Cinna. Land 35 feet in every direction.
EDCS-08700680.summary: A funerary boundary inscription specifying land dimensions and listing family members, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700682.text_edition: \n Genio municipii Florentinorum Marcus Servilius Onesimus ob honorem seviratus \n
EDCS-08700682.translation: To the Genius of the people of Florentia, Marcus Servilius Onesimus, for the honor of the office of sevir.
EDCS-08700682.summary: A dedication made by Marcus Servilius Onesimus to the Genius of Florentia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700683.text_edition: \n Furiae Sabiniae Tranquillinae Augustae coniugi Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Antonii Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti ordo municipii Florentini Iliberritani devotus numini maiestatique sumptu publico posuit decurionum decreto\n
EDCS-08700683.translation: To Furia Sabinia Tranquillina, Augustan consort of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus. The town council of Florentia Iliberritana, devoted to his divine majesty, erected this at public expense by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700683.summary: A public monument dedicated by the town council of Florentia Iliberritana to the Empress Furia Sabinia Tranquillina, wife of Gordian III, likely from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700685.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Probo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto numini maiestatique eius devotus ordo Iliberritanorum dedicatissimi sic decurionum decreto\n
EDCS-08700685.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, devoted to his divine majesty. The town council of Iliberris dedicates this most devotedly, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700685.summary: A public monument dedicated by the town council of Iliberris to Emperor Probus, likely from 276-282 CE.

EDCS-08700686.text_edition: \n Publio Cornelio Publi filio Galeria Anullino Iliberritano praefecto urbi consuli proconsuli provinciae Africae praetori tribuno plebis quaestori legato provinciae Narbonensis proconsuli provinciae Baeticae legato legionis VII geminae piae felicis legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Baetiae curatori alvei Tiberis et riparum \n
EDCS-08700686.translation: To Publius Cornelius Anullinus, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, of Iliberris, prefect of the city, consul, proconsul of the province of Africa, praetor, tribune of the plebs, quaestor, legate of the province of Narbonensis, proconsul of the province of Baetica, legate of Legio VII Gemina Pia Felix, legate of Augustus pro praetore of the province of Baetica, curator of the Tiber River and its banks.
EDCS-08700686.summary: A public inscription honoring Publius Cornelius Anullinus, listing his extensive career of public offices, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700687.text_edition: \n Corneliae Publi filiae Severinae flaminicae Augustae matri Valerii Vegeti consulis Florentini Iliberritani decurionum decreto\n
EDCS-08700687.translation: To Cornelia Severina, daughter of Publius, Augustan flaminica, mother of Valerius Vegetus, consul of Florentia Iliberritana. Erected by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700687.summary: A public monument dedicated by the decurions of Florentia Iliberritana to Cornelia Severina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700688.text_edition: \n Etriliae Afrae Valerii Vegeti consulis Florentini Iliberritani decurionum decreto\n
EDCS-08700688.translation: To Etrillia Afra, wife of Valerius Vegetus, consul of Florentia Iliberritana. Erected by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700688.summary: A public monument dedicated by the decurions of Florentia Iliberritana to Etrillia Afra, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700689.text_edition: \n Gnaeo Papirio filio Galeria Aeliano Aemilio Tuscillo quaestori provinciae Achaiae tribuno plebis praetori legato Augusti legionis XIIII geminae? \n
EDCS-08700689.translation: To Gnaeus Papirius Aelianus Tuscillus, son of Gnaeus, of the Galeria tribe, quaestor of the province of Achaia, tribune of the plebs, praetor, legate of Augustus of Legio XIIII Gemina?
EDCS-08700689.summary: An inscription commemorating Gnaeus Papirius Aelianus Tuscillus, listing his Roman magistracies and military commands, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700693.text_edition: \n Lucio Galerio Luci filio Ga<l=IO>(eria) Valeriano IIviro po<nt=VI>(ifici) perpetuo <muni=NN>c<ip=LL>i <fl=TT>orentini <I=P>lib<e=F>ritani \n
EDCS-08700693.translation: To Lucius Galerius Valerianus, son of Lucius, of the Galerian tribe, perpetual pontiff, municipal magistrate of Florentia, and freedman of the city.
EDCS-08700693.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Galerius Valerianus, a prominent citizen and official of Florentia, likely dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-08700694.text_edition: \n filia Patricia ob ho<n=M>orem sacerdotii epulo populo dato dedit dedicavit \n
EDCS-08700694.translation: His daughter Patricia, in honor of the priesthood, gave and dedicated a feast to the people.
EDCS-08700694.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Patricia, daughter of the deceased, who provided a public feast in honor of a priesthood, probably erected in the early imperial period.

EDCS-08700696.text_edition: \n --- lib(ertus) Sergia Perseus ob honorem VIviratus fori et basilicae intercolumnia cum cancellis et trabaeculis sic et postibus pecunia sua exornata <d>edit\n
EDCS-08700696.translation: Sergia Perseus, freedwoman, for the honor of the six-man magistracy of the forum and basilica, decorated the intercolumniations with their beams and cornices, and also the doorposts, with his own money.
EDCS-08700696.summary: This inscription details a private donation by Sergia Perseus, a freedwoman, for the ornamentation of the forum and basilica in honor of her holding a VIviratus office, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-08700698.text_edition: \n Lucio Atilio Luci filio Hibero decurionum decreto\n
EDCS-08700698.translation: To Lucius Atilius Liber, son of Lucius, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700698.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Atilius Liber, erected by a decree of the local council, suggesting a public honor in a Roman town, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08700699.text_edition: \n Lucio Cornelio Luci filio Galeria Flacco Fabia Hygia mater loco accepto decurionum decreto posuit\n
EDCS-08700699.translation: Fabia Hygia, mother, by decree of the decurions, placed [this] in the accepted location for Lucius Cornelius Flaccus, son of Lucius, of the Galerian tribe.
EDCS-08700699.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Fabia Hygia in memory of her son Lucius Cornelius Flaccus, by decree of the town council, probably from the imperial period.

EDCS-08700700.text_edition: \n Corneliae Luci filiae Cornelianae Publius Valerius Lucanus uxori indulgentissimae donum dedit loco dato decurionum decreto\n
EDCS-08700700.translation: Publius Valerius Lucanus gave this gift, by decree of the decurions, for his exceedingly loving wife Cornelia, daughter of Lucius, of the Cornelian tribe, in the place granted.
EDCS-08700700.summary: This inscription records a donation by Publius Valerius Lucanus to his wife Cornelia, made by decree of the decurions, likely a funerary monument from the imperial period.

EDCS-08700701.text_edition: \n Publio Manilio Publi filio Galeria Urbano decurionum decreto Manilia Publi filia Tertulla soror honore usa impensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700701.translation: By decree of the decurions, Manilia Tertulla, daughter of Publius, sister, having enjoyed the honor, remitted the expense for Publius Manilius Urbanus, son of Publius, of the Galerian tribe.
EDCS-08700701.summary: This inscription indicates that Manilia Tertulla, after holding an honor, waived the associated costs for Publius Manilius Urbanus, by decree of the decurions, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08700703.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aemilia Romula nnum XXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700703.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aemilia Romula, aged 25, pious, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700703.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Aemilia Romula, who died at age 25, and includes a standard epitaph wishing her peace, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700704.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Annia Donata vixit annosVIII menses VII\n
EDCS-08700704.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Annia Donata lived 8 years and 7 months.
EDCS-08700704.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Annia Donata, recording her young age at death, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700705.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai filius Galeria Severus annorum L Marcus Iulius Cai filius Galeria Fronto annorum <L=I>XV Marcus Iulius Marci filius Galeria Front--- ann(orum) -XXIII Marcus Iulius Cai filius Galeria Severus \n
EDCS-08700705.translation: Gaius Julius Severus, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, aged 50. Marcus Julius Fronto, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, aged 15. Marcus Julius [Severus?], son of Marcus, of the Galerian tribe, aged 23. Gaius Julius Severus, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe.
EDCS-08700705.summary: This inscription lists members of the Julius family, possibly all from the same household or lineage, noting their names, patronymics, tribal affiliation, and ages, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700717.text_edition: \n Socii quinquagensimae anni Tenati Silvini donum dant\n
EDCS-08700717.translation: The comrades of Tenatius Silvinus, in his fiftieth year, give this gift.
EDCS-08700717.summary: This inscription records a gift made by comrades to Tenatius Silvinus on his fiftieth birthday.

EDCS-08700719.text_edition: \n Publio Manlio Publi filio Galeria Manliano Iliberritano huic ordo publice locum sepulturae impensam funeris statuam decrevit Publius Manlius Pax pater honore accepto impensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700719.translation: The town council decreed a burial plot, funeral expenses, and a statue for Publius Manlius Gallus, son of Publius, of the Galerian tribe, from Iliberri; Publius Manlius Pax, his father, having accepted the honor, remitted the expenses.
EDCS-08700719.summary: This inscription records a posthumous honor decreed by a town council for Publius Manlius Gallus and the father's repayment of funeral expenses.

EDCS-08700721.summary: This is a simple dedication to Publicia Laetina, likely for a tomb or shrine.

EDCS-08700723.text_edition: \n Imperatoris Domi[tiani] Caesaris Augusti Germanici Finis callis publici\n
EDCS-08700723.translation: Boundary of a public road, belonging to the Emperor Domitian Caesar Augustus Germanicus.
EDCS-08700723.summary: This inscription marks the boundary of a public road under the authority of Emperor Domitian.

EDCS-08700732.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Verecunda annorum XXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700732.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Verecunda, aged thirty, pious to her family, lies here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08700732.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Verecunda, recording her age and piety.

EDCS-08700733.text_edition: \n Publius Cornelius Publi filius Quirina Gallicus IIvir Ilurconensis Statam Matrem cum suis ornamentis de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700733.translation: Publius Cornelius Gallicus, son of Publius, of the Quirine tribe, twice a magistrate of Ilurconensis, gave and dedicated Statam Mater with her adornments from his own funds.
EDCS-08700733.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue of Statam Mater by Publius Cornelius Gallicus, a local magistrate.

EDCS-08700739.translation: Publius Iunius Bo, son of Bo.
EDCS-08700739.summary: This is a simple dedication, likely for a tomb, of Publius Iunius Bo.

EDCS-08700740.text_edition: \n Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio Divi Iuli nepoti Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XXVIII consuli IIII imperatori VIII auguri XVviro sacris faciundis VIIviro epulonum Titus Papirius Severus\n
EDCS-08700740.translation: Titus Papirius Severus [dedicated this to] Tiberius Caesar, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power 28 times, consul 4 times, imperator 8 times, augur, and member of the college of fifteen for sacred rites, and member of the college of seven for feasts.
EDCS-08700740.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Titus Papirius Severus to Emperor Tiberius, listing his numerous titles and offices.

EDCS-08700742.text_edition: \n Lucio Aelio Commodo Augusti filio decreto decurionum Ilurconensium anno Cai Anni Senecae et Quinti Corneli Macri IIvirum\n
EDCS-08700742.translation: By decree of the town council of Ilurconenses, in the year when Caius Annius Seneca and Quintus Cornelius Macr were duumvirs, [this was dedicated to] Lucius Aelius Commodus, son of Augustus.
EDCS-08700742.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by the town council of Ilurconenses to Lucius Aelius Commodus during a specific consular year.

EDCS-08700743.text_edition: \n ------] Tiberius Plautius tinus Tiberi Plauti Silvani Aeliani consulis II praefecti urbis libertus ndam curavit\n
EDCS-08700743.translation: [...], freedman of Tiberius Plautius Tinus Tiberi Plauti Silvani, twice consul and urban prefect, arranged for this to be built.
EDCS-08700743.summary: This inscription records that a freedman of a prominent official oversaw the construction of this monument.

EDCS-08700744.text_edition: \n Fabiae Broccillae decreto ordinis Ilurconensis Lucius Fabius Avitus pater honore accepto impensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700744.translation: At the decree of the town council of Ilurco, Lucius Fabius Avitus, the father, having received the honor, remitted the expense.
EDCS-08700744.summary: A decree of the town council of Ilurco recording the remission of expenses by Lucius Fabius Avitus, likely in connection with a public honor.

EDCS-08700745.translation: Publius Cornelius, son of Publius, of Ilurco.
EDCS-08700745.summary: A simple dedication or identification naming Publius Cornelius, son of Publius, from Ilurco.

EDCS-08700746.translation: To Perpetuus, son of Longinus, of Ilurco.
EDCS-08700746.summary: A dedication to Perpetuus, son of Longinus, of Ilurco.

EDCS-08700747.text_edition: \n Urcestar Tascaseceris filius Ilurconensis annorum LXXXVII sit tibi terra levis Ni<g=C>ellus filius impensa sua curavit \n
EDCS-08700747.translation: Urcestar, son of Tascaseceris, of Ilurco, aged 87. May the earth lie lightly on you. His son Nigellus had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-08700747.summary: A funerary epitaph for Urcestar, son of Tascaseceris, an 87-year-old resident of Ilurco, erected by his son Nigellus.

EDCS-08700748.translation: Lucius Aemilius Antiochus, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-08700748.summary: An identification or dedication of Lucius Aemilius Antiochus, a freedman of Lucius.

EDCS-08700749.text_edition: \n Barbara Flori liberta hic sita est annorum XXXV te rogo praeteriens ut legas et dicas sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700749.translation: Barbara, freedwoman of Florus, lies here, aged 35. I ask you, as you pass by, to read this and say, 'May the earth lie lightly on you.'
EDCS-08700749.summary: A tomb inscription for Barbara, freedwoman of Florus, aged 35, who asks passersby to wish her peace.

EDCS-08700750.text_edition: \n Lucius C F sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700750.translation: Lucius, son of Gaius. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700750.summary: A funerary epitaph identifying Lucius, son of Gaius, with a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-08700751.summary: A simple statement indicating that Cornelius Ruso commissioned or created the object this inscription is on.

EDCS-08700752.text_edition: \n Marcus Valerius Marci libertus Cissus Valeriae Marci libertae Phryni\n
EDCS-08700752.translation: Marcus Valerius Cissus, freedman of Marcus, [for] Valeria Phryne, freedwoman of Marcus.
EDCS-08700752.summary: A dedication by Marcus Valerius Cissus, freedman of Marcus, for Valeria Phryne, freedwoman of Marcus.

EDCS-08700759.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly indicating a dedication by Primus to someone named Valerius.

EDCS-08700760.text_edition: \n ------] Bremusa annorum XXV pia in suis hic sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700760.translation: To Bremusa, aged 25, dutiful to her family, may the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-08700760.summary: A funerary epitaph for Bremusa, who died at age 25.

EDCS-08700764.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Eutychia nnorum XXVIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700764.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Eutychia, aged 28, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700764.summary: A funerary epitaph for Eutychia, who died at age 28.

EDCS-08700765.text_edition: \n Titus Papirius Severus Laribus vialibus votum solvit\n
EDCS-08700765.translation: Titus Papirius Severus fulfilled his vow to the household gods of the roads.
EDCS-08700765.summary: A dedication to Laribus vialibus (household gods of roads) by Titus Papirius Severus, likely recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08700767.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Aemilius Cantabrinus Sexsitanus nnorum LXXII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700767.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Aemilius Cantabrinus of Sexsesta, aged 72, dutiful to his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700767.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Aemilius Cantabrinus of Sexsesta, who died at age 72.

EDCS-08700768.text_edition: \n Caius Iunius Sev<e>rus Caleculensis annorum LXXX amici et convictores cinus posuerunt hic situs est sit terra levis\n
EDCS-08700768.translation: Caius Iunius Severus of Calecula, aged 80, his friends and drinking companions erected this. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-08700768.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iunius Severus of Calecula, erected by his friends and drinking companions.

EDCS-08700769.text_edition: \n Annia Cai filia Rustica annorum XXII hic sita est sit terra levis\n
EDCS-08700769.translation: Annia Rustica, daughter of Caius, aged 22, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700769.summary: A funerary epitaph for Annia Rustica, who died at age 22.

EDCS-08700770.text_edition: \n Annia Cai filia Rustica annorum XXII h<i=L>c sita est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08700770.translation: Annia Rustica, daughter of Caius, aged 22, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700770.summary: A funerary epitaph for Annia Rustica, who died at age 22.

EDCS-08700771.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Murria Crescentina Ilurconensis annorum CXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700771.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Murria Crescentina of Ilurco, aged 115, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700771.summary: A funerary epitaph for Murria Crescentina of Ilurco, who died at the remarkable age of 115.

EDCS-08700772.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Octavius Cnensis nnorum pius in suis huic ordo decrevit funeris impensam \n
EDCS-08700772.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Octavius of Cnesis, dutiful to his family, the town council decreed the expenses of his funeral for him here.
EDCS-08700772.summary: A funerary inscription where the town council decreed the expenses for the funeral of Lucius Octavius of Cnesis.

EDCS-08700773.text_edition: \n filius Flavianus annorum XIIX filius Luxianus annorum LXV filia Sila annorum Rufa filio et viro\n
EDCS-08700773.translation: Flavianus, aged 18, son Luxianus, aged 65, and Rufa, daughter, for her son and husband.
EDCS-08700773.summary: A fragmented inscription possibly listing family members and their ages, with the last line indicating a dedication for a son and husband.

EDCS-08700776.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Iulius Lev<i=T>s nnm ockto<gi=CT>nta XXXXXXXX sic \n
EDCS-08700776.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Julius Levus, octogenarian. [--------]
EDCS-08700776.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Julius Levus, a man who lived to be eighty, with illegible text following.

EDCS-08700780.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marinus annorum XII mensium VIIII dierum XV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis loco IV\n
EDCS-08700780.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marinus, aged twelve years, nine months, and fifteen days, pious in his own (grave), is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Location IV.
EDCS-08700780.summary: A funerary inscription for a young boy named Marinus, detailing his age at death and burial location.

EDCS-08700781.text_edition: \n Grapte Cai P R serva annorum XIIX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700781.translation: Grapte, slave of Caius P.R., aged eighteen years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700781.summary: A funerary inscription for Grapte, a slave girl, detailing her age and burial.

EDCS-08700782.text_edition: \n Templum Herculis quot sic Lucius Vibius Fetialis rei publicae Osquensi promiserat receptis a re publica HS VI milibus Lucius Vibius Fetialis nepos eius fecit et cum signo Herculis dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700782.translation: Lucius Vibius Fetialis, his grandson, made and dedicated the Temple of Hercules, with its statue, which Lucius Vibius Fetialis had promised to the Republic of the Oscan people, having received 6,000 sesterces from the Republic.
EDCS-08700782.summary: A dedication of a Temple of Hercules by Lucius Vibius Fetialis, honoring his grandfather's promise to the Republic of Oscan people.

EDCS-08700784.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Fabius Luci filius Quirina Chrysippus Obulconensis dedit\n
EDCS-08700784.translation: Lucius Fabius, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, from Obulco, dedicated this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-08700784.summary: A dedication of an offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Fabius Chrysippus of Obulco.

EDCS-08700785.text_edition: \n Iuliae Augustae matri castrorum coniugi Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici res publica Ulisitana decurionum decreto\n
EDCS-08700785.translation: The Republic of Ulisitanus, by decree of the decurions, [dedicated this] to Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, wife of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus.
EDCS-08700785.summary: A dedication by the municipality of Ulisitanus to Empress Julia Augusta, wife of Emperor Septimius Severus, likely by official decree.

EDCS-08700786.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Terentius Fabianus Ulisitanus I<Ivi=TV>r annorum XXXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08700786.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Terentius Fabianus Ulisitanus, aged forty-five years, pious in his own (grave), is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700786.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Terentius Fabianus of Ulisitanus, detailing his age and burial.

EDCS-08700787.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Iunius Barbarus Ulisitanus decurio annorum XXII \n
EDCS-08700787.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Iunius Barbarus Ulisitanus, a decurion, aged twenty-two years.
EDCS-08700787.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Iunius Barbarus, a decurion from Ulisitanus, aged twenty-two.

EDCS-08700788.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gaius Fabius Adventus Ulisitanus annorum XXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700788.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Fabius Adventus Ulisitanus, aged thirty years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700788.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Fabius Adventus of Ulisitanus, detailing his age and burial.

EDCS-08700789.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Fabius Carpus Ulisitanus annorum LXXXXII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700789.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Fabius Carpus Ulisitanus, aged ninety-two years, pious in his own (grave), is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700789.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Fabius Carpus of Ulisitanus, detailing his age and burial.

EDCS-08700790.summary: A woman named Cornelia erected this monument.

EDCS-08700791.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Faventinus servus Augustorum nnum LXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700791.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Faventinus, a servant of the emperors, aged seventy-five, pious, rests here with his own; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700791.summary: A funerary inscription for Faventinus, a servant of the emperors, who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-08700792.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum M F Pollentina annorum LXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700792.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Pollentina, daughter of M. F., aged seventy, pious, rests here with her own; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700792.summary: A funerary inscription for Pollentina, daughter of M. F., who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-08700793.text_edition: \n Hutos apoleson Marcum Domitium Nigrum Firmanae filium\n
EDCS-08700793.translation: Hutos, the son of Apleson, [dedicated this to] Marcus Domitius Niger, son of Firmana.
EDCS-08700793.summary: An inscription recording a dedication by Hutos to Marcus Domitius Niger.

EDCS-08700794.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio divo Traiano Optumo sic Augusto Germanico Dacico Parthico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XXI imperatori XIII consuli VI pater sic patriae optumo sic maxumoque sic principi conservatori generis humani res publica Aratispitanorum decrevit divo dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700794.translation: The Republic of the Aratispitani decreed and dedicated this to the divine Trajan Optimus Augustus, conqueror of Germany, Dacia, and Parthia, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 21st time, imperator for the 13th time, consul for the 6th time, father of the fatherland, the best and greatest, prince of conservatives of the human race.
EDCS-08700794.summary: A decree by the Republic of Aratispitani dedicating a monument to Emperor Trajan, dating to his 21st year of tribunician power (c. 117 AD).

EDCS-08700795.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVI consuli III patri patriae res publica Aratispitana decrevit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700795.translation: The Republic of Aratispitana decreed and dedicated this to Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 16th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-08700795.summary: A decree by the Republic of Aratispitana dedicating a monument to Emperor Hadrian, dating to his 16th year of tribunician power (c. 132 AD).

EDCS-08700796.text_edition: \n Publio Nonio Asprenate Marco Aquila Iuliano consulibus Quintus Lucius Fenestella hospitium fecit cum senatu populoque civitatis Aratispitanae eumque sic liberos posterosque eorum in fidem clientelamque suam liberorum posterorumque suorum recepit egit Publius Vibius Rufinus magistratus\n
EDCS-08700796.translation: In the consulship of Publius Nonius Asprenas and Marcus Aquila Julianus, Quintus Lucius Fenestella made a guest-friendship agreement with the senate and people of the Aratispitana community, and received himself, his children, and their descendants into his protection and patronage, and that of his children and descendants. This was acted upon by the magistrate Publius Vibius Rufinus.
EDCS-08700796.summary: A record of a hospitality agreement between Quintus Lucius Fenestella and the community of Aratispita, during the consulship of Nonius Asprenas and Aquila Julianus (likely 31 AD).

EDCS-08700797.text_edition: \n Marco Fulvio Senecioni Aratispitano optumo et praestantissumo civi ob merita eius amici posuerunt Publius Licinius Aemilianus Publius Licinius R Lucius Licinius Vibianus Lucius Licinius Licinianus Lucius Fabius Silvinus Caius Fabius Fabianus Lucius Fabius Optatus Marcus Iunius Montanus Marcus Iunius Martialis Marcus Iunius Maternus Marcus Flavius Maxuminus Lucius Flavius Severus Caius Calpurnius Celer Caius Calpurnius Perpetuus Caius Aemilius Anthus Titus nnius Dionysius Lucius Aurelius Capratinus\n
EDCS-08700797.translation: For Marcus Fulvius Senecio of Aratispita, an excellent and most outstanding citizen, his friends erected this in recognition of his merits: [list of names].
EDCS-08700797.summary: Friends erected this monument to honor Marcus Fulvius Senecio of Aratispita for his outstanding civic contributions.

EDCS-08700798.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Licinius Licinianus Aratispitanus vixit annis LXXVII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis L R P\n
EDCS-08700798.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Licinius Licinianus of Aratispita lived seventy-seven years, pious, rests here with his own; may the earth lie lightly on you. L.R.P.
EDCS-08700798.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Licinius Licinianus of Aratispita, who died at the age of 77.

EDCS-08700799.text_edition: \n Quintus Porcius Felicio et Quintus Porcius Rufus aram Martis de sua pecunia dederunt dedicaverunt\n
EDCS-08700799.translation: Quintus Porcius Felicio and Quintus Porcius Rufus dedicated this altar of Mars with their own money and dedicated it.
EDCS-08700799.summary: Quintus Porcius Felicio and Quintus Porcius Rufus dedicated an altar to Mars at their own expense.

EDCS-08700800.text_edition: \n Gaio Licinio Agrino Osquensi IIviro bis Gaius Licinius Agrippinus filius optumo patri accepta exedra ab ordine municipum municipii Osquensis statuam cum ornamentis exedrae dato epulo dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700800.translation: To Gaius Licinius Agrippinus, son of Gaius Licinius Agrippinus, twice chief magistrate of the Osquenses, for his most excellent father, Gaius Licinius Agrippinus, the order of the municipium of Osquensis, having received a portico, gave and dedicated a statue with its adornments, following the provision of a banquet.
EDCS-08700800.summary: This is a dedication of a statue and portico by the municipium of Osquensis to the father of a prominent citizen, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08700801.text_edition: \n Gaio Licinio Agrino Osquensi Gaius Licinius Agrippinus pater statuam dato epulo dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700801.translation: To Gaius Licinius Agrippinus, father of Gaius Licinius Agrippinus, Gaius Licinius Agrippinus gave and dedicated a statue following the provision of a banquet.
EDCS-08700801.summary: This records the dedication of a statue by Gaius Licinius Agrippinus to his father, Gaius Licinius Agrippinus, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08700803.text_edition: \n Sentia Luci filia Materna ex suo testamento fieri iussit Porciae Publi filiae Augustinae - Porcius Luci filius Quirina Clemens posuit\n
EDCS-08700803.translation: Sentia Maternus, daughter of Lucius, ordered this to be made from her estate in her will; Porcius Clemens, son of Lucius, Quirina tribe, erected it to Porcia Augustina, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-08700803.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a woman's bequest for her tomb and the erection of the monument by another individual, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700804.translation: To our kinsman Hebenus, his freedman.
EDCS-08700804.summary: This is a dedication by a freedman to his kinsman, likely a tombstone inscription, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700805.text_edition: \n Publius Coelius filius Rutilus IV F A N hic sit- \n
EDCS-08700805.translation: Publius Coelius Rutilus, son of Publius, IV F A N, is here.
EDCS-08700805.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Publius Coelius Rutilus, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700806.text_edition: \n Genio municipi Antikariensis Iulia Marci filia Cornelia Materna mater Marci Corneli Agricolae testamento poni iussit\n
EDCS-08700806.translation: To the spirit of the municipium of Antikaria, Iulia Cornelia Materna, daughter of Marcus, mother of Marcus Cornelius Agricola, ordered this to be placed by her will.
EDCS-08700806.summary: This records a woman's testamentary wish to dedicate a monument to the genius of the town of Antikaria, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700807.text_edition: \n Sextus Peducaeius Sexti filius Herophilus Isi Sarapi donum dedit libens merito\n
EDCS-08700807.translation: Sextus Herophilus, son of Sextus Peducaeus, gave this gift to Isis and Serapis, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08700807.summary: This is a votive offering to the gods Isis and Serapis by Sextus Herophilus, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700808.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Silvia Marci filia Severiana ex voto\n
EDCS-08700808.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Silvia Severiana, daughter of Marcus, by her vow.
EDCS-08700808.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Silvia Severiana, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700809.text_edition: \n Numini Augusti Cornelius Sedigitus de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700809.translation: Cornelius Sedigitus dedicated this to the divine will of Augustus from his own funds.
EDCS-08700809.summary: This is a dedication to the deified Augustus by Cornelius Sedigitus, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08700811.text_edition: \n Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio Divi Iuli nepoti Augusto imperatori pontifici maxumo sic tribunicia potestate XXVI consuli IIII\n
EDCS-08700811.translation: To Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of Divus Iulius, emperor, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 26th time, consul for the 4th time.
EDCS-08700811.summary: This is an inscription detailing the titles and honors of Emperor Tiberius, dating to 26 CE.

EDCS-08700812.text_edition: \n Iuliae Augustae Drusi filiae divi Augusti matri Tiberi Caesaris Augusti principis et conservatoris et Drusi Germanici genetricis orbis Marcus Cornelius Proculus pontufex sic Caesarum\n
EDCS-08700812.translation: To Julia Augusta, daughter of Drusus, mother of the divine Augustus, mother of the emperor Tiberius Caesar Augustus, the preserver, and mother of Drusus Germanicus, Marcus Cornelius Proculus, pontifex, set this up in honor of the Caesars.
EDCS-08700812.summary: This inscription is a dedication honoring Julia Augusta, mother of Emperor Tiberius, erected by Marcus Cornelius Proculus, a pontifex, likely during the Julio-Claudian period.

EDCS-08700813.text_edition: \n Germanico Caesari Tiberi Augusti filio divi Augusti nepoti Divi Iuli pronepoti consuli II imperatori II auguri flamini Augustali Marcus Cornelius Proculus pontufex sic Caesarum\n
EDCS-08700813.translation: To Germanicus Caesar, son of Tiberius Augustus, grandson of the divine Augustus, great-grandson of the divine Julius, consul for the second time, imperator for the second time, flamen Augustalis, Marcus Cornelius Proculus, pontifex, set this up in honor of the Caesars.
EDCS-08700813.summary: This inscription honors Germanicus Caesar, son of Emperor Tiberius, erected by Marcus Cornelius Proculus, a pontifex, likely during the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08700814.text_edition: \n Druso Caesari Tiberi Augusti filio divi Augusti nepoti Divi Iuli pronepoti tribunicia potestate II consuli II pontufex sic Cornelius Bassus pontufex Caesarum de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700814.translation: To Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius Augustus, grandson of the divine Augustus, great-grandson of the divine Julius, with tribunician power for the second time, consul for the second time, Cornelius Bassus, pontifex, dedicated this at his own expense.
EDCS-08700814.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to Drusus Caesar, son of Emperor Tiberius, by Cornelius Bassus, a pontifex, likely during the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08700818.text_edition: \n Lucio Calpurnio Senecioni Terentia Luci liberta Felicula testamento poni iussit Fabia Luci filia Fabulla soror et heres dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700818.translation: Terentia Felicula, freedwoman of Lucius Terentius, ordered this to be set up in her will; Fabia Fabulla, daughter of Lucius Fabius, sister and heir, dedicated it.
EDCS-08700818.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Terentia Felicula ordered a monument in her will, which was then dedicated by her sister and heir, Fabia Fabulla.

EDCS-08700819.text_edition: \n Terentia Luci liberta Felicula filio Hispaniano poni iussit Fabia Fabulla soror et heres dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700819.translation: Terentia Felicula, freedwoman of Lucius Terentius, ordered this to be set up for her son Hispanianus; Fabia Fabulla, sister and heir, dedicated it.
EDCS-08700819.summary: This funerary inscription records that Terentia Felicula arranged for a monument for her son Hispanianus, which was dedicated by her sister and heir, Fabia Fabulla.

EDCS-08700820.text_edition: \n Publio Quintio Publi filio Hospiti Antikariensi sic Hospitalis filio Publius Quintius Hospitalis de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700820.translation: Publius Quintius Hospitalis, son of Publius Quintius Hospitius from Antikaria, dedicated this at his own expense to his son, Publius Quintius Hospitalis.
EDCS-08700820.summary: This inscription records Publius Quintius Hospitalis dedicating a monument at his own expense to his son of the same name, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-08700821.text_edition: \n Quintiae Publi filiae Calvinae Antikariensi sic Hospitalis filiae Publius Quintius Hospitalis de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700821.translation: Publius Quintius Hospitalis dedicated this at his own expense to his daughter Quintia Calvina from Antikaria.
EDCS-08700821.summary: This inscription records Publius Quintius Hospitalis dedicating a monument at his own expense to his daughter Quintia Calvina, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-08700822.text_edition: \n Quintiae Publi filiae Gallae Antikariensi sic Hospitalis filiae Publius Quintius Hospitalis de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700822.translation: Publius Quintius Hospitalis dedicated this at his own expense to his daughter Quintia Galla from Antikaria.
EDCS-08700822.summary: This inscription records Publius Quintius Hospitalis dedicating a monument at his own expense to his daughter Quintia Galla, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-08700823.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Licinia Logas Osquensis nnorum XXXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700823.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Licinia Logas from Oskos, aged 35, rests here in her own plot; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700823.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Licinia Logas of Oskos at the age of 35.

EDCS-08700824.text_edition: \n Nomas Patriciensis nnorum pia in suis hic sita est \n
EDCS-08700824.translation: Nomas Patriciensis rests here in her own plot.
EDCS-08700824.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Nomas Patriciensis.

EDCS-08700825.text_edition: \n Lucius Pompeius Rufus Limicus annorum XXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Lucius Calpurnius Vegetus Limicus annorum XVI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700825.translation: Lucius Pompeius Rufus Limicus, 30 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Lucius Calpurnius Vegetus Limicus, 16 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700825.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the deaths of two individuals, Lucius Pompeius Rufus Limicus and Lucius Calpurnius Vegetus Limicus.

EDCS-08700826.text_edition: \n Lucius Argentarius Nymphio Lucio Argentario patri Arescusae sorori se vivo in loco monimentum de suo posuit\n
