EDCS-08700826.translation: Lucius Argentarius Nymphio, while still alive, dedicated this monument in this place at his own expense to his father Lucio Argentario and his sister Arescusa.
EDCS-08700826.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Argentarius Nymphio for his deceased father and sister, erected during his lifetime.

EDCS-08700827.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fabio Gai liberto Paulo alumno dulcissimo nnorum V pio in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700827.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. For Paulus, freedman of the gods, the dearest adopted son, 5 years old, pious, lies here in his own place. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700827.summary: A funerary epitaph for Paulus, a young boy, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-08700829.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Ianuarius nnum LXXX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700829.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Ianuarius, 80 years old, pious, lies here in his own place. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700829.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ianuarius, an elderly man.

EDCS-08700834.text_edition: \n Sacrum Dianae aram Marius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08700834.translation: Sacred to Diana. Marius gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08700834.summary: A votive inscription recording Marius fulfilling a vow to the goddess Diana.

EDCS-08700835.text_edition: \n Libertatis Augustae signum cum sua basi Caius Fabius Cai filius Quirina Fabianus pecunia sua dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700835.translation: Gaius Fabius Fabianus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, dedicated and consecrated the symbol of Imperial Liberty with its base at his own expense.
EDCS-08700835.summary: A dedication of a statue representing Imperial Liberty, funded and erected by Gaius Fabius Fabianus.

EDCS-08700836.text_edition: \n Marcus Cornelius Primigenius Singiliensis ob beneficium quod ab ordine Singiliensi locum acceperam in quo statuam ponerem Marci Corneli Saturnini filii mei lupam cum infantibus duobus dedi dedicavi\n
EDCS-08700836.translation: Marcus Cornelius Primigenius Singiliensis dedicated and consecrated, because of the favor he received from the council of Singilia, a statue of my son, Marcus Cornelius Saturninus, a she-wolf with two cubs, in the place where I could set it up.
EDCS-08700836.summary: A dedication of a statue group (she-wolf with cubs) by Marcus Cornelius Primigenius Singiliensis to honor his son, Marcus Cornelius Saturninus, and to thank the council of Singilia.

EDCS-08700837.text_edition: \n Marti Augusto Lucius Iunius Maurus Larum Augustorum magister dedit Iunia Maurina filia dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700837.translation: To Mars Augustus. Lucius Iunius Maurus, magistrate of the Augustan Lararium, dedicated it. Iunia Maurina, his daughter, consecrated it.

EDCS-08700839.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VI imperatori VI sic consuli III patri patriae Marcus Acilius Cai filius Quirina Ruga Singiliensis de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700839.translation: Marcus Acilius Ruga, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe from Singilia, dedicated and consecrated at his own expense to the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontiff maximum, with tribunician power for the sixth time, consul for the sixth time, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-08700839.summary: An official dedication to Emperor Hadrian by Marcus Acilius Ruga, listing the Emperor's titles and powers, likely during his sixth consulship.

EDCS-08700840.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Augusto tribunicia potestate VI \n
EDCS-08700840.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, with tribunician power for the sixth time.
EDCS-08700840.summary: An incomplete inscription recording a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, mentioning his sixth tribunician power.

EDCS-08700841.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Imperatori Flavio Valerio Constantio Pio Felici invicto Augusto res publica Barbensium dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700841.translation: The community of the Barbensians dedicated this to our lord, the Emperor Flavius Valerius Constantius, the Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-08700841.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by the community of Barbensians to Emperor Constantius. It is likely from the 4th century AD.

EDCS-08700842.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Valerio Liciniano Licinio Pio Felici invicto Augusto \n
EDCS-08700842.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Valerius Licinianus Licinius, the Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-08700842.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Licinius, likely from the early 4th century AD, indicating his imperial titles.

EDCS-08700843.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Imperatori Caesari [[]] iano Pio Felici? invicto Augusto res publica Barbensium dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700843.translation: The community of the Barbensians dedicated this to our lord, the Emperor Caesar [---]ianus, the Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-08700843.summary: This incomplete inscription records a dedication by the Barbensians to an emperor whose name is partially lost, likely from the 4th century AD.

EDCS-08700844.text_edition: \n Publio Magnio Quinti filio Quirina Rufo Magoniano tribuno militum IIII procuratori Augusti XX hereditatium per Hispaniam Baeticam et Lusitaniam item procuratori Augusti per Baeticam ad kalendarium Vegetianum item procuratori Augusti provinciae Baeticae ad ducena Acilia Plecusa amico optimo et bene de provincia semper merito dono dat\n
EDCS-08700844.translation: Acilia Plecusa, an excellent friend, always deserving of the province, gave this as a gift to Publius Magnilius Rufus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, military tribune, procurator of Augustus in charge of 20% inheritance tax for all of Spain and Lusitania, also procurator of Augustus in Baetica for the Vegetian calendar, and also procurator of the province of Baetica for revenues of 200,000 sesterces.
EDCS-08700844.summary: This inscription records a gift presented by Acilia Plecusa to Publius Magnilius Rufus, detailing his various procuratorial appointments and military service, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08700846.text_edition: \n Carviliae Publi filiae Censonillae Magni Rufi procuratoris Augusti XX hereditatium per Hispaniam Baeticam et Lusitaniam item procuratoris Augusti per Baeticam ad kalendarium Vegetianum item procuratoris Augusti provinciae Baeticae ad ducena Acilia Plecusa amicae optimae dono dat\n
EDCS-08700846.translation: Acilia Plecusa gave this as a gift to Carvilia Censonilla, daughter of Publius, wife of Magnus Rufus, her excellent friend, who was procurator of Augustus in charge of 20% inheritance tax for all of Spain and Lusitania, also procurator of Augustus in Baetica for the Vegetian calendar, and also procurator of the province of Baetica for revenues of 200,000 sesterces.
EDCS-08700846.summary: This inscription records a gift from Acilia Plecusa to Carvilia Censonilla, the wife of Magnus Rufus, honoring her and referencing her husband's procuratorial roles, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08700847.text_edition: \n Gaio Vallio Maxumiano procuratori Auggustorum egregio viro ordo Singiliensis Barbensis ob municipium diutina obsidione et bello Maurorum liberatum patrono curantibus Gaio Fabio Rustico et Lucio Aemilio Pontiano\n
EDCS-08700847.translation: The council of Singiliensis Barbensis gave this to Gaius Vallius Maxumianus, an outstanding man and an excellent procurator of the Augusti, as patron, because he freed the municipality from a long siege and war with the Moors, with Gaius Fabius Rusticus and Lucius Aemilius Pontianus overseeing.
EDCS-08700847.summary: This inscription records the honorific dedication by the council of Singiliensis Barbensis to their patron, Gaius Vallius Maxumianus, for freeing them from a siege and war with the Moors, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08700848.text_edition: \n Manio Acilio Quirina Frontoni Singiliensi Barbensi praefecto fabrum decreto decurionum municipum municipii Singiliensis Barbensis Acilia Plecusa patrono et marito honore accepto impensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700848.translation: Acilia Plecusa repaid the expense after receiving the honor, to Manius Acilius Fronton, of the Singiliensis Barbensis tribe, prefect of the craftsmen, by decree of the decurions of the free municipality of Singiliensis Barbensis, her patron and husband.
EDCS-08700848.summary: This inscription records Acilia Plecusa's repayment of expenses for an honor bestowed upon her patron and husband, Manius Acilius Fronton, by the local council, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08700849.text_edition: \n Lucio Clodio liberi Singiliensis Barbensis L(ucius) Clodius Montanus B pontifex perpetuus municipum municipii liberi Singiliensis editis circiensibus \n
EDCS-08700849.translation: Lucius Clodius Montanus B, perpetual pontiff of the citizens of the free municipality of Singiliensis, gave these public games in the circus, to Lucius Clodius, freedman of Singiliensis Barbensis.
EDCS-08700849.summary: This inscription records Lucius Clodius Montanus B, a perpetual pontiff, funding public games for the citizens of Singiliensis, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08700850.text_edition: \n Marco Hirrio Marci filio Quirina Anniano IIviro cives et incolae municipii liberi Singiliensis ex aere conlato ob merita dono dederunt\n
EDCS-08700850.translation: The citizens and inhabitants of the free municipality of Singiliensis gave this as a gift, from a money collection, to Marcus Hirrius Annianus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, duumvir, for his merits.
EDCS-08700850.summary: This inscription records a gift from the citizens and inhabitants of Singiliensis to the duumvir Marcus Hirrius Annianus for his services, funded by a public collection, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08700851.text_edition: \n Lucio Memmio Quirina Severo aedili IIviro decreto decurionum Lucius Memmius Severus honore usus impensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700851.translation: Lucius Memmius Severus repaid the expense after receiving the honor, to Lucius Memmius Severus, aedile and duumvir, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700851.summary: This inscription records Lucius Memmius Severus repaying the costs associated with an honor bestowed upon him by the decurions in his roles as aedile and duumvir, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08700852.text_edition: \n Gaio Mummio Gai filio Quirina Hispano pontifici cives et incolae municipes municipii Flavii liberi Singiliensis ex aere conlato ob merita dederunt\n
EDCS-08700852.translation: To Gaius Mummius Hispanus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, priest, the citizens and residents of the free municipality of Singiliensis, from a common fund, gave this for his merits.
EDCS-08700852.summary: This inscription records a statue or honor dedicated by the citizens and residents of Singiliensis to Gaius Mummius Hispanus, a priest, in recognition of his services.

EDCS-08700853.text_edition: \n Marco Valerio Marci filio Marci nepoti Gai pronepoti Quirina Proculino IIviro municipum municipii liberi Singiliensis cives et incolae ex aere conlato hic in IIviratu publicos ludos et totidem dierum privatos dedit item populum universum in municipio habitantem et incolas oleo et balineo gratuito dato pervocavit item quo die ludos iuvenum in theatro dedit gymnasium et balinea viris et mulieribus gratuita praestitit huic cives et incolae pridie Kalendas Ianuarias abeunti e IIviratu ob rem publicam bene atministratam sic consensu omnium in foro publice gratias egerunt et hostias quas inmolaret item statuam ex aere conlato dederunt ordo decreto locum eligere permisit IIvir Aulo Cornelio Palma Frontoniano II Publio Calvisio Tullo consulibus\n
EDCS-08700853.translation: To Marcus Valerius Proculinus, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, great-grandson of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, twice chief magistrate of the free municipality of Singiliensis, the citizens and residents, from a common fund, gave this; he, during his term as chief magistrate, provided public games and as many private games, and also invited the entire population of the municipality and residents to oil and baths freely provided; also on the day he held youth games in the theatre, he provided a gymnasium and baths for men and women freely; to him, on the day before the Kalends of January, as he left his office, the citizens and residents publicly gave thanks in the forum for his well-administered public service, and also gave him sacrificial victims and a bronze statue from a common fund; the council permitted him to choose the location; in the consulship of Aulus Cornelius Palma Frontonianus and Publius Calvisius Tullus.
EDCS-08700853.summary: This inscription details the generous public benefactions of Marcus Valerius Proculinus, a chief magistrate of Singiliensis, including games, baths, and gymnasiums, for which he was publicly thanked and honored with a statue.

EDCS-08700854.text_edition: \n Lucio Iunio Notho VIviro Augustali perpetuo cives Singilienses et incolae ex aere conlato\n
EDCS-08700854.translation: To Lucius Junius Nothus, perpetual Six-Lictor of the Augustals, the citizens and residents of Singilienses, from a common fund, [gave this].
EDCS-08700854.summary: This inscription records a dedication, likely a statue, by the citizens and residents of Singilienses to Lucius Junius Nothus, a perpetual Six-Lictor of the Augustals.

EDCS-08700855.text_edition: \n Lucio Iunio Notho ordo Singiliensium statuam et honores quos cuique plurimos libertino decrevit\n
EDCS-08700855.translation: To Lucius Junius Nothus, the council of Singiliensium decreed him a statue and the greatest honors that could be granted to a freedman.
EDCS-08700855.summary: This inscription states that the council of Singiliensium decreed honors and a statue to Lucius Junius Nothus, recognizing him with the highest possible honors for a freedman.

EDCS-08700856.text_edition: \n Caio Sempronio Nigellioni VIviro Augustali in colonia Patricia et in municipio Singiliensi VIviro Augustali perpetuuo decreto decurionum municipi municipum Singiliensium honorem accepit impensam remisit huic ordo Singiliensis recepto in civium numerum quantum cui<que> plurimum libertino decrevit item huic ordo Singiliensis vetus eadem quae supra in universum decreverat suo quoque nomine decrevit\n
EDCS-08700856.translation: To Gaius Sempronius Nigellio, Six-Lictor of the Augustals in the colony Patricia and perpetual Six-Lictor of the Augustals in the municipality Singiliensis, he accepted the honor by decree of the decurions of the municipality of the citizens of Singiliensis and waived the expense; to him, the council of Singiliensis, after admitting him to citizenship, decreed the greatest honors that could be granted to any freedman; also to him, the old council of Singiliensis decreed the same things as it had decreed above for him in general, also in its own name.
EDCS-08700856.summary: This inscription details honors bestowed upon Gaius Sempronius Nigellio by the councils of both the colony Patricia and the municipality of Singiliensis, recognizing him as a perpetual Six-Lictor of the Augustals.

EDCS-08700858.text_edition: \n Lucius Clodius Montanus Singiliensis vetus ob honorem atus solum basilicae marmore stravit marmoratum cum aereis valvis aeratis adituum IIII adscensus quae restituerant diviso epulo \n
EDCS-08700858.translation: Lucius Clodius Montanus of Singiliensis, an old man, for the honor of the [public building], paved the basilica floor with marble, along with bronze-clad doors, the four entrances and the ascent, which they had restored, after dividing the feast.
EDCS-08700858.summary: This inscription records Lucius Clodius Montanus's contribution to the restoration of a basilica in Singiliensis, specifically paving the floor and providing bronze-clad doors.

EDCS-08700859.text_edition: \n Manio Acilio Phlegonti Singiliensi Barbensi Acilia Plecusa mater dedit dedicavit huic ordo sanctissimus Singiliensis Barbensis ornamenta decurionalia decrevit\n
EDCS-08700859.translation: To Manius Acilius Phlegon of Singiliensis Barbensis, his mother Acilia Plecusa dedicated and consecrated this; the most venerable council of Singiliensis Barbensis decreed him decurional ornaments.
EDCS-08700859.summary: This inscription records that Acilia Plecusa dedicated something to her son Manius Acilius Phlegon of Singiliensis Barbensis, to whom the council decreed decurional ornaments.

EDCS-08700860.text_edition: \n Aciliae Mani filiae Septuminae! Singiliensi Barbensi decreto decurionum municipum municipii Singiliensis Barbensis Acilia Plecusa mater honore accepto impensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700860.translation: To Acilia Septumina, daughter of Manius, of Singiliensis Barbensis, her mother Acilia Plecusa, having accepted the honor, waived the expense, by decree of the decurions of the municipality of Singiliensis Barbensis.
EDCS-08700860.summary: This inscription records that Acilia Septumina, daughter of Manius, was honored by the council of Singiliensis Barbensis, and her mother Acilia Plecusa paid the associated expenses.

EDCS-08700861.text_edition: \n Marco Cornelio Marci filio Saturnino Singiliensi huic ordo Singiliensis locum dedit decrevit Marcus Cornelius Primigenius pater et Attia Titulla mater posuerunt\n
EDCS-08700861.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Saturninus, son of Marcus, of Singiliensis, the council of Singiliensis gave him a place and decreed [this]; Marcus Cornelius Primigenius, father, and Attia Titulla, mother, placed this.
EDCS-08700861.summary: This inscription records that the council of Singiliensis granted a plot of land to Marcus Cornelius Saturninus, and his parents placed the monument.

EDCS-08700862.text_edition: \n Corneliae Blandinae Singiliensi Lucius Cornelius Themison pater et Cornelia Blanda mater posuerunt huic ordo municipum municipii liberi Singiliensis inpensam funeris et locum sepulturae decrevit\n
EDCS-08700862.translation: To Cornelia Blandina of Singiliensis, Lucius Cornelius Themison, father, and Cornelia Blanda, mother, placed this; the council of the free municipality of Singiliensis decreed her the expenses for the funeral and a burial place.
EDCS-08700862.summary: This inscription records that the council of the free municipality of Singiliensis granted funeral expenses and a burial plot to Cornelia Blandina, as indicated by her parents who erected the monument.

EDCS-08700863.text_edition: \n Marco Hirrio Prolixo cives et incolae municipii liberi Singiliensis ex aere conlato ob merita dono dederunt\n
EDCS-08700863.translation: The citizens and residents of the free municipality of Singilia, with funds collected from their contributions, presented this gift to Marcus Hirrius Prolixus in recognition of his services.
EDCS-08700863.summary: A public monument likely honoring Marcus Hirrius Prolixus for his merits to the free municipality of Singilia, funded by its citizens and residents.

EDCS-08700864.text_edition: \n Lolliae Luci filiae Marcianae decreto decurionum municipum municipii liberi Singiliensis Lucius Lollius Luci filius Aelianus et Calpurnia Clementina parentes et Lucius? Iunius Nothus Cornelius Quietinus maritus honore accepto impensam remiserunt\n
EDCS-08700864.translation: By decree of the decurions of the free municipality of Singilia, Lucius Lollius Aelianus (son of Lucius, grandson of Lucius) and Calpurnia Clementina, parents, and Lucius (?) Iunius Nothus Cornelius Quietinus, husband, accepted this honor and waived the expenses.
EDCS-08700864.summary: A decree by the local council of Singilia honoring Lollia Marciana, whose parents and husband agreed to the honor but covered the associated costs.

EDCS-08700866.text_edition: \n Manio Acilio Frontoni Singiliensi Barbensi nepoti Aciliae Plecusae\n
EDCS-08700866.translation: To Manius Acilius Frontonus of Singilia and Barbens, grandson of Acilia Plecusa.
EDCS-08700866.summary: A dedication likely from a tombstone honoring Manius Acilius Frontonus, noting his parentage and connection to Acilia Plecusa.

EDCS-08700867.text_edition: \n Aciliae Sedatae Septuminae sic Singiliensi Barbensi nepti Aciliae Plecusae\n
EDCS-08700867.translation: To Acilia Sedata Septumina, likewise of Singilia and Barbens, granddaughter of Acilia Plecusa.
EDCS-08700867.summary: A dedication likely from a tombstone honoring Acilia Sedata Septumina, noting her origin and connection to Acilia Plecusa.

EDCS-08700868.text_edition: \n Gaio Fabio Gai filio Quirina Fabiano Singiliensi II \n
EDCS-08700868.translation: To Gaius Fabius Fabianus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, of Singilia (elected twice?).
EDCS-08700868.summary: A dedication likely from a tombstone for Gaius Fabius Fabianus, noting his tribal affiliation and origin in Singilia.

EDCS-08700869.text_edition: \n Rutiliae Fructuosae Lucius Iunius Nothus uxori\n
EDCS-08700869.translation: To Rutilia Fructuosa, from Lucius Iunius Nothus, his wife.
EDCS-08700869.summary: A dedication likely from a tombstone for Rutilia Fructuosa, set up by her husband Lucius Iunius Nothus.

EDCS-08700870.text_edition: \n Singiliensis Ar fecit sibi et IRIDII \n
EDCS-08700870.translation: Ar fecit sibi et IRIDII [from] Singilia.
EDCS-08700870.summary: An inscription indicating that 'Ar' (likely an abbreviation) made this for himself and the IRIDII, originating from Singilia.

EDCS-08700872.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Cornelius Firmus nnorum XXI pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700872.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Cornelius Firmus, aged twenty-one, pious in his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700872.summary: A tombstone inscription for Marcus Cornelius Firmus, who died at age 21, likely erected by his family.

EDCS-08700873.text_edition: \n Macer Ildetis filius annorum XXXXIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700873.translation: Macer, son of Ildetis, aged forty-four, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700873.summary: A tombstone inscription for Macer, son of Ildetis, who died at age 44.

EDCS-08700874.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Postumia Optatina nnorum LX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700874.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Postumia Optatina, aged sixty, pious in her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700874.summary: A tombstone inscription for Postumia Optatina, who died at age 60, likely erected by her family.

EDCS-08700875.text_edition: \n Marcus Sempronius Luci filius Cephalo Cornelianus\n
EDCS-08700875.translation: Marcus Sempronius Cephalo, son of Lucius, of the Cornelia tribe.
EDCS-08700875.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Sempronius Cephalo, who was a member of the Cornelia tribe.

EDCS-08700876.translation: Severus lived for 4 months and 12 days.
EDCS-08700876.summary: This tombstone records the lifespan of Severus, who died in infancy.

EDCS-08700877.text_edition: \n Telete nnorum XXIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700877.translation: Telete, aged 23 years, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700877.summary: This tombstone records the death of Telete at the age of 23 and includes a common epitaph wishing her peace.

EDCS-08700891.text_edition: \n Ex voto Iunoni Marcus Cornelius I et uxor\n
EDCS-08700891.translation: As vowed, Marcus Cornelius I and his wife to Juno.
EDCS-08700891.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Juno by Marcus Cornelius I and his wife.

EDCS-08700893.text_edition: \n Caio Sempronio Galeria Pulverino Arvero Nigri filius dat\n
EDCS-08700893.translation: Caius Sempronius Arveros, son of Nigrus, from the Galeria tribe, known as Pulverinus, gives this.
EDCS-08700893.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Caius Sempronius Arveros.

EDCS-08700895.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Genetivos sic annorum XVIII \n
EDCS-08700895.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Genetivos, aged 18 years.
EDCS-08700895.summary: This is a tombstone for Genetivos, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-08700896.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Cornelius Secundinus Singiliensis nnorum LXI pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700896.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Cornelius Secundinus Singiliensis, aged 61 years, pious towards his own, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700896.summary: This tombstone records the death of Quintus Cornelius Secundinus Singiliensis at the age of 61 and includes a common epitaph wishing him peace.

EDCS-08700897.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Dis Manibus sacrum Flavi<u>s Sextio annorum XXVIII pius in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700897.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Flavius Sextius, aged 28 years, pious towards his own, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700897.summary: This tombstone records the death of Flavius Sextius at the age of 28 and includes a common epitaph wishing him peace.

EDCS-08700898.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Themison annorum XXXXIII pius in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700898.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Themison, aged 43 years, pious towards his own, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700898.summary: This tombstone records the death of Themison at the age of 43 and includes a common epitaph wishing him peace.

EDCS-08700899.text_edition: \n Sacrum Herculi Cai filius Severus solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08700899.translation: Caius Severus, son of Caius, willingly and deservedly made this sacred offering to Hercules.
EDCS-08700899.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating an offering to the god Hercules by Caius Severus.

EDCS-08700901.text_edition: \n Fonti divino aram Lucius Postumius Satullus ex voto dono dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700901.translation: Lucius Postumius Satullus gave and dedicated this altar to the divine Fontes, fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-08700901.summary: This is an altar dedicated to the divine Fontes by Lucius Postumius Satullus in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700902.text_edition: \n Genio municipi Nescaniensis Lucius Postumius Glycon Nescaniensis signum caprae pecunia sua testamento ex HS Miliaria nummum fieri et Nescaniae in foro poni iussit quot donum ut consummari posset Marcus Cornelius Niger Nescaniensis heres eius adiectis de suo ad impensas operis HS C nummum dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700902.translation: Lucius Postumius Glycon of Nescanius ordered by his will that a statue of a goat be made from his money at a cost of one million sesterces and placed in the forum of Nescanius, for the genius of the municipality of Nescanius. His heir, Marcus Cornelius Niger of Nescanius, in order that the gift could be completed, dedicated it, adding one hundred thousand sesterces of his own money to the expenses of the work.
EDCS-08700902.summary: This inscription records the will of Lucius Postumius Glycon, which funded a statue for the genius of Nescanius, and its completion and dedication by his heir, Marcus Cornelius Niger, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700903.text_edition: \n Genio municipi Nescaniensis Licinia Nigella Osquensis nomine suo et nomine Fabi Firmani mariti sui testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-08700903.translation: Licinia Nigella of Nescanius ordered by her will that this be made, in her own name and in the name of her husband, Fabius Firmus.
EDCS-08700903.summary: This inscription records Licinia Nigella's posthumous dedication for the genius of the municipality of Nescanius, intended to be made in her name and that of her husband, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700904.text_edition: \n Iovem Pantheum Augustum cum aede et tetrastylo solo publico Lucius Calpurnius Gallio et Caius Marius Clemens Nescanienses curatores iuvenum Laurensium dederunt dedicaverunt Kalendis Iuliis Publio Septumio Apro Marco Sedatio Severiano consulibus\n
EDCS-08700904.translation: Lucius Calpurnius Gallio and Gaius Marius Clemens of Nescanius, curators of the young men of Laurentum, gave and dedicated Jupiter Pantheus Augustus with his temple and tetrastyle, on public land, on the Kalends of July, in the consulship of Publius Septumius Apro and Marcus Sedatius Severianus.
EDCS-08700904.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple to Jupiter Pantheus Augustus, including its structure, by the curators of the youth of Laurentum, dated to July 1, 105 AD.

EDCS-08700905.text_edition: \n Numini divorum Auggustorum Caius Publicius Fortunatus libertus municipii Flavii Nescaniensis aram solo publico sua pecunia dono dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700905.translation: Gaius Publicius Fortunatus, freedman of the municipality of Nescanius, gave and dedicated this altar to the divine numen of the Augusti, on public land, with his own money.
EDCS-08700905.summary: This is an altar dedicated to the divine numen of the Emperors by Gaius Publicius Fortunatus, a freedman of the municipality of Nescanius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700906.text_edition: \n T D Lucius C E dono dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700906.translation: Lucius C. E. gave and dedicated this.
EDCS-08700906.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription recording a dedication by Lucius C. E., likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700907.summary: This is a highly abbreviated inscription that likely records the name Decimius Quirinus and possibly some official position, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700908.text_edition: \n Quintus Mamilius Martialis libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-08700908.translation: Quintus Mamilius Martialis willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08700908.summary: This inscription records Quintus Mamilius Martialis willingly fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700909.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Vespasiano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VIII imperatori XIIX consuli VIIII patri patriae Lucius Porcius Sabellus IIvir pequnia sua dono dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700909.translation: Lucius Porcius Sabellus, twice chief magistrate, gave and dedicated this to the Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, chief priest, in his eighth tribunician power, thirteenth imperial acclamation, ninth consulship, father of the country, with his own money.
EDCS-08700909.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Vespasian by Lucius Porcius Sabellus, a local magistrate, likely dating to 79 AD.

EDCS-08700910.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano Augusto Germanico Dacico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate X<V>III imperatori VI consuli VI patri patriae optumo maxsumoque sic principi Nescanienses decurionum decreto\n
EDCS-08700910.translation: The people of Nescanius, by decree of the decurions, [dedicated this] to the Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Nerva, Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, chief priest, in his eighteenth tribunician power, sixth imperial acclamation, sixth consulship, the best and greatest father of the country, and the foremost prince.
EDCS-08700910.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the decurions of Nescanius to Emperor Trajan, likely dating to 115 AD.

EDCS-08700911.text_edition: \n Caio Mario Quirina Clementi Nescaniensi ordo Nescaniensium statuam poni iussit et decrevit Fabia Restituta mater honore accepto impensam remisit epulo dato decurionibus et filiis eorum Nescaniensium singulis Xdenarios binos civibus atque incolis item servis stationariis singulis Xdenarios singulos dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700911.translation: The council of Nescanium ordered a statue to be erected to Gaius Marius Quirinus Clementianus of Nescanium; Fabia Restituta, his mother, having received the honor, remitted the expense by giving a banquet to each of the councilors and their sons, and to each of the citizens and residents, and likewise to each of the stationary guards, two denarii, and to single men, one denarius.
EDCS-08700911.summary: This is a municipal decree from Nescanium honoring Gaius Marius Quirinus Clementianus with a statue, with his mother remitting the cost through a public banquet, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700912.text_edition: \n Lucio Calpurnio Quirina Macrino ordo Nescaniensium statuam publice decrevit Lucius Calpurnius Macer pater honore accepto impensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700912.translation: The council of Nescanium officially decreed a statue to Lucius Calpurnius Macrinus of Quirina; Lucius Calpurnius Macer, his father, having received the honor, remitted the expense.
EDCS-08700912.summary: This inscription records a municipal decree from Nescanium for a statue to Lucius Calpurnius Macrinus, with his father remitting the cost, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700913.text_edition: \n Lucio Aelio Quirinae sic Melae Nescaniensi Aelia Optata mater statuam epuloque dato decurionibus dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700913.translation: Aelia Optata, mother of Lucius Aelius Mela of Quirina, Nescanium, dedicated a statue and gave a banquet to the councilors.
EDCS-08700913.summary: This is a dedication of a statue and a banquet by Aelia Optata to honor her son, Lucius Aelius Mela, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700914.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Cornelius Agricola annorum XXV pius in suis\n
EDCS-08700914.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Cornelius Agricola, twenty-five years old, dutiful towards his own (family).
EDCS-08700914.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Cornelius Agricola, recording his age and piety, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700915.text_edition: \n Marcus Sempronius Cant Ne<s>caniensis Cornelia Marci filia Aemilia Luci filia Marcus Sempronius Sisanna monumentum sibi uxsori sic patri matri vivos aedificandum curavit\n
EDCS-08700915.translation: Marcus Sempronius Cantus of Nescanium, Cornelia, daughter of Marcus, (and) Aemilia, daughter of Lucius, had this monument built for themselves, their wives, their father, and their mother, while they were alive.
EDCS-08700915.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription commissioned by Marcus Sempronius Cantus and two women, for themselves and their parents, indicating the practice of building one's own tomb, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700916.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Vibius Lucanus UrC testamento poni iussit ex HS VIsex milibus\n
EDCS-08700916.translation: Lucius Vibius Lucanus of UrC ordered this to be erected to Jupiter Best and Greatest by testament, from 6,000 sesterces.
EDCS-08700916.summary: This inscription records a testamentary dedication of a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Vibius Lucanus, funded by 6,000 sesterces, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700917.text_edition: \n Victoriam Augustam Quintus Fabius Luci filius Galeria Fabullus testamento fieri ponique iussit ex HS IIIIquattuor milibus huic dono Lucius Fabius Luci filius Galeria Fabianus heres XX non deduxit et atmodum HS VIsex milibus suo dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700917.translation: Quintus Fabius Fabullus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, ordered this monument to Victory Augusta to be made and erected by testament, from 4,000 sesterces; Lucius Fabius Fabianus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, his heir, did not deduct 20 (sesterces?) from this gift, and gave and dedicated 6,000 sesterces of his own.
EDCS-08700917.summary: This is a testamentary dedication of a monument to Victory Augusta, detailing the bequest amount and a complex financial arrangement involving the heir's additional contribution, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700918.text_edition: \n Aureliae Feseniae Antullus Germanus Euhemerus Cupitus liberti Calpurnius Hartus Calpurnius Hilaris de sua pecunia dederunt\n
EDCS-08700918.translation: Aurelia Fesenia, Antullus Germanus, Euhemerus Cupitus, freedmen of Calpurnius Hartus, (and) Calpurnius Hilaris gave (this) from their own money.
EDCS-08700918.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by freedmen and possibly other individuals from their own funds, honoring Aurelia Fesenia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700919.text_edition: \n Cordiae Luci filiae Sergianae Lucius Fabius Luci filius Galeria Fabianus uxori\n
EDCS-08700919.translation: Lucius Fabius Fabianus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, (erected this) to his wife Cordia Sergiana, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-08700919.summary: This is the dedication of a monument by Lucius Fabius Fabianus to his wife Cordia Sergiana, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700920.text_edition: \n Lucio Fabio Galeria Gallo Lucius Fabius Galeria Fabianus filio\n
EDCS-08700920.translation: Lucius Fabius Fabianus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, (erected this) to his son Lucius Fabius Gallus.
EDCS-08700920.summary: This is the dedication of a monument by Lucius Fabius Fabianus to his son Lucius Fabius Gallus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700921.text_edition: \n Decimo Iunio Decimi P Titi liberto aniano Decimus Caecilius Parthenius amico optumo posuit\n
EDCS-08700921.translation: Decimus Caecilius Parthenius erected this for his excellent friend Decimus Iunius Anianus, freedman of Decimus Iunius.
EDCS-08700921.summary: A funerary monument for Decimus Iunius Anianus, set up by his friend Decimus Caecilius Parthenius, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08700923.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Aurelius Thebais annorum LXXXXVI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700923.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aurelius Thebais, aged 96, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700923.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Lucius Aurelius Thebais, aged 96, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700924.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelia Agatho annorum LX pia in <suis> hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700924.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Agatho, aged 60, lies here among her family. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700924.summary: A tombstone for Aurelia Agatho, aged 60, a pious woman buried with her family, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700925.text_edition: \n C Fusca nnorum LXXII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700925.translation: C. Fusca, aged 72, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700925.summary: A tombstone recording the death of C. Fusca, aged 72, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700928.text_edition: \n Heglecte nnorum XXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700928.translation: Heglecte, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700928.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Heglecte, aged 25, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700930.text_edition: \n Tiberio Sempronio Cai filio Prisco P M\n
EDCS-08700930.translation: To Tiberius Sempronius Priscus, son of Caius. Pontifex Maximus.
EDCS-08700930.summary: A dedication to Tiberius Sempronius Priscus, son of Caius, with the title Pontifex Maximus, likely an honorary inscription from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700934.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelia Victorina fecit memoriam F filio suo Aurelio Caro vixit annos XXVI menses VI dies X sit terra levis\n
EDCS-08700934.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelia Victorina made this memorial for her son Aurelius Caro, who lived 26 years, 6 months, and 10 days. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700934.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Caro, son of Cornelia Victorina, detailing his lifespan of 26 years, 6 months, and 10 days, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700935.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Vespasianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis VIIII imperator XIIX consul VIII pater patriae salutem dicit IIIIviris et decurionibus Saborensium cum multis difficultatibus infirmitatem vestram premi indicetis permitto vobis oppidum sub nomine meo ut voltis in planum extruere vectigalia quae ab Divo Augusto accepisse dicitis custodio si qua nova adicere voltis de his proconsulem adire debebitis ego enim nullo respondente constituere nil possum decretum vestrum accepi VIII Kalendas Augustas legatos dimisi IIII Kalendas easdem valete IIviri Caius Cornelius Severus et Marcus Septimius Severus publica pecunia in aere inciderunt\n
EDCS-08700935.translation: Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, imperator for the 18th time, consul for the 8th time, father of the fatherland, sends greetings to the magistrates and decurions of Saborenses. You indicate that your town is pressed with many difficulties and weakness; I permit you to build a town under my name as you wish. I preserve the revenues which you say you received from the Divine Augustus. If you wish to add any new ones, you must approach the proconsul; for I can establish nothing without a response. I received your decree on the eighth day before the Kalends of August, and sent away the envoys on the fourth day before the Kalends of the same month. Farewell. The magistrates Caius Cornelius Severus and Marcus Septimius Severus had this inscribed on bronze at public expense.
EDCS-08700935.summary: An imperial rescript from Emperor Vespasian (69-79 CE) to the town of Saborenses, granting permission to rebuild and manage revenues, with magistrates Caius Cornelius Severus and Marcus Septimius Severus overseeing the inscription.

EDCS-08700937.text_edition: \n liae Cai filiae Anae aviae paternae Marcus Pupius Caldus dedit\n
EDCS-08700937.translation: Marcus Pupius Caldus gave this to his paternal grandmother, Iulia Ana, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-08700937.summary: A dedication made by Marcus Pupius Caldus for his paternal grandmother, Iulia Ana, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700938.translation: To Lucius Cornelius Macro, son of Quirinus.
EDCS-08700938.summary: A dedication to Lucius Cornelius Macro, son of Quirinus, likely an honorary inscription from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700939.translation: To Marcus Pupius Quietus, his uncle.
EDCS-08700939.summary: This is an epitaph dedicated to Marcus Pupius Quietus by his nephew, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08700940.text_edition: \n Aemilius Sisenna Quirina Saborensis vivo se posuit\n
EDCS-08700940.translation: Aemilius Sisenna of the Saborenses tribe, Quirina, set this up for himself in his lifetime.
EDCS-08700940.summary: This is an autobiographical epitaph set up by Aemilius Sisenna during his lifetime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700941.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum dius Luminatius ulensis nnorum -XXXV hic situs est <s>(it) tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700941.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Luminatius of Ulensis, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700941.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Luminatius of Ulensis, who died at age 35, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700942.text_edition: \n Stercusia Marci Aeli Aeliani Singiliensis serva annorum LIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700942.translation: Stercusia, slave of Marcus Aelius Aelianus Singiliensis, aged 53, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700942.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Stercusia, a slave of Marcus Aelius Aelianus Singiliensis, who died at age 53, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700944.text_edition: \n Aemilia Quinti filia Tuscilla annorum XXXXIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700944.translation: Aemilia Tuscilla, daughter of Quintus, aged 44, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700944.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Tuscilla, daughter of Quintus, who died at age 44, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700945.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Calpurnia Rhodope nnorum XX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700945.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Calpurnia Rhodope, aged 20, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700945.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calpurnia Rhodope, who died at age 20, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700946.text_edition: \n Caius Memmius Epaphroditus annorum LV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Nice soror cum Epaphrodito et natis cippum marmoreum dedit\n
EDCS-08700946.translation: Caius Memmius Epaphroditus, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Nice, his sister, with Epaphroditus and their children, dedicated this marble marker.
EDCS-08700946.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Memmius Epaphroditus, who died at age 55, with a dedication from his sister and her children, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700947.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Memmia Auctina annorum LXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700947.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Memmia Auctina, aged 70, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700947.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Memmia Auctina, who died at age 70, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700948.text_edition: \n Numeria Optata nnorum XXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700948.translation: Numeria Optata, aged 30, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700948.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Numeria Optata, who died at age 30, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700949.text_edition: \n Septimia Marci filia Severa nnorum LXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis testamento poni iussit in fronte pedes XXXX in agro pedes XXXX\n
EDCS-08700949.translation: Septimia Severa, daughter of Marcus, aged 70, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. She ordered this to be placed by her will: 40 feet in front, 40 feet in the land.
EDCS-08700949.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Septimia Severa, daughter of Marcus, who died at age 70, specifying burial plot dimensions as per her will, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700951.text_edition: \n Lo<g=C>as nnorum LV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08700951.translation: Logas, 55 years old, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700951.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Logas at the age of 55.

EDCS-08700952.text_edition: \n Pupia Marci filia Prisca nnorum LXIII pia in suis sit tibi terra levis in fronte pedes XXX in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-08700952.translation: Pupia Prisca, daughter of Marcus, 63 years old, dutiful to her family, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. 30 feet in front, 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-08700952.summary: A funerary inscription for Pupia Prisca, who died at 63, also specifying land dimensions for her burial plot.

EDCS-08700953.summary: A very brief funerary inscription for Aurelia Euprosine, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700954.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Victor annorum LV pius in suis hic est situs sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700954.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the departed. Victor, 55 years old, dutiful to his family, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700954.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Victor, who died at 55.

EDCS-08700955.text_edition: \n Myrtale Valeriani serva annorum XX sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700955.translation: Myrtale, servant of Valerianus, 20 years old, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700955.summary: A funerary inscription for Myrtale, a servant, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-08700956.text_edition: \n Nicias Cai Memmi Galli servus annorum XXXI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis mater et Cronice soror dederunt\n
EDCS-08700956.translation: Nicias, slave of Gaius Memmius Gallus, 31 years old, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. His mother and sister Cronice dedicated this.
EDCS-08700956.summary: A funerary inscription for Nicias, slave of Gaius Memmius Gallus, who died at 31, dedicated by his mother and sister.

EDCS-08700957.text_edition: \n Optatus Cai Memmi Galli servus nnrum XXXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700957.translation: Optatus, slave of Gaius Memmius Gallus, 40 years old, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700957.summary: A funerary inscription for Optatus, slave of Gaius Memmius Gallus, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-08700958.text_edition: \n ------] GV SE X IA Victorius dono dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700958.translation: [...] Victorius gave and dedicated as a gift.
EDCS-08700958.summary: A fragmented inscription recording a dedication made by Victorius.

EDCS-08700959.text_edition: \n Minervae Augustae sacrum nius Quirina Optatus cohortis V Baeticae ORES \n
EDCS-08700959.translation: Sacred to Minerva Augusta. Quirina Optatus of the 5th Cohort of the Baetica [cavalry?]...
EDCS-08700959.summary: A dedication to Minerva Augusta, possibly by a soldier named Quirina Optatus from a cavalry unit in Hispania Baetica.

EDCS-08700960.text_edition: \n Caio Cordio Cai filio Quirina Optato decreto decurionum Ilipulae Minoris Caius Cordius Fontanus filius honore usus impensam remisit epuloque dato dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700960.translation: Gaius Cordius Fontanus, son, having received the honor by decree of the decurions for Gaius Cordius Optatus, son of Gaius, Quirinian tribe, of Ilipula Minor, waived the expense and dedicated it after providing a banquet.
EDCS-08700960.summary: A funerary monument erected by Gaius Cordius Fontanus for his son Gaius Cordius Optatus, honoring him with a public decree and a banquet.

EDCS-08700961.text_edition: \n Lucio Flavio Luci filio Quirina Gallo IIIIviro IIviro bis decreto decurionum Ilipulae Minoris Lucius Flavius Gallus honore usus impensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700961.translation: To Lucius Flavius Gallus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, who served as *quattuorvir* and twice as *duovir* by decree of the town councilors of Ilipula Minor, Lucius Flavius Gallus, having enjoyed the honor, waived the expenses.
EDCS-08700961.summary: This is a public benefaction record from Ilipula Minor, likely from the Roman Imperial period, where a magistrate named Lucius Flavius Gallus forwent reimbursement for his public duties.

EDCS-08700963.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Thisbe serva nnorum LXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700963.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the departed. Thisbe, a slave woman, aged 65, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700963.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a slave woman named Thisbe, dating likely from the Roman Imperial period, with the standard wish for light earth.

EDCS-08700965.text_edition: \n Lucio Mussidio Cai filio Se Caio Mussidio Cai filio SeP Mussidiae Char Caius Mussidius Optatus\n
EDCS-08700965.translation: To Lucius Mussidius, son of Caius, of the Sergia tribe; to Caius Mussidius, son of Caius, of the Sergia tribe; and to Mussidia Char, daughter of Caius. Caius Mussidius Optatus [dedicated this].
EDCS-08700965.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary monument or dedication honoring at least three individuals: Lucius Mussidius, Caius Mussidius, and Mussidia Char, erected by Caius Mussidius Optatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700967.summary: This is a short dedication to the divine personification of Imperial fame, Fama Augusta, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700968.summary: This very brief inscription records the erection of a statue by a person named Macer, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700972.text_edition: \n Paquina Novati filia nnrum XXV hic sita est\n
EDCS-08700972.translation: Paquina, daughter of Novatius, aged 25, is buried here.
EDCS-08700972.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Paquina, daughter of Novatius, who died at the age of 25, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700973.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely referring to a woman identified by her father's name, Erotis, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700975.text_edition: \n Herculem sic Primigenium sic Augustum! sacrum cinius Gai filius Cer animo libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-08700975.translation: To Hercules, the Primal One; to Augustus! Sacred. Cinis, son of Gaius, of the Cornelia tribe, willingly fulfilled his vow with a free heart.
EDCS-08700975.summary: This is a votive inscription from the Roman Imperial period, dedicated to Hercules and Augustus by Cinis, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-08700976.text_edition: \n Iussu dominae Isidi Bulsae Caius Licinius Flavinus fontem et aedem dedit\n
EDCS-08700976.translation: By order of the mistress, Caius Licinius Flavinus dedicated a fountain and a shrine to Isis Bulsa.
EDCS-08700976.summary: This is a dedication of a fountain and shrine to the goddess Isis Bulsa by Caius Licinius Flavinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700977.text_edition: \n Quintus Memmius Optati filius Quirina Niger statuas duas aereas unam nominis sui alteram patris poni iussit Caius Memmius Severus heres solo suo fecit\n
EDCS-08700977.translation: Quintus Memmius Niger, son of Optatus, of the Quirina tribe, ordered two bronze statues to be erected, one of his own name, the other of his father. Caius Memmius Severus, the heir, made it on his own land.
EDCS-08700977.summary: This inscription records Quintus Memmius Niger commissioning two bronze statues, with the heir Caius Memmius Severus carrying out the construction on his land, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700979.text_edition: \n Caius Memmius Optati filius Quirina Severus statuas duas aereas unam nominis sui alteram fili sui poni iussit Caius Memmius Rufus heres fecit\n
EDCS-08700979.translation: Gaius Memmius Severus, son of Optatus, of the Quirina tribe, ordered two bronze statues to be erected, one in his own name and the other in his son's name. Gaius Memmius Rufus, the heir, made them.
EDCS-08700979.summary: This inscription records the erection of two bronze statues by Gaius Memmius Severus, with the heir Gaius Memmius Rufus acting as builder, likely a public dedication.

EDCS-08700980.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Iunius Marci filius Martialis Italicensis nnorum LXV-- pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700980.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Iunius Martialis, son of Marcus, from Italica, aged 65, pious in his own property, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700980.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iunius Martialis, recording his name, age, and a wish for a light burial, dated to his lifetime.

EDCS-08700984.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Equitius Quinti libertus Primigenius Ventipponensis nnorum LXX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Equitia Quinti liberta Fusca Ventipponensia nnorum LX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700984.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Equitius Primigenius, freedman of Quintus, from Ventippo, aged 70, pious in his own property, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Equitia Fusca, freedwoman of Quintus, from Ventippo, aged 60, pious in her own property, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700984.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two individuals, Quintus Equitius Primigenius and Equitia Fusca, noting their names, origins, ages, and piety, likely dating to the late Roman period.

EDCS-08700985.text_edition: \n Numisia Luci filia Faustina nnorum X \n
EDCS-08700985.translation: Numisia Faustina, daughter of Lucius, aged 10.
EDCS-08700985.summary: This is a tombstone fragment for Numisia Faustina, recording her name and age, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700988.text_edition: \n pia in suis hic sita est dicite qui legitis sit tibi terra levis cui bene meritae uxori sa<n=V>ctissimae Chrysanthus dignum honorem dedit \n
EDCS-08700988.translation: Pious in her own property, she is buried here. Say, you who read this, may the earth lie lightly on you. Chrysanthus gave worthy honor to his most holy wife, who deserved well.
EDCS-08700988.summary: This funerary inscription honors a wife named Chrysanthus, describing her as pious and deserving, with a wish for a light burial, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700989.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Modesta annorum L pia in suis hic sita est dicite qui legitis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700989.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Modesta, aged 50, pious in her own property, is buried here. Say, you who read this, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700989.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Modesta, recording her name, age, and location of burial, dated to her lifetime.

EDCS-08700990.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly part of a funerary text, recording an unknown name and age.

EDCS-08700992.translation: From the workshop of Hilarius the Fab(er).
EDCS-08700992.summary: This inscription indicates the origin of a work, likely a craft product or a construction, from the workshop of Hilarius, probably dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700993.summary: This inscription, likely an ownership mark or a dedication fragment, refers to Iulius Maximus, possibly dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700994.text_edition: \n Marcus Caecilius Luci filius Quirina Se XVIII verus nnum LV pius in XVIII suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis huic ordo municipii Flavii XXXIII sic Onigitani decrevit laudationem lo XXVI cum sepulturae funeris impen XXV sam exequias publica<s> statuam XXIII\n
EDCS-08700994.translation: Marcus Caecilius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, aged 18, [his age] 55, pious in his own property, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. For him, the town council of Flavius decreed 33 [units, likely of currency], with funeral expenses of 25 [units], public obsequies, a statue [for] 23 [units].
EDCS-08700994.summary: This inscription records the burial of Marcus Caecilius Severus and details honors and expenses decreed by the town council for his funeral, suggesting a significant local official or prominent citizen, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700995.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publi Marei Ventipponensi nnorum XXVI pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700995.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Mareius Ventipponensis, aged 26, pious among his own people, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700995.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Publius Mareius Ventipponensis, who died at age 26, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08700996.text_edition: \n Quintus Vibius Quinti libertus Firmillo et Urvinia Titi liberta Tyche hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-08700996.translation: Quintus Vibius Firmillus, freedman of Quintus, and Urvinia Tyche, freedwoman of Titus, lie here. May the earth lie lightly upon you both.
EDCS-08700996.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Quintus Vibius Firmillus and Urvinia Tyche, likely freed slaves, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08701000.text_edition: \n Attia Luci liberta Qlauro Tiche sic optimo viro de sua pecunia dono dedit\n
EDCS-08701000.translation: Attia Tyche, freedwoman of Lucius, gave this with her own money to her most excellent husband, Claura.
EDCS-08701000.summary: A funerary dedication by Attia Tyche for her husband Claura, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08701001.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Attia Luciani Olaurensis nnorum XXXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701001.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Attia, daughter of Lucianus of Olaure, aged 35, pious among her own people, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701001.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Attia, daughter of Lucianus of Olaure, who died at age 35, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08701003.translation: Clodia Augustina, daughter of Lucius, of Olaure.
EDCS-08701003.summary: A funerary inscription for Clodia Augustina, daughter of Lucius of Olaure, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08701004.translation: Macrobius, son of Quintus, of Ostippo, aged...
EDCS-08701004.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription for Macrobius, son of Quintus of Ostippo, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08701005.text_edition: \n Lucius Modius Luci filius Cosmus Olaurensis nnorum XXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701005.translation: Lucius Modius Cosmus, son of Lucius, of Olaure, aged 25, pious among his own people, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701005.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Lucius Modius Cosmus, who died at age 25, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08701006.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gaius Oius Secundus Ostipponensis nnorum CXXV sic pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701006.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Oius Secundus of Ostippo, aged 125, pious among his own people, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701006.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Gaius Oius Secundus of Ostippo, who lived to the remarkable age of 125, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08701007.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Valerius Peculiaris Olaurensis nnorum XXX pius in suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701007.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Valerius Peculiaris of Olaure, aged 30, pious among his own people, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701007.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Lucius Valerius Peculiaris of Olaure, who died at age 30, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08701010.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum A Silvinus nnorum XXXV pius in suis hic situs sit terra levis\n
EDCS-08701010.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. A. Silvinus, aged 35, pious among his own people, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701010.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of A. Silvinus, who died at age 35, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08701011.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aemilia Marci filia Proculina nnorum XVI pia in suis sit tibi terra levis dicite qui legitis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701011.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aemilia Proculina, daughter of Marcus, aged 16 years, dutiful to her family. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Say, you who read, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701011.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Aemilia Proculina at age 16, with a standard invocation for the earth to lie lightly upon her.

EDCS-08701012.summary: This very brief inscription records the death of Fortunata at the age of 10.

EDCS-08701013.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum hic invocatur Fructuosus annorum XXVII pius in suos sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701013.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Here is invoked Fructuosus, aged 27 years, dutiful to his family. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701013.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Fructuosus at age 27, emphasizing his dutiful nature towards his family.

EDCS-08701014.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Ignatia Assata nnum XXIII pia in suis hi<c=S> sita est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08701014.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Ignatia Assata, aged 23 years, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701014.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Ignatia Assata, who died at age 23, highlighting her devotion to her family.

EDCS-08701015.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iucundus nnorum V pius in <s>(uis) hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701015.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iucundus, aged 5 years, dutiful to his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701015.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iucundus at the young age of 5, noting his dutifulness to his family.

EDCS-08701016.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Porcius Ofellus annorum XXXXVIIII pius in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701016.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Porcius Ofellus, aged 39 years, dutiful to his family. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701016.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Porcius Ofellus at age 39, emphasizing his dutiful nature towards his family.

EDCS-08701017.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Stephanus annorum XIIX pius in suis hic situs est \n
EDCS-08701017.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Stephanus, aged 18 years, dutiful to his family, is buried here.
EDCS-08701017.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Stephanus at the age of 18, highlighting his dutifulness to his family.

EDCS-08701020.text_edition: \n Claudiae Musae annorum LX Fulvia Margaris matri carissimae hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701020.translation: To Claudia Musa, aged 60 years. Fulvia Margaris, her dearest mother, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701020.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Claudia Musa at age 60, dedicated by her loving mother, Fulvia Margaris.

EDCS-08701022.summary: This brief inscription records the death of Sabinus, identifying him as a son and stating his age at death.

EDCS-08701024.text_edition: \n Saluti Augustae Lucius Sempronius Luci filius Galeria Atticus de suo dedit\n
EDCS-08701024.translation: To Salus Augusta. Lucius Sempronius Atticus, son of Lucius, dedicated this from his own funds.
EDCS-08701024.summary: This dedicatory inscription records Lucius Sempronius Atticus's personal offering to the goddess Salus Augusta.

EDCS-08701025.text_edition: \n annia Septuma sic votum animo libens solvit\n
EDCS-08701025.translation: Annia Septuma willingly and gladly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-08701025.summary: This inscription records an individual, Annia Septuma, fulfilling a personal vow, likely on a votive monument.

EDCS-08701026.text_edition: \n Druso Caesari Tiberi filio consuli Quintus Larius Luci filius Niger Xvir maximus de sua pecunia dedit\n
EDCS-08701026.translation: Quintus Larius Lucius filius Niger, a member of the board of ten men, dedicated this at his own expense to Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius, the consul.
EDCS-08701026.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Quintus Larius Lucius filius Niger to Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius, during his consulship.

EDCS-08701027.text_edition: \n Lucius Aelius C fieri iussit suo et Aelii patris sui et Enniae A nomine summa ab herede DED consummandum R item ordo decrevit IM usum \n
EDCS-08701027.translation: Lucius Aelius ordered this to be made for himself, his father Aelius, and for Ennia by name, to be completed by the heir with the highest sum of money; the council also decreed this for their use.
EDCS-08701027.summary: This inscription records an order by Lucius Aelius for the construction of something, intended for himself, his father, and Ennia, with a decree from the council for its use.

EDCS-08701028.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Dom(itius) Romulus Ostipponensis sevir Augustalis annorum LXXXV hic sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701028.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Domitius Romulus of Ostippo, Augustalis priest, aged 85, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701028.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Domitius Romulus of Ostippo, an Augustalis priest, dated by his age and social status.

EDCS-08701029.text_edition: \n Quinto Sulpicio Quieto Calpurnia Galla mater\n
EDCS-08701029.translation: Calpurnia Galla, mother, for Quintus Sulpicius Quietus.
EDCS-08701029.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Calpurnia Galla for her son Quintus Sulpicius Quietus.

EDCS-08701030.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aelia Memmesis sic Aeliae Vetustinae liberta Cordubensis annorum XXXVII pia in suis hic sita est sit terra levis\n
EDCS-08701030.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelia Memmesis, freedwoman of Aelia Vetustina of Corduba, aged 37, dutiful to her family, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701030.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Memmesis, a freedwoman of Aelia Vetustina of Corduba, noting her age and piety.

EDCS-08701031.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Mummia mulieris lib(erta) Fortunata Ostipponensis nnorum LI pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701031.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunata, freedwoman of Mummia, of Ostippo, aged 51, dutiful to her family, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701031.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fortunata, a freedwoman of Mummia of Ostippo, indicating her age and devotion to her family.

EDCS-08701032.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Naevius Marci filius Quirina Germanianus Ostipponensis nnorum LXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701032.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Naevius Germanianus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, of Ostippo, aged 65, dutiful to his family, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701032.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Naevius Germanianus of Ostippo, providing his tribal affiliation, age, and filial piety.

EDCS-08701033.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius A Crescens nnorum LXXXI pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701033.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius A. Crescens, aged 81, dutiful to his family, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701033.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius A. Crescens, noting his age and his devotion to his family.

EDCS-08701035.text_edition: \n Haple serva annorum XXIIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701035.translation: Haple, a servant, aged 24, dutiful to her family, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701035.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Haple, a servant, noting her age and her piety.

EDCS-08701036.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Hermes vixit annis CXIII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701036.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Hermes lived 113 years, dutiful to his own. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701036.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Hermes, who lived to 113 years, dated by the style of the text to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701037.text_edition: \n Laria Quinti liberta Tuche annorum XI hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701037.translation: Tuche, freedwoman of Laria Quinti, aged 11 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701037.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Tuche, the freedwoman of Laria Quinti, who died at the age of 11, typical of the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701038.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Mettia Saturnina annorum <L=I>X hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Saturninus matri merentissimae fecit \n
EDCS-08701038.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Mettia Saturnina, aged 61 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Saturninus made this for his most deserving mother.
EDCS-08701038.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Mettia Saturnina, aged 61, with a dedication from her son Saturninus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701039.text_edition: \n Rufinus Rufi filius nnorum LXV pius in suis hic situs est dic qui legis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701039.translation: Rufinus, son of Rufus, aged 65 years, dutiful to his own, is buried here. Reader, say: May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701039.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Rufinus, son of Rufus, who died at 65, common in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701040.text_edition: \n Marcus Rutilius Marci libertus Diomedes nnorum LXXXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701040.translation: Marcus Rutilius Diomedes, freedman of Marcus, aged 90 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701040.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Marcus Rutilius Diomedes, freedman of Marcus, who died at 90, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701045.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Mummius Maurus annorum LXXVII pius in suis hic situs est\n
EDCS-08701045.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Mummius Maurus, aged 77 years, dutiful to his own, is buried here.
EDCS-08701045.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Lucius Mummius Maurus, aged 77, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701050.text_edition: \n m qui excoli ex HS XII epulo ordini et plebi dato M IPI circensibus editis dedit Annia lais uxor et heres eius NO XX dedicavit DE parium DO statuam d d\n
EDCS-08701050.translation: He who provided from 12 sesterces a feast for the order and the common people, and games in the circus, Annia Lais, his wife and heir, dedicated this statue, granted by decree.
EDCS-08701050.summary: An inscription recording a public dedication of games and a feast funded by a bequest of 12,000 sesterces, made by the wife and heir Annia Lais, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701053.text_edition: \n Lucius Caesius Maximinus Cedripponensis annorum XXI hic interfectus est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701053.translation: Lucius Caesius Maximinus of Cedrippe, aged 21 years, was killed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701053.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the violent death of Lucius Caesius Maximinus of Cedrippe at age 21, typical of Roman provincial inscriptions.

EDCS-08701054.text_edition: \n Publius Acilius Publi libertus Antiochus sepultus est fundo suo pago Singiliensi\n
EDCS-08701054.translation: Publius Acilius Antiochus, freedman of Publius, was buried on his own land in the pagus Singiliensis.
EDCS-08701054.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Publius Acilius Antiochus, freedman of Publius, on his own property, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701055.text_edition: \n Calliope Cai Manli Torquati serva nnum XXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701055.translation: Calliope, slave of Caius Manlius Torquatus, aged 35 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701055.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Calliope, a slave of Caius Manlius Torquatus, who died at 35, common in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701056.translation: Fabia, daughter of Lucius, of the tribe Cornelia, (was buried here).
EDCS-08701056.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fabia, likely from the Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-08701057.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum L Fructosae pia in suis nnm XXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701057.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Fructosus, dutiful to his own, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701057.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Fructosus, a man who died at 25, buried by his family, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701058.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum L Nigella annorum CXL sic pia in nepotem hic sita est SITV\n
EDCS-08701058.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nigella, aged 140, dutiful to her grandson, is buried here. (May the earth lie lightly).
EDCS-08701058.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nigella, who is recorded as having lived an exceptionally long life of 140 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701062.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription indicating the age of the deceased, Lucius, as 22.

EDCS-08701069.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Polydorus servus nnorum XXII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701069.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Polydorus, a slave, aged 22, dutiful to his own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701069.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Polydorus, a slave who died at 22, dedicated by his family, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701071.text_edition: \n Titus Turpilius Titi filius Papiria miles legionis VI hic situs est\n
EDCS-08701071.translation: Titus Turpilius, son of Titus, of the Papiria tribe, a soldier of the Sixth Legion, is buried here.
EDCS-08701071.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Turpilius, a soldier of the Sixth Legion, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701072.text_edition: \n Elpis serva dominis cara annorum XXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701072.translation: Elpis, a slave, dear to her masters, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701072.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Elpis, a slave who died at 25, dedicated by her masters, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701074.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Sterceia serva nnorum XXXIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701074.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sterceia, a slave, aged 33, dutiful to her own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701074.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sterceia, a slave who died at 33, dedicated by her family, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701075.text_edition: \n Vibia Luci liberta Pia hic sita locus pedum XII\n
EDCS-08701075.translation: Vibia Pia, freedwoman of Lucius, is buried here. The plot is 12 feet.
EDCS-08701075.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vibia Pia, a freedwoman, which also specifies the size of her burial plot, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701082.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Gavius Quinti filius Priscus Quirina Ventipponensis nnorum L hic situs est sit tibi terra levis huic ordo municipum municipii Ventipponensis decrevit locum sepulturae funeris inpensam\n
EDCS-08701082.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Gavius Priscus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, from Ventippo, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. The council of the municipality of Ventippo decreed a burial place and funeral expenses for him.
EDCS-08701082.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Gavius Priscus, a local official from Ventippo who died at 50, whose burial and funeral expenses were paid for by the town council, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701083.text_edition: \n Calpurnia Restituta ANIP nnorum LXXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terrra levis\n
EDCS-08701083.translation: Calpurnia Restituta, 80 years old, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
