EDCS-09000005.text_edition: \n Aelia Marci filia Senilla Luci Carvili Recti domus Augustae sacerdos prima et perpetua et Quintus Cornelius Longus Carvilius Luci filius Galeria Rusticus filius de sua pecunia dederunt dedicaverunt\n
EDCS-09000005.translation: Aelia Senilla, daughter of Marcus, of the house of Lucius Carvilius Rectus, first and perpetual priestess, and Quintus Cornelius Longus, son of Lucius Carvilius Galeria Rusticus, dedicated and consecrated from their own money.
EDCS-09000005.summary: A dedication by Aelia Senilla, a priestess, and Quintus Cornelius Longus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000006.text_edition: \n Cornelio C filio Galeria Vetulo II viro pontifici Caesaris primo Cornelia Luci filia uxor post mortem\n
EDCS-09000006.translation: To Cornelius, son of C., Galeria Vetulo, wife of the second duovir and first priest of Caesar, after death.
EDCS-09000006.summary: A funerary dedication to Cornelius, son of C., possibly by his wife Galeria Vetulo, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09000007.text_edition: \n Corneliae Luci filiae Sillibori Vetuli pleps Latoniensis honorem accepit inpensam remisit\n
EDCS-09000007.translation: Cornelia, daughter of Lucius, of the Sillibori tribe of Lanuvium, received the honor and repaid the expense.
EDCS-09000007.summary: This inscription from Lanuvium records an honor received and an expense repaid by Cornelia, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-09000008.text_edition: \n Proculinae nostrae Iulius et Caesienus Firmo de suo dant\n
EDCS-09000008.translation: Iulius and Caesienus give to our Proculina from their own resources.
EDCS-09000008.summary: This inscription records a gift given by Iulius and Caesienus to Proculina.

EDCS-09000009.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelius Cnaei filius Cinna vixit annis quasi XXX pius in suis faciendum curavit locum et titulum sepulturae filius eius Firminus\n
EDCS-09000009.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cornelius Cinna, son of Cnaeus, lived for almost 30 years, dutiful to his family, he saw to the construction. His son Firminus provided the place and the tombstone.
EDCS-09000009.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Cornelius Cinna, who lived for nearly 30 years, and states that his son Firminus arranged for his burial.

EDCS-09000010.text_edition: \n Publius Cornelius Primigenius annorum XXXI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000010.translation: Publius Cornelius Primigenius, aged 31, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000010.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Publius Cornelius Primigenius at the age of 31.

EDCS-09000011.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelia Delphis Marci Vesivi Muri annorum LX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000011.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cornelia Delphis, aged 60, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000011.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Cornelia Delphis at the age of 60.

EDCS-09000012.text_edition: \n Cornelia Suriacis sic annorum XXXXI pia in suis amica<e=S> m<e=F>r<e=F>nti aram posu<e=F>runt SFSTTF \n
EDCS-09000012.translation: Cornelia Suriacis, aged 41, dutiful to her family and beloved, erected an altar. SFSTTF.
EDCS-09000012.summary: This inscription records Cornelia Suriacis, aged 41, erecting an altar, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-09000013.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Marci filia Sempronia vixit annis LVIII pia in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000013.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia, daughter of Marcius Sempronius, lived for 58 years, dutiful to her family. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000013.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia, daughter of Marcius Sempronius, at the age of 58.

EDCS-09000014.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Porcia Publi filia Antistia Materniana vixit annis LX hic sita est Marcus Aemilius Marci filius Eugenetus uxori bene merenti sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000014.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Porcia Antistia Materniana, daughter of Publius, aged 60, lies here. Marcus Aemilius Eugenetus, son of Marcus, erected this for his deserving wife. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000014.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Porcia Antistia Materniana, aged 60, and states that her husband Marcus Aemilius Eugenetus erected it.

EDCS-09000015.text_edition: \n Marcus Publicius Stephanus Publicia <M>(arci) liberta Arbuscula [[]] Fabia Luci liberta UninI\n
EDCS-09000015.translation: Marcus Publicius Stephanus, freedwoman of Marcus; Arbuscula; [---]; Fabia, freedwoman of Lucius UninI.
EDCS-09000015.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, possibly freedpersons, identified by their names and patrons.

EDCS-09000016.text_edition: \n Publicia Kamerina annorum LI hic sita est sit tibi terra levis MA\n
EDCS-09000016.translation: Publicia Kamerina, aged 51, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. MA.
EDCS-09000016.summary: This tombstone records the death of Publicia Kamerina at the age of 51.

EDCS-09000017.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Valerius Firmanus vixsit sic annos LX pius in suis hic situs sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000017.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Valerius Firmanus lived 60 years, dutiful to his own. May the earth lie lightly on him here.
EDCS-09000017.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Valerius Firmanus at age 60, with a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-09000018.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Vibius Palaemon annorum \n
EDCS-09000018.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Vibius Palaemon, aged...
EDCS-09000018.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Vibius Palaemon, whose age is incomplete.

EDCS-09000019.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Vibius Severus nnorum XXXXX pius in suis S S \n
EDCS-09000019.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Vibius Severus, aged 50, dutiful to his own. [Restored text indicating burial or dedication.]
EDCS-09000019.summary: A funerary inscription for Vibius Severus, who died at age 50.

EDCS-09000026.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Cornelius Publi libertus Clarus annorum XXII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis posuit pius pater\n
EDCS-09000026.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Cornelius Clarus, freedman of Publius, aged 22, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on him. His devoted father erected this.
EDCS-09000026.summary: A tombstone erected by a father for his son, Publius Cornelius Clarus, who died at age 22.

EDCS-09000028.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius Luci filius Luci nepos Silur\n
EDCS-09000028.translation: Lucius Cornelius, son of Lucius, grandson of Lucius, Silur[...]
EDCS-09000028.summary: A funerary or honorary inscription identifying Lucius Cornelius by his lineage.

EDCS-09000029.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Nusatita puer serve sic pronatus natione Tracie sic plus minus annorum III iacet petito beneficio in locum Campanianensem\n
EDCS-09000029.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Nusatita, a boy, born of a slave, of Thracian nationality, approximately 3 years old, lies here, having sought a favor and obtained a place in the Campanian region.
EDCS-09000029.summary: A tombstone for a young Thracian boy named Nusatita, who died around age 3 and was buried in the Campanian region.

EDCS-09000030.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publicia Fortunalis annorum XXI pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000030.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Publicia Fortunalis, aged 21, dutiful to her own, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on her.
EDCS-09000030.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Publicia Fortunalis at age 21, with a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-09000031.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Lictoria Gallica, aged 60, dutiful to her own. Publius Cornelius Maurus erected this. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly on him.
EDCS-09000031.summary: A tombstone for Lictoria Gallica, aged 60, erected by Publius Cornelius Maurus, likely her husband or relative.

EDCS-09000032.text_edition: \n Cornelius Cervi filius Niger pater a Galduriaunin ? uxor \n
EDCS-09000032.translation: Cornelius Niger, son of Cervius, father, [and] Galduria (?) [his?] wife.
EDCS-09000032.summary: An inscription identifying Cornelius Niger as a father and mentioning his wife Galduria.

EDCS-09000034.text_edition: \n Sacrum Polluci Sextus Quintius Sexti Quinti Successini libertus Fortunatus ob honorem VIviratus ex decreto ordinis soluta pecunia petente populo donum de sua pecunia dato epulo civibus et incolis et circensibus factis dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000034.translation: Sacred to Pollux. Sextus Quintius Fortunatus, freedman of Sextus Quintius Successinus, gave this gift from his own funds in honor of the office of VIvir, by decree of the council, with payment made, at the request of the people, with a banquet given to citizens and residents and with games.
EDCS-09000034.summary: An inscription recording a donation by Sextus Quintius Fortunatus, a freedman, to honor his VIvirate office, including public festivities.

EDCS-09000035.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Caesari Augusto Germanico imperatori pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate IIII consuli designato IIII patri patriae \n
EDCS-09000035.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, Emperor, Chief Priest, holding tribunician power for the fourth time, consul designate for the fourth time, Father of the Fatherland.
EDCS-09000035.summary: This inscription records titles bestowed upon Emperor Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, likely part of a public monument from his reign.

EDCS-09000037.text_edition: \n Tiberio Sempronio Graccho deductori populus Iliturgitanus\n
EDCS-09000037.translation: To Tiberius Sempronius Gracchus, founder, by the people of Iliturgi.
EDCS-09000037.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the people of Iliturgi to Tiberius Sempronius Gracchus, likely honoring him as a founder or benefactor.

EDCS-09000038.text_edition: \n ------] SAMIG CS EORV III et Septimius Priscus mi(les) cohortis IIII praetoriae Aurelius Marcellinus miles cohortis VIIII praetoriae Valerius Martinianus miles cohortis VI praetoriae S R\n
EDCS-09000038.translation: [---] Samig, of the third [cohort?], and Septimius Priscus, soldier of the 4th Praetorian Cohort, Aurelius Marcellinus, soldier of the 9th Praetorian Cohort, Valerius Martinianus, soldier of the 6th Praetorian Cohort. [---]
EDCS-09000038.summary: This fragmented inscription lists several soldiers, likely from Praetorian Cohorts, possibly recording a dedication or funerary information.

EDCS-09000039.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Septimia Septimi Sabiniani militis cohortis VIII praetoriae et Aemiliae Iustae filia Adventa bene merens a suis propter quam ragamus parentes pientissumi sic collegas succedentes deincepsque successores sic ne quis vestrum talem dolorem esperiscatur ut huiius sic Manibus lucerna quotidiana ex ratione publika sic vestra poni \n
EDCS-09000039.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Adventa, daughter of Septimia and the soldier of the 8th Praetorian Cohort Septimius, and of Aemilia Iusta, having shown kindness, and for whose sake we, her most devoted parents, implore you, colleagues succeeding and in turn successors, that no one among you may feel such grief, but that a daily lamp may be placed for the spirits of this person from your public funds.
EDCS-09000039.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by parents for their deceased daughter Adventa, requesting that a daily lamp be maintained by her colleagues and successors.

EDCS-09000040.text_edition: \n Memoriae Aemiliae Quinti filiae Iustae Ossigitanae matris indulgentissimae filius fecit\n
EDCS-09000040.translation: For the memory of Aemilia Iusta of Ossigi, most loving mother. Her son made this.
EDCS-09000040.summary: This inscription is a memorial monument erected by a son for his mother, Aemilia Iusta, who was from Ossigi.

EDCS-09000041.text_edition: \n Sexto Quintio Sexti filio Galeria Vitali Iliturgitano Quintia Vitalis mater filio piissumo sic accepto loco decreto decurionum de suo posuit\n
EDCS-09000041.translation: Quintia Vitalis placed this for her son Sextus Quintius Vitalis of Iliturgi, son of Sextus, of the Galerian tribe, most devoted son, having received the place by decree of the decurions, from her own property.
EDCS-09000041.summary: This inscription records a mother's dedication of a burial plot, acquired by decree of the local council, to her devoted son Sextus Quintius Vitalis of Iliturgi.

EDCS-09000042.translation: To [---] Aulus Valerius Priscus, father, for his son.
EDCS-09000042.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a father to his son, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-09000043.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription that likely records a dedication or funerary context.

EDCS-09000044.text_edition: \n ------] Octavia Iliturgitana nnum X pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000044.translation: [---] Octavia of Iliturgi, [who lived] 10 years, pious, is buried here on her own property. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000044.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a young girl named Octavia of Iliturgi, stating her age and pious nature, and including a common funerary wish.

EDCS-09000045.text_edition: \n Aulo Annio Masculi liberto Pyladi ex testamento hoc monimentum seu sepulcrum est heredem non sequitur\n
EDCS-09000045.translation: This monument or tomb belongs to Aulus Annius Masculi, freedman Pylades, and does not follow an heir.
EDCS-09000045.summary: This inscription declares that the monument or tomb belonging to the freedman Pylades is independent of inheritance claims.

EDCS-09000046.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Octavia Laeda annorum XXXV sita hic pia in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000046.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Octavia Laeda, aged 35, lies here, dutiful to her own. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000046.summary: A funerary epitaph for Octavia Laeda, who died at 35.

EDCS-09000059.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum ctorina Cantigitana Marcianus li A \n
EDCS-09000059.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Ctorina Cantigitana. Marcianus [---].
EDCS-09000059.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription, likely commemorating Ctorina Cantigitana.

EDCS-09000060.text_edition: \n Gaius Aelius Epaphroditus annorum L pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000060.translation: Gaius Aelius Epaphroditus, aged 50, pious to his own, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000060.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Aelius Epaphroditus, who died at 50.

EDCS-09000061.text_edition: \n Signum Martis Augusti Aulus Terentius Auli filius Galeria Rusticus aedilis IIvir pontifex municipum municipii Triumphalis ludis scaenicis factis de sua pecunia dedit\n
EDCS-09000061.translation: Aulus Terentius Rusticus, son of Aulus, of the Galerian tribe, aedile twice, duumvir, pontiff, of the citizens of the municipality of Triumphalis, dedicated this statue of Mars Augustus, having put on the scenic games at his own expense.
EDCS-09000061.summary: A dedication of a statue of Mars Augustus by Aulus Terentius Rusticus, a local magistrate, to accompany public games.

EDCS-09000062.text_edition: \n Polluci Augusto Porcia Gamice flaminica municipum municipii Triumphalis dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000062.translation: Porcia Gamice, priestess of the citizens of the municipality of Triumphalis, dedicated and consecrated this to Pollux Augustus.
EDCS-09000062.summary: A dedication to Pollux Augustus by Porcia Gamice, a priestess of the municipality of Triumphalis.

EDCS-09000063.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto Runcanius Victor optio cohortis I Asturum et Callaecorum \n
EDCS-09000063.translation: Runcanius Victor, optio of the First Cohort of Astures and Callaeci, [dedicated this] to Silvanus Augustus.
EDCS-09000063.summary: A dedication to Silvanus Augustus by Runcanius Victor, an officer in a cohort of Astures and Callaeci.

EDCS-09000064.text_edition: \n Veneri Augustae Lucius Cornelius Amandus Lucius Cornelius Terpnus \n
EDCS-09000064.translation: Lucius Cornelius Amandus and Lucius Cornelius Terpnus [dedicated this] to Venus Augusta.
EDCS-09000064.summary: A dedication to Venus Augusta by Lucius Cornelius Amandus and Lucius Cornelius Terpnus.

EDCS-09000065.text_edition: \n Agrippae Caesari Augusti filio pontu sicfici\n
EDCS-09000065.translation: [Dedicated] to Agrippa Caesar, son of Augustus, pontiff.
EDCS-09000065.summary: A fragmentary dedication to Agrippa Caesar, son of Augustus and a pontiff.

EDCS-09000066.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico pontifici maximo imperatori X tribunicia potestate VI consuli II pacatori orbis res publica Isturgitanorum decreto decurionum dedit\n
EDCS-09000066.translation: The public body of Isturgi, by decree of the decurions, dedicated this to the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, chief pontiff, imperator ten times, with tribunician power six times, consul twice, pacifier of the world.
EDCS-09000066.summary: A dedication by the municipality of Isturgi to Emperor Septimius Severus, commemorating his titles and achievements.

EDCS-09000067.text_edition: \n Quinto Pompeio Quinti filio Serano fecit Manilia Marci filia Navina sponsa\n
EDCS-09000067.translation: Manilia Navina, daughter of Marcius, his betrothed, made [this] for Quintus Pompeius Seranus, son of Quintus.
EDCS-09000067.summary: A memorial stone made by Manilia Navina for her betrothed, Quintus Pompeius Seranus.

EDCS-09000068.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelia Primitiva nnum XXVI hic est sita\n
EDCS-09000068.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Cornelia Primitiva, aged 26, lies here.
EDCS-09000068.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Cornelia Primitiva at the age of 26.

EDCS-09000069.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Herennius Anninius annuclus et mensis I sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000069.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Herennius Anninius, aged one year and one month. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000069.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Herennius Anninius at the age of one year and one month.

EDCS-09000070.text_edition: \n Phyteros sic annorum III hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000070.translation: Phyteros, aged 3 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000070.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Phyteros at the age of 3 years.

EDCS-09000071.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Primitivus annorum XII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000071.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Primitivus, aged 12 years, dutiful to his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000071.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Primitivus at the age of 12 years.

EDCS-09000072.text_edition: \n Valeria Publi liberta Nice Valeria Publi liberta Aucta Rustici\n
EDCS-09000072.translation: Nice, freedwoman of Valeria Publius. Aucta, freedwoman of Valeria Publius and Rusticus.
EDCS-09000072.summary: A fragmentary inscription that appears to be a list of names, likely related to freedwomen.

EDCS-09000073.text_edition: \n Seppia Luci liberta Lyde nnorum V pia in suis sit tibi terra levis Seppia Luci liberta Psyche mater nnorum XXXX pia in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000073.translation: Seppia Lyde, freedwoman of Seppius Lucius, aged 5 years, dutiful to her family. May the earth lie lightly on you. Seppia Psyche, mother, freedwoman of Seppius Lucius, aged 40 years, dutiful to her family. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000073.summary: A tombstone recording the deaths of Seppia Lyde, aged 5, and her mother Seppia Psyche, aged 40.

EDCS-09000074.text_edition: \n Caius Venaecius Publi filius Voconianus flamen Divorum Auggustorum praefectus cohortis I Chalcedonensis tribunus legionis III Gallicae felicis praefectus alae I Lemavorum Fortunae signum aureum pondo V item Mercurio pondo V pateram pondo librae ex voto et bases II argenteas pondo V libens solvit\n
EDCS-09000074.translation: Gaius Venaecius Voconianus, son of Publius, Flamen of the Divine Augusti, Prefect of the 1st Cohort of Chalcedon, Tribune of the 3rd Lucky Gallic Legion, Prefect of the 1st Wing of the Lemavi, willingly paid his vow with a golden statue of Fortuna weighing 5 pounds, and likewise Mercury weighing 5 pounds, and a paterae weighing a pound, and 2 silver bases weighing 5 pounds.
EDCS-09000074.summary: A dedication of statues and bases to Fortuna and Mercury by Gaius Venaecius Voconianus, a military and religious official, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09000075.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Augusto sacrum in honore pontificatus Lucius Calpurnius Luci filius Galeria Silvinus IIvir bis flamen sacrorum publicorum municipii Albensis Urgavonensis pontifex domus Augustae de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000075.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater Augustus. In honor of his pontificate, Lucius Calpurnius Silvinus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, twice duumvir, flamen of public sacrifices of the municipality of Albensis Urgavonensis, and pontifex of the imperial household, dedicated and consecrated this from his own funds.
EDCS-09000075.summary: A dedication of a sacred object to Liber Pater Augustus by Lucius Calpurnius Silvinus, a prominent local official, in honor of his religious offices.

EDCS-09000076.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XXXIIII consuli XIII patri patriae Victoriae sacrum Lucius Aemilius Luci filius Nigellus aedilis IIvir de sua pecunia fecit\n
EDCS-09000076.translation: Sacred to Victory. Lucius Aemilius Nigellus, son of Lucius, aedile and duumvir, made this from his own funds in honor of Emperor Caesar Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 34th time, consul for the 13th time, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-09000076.summary: A dedication to Victory by Lucius Aemilius Nigellus, an official, likely during the reign of Emperor Augustus, commemorating his civic roles.

EDCS-09000080.translation: By decree of the town councilors to Iulia Augusta.
EDCS-09000080.summary: A dedication or honorific inscription to Iulia Augusta, enacted by a local town council.

EDCS-09000083.text_edition: \n Marco Helvio Marci filio Galeria Novato IIviro pontifici divi Augusti T \n
EDCS-09000083.translation: To Marcus Helvius Novatus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, twice-man, pontiff, by decree of the divine Augustus.
EDCS-09000083.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Helvius Novatus, a local magistrate and priest, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09000084.text_edition: \n Marco Helvio Varo Augustali perpetuo decreto decurionum Helvia Procula uxor honorem accepit inpensam remisit\n
EDCS-09000084.translation: To Marcus Helvius Varus, perpetual Augustalis; by decree of the town councillors, his wife Helvia Procula accepted the honor and waived the expense.
EDCS-09000084.summary: This records a posthumous honor granted to Marcus Helvius Varus by the town councillors, with his wife Helvia Procula undertaking the associated costs.

EDCS-09000086.text_edition: \n Quinto Mario Quinti filio Hispano patri decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09000086.translation: To Quintus Marius Hispanus, son of Quintus, by decree of the town councillors.
EDCS-09000086.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Marius Hispanus, likely a local notable, by decree of the town councillors.

EDCS-09000088.summary: This is a simple naming of Marcus Aemilius Secundus, possibly a tombstone or a dedication.

EDCS-09000089.summary: This inscription names Marcus Helvius Lochus, perhaps indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-09000097.text_edition: \n Aulus Cantilius IIvir bis editis ludis \n
EDCS-09000097.translation: To Aulus Cantilius, twice-man, who twice presented games.
EDCS-09000097.summary: This honors Aulus Cantilius for his service as a twice-man and for sponsoring public games.

EDCS-09000099.text_edition: \n Marcus Horatius Marci filius Galeria Bodonilur IIvir Lucretia Luci filia Sergieton uxor\n
EDCS-09000099.translation: Marcus Horatius, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, twice-man; his wife Lucretia, daughter of Lucius, of the Sergieton tribe.
EDCS-09000099.summary: This inscription names Marcus Horatius, a twice-man, and his wife Lucretia, likely marking a public building or monument.

EDCS-09000100.text_edition: \n E S Publius Manlius Atictus votum solvit\n
EDCS-09000100.translation: Publius Manlius Atictus paid his vow.
EDCS-09000100.summary: This records Publius Manlius Atictus fulfilling a vow, likely a private religious offering.

EDCS-09000101.text_edition: \n Caius Cornelius Cai filius Cai nepos Galeria Caeso aedilis flamen IIvir municipi Pontificiensis Caius Cornelius Caeso filius sacerdos Geni municipi scrofam cum porcis triginta impensa ipsorum dederunt dedicaverunt PONTIFEX \n
EDCS-09000101.translation: Gaius Cornelius Caeso, son of Gaius, grandson of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, flamen, and twice-man of the municipium of Pontifeciensis; Gaius Cornelius Caeso, son, priest of the Genius of the municipium, dedicated and offered a sow with thirty piglets at their own expense. PONTIFEX.
EDCS-09000101.summary: This lengthy inscription records a dedication of a sow and piglets by father and son, both named Gaius Cornelius Caeso, who held significant civic and religious offices in the municipium of Pontifeciensis.

EDCS-09000104.text_edition: \n Gratus pontifex Augusti Pomptili na quae fecerat na eo nomine et Pomptiliae filiae suae dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000104.translation: Gratus, priest of Augustus, dedicated and gave to Pomptilia, his daughter, what he had made in her name.
EDCS-09000104.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gratus, a priest of Augustus, to his daughter Pomptilia.

EDCS-09000105.text_edition: \n - Quintius Quinti filius Quinti nepos Quinti pronepos Quinti abnepos Galeria Hispanus tus aedilis flamen IIvir pontifex municipi Pontificiensis curator Baetis praefectus cohortis P rum equitatae comes et adsessor legati ad s et adsessor proconsulis provinciae Galliae Narbonensis compluribus immunitatibus et beneficiis INTER DIFFVSE principibus honoratus tabernas et post horreum solo empto ab re publica de sua pecunia dono dedit\n
EDCS-09000105.translation: Quintus Galeria Hispanus, son of Quintus, grandson of Quintus, great-grandson of Quintus, great-great-grandson of Quintus, aedile, priest, duovir, pontifex of the municipality of Pontifex, curator of the Baetis, prefect of an auxiliary cavalry cohort, companion and assessor to a legate, and assessor to the proconsul of the province of Gallia Narbonensis, honored with numerous immunities and privileges among the leading men, by buying from the state with his own money, donated shops and a post office in exchange for the granary.
EDCS-09000105.summary: This inscription records the dedication of shops and a post office by Quintus Galeria Hispanus, a prominent local official, to the municipality of Pontifex.

EDCS-09000106.text_edition: \n Lucius Porcius Luci filius Galeria Stilo Obulconensis nnum LXV aedilis IIvir designatus pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis huic ordo Pontificiensis Obulconensis locum sepulturae impensam funeris laudationem statuam equestrem decrevit II\n
EDCS-09000106.translation: Lucius Porcius Stilo Obulconensis, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 65, aedile, designated duovir, pious in his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. For him, the order of Pontifex Obulconensis decreed a burial place, funeral expenses, an eulogy, and an equestrian statue.
EDCS-09000106.summary: This epitaph records the death of Lucius Porcius Stilo Obulconensis and honors him with various civic commendations by the order of Pontifex Obulconensis.

EDCS-09000107.text_edition: \n filius Pyramus IIvir Patriciensis et Pontificiensis annorum LXXX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000107.translation: Pyramus, son of [---], of the Patriciensis and Pontifikiensis tribes, aged 80, pious in his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000107.summary: This is a simple epitaph for Pyramus, likely a resident of Patriciensis and Pontifikiensis, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-09000108.text_edition: \n Marco Valerio Marci filio Marci nepoti Quinti pronepoti Galeria Pullino IIviro legato perpetuo municipii Pontificiensis praefecto fabrum flamini pontifici Augusti municipes et incolae\n
EDCS-09000108.translation: For Marcus Valerius, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, great-grandson of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, Pullinus, duovir for life of the municipality of Pontifex, prefect of the engineers, pontifex of Augustus, the townspeople and inhabitants [dedicated this].
EDCS-09000108.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the citizens and residents to Marcus Valerius Pullinus, a long-serving and high-ranking official of the municipality of Pontifex.

EDCS-09000109.text_edition: \n Marcus Valerius Marci filius Cerialis nnorum XII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Marcus Valerius Marci libertus Tertullus VIvir Augustalis nnorum LVII sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000109.translation: Marcus Valerius Cerialis, son of Marcus, aged 12, pious in his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Marcus Valerius Tertullus, freedman of Marcus, Augustalis priest, aged 57, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000109.summary: This epitaph records the burial of two individuals: Marcus Valerius Cerialis, a boy, and Marcus Valerius Tertullus, a freedman and Augustalis priest.

EDCS-09000110.text_edition: \n Liberalis infector hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000110.translation: Liberalis the dyer lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000110.summary: This is a simple epitaph for Liberalis, identified by his profession as a dyer.

EDCS-09000111.text_edition: \n Marcus Calpurnius Marci filius Marci nepos Galeria Mo nnorum LXXXII huic ordo ob merita \n
EDCS-09000111.translation: For our Marcus Calpurnius, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, aged 82, the order [honored him] for his merits.
EDCS-09000111.summary: This inscription records a posthumous honor granted by the town council to Marcus Calpurnius for his merits.

EDCS-09000112.translation: For our Aelia, Hyllus her freedman [dedicated this].
EDCS-09000112.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Hyllus, a freedman, to his former mistress Aelia.

EDCS-09000114.text_edition: \n Corneliae Marti filiae Scitae Laetus pater et Laetus filius dederunt\n
EDCS-09000114.translation: For Cornelia, daughter of Martis, of the Scitae tribe, Laetus the father and Laetus the son dedicated [this].
EDCS-09000114.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Laetus and his son to Cornelia, daughter of Martis.

EDCS-09000115.translation: For our Pollio, Ianuarius his freedman [dedicated this].
EDCS-09000115.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Ianuarius, a freedman, to his former patron Pollio.

EDCS-09000116.text_edition: \n Lucio Sempronio Postumo Laetus pater et Laetus filius dederunt\n
EDCS-09000116.translation: Lucius Sempronius Postumus the father and Lucius Postumus the son dedicated this.
EDCS-09000116.summary: This is a dedication of a monument, likely a tomb, by a father and son named Lucius Sempronius Postumus and Lucius Postumus.

EDCS-09000117.text_edition: \n Lucius Antistius Gra natus nnorum LXXXIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Antistia Philusa annorum LXVII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000117.translation: Lucius Antistius Gracchus, aged 83, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Antistia Philusa, aged 67, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000117.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Lucius Antistius Gracchus and Antistia Philusa, with a wish for peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-09000118.text_edition: \n In agro pedes XIIII in fronte pedes XIIII Cornelia Cnaei liberta Barbara hic sita est nnorum XXXIII sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000118.translation: In the land 14 feet, in front 14 feet. Cornelia Barbara, freedwoman of Cnaeus, is buried here, aged 33; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000118.summary: This tombstone specifies the dimensions of a burial plot and records the burial of Cornelia Barbara, a freedwoman.

EDCS-09000119.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Cornelius Felix nnorum LX et Publius Cornelius Valerianus filius nnorum XVIIII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000119.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Publius Cornelius Felix, aged 60, and his son Publius Cornelius Valerianus, aged 19, pious to his own, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000119.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Publius Cornelius Felix and his son Publius Cornelius Valerianus, with a dedication to the underworld spirits.

EDCS-09000120.text_edition: \n Publius Cornelius Publi libertus Theodulus nnorum XXXIII pius in suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000120.translation: Publius Cornelius Theodulus, freedman of Publius, aged 33, pious to his own, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000120.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Publius Cornelius Theodulus, a freedman, and his devotion to his family.

EDCS-09000121.text_edition: \n Publius Cornelius Publi filius Victor annorum XVIII pius in suos hic situs est\n
EDCS-09000121.translation: Publius Cornelius Victor, son of Publius, aged 18, pious to his own, is buried here.
EDCS-09000121.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Publius Cornelius Victor, a young man devoted to his family.

EDCS-09000122.text_edition: \n Marcus Fabius Marci filius Galeria Marullus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000122.translation: Marcus Fabius Marullus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000122.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Marcus Fabius Marullus, including his name, father's name, and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-09000123.text_edition: \n Locus pedum in fronte ---XII in agro XV Offilliena Luci liberta Fausta hic sita est\n
EDCS-09000123.translation: A plot of 12 feet in front, 15 in the land. Offilliena Fausta, freedwoman of Lucius, is buried here.
EDCS-09000123.summary: This tombstone specifies the dimensions of a burial plot and records the burial of Offilliena Fausta, a freedwoman.

EDCS-09000124.text_edition: \n Marcus Porcius Marci filius heredibus mando etiam cinere ut meo vina subspargant ut volitet meus ebrius papilio ipsa ossa tegant herbae si quis titulum ad mei nominis astiterit dicat avidus ignis quod corpore resoluto se vertit in favillam\n
EDCS-09000124.translation: To the departed spirits I entrust my ashes. May my ashes be sprinkled with wine, so that my drunken butterfly may flutter, and herbs may cover my bones. If anyone stands by my monument and speaks greedily of fire, let him say that, when the body is resolved, it turns to ash.
EDCS-09000124.summary: This epitaph is a poetic testament from Marcus Porcius Marullus, son of Marcus, instructing how his ashes should be treated and reflecting on mortality.

EDCS-09000125.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Primigenia annorum XXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000125.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Primigenia, aged 25, pious to her own, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000125.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Primigenia and her devotion to her family, with a dedication to the underworld spirits.

EDCS-09000126.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publia Severina nnorum XXXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000126.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Publia Severina, aged 35, pious toward her own, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000126.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publia Severina, a woman aged 35, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000127.text_edition: \n Sempronia Marci filia Campana annorum LXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000127.translation: Sempronia, daughter of Marcus, Campana, aged 65, pious toward her own, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000127.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sempronia Campana, a woman aged 65, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000133.text_edition: \n nicio Luci filio Luci nepoti Luci pronepoti Galeria Mentoni Man aedili IIviro III praefecto IIvirali potestate praefecto fabrum pontufici Augusti optimo decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09000133.translation: To Nicio, son of Lucius, grandson of Lucius, great-grandson of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, Menton, former aedile, IIvir, praefect, former military engineer, and pontiff of Augustus, the best, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09000133.summary: This is a dedication to Nicio, honoring his various public offices and honors bestowed by decree of the decurions, likely from a Roman imperial municipality.

EDCS-09000134.text_edition: \n Cornelius Luci filius nepos pronepos Galeria na aedilis post mortem testamento statuas sibi et patri equestres item matri pedestrem poni iussit quas Cornelia Luci filia Anus soror ponendas curavit\n
EDCS-09000134.translation: Cornelius, son of Lucius, grandson and great-grandson, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, after death by his will ordered equestrian statues of himself and his father, and a pedestrian statue of his mother, to be erected, which his sister Cornelia, daughter of Lucius, Anus, saw to their placement.
EDCS-09000134.summary: This is a funerary monument text recording a posthumous commission by Cornelius, aedile, for equestrian and pedestrian statues, arranged by his sister Cornelia.

EDCS-09000135.text_edition: \n Publius Rutilius Publi libertus Menelavo<s> incola ex decreto decurionum municipum municipi Pontificiensis de suo posuit []M\n
EDCS-09000135.translation: Publius Rutilius Menelaus, freedman of Publius, a resident, by decree of the decurions of the municipality of Pontifex, erected at his own expense.
EDCS-09000135.summary: This is a dedication by Publius Rutilius Menelaus, a freedman, for a public work erected at his own expense by decree of the municipal decurions, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000137.text_edition: \n Optatae Marcus Septumius et Agele serva dant\n
EDCS-09000137.translation: Marcus Septimius and his slave Agele give (this) to Optata.
EDCS-09000137.summary: This is a dedication by Marcus Septimius and his slave Agele to a person named Optata, likely a religious offering.

EDCS-09000138.text_edition: \n Publius Stenius Publi libertus Hylas hic situs est sit tibi terra levis locus in fronte pedum XXXVI in agro pedum XXXVIII\n
EDCS-09000138.translation: Publius Stenius Hylas, freedman of Publius, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. The plot is 36 feet in frontage and 38 feet in depth.
EDCS-09000138.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Stenius Hylas, a freedman, also recording the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-09000139.summary: This is a brief votive inscription by an individual identified as a banker (argentarius), likely recording the fulfillment of a religious vow.

EDCS-09000140.text_edition: \n Numini Sancto Deo Silvano Successianus Augusti nostri servus ex voto cum suis posuit\n
EDCS-09000140.translation: Successianus, slave of our Emperor, with his family, set up (this) to the Holy Spirit of God Silvanus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-09000140.summary: This is a dedication by Successianus, an imperial slave, and his family to the god Silvanus, in fulfillment of a vow during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000141.text_edition: \n Aelia mulieris liberta Phyrrice sic nnorum XXII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000141.translation: Aelia Phyrrice, freedwoman of Aelia, aged 22, pious toward her own, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000141.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Phyrrice, a freedwoman aged 22, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000142.text_edition: \n Quintus Cornelius flamen Divorum Augustorum provinciae Baeticae suo nomine et Valeriae Luci filiae Cornelianae et Marti et Luci Stertini Quintiliani tribuni militum legionis VII geminae et Rustici Aproni Proculi provinciae Baeticae aedem et stratam et statuas marmoreas\n
EDCS-09000142.translation: Quintus Cornelius, flamen of the Divine Emperors of the province of Baetica, dedicated in his own name and for Valeria Cornelia, daughter of Lucius, a temple, a street, and marble statues to Mars, and to Lucius Stertinius Quintilianus, military tribune of the Legio VII Gemina, and to Rusticus Apronius Proculus of the province of Baetica.
EDCS-09000142.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple, street, and statues by Quintus Cornelius, a priest of the deified emperors, to Mars and other individuals.

EDCS-09000143.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication or memorial to Lucius Papirius Senecio.

EDCS-09000147.text_edition: \n Aesculapio Augusto Caius Fulvius Pylades VIvir Augustalis ex HS VI (sex milibus) testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-09000147.translation: Caius Fulvius Pylades, a VIvir Augustalis, ordered by his will to be made at a cost of 6,000 sesterces, to the divine Asclepius Augustus.
EDCS-09000147.summary: This inscription records a bequest by Caius Fulvius Pylades, a member of the vigintiviri Augustales, for the construction of a shrine to Asclepius Augustus.

EDCS-09000148.text_edition: \n Lupae Romanae Marcus Valerius Phoebus VIvir Augustalis cui ordo municipii Eporensis ob merita cenis publicis inter decuriones convenire permisit aliaque ornamenta decrevit insertis statuam ponendam\n
EDCS-09000148.translation: To the Roman Wolf, Marcus Valerius Phoebus, a VIvir Augustalis, to whom the town council of Eporedia permitted, because of his merits, to dine at the public expense among the decurions and decreed other honors, including the placement of a statue.
EDCS-09000148.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Valerius Phoebus, a VIvir Augustalis of Eporedia, who was granted privileges by the town council for his merits.

EDCS-09000150.text_edition: \n Lucio Caesari divi Augusti filio principi iuventutis consuli designato auguri \n
EDCS-09000150.translation: To Lucius Caesar, son of the divine Augustus, prince of the youth, designated consul, augur.
EDCS-09000150.summary: This inscription is a dedication or honorific title for Lucius Caesar, son of Augustus.

EDCS-09000151.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Caesari Augusto Germanico tribunicia potestate III consuli III pontifici maximo patri patriae Lucius Aemilius Galeria Lucius Aemilius filius curaverunt\n
EDCS-09000151.translation: Lucius Aemilius and his son Lucius Aemilius of the Galeria tribe, saw to the construction of this for Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, holder of tribunician power for the third time, consul for the third time, pontifex maximus, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-09000151.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Aemilius and his son oversaw the construction of a monument for Emperor Claudius.

EDCS-09000153.text_edition: \n Publio Attennio Cai filio Galeria Afro flamini Augusti decreto decurionum patrono\n
EDCS-09000153.translation: To Publius Attennius Afro, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, Augustan flamen, patron by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09000153.summary: This inscription honors Publius Attennius Afro, an Augustan flamen and patron, in recognition of a decree by the local council.

EDCS-09000154.text_edition: \n Luci Calpurni Luci fili flaminis Fulcinia Luci filia Attunna Calpurnia Luci liberta Uprenna \n
EDCS-09000154.translation: Lucius Calpurnius, son of Lucius, flamen; Fulcinia Attunna Calpurnia, freedwoman of Lucius, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-09000154.summary: This inscription lists the names of Lucius Calpurnius, a flamen, and Fulcinia Attunna Calpurnia, a freedwoman.

EDCS-09000155.translation: To Lucius Modius Priscus, duovir, the people.
EDCS-09000155.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the people to Lucius Modius Priscus, a duovir.

EDCS-09000156.text_edition: \n sacerdoti Herculis Modia Rusticula mater dedit\n
EDCS-09000156.translation: Modia Rusticula gave this to the priest of Hercules.
EDCS-09000156.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication to a priest of Hercules by his mother, Modia Rusticula.

EDCS-09000157.text_edition: \n Marco Atilio Salviani filio Salviano decreto decurionum Marcus Atilius Atticus consobrinus honore usus impensam remisit\n
EDCS-09000157.translation: To Marcus Atilius Salvianus, son of Salvianus. By decree of the town council, Marcus Atilius Atticus, his cousin, having received the honor, waived the expense.
EDCS-09000157.summary: This inscription records a posthumous honor voted by the town council for Marcus Atilius Salvianus, with his cousin declining to spend money on it.

EDCS-09000158.translation: To Iulia Rectina, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-09000158.summary: This is a dedication to Iulia Rectina, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-09000159.translation: Lucretia Grata, wife of the Princeps.
EDCS-09000159.summary: This inscription names Lucretia Grata, the wife of a prominent local official (Princeps).

EDCS-09000160.text_edition: \n Lucio Mamilio Tusco decreto decurionum Lucius Mamilius Avitus filius honore accepto impensam remisit\n
EDCS-09000160.translation: To Lucius Mamilius Tuscus. By decree of the town council, Lucius Mamilius Avitus, his son, having accepted the honor, waived the expense.
EDCS-09000160.summary: This inscription records a posthumous honor voted by the town council for Lucius Mamilius Tuscus, with his son declining to spend money on it.

EDCS-09000161.text_edition: \n Persiae Luci filiae matri de suo\n
EDCS-09000161.translation: To Persis, daughter of Lucius, from her own funds.
EDCS-09000161.summary: This is a dedication by Persis, daughter of Lucius, funded by herself.

EDCS-09000162.text_edition: \n Publio Valerio Egerino Lucius Marius Persini libertus Phileros\n
EDCS-09000162.translation: To Publius Valerius Egerinus, from Lucius Marius Persini's freedman Phileros.
EDCS-09000162.summary: This is a dedication to Publius Valerius Egerinus, set up by Phileros, a freedman of Lucius Marius Persini.

EDCS-09000163.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus sacrum Antistius Patriciensis vixit nnis LXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000163.translation: Sacred to the Deities of the Underworld. Antistius Patriciensis lived 75 years, dutiful to his family; he lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000163.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antistius Patriciensis, who lived 75 years, noting his piety and including a common Latin epitaph formula.

EDCS-09000164.text_edition: \n Quintus Domitius Quinti libertus Macer Consaburensis locus pedum in fronte XV in agro pedum XX\n
EDCS-09000164.translation: Quintus Domitius Macer, freedman of Quintus, from Consabura. A plot 15 feet in front and 20 feet in the rear.
EDCS-09000164.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial plot size for Quintus Domitius Macer, a freedman from Consabura.

EDCS-09000165.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum M Cai libert- NA XXXV \n
EDCS-09000165.translation: Sacred to the Deities of the Underworld. Freedman of M. Caius, aged 35.
EDCS-09000165.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a freedman of M. Caius, giving his age.

EDCS-09000166.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caesiae Methoce sic annorum L pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000166.translation: Sacred to the Deities of the Underworld. Caesia Methoce lies here, having lived 50 years, dutiful to her family. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000166.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caesia Methoce, who lived 50 years, noting her piety and including a common Latin epitaph formula.

EDCS-09000167.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gnome annorum XXXIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000167.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gnome, 33 years old, piously laid to rest here among her family; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000167.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Gnome who died at age 33, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000168.text_edition: \n Caius Memmius Cai filius Valeria Cai filia\n
EDCS-09000168.translation: Gaius Memmius, son of Caius, and Valeria, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-09000168.summary: This inscription appears to record the names of two individuals, likely a married couple or relatives, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000169.text_edition: \n Roscia Cnaei filia Maurilla sibi et Marco Clodio Marci filio Marcellino viro suo testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-09000169.translation: Roscia Maurilla, daughter of Cnaeus, ordered by will that this monument be made for herself and for her husband, Marcus Clodius Marcellinus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-09000169.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by Roscia Maurilla in her will for herself and her husband, Marcus Clodius Marcellinus, indicating a date within the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000170.text_edition: \n Caius Sar Acutius anuclus et mensium V dierum V hic situs est sit tibi <t=L>(erra) levis \n
EDCS-09000170.translation: Gaius Sar Acutius, one year old and 5 months and 5 days, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000170.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child named Gaius Sar Acutius, who died at less than two years of age, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000187.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lasciva pia frugi annum agens XVII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000187.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lasciva, pious and virtuous, 17 years old, is laid to rest here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000187.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young woman named Lasciva who died at age 17, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000188.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ex voto Lapa Catuli serv-\n
EDCS-09000188.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, according to a vow, Lapa Catuli, a slave-
EDCS-09000188.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a person identified as Lapa Catuli, a slave, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000189.text_edition: \n Caio Pomponio Quirina Marullo IIviro Lucius Aemilius Avitus avonculus sic et Caius Pomponius Lupus frater heredes statuam quam is testamento ex HS IIICC sibi poni iussit adiectis HS CC posuerunt huic ordo decrevit laudationem exsequias publicas funeris inpensam locum sepulturae statuam\n
EDCS-09000189.translation: Lucius Aemilius Avitus, the uncle, and Gaius Pomponius Lupus, the brother, erected this statue for Gaius Pomponius Marullus, IIvir of the Quirina tribe, for whom he ordered by will that a statue be erected for himself at a cost of 2300 sesterces, with an additional 200 sesterces; the town council decreed for him a eulogy, public funeral rites, the cost of the funeral, and a burial place, as well as the statue.
EDCS-09000189.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue and the bestowal of public honors and funeral expenses by a town council for Gaius Pomponius Marullus, a local magistrate (IIvir), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000190.translation: Cirrata; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000190.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an individual named Cirrata, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000191.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelianus annorum XXIIII pius in <s>(uis) hic situs est s(it) l(evis) tibi terra vale\n
EDCS-09000191.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cornelianus, 24 years old, pious towards his family, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Farewell.
EDCS-09000191.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a man named Cornelianus who died at age 24, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000192.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Euche annorum XVII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000192.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Euche, 17 years old, is laid to rest here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000192.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young woman named Euche who died at age 17, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000193.text_edition: \n Caius Fulvius Pylades sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-09000193.translation: May the earth lie lightly on you, Gaius Fulvius Pylades.
EDCS-09000193.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Fulvius Pylades, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000194.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-09000194.text_edition: \n Crasso IIviro decreto decurionum huic post mortem laudatio inpensa funeris statua decreta sunt fili eius honore usi sunt inpensam remiserunt\n
EDCS-09000194.translation: By decree of the town councillors, Crassus, as aedile of the duumvirs, was granted posthumous praise and a statue at the expense of his funeral; his sons used this honour and waived the expenses.
EDCS-09000194.summary: A decree by the town councillors of an unidentified city honoring Crassus, an aedile, with posthumous praise and a statue, with his sons later remitting the funeral costs, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000195.text_edition: \n Porcia Placida Dis Manibus pia in suis \n
EDCS-09000195.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Porcia Placida, dutiful to her own.
EDCS-09000195.summary: A funerary epitaph for Porcia Placida, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000196.text_edition: \n Quinto Marcio Barea Tito Rustio Nummio Gallo consulibus senatus populusque Baxonensis hospitium fecit cum colonis coloniae Claritatis Iuliae ipsis liberis posterisque suis egerunt legati Marcus Fabius Quinti filius Rufus Caius Terentius Publi filius Macer Armeni felix Imperatore [Philippo] Augusto et Ph[ilippo] Caesare consulibus collegium corporis fabrorum subaedianorum Patriciensium Cordubensium Bellum Licinianum se liberosque suos patronum cooptaverunt postea Bellus Licinianus corpus fabrorum subaedianorum Cordubensium Patriciensium in familiam clientelamque suam recepit\n
EDCS-09000196.translation: In the consulship of Quintus Marcius Barea and Titus Rustius Nummius Gallus, the Senate and People of Baxones made a guest-friendship treaty with the colonists of the Colony of Claritas Iulia, for themselves and their children and descendants. The envoys were Marcus Fabius Rufus, son of Quintus, and Caius Terentius Macer, son of Publius. In the consulship of Emperor [Philip] Augustus and Caesar [Philip], the guild of the under-roofed builders of Patriciensium in Corduba elected Bellum Licinianus and his children as their patron. Later, Bellus Licinianus took the guild of the under-roofed builders of Patriciensium in Corduba into his family and patronage.
EDCS-09000196.summary: A record of a guest-friendship treaty between the people of Baxones and the colonists of Claritas Iulia, followed by the adoption of Bellum Licinianus as patron by a guild of builders in Corduba, dating to the consulship of Philip the Arab and his son, 247 CE.

EDCS-09000198.translation: To our Rufus, from his superior clerk.
EDCS-09000198.summary: A dedication to 'Rufus' by his superior clerk, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000199.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Natalis annorum XXXX pia in su<os=AN> sit terra levis \n
EDCS-09000199.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Natalis, aged 40, dutiful to her own. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000199.summary: A funerary epitaph for Natalis, aged 40, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000202.summary: An inscription indicating origin from the workshop of Celsus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000211.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Acilius Luci filius Galeria Barba IIvir V nnorum LXIIII Acilia Luci filia Lepidina nnorum LVI Lucius Acilius Luci filius Galeria Terentianus IIvir nnorum LVI Corneliae Quinti filiae Lepidinae nnum LXI uxori indulgentissumae sic maritus piissumus sic flaminicae municipium Saciliense decreto decurionum funerum impensas laudationes loca sepulturae statuas dedit hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-09000211.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Acilius Barba, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, duumvir, aged 62. Acilia Lepidina, daughter of Lucius, aged 56. Lucius Acilius Terentianus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, duumvir, aged 56. To his most indulgent wife Cornelia Lepidina, daughter of Quintus, aged 61. Thus the most dutiful husband. Thus the flaminica. The municipality of Saciliense, by decree of the town councillors, granted funeral expenses, laudations, burial plots, and statues. They are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you all.
EDCS-09000211.summary: A family funerary monument from Saciliense dedicated to Lucius Acilius Barba, his daughter Acilia Lepidina, his son Lucius Acilius Terentianus, and his wife Cornelia Lepidina, with the municipality providing funeral honors, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000212.text_edition: \n Faustus offector hic situs est sit tibi terra levis in fronte locus pedum XXV in agro pedum XXIIII\n
EDCS-09000212.translation: Faustus the cobbler is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. In front, a space of 25 feet; in the field, 24 feet.
EDCS-09000212.summary: A funerary epitaph for Faustus the cobbler, including the dimensions of his burial plot, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000213.text_edition: \n Quintus Cassius Nigri filius Celtiber ordine turbato maior sepelire minorem debuit et patria condere ab arce procul ID triste Cperdito an VE rapto terra nec mollius in patria fuerat SI tu mihi sola places sed S illic mater eras F IONVCITS mutais spatio quo I te qua morte ratis VT C unius et pueri OT VAE PROPERASC DC \n
EDCS-09000213.translation: Quintus Cassius Nigri filius Celtiber, being the younger, ought to bury the elder and in his homeland. from the citadel afar. ID sad. Clost. or stolen. VE. earth. nor more softly in homeland. SI you alone please me. but S there mother you were. F IONVCITS change space Q I you what death thought. VT C of one and child. OT WAE HURRY. DC.
EDCS-09000213.summary: A heavily damaged and fragmented funerary epitaph that seems to express grief and lamentation over a death, possibly involving a young person, with a complex and partially reconstructed text, dating uncertain but likely Roman Imperial.

EDCS-09000214.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fortunata vixit annis XXXXII pia in suis S \n
EDCS-09000214.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunata lived for 42 years, devoted to her family.
EDCS-09000214.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and piety of Fortunata, who died at age 42.

EDCS-09000215.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucilla annorum XXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000215.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucilla, aged 25, devoted to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000215.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucilla, who died at age 25 and is buried here.

EDCS-09000216.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a tombstone, naming Moschion and his father, also Moschion.

EDCS-09000217.text_edition: \n Genio coloniae Patriciae Lucius Flaminius Severus Aurgitanus votum solvit libens animo\n
EDCS-09000217.translation: To the Genius of the colony Patricia. Lucius Flaminius Severus of Aurgitanum willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09000217.summary: This inscription records Lucius Flaminius Severus of Aurgitanum fulfilling a vow to the Genius of the colony Patricia.

EDCS-09000218.text_edition: \n Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio Divi Iuli nepoti Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XXIX consuli IV imperatori VIII Lucius Sempronius Lacus magister Larum Augustorum dedit\n
EDCS-09000218.translation: Lucius Sempronius Lacus, master of the Lares Augustorum, dedicated this to Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 29th time, consul for the 4th time, imperator for the 8th time.
EDCS-09000218.summary: This inscription records Lucius Sempronius Lacus dedicating a monument to Emperor Tiberius, detailing Tiberius's titles and powers.

EDCS-09000219.text_edition: \n Lucius Aemilius Martialis nnorum XXV Lucius Aemilius December nnorum LXX Iulia mulieris lib(erta) Primula nnorum L \n
EDCS-09000219.translation: Lucius Aemilius Martialis, aged 25; Lucius Aemilius December, aged 70; Primula, freedwoman of Iulia, aged 50.
EDCS-09000219.summary: This inscription lists three individuals with their ages, likely recording names on a tomb or as part of a family group.

EDCS-09000220.text_edition: \n Marcus Attius Marci libertus Leander annorum LXXV pius in suis sit tibi terra levis gener posuit\n
EDCS-09000220.translation: Marcus Attius Leander, freedman of Marcus, aged 75, devoted to his family. His son-in-law placed this. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000220.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Marcus Attius Leander, his devotion to his family, and that it was erected by his son-in-law.

EDCS-09000221.text_edition: \n Lucius Aurelius Lucanus hic situs est in fronte pedes VIII carus suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000221.translation: Lucius Aurelius Lucanus is buried here. 8 feet in front. Dear to his family. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000221.summary: This is a funerary epitaph specifying the burial location and dimensions for Lucius Aurelius Lucanus.

EDCS-09000222.summary: This is a simple ownership or dedicatory inscription, likely for a tomb, identifying Gnaeus Pompeius Sudegronis.

EDCS-09000223.text_edition: \n Marcus Terentius Lukanus sic annorum LX pius in suis hic situs est \n
EDCS-09000223.translation: Marcus Terentius Lukanus, aged 60, devoted to his family, is buried here.
EDCS-09000223.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and piety of Marcus Terentius Lukanus, who is buried here.

EDCS-09000226.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Rutil Lucius Aelius AVG NSTRILIVS cum filiis suis dederunt dedicaverunt\n
EDCS-09000226.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Quintus Rutil Lucius Aelius Augustrilis with his sons gave and dedicated (this).
EDCS-09000226.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Quintus Rutil Lucius Aelius Augustrilis and his sons, likely on a public building or altar.

EDCS-09000228.text_edition: \n Tiberius Iulius Philoponus nnorum LXXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000228.translation: Tiberius Iulius Philoponus, 85 years old, pious towards his family, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-09000228.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Iulius Philoponus, who died at the age of 85, recording his piety and including a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-09000229.text_edition: \n Publius Manilius S Fabia Luci filia Mo\n
EDCS-09000229.translation: Publius Manilius, son of Lucius, of the Fabia tribe, [wife] Mo[...]
EDCS-09000229.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph or dedication, mentioning Publius Manilius and possibly his wife.

EDCS-09000233.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius filius Sergia aedilis IIvir lacus siliceos effigies aheneas de sua pecunia fecit\n
EDCS-09000233.translation: Lucius Cornelius, son of Sergia, as aedile and twice duovir, made the siliceous lake and bronze statues from his own money.
EDCS-09000233.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Cornelius, holding the offices of aedile and duovir, funded the creation of a siliceous lake and bronze statues.

EDCS-09000235.text_edition: \n Colonia Patricia Lucius Iunius Publi filius Sergia Paulinus pontifex flamen perpetuus IIvir colonorum coloniae Patriciae flamen provinciae Baeticae edito ob honorem flaminatus munere gladiatorio et duabus lusionibus statuas quas ob honores coniunctos promiserat ex HS CCCC (quadringentis milibus) posuit et factis circiensibus dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000235.translation: Lucius Iunius Publi filius Sergia Paulinus, pontiff, perpetual priest, duovir of the colonists of Colonia Patricia, priest of the province of Baetica, has set up the statues which he had promised for combined honors, at a cost of 400,000 sesterces, and dedicated them after holding gladiatorial games and two spectacles.
EDCS-09000235.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Lucius Iunius Sergia Paulinus, a prominent local official in Colonia Patricia, detailing his expenditure on public games and statues.

EDCS-09000237.text_edition: \n Fatis Augustis et Nutrici Augustae Tiberius Claudius Dion aram dono dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000237.translation: Tiberius Claudius Dion gave and dedicated this altar as a gift to the August Fates and the August Mother.
EDCS-09000237.summary: This is a dedication of an altar by Tiberius Claudius Dion to the August Fates and the August Mother.

EDCS-09000238.text_edition: \n Fonti sacro votum solvit libens merito Caius Aemilius Oculatus\n
EDCS-09000238.translation: Caius Aemilius Oculatus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the sacred Spring.
EDCS-09000238.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Caius Aemilius Oculatus fulfilling a vow to a sacred spring.

EDCS-09000241.text_edition: \n Genio Cornelius Luci filius Cai nepos ex argenti \n
EDCS-09000241.translation: Cornelius, son of Lucius, grandson of Caius, from silver...
EDCS-09000241.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or a statement of benefaction involving silver, from a member of the Cornelius family.

EDCS-09000242.text_edition: \n Genio coloniae Claritatis Iuliae et coloniae Patriciae Caius Valerius ex argenti mille libris fieri ponique in templo Tutelae iussit RES eius implorato ab indulgentia SA item PDV sacra refici ne Minervae Patrici E Geni corona aurea pontificali facienda S\n
EDCS-09000242.translation: Caius Valerius, from a thousand pounds of silver, ordered this to be made and placed in the temple of Tutela for the genius of the colony Claritas Iulia and of Colonia Patricia, and also ordered the sacred rites to be restored for Minerva Patrici E Geni, with a golden pontifical crown to be made.
EDCS-09000242.summary: This inscription records a significant benefaction by Caius Valerius, who funded temple repairs, religious rites, and a golden crown in honor of the genii of two colonies and the goddess Minerva.

EDCS-09000243.text_edition: \n --- c(olonorum) coloniae Patriciae EMENERICVS testamento poni iussit ex HS CC uxor XX non deduxit\n
EDCS-09000243.translation: [...] of the colonists of Colonia Patricia. Emericus ordered it to be placed by his will, from 200,000 sesterces; his wife did not deduct 20 [...]
EDCS-09000243.summary: This is a testamentary dedication by Emericus for the colonists of Colonia Patricia, specifying a sum of money and mentioning a partial omission by his wife.

EDCS-09000244.text_edition: \n Genio oppidi Sabetani Caius Fabius Nigellio dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000244.translation: Gaius Fabius Nigellio dedicated this to the genius of the town of Sabetus.
EDCS-09000244.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the genius of the town of Sabetus by Gaius Fabius Nigellio.

EDCS-09000245.text_edition: \n Genio pagi Augusti Caius Fabius Nigellio dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000245.translation: Gaius Fabius Nigellio dedicated this to the genius of the Augustan district.
EDCS-09000245.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the genius of the Augustan district by Gaius Fabius Nigellio.

EDCS-09000247.text_edition: \n Pro salute Imperatoris domini nostri [Marci Aureli] [Severi Alexandri] Pii Felicis Augusti tauribolium fecit Publicius Fortunatus thalamas suscepit chrionis Coelia Ianuaria adstante Ulpio Heliade sacerdote aram sacris suis dederunt dedicaverunt Maximo et Urbano consulibus\n
EDCS-09000247.translation: For the safety of our emperor, Lord Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, Publicius Fortunatus performed a bull sacrifice, and the attendants Coelia Ianuaria, with the priest Ulpius Heliades assisting, offered their sacred rites and dedicated this altar, in the consulship of Maximus and Urbanus.
EDCS-09000247.summary: This inscription records a dedication of an altar and a bull sacrifice performed for the safety of Emperor Alexander Severus, with specific individuals named, dating to the consulship of Maximus and Urbanus.

EDCS-09000248.text_edition: \n Ex iussu Matris Deum pro salute imperii tauribolium fecit Publicius Valerius Fortunatus thalamas suscepit crionis Porcia Bassemia sacerdote Aurelio Stephano dedicata VIII Kalendas Apriles Pio et Proculo consulibus\n
EDCS-09000248.translation: By order of the Mother of the Gods, Publicius Valerius Fortunatus performed a bull sacrifice for the safety of the empire; the attendants, Porcia Bassemia, with the priest Aurelius Stephano, offered their sacred rites, dedicated on the eighth day before the Kalends of April, in the consulship of Pius and Proculus.
EDCS-09000248.summary: This inscription records a bull sacrifice performed for the safety of the empire by Publicius Valerius Fortunatus, dedicated on March 25th in the consulship of Pius and Proculus.

EDCS-09000249.text_edition: \n III Clodia adstante Ulpio Heliade sacerdote aram sacris suis dederunt dedicaverunt Maximo et Urbano consulibus\n
EDCS-09000249.translation: III Clodia, with the priest Ulpius Heliades assisting, offered their sacred rites and dedicated this altar, in the consulship of Maximus and Urbanus.
EDCS-09000249.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar by III Clodia and the priest Ulpius Heliades, dating to the consulship of Maximus and Urbanus.

EDCS-09000251.text_edition: \n Nemesi Cornelii Restitutus et Africanus exacto flamonio votum reddiderunt\n
EDCS-09000251.translation: Cornelius Restitutus and Africanus, having completed their term as flamens, rendered their vow to Nemesis.
EDCS-09000251.summary: This inscription records Cornelius Restitutus and Africanus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Nemesis after completing their priestly duties.

EDCS-09000253.text_edition: \n Quintus M Theseus deo Silvano animo libens posuit solvit\n
EDCS-09000253.translation: Quintus M. Theseus willingly placed this statue and paid his vow to the god Silvanus.
EDCS-09000253.summary: This inscription records Quintus M. Theseus dedicating a statue to the god Silvanus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09000257.text_edition: \n Decreto decurionum colonorum coloniae Patriciae reposita anno Marci Lucreti Mariani et Quinti Vibi Laeti IIvirum colonorum coloniae Patriciae\n
EDCS-09000257.translation: By decree of the decurions and colonists of the colony Patricia, restored in the year of the IIviri of the colonists of colony Patricia, Marcus Lucretius Marianus and Quintus Vibius Laetus.
EDCS-09000257.summary: This inscription records the restoration of something in the colony Patricia, dated by the year of the IIviri Marcus Lucretius Marianus and Quintus Vibius Laetus.

EDCS-09000258.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription beginning with the name Lucius Junius Bil[---].

EDCS-09000259.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription indicating a dedication made by Sabina.

EDCS-09000260.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely marking a boundary, mentioning a person named Thaddaeus, possibly from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09000264.text_edition: \n Dionisia sic Denatiai sic ancilla rogat deibus sic ego rogo bono bono deibus sic rogo oro bono einfereis sic bono Salpina rogo oro et bonis inferis ut dioso sic quod fit deibus sic inferabus sic ut hoc quod sit causa et ecquod votum feci ut solvat rogo ut illam ducas sic rogo oro\n
EDCS-09000264.translation: Dionisia, servant of Denatia, asks on behalf of the gods. I ask, for the good of the underworld gods, I ask and implore, and for the good of those in the underworld, so that she may be well, as is right for the gods and those in the underworld, that He may resolve this matter, and whatever vow I made, I ask that You lead her, I ask and implore.
EDCS-09000264.summary: This is a personal plea inscribed by Dionisia, a servant, addressing the gods of the underworld to resolve a vow and ensure well-being, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09000265.text_edition: \n Caius Numisius Sexto Caius Numisius Philemon Numisia Heraclia Calipso Numisiorum Caia Avilia Irena Caius Numisius Epaphroditus Caius Numisius Aeschinus Scintilla Numisiorum\n
EDCS-09000265.translation: Gaius Numisius Sextus, Gaius Numisius Philemon, Numisia Heraclia, Calipso, [members] of the Numisii family; Caia Avilia Irena, Gaius Numisius Epaphroditus, Gaius Numisius Aeschinus, Scintilla, [members] of the Numisii family.
EDCS-09000265.summary: This inscription lists members of the Numisii family, likely as part of a funerary monument or family record, possibly from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09000266.text_edition: \n Priamus libertus mutus sit omnibus modis n<e=I> quis possit de hereditate silient sic quet sic hannue sic verbum facere omnes omutesquant mentia ore sinomutesquant conariNOT CVO sibiGEN malevolus OM omutesquant DSVE anue hered mutuis IN SI RE \n
EDCS-09000266.translation: Let Priamus, the mute freedman, be in all ways silent, so that no one can make a word concerning the inheritance, [or?] concerning the silence, [or?] concerning the ancestor, everyone should be silent in mind and voice, let them not try to speak badly for themselves. God bless the heir, and the mute [inheritance].
EDCS-09000266.summary: This is a peculiar inscription attempting to enforce silence regarding an inheritance through a curse or divine invocation on a freedman named Priamus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09000268.text_edition: \n Titus noster Fausta Fausti Pollio filius Casius Clipius Munnitia\n
EDCS-09000268.translation: Our Titus, Fausta, Faustus's son Pollio Casius Clipius, Munnitia.
EDCS-09000268.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary dedication or memorial, listing individuals possibly related to a deceased person named Titus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09000269.summary: This is a simple dedication of a site or object to the deified Emperor Augustus, likely dating from the 1st century CE.

EDCS-09000271.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli patri patriae proconsuli provincia Baetica ex decreto concili devota numini maiestatique eius dedicavit flamonio Luci Valeri Fuscini Cordubensis\n
EDCS-09000271.translation: Dedicated to Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, Consul, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul of the province of Baetica, by decree of the council, devoted to his divine majesty, by the flaminius of Lucius Valerius Fuscinius of Corduba.
EDCS-09000271.summary: This is a public dedication of a monument to Emperor Philip the Arab (reigned 244-249 CE) in the province of Baetica, commissioned by a local official.

EDCS-09000272.text_edition: \n Marco Iulio Philippo nobilissimo Caesari filio Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Iuli Philippi Pii Felicis Invicti Augusti \n
EDCS-09000272.translation: To Marcus Julius Philippus, most noble Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus.
EDCS-09000272.summary: This is an inscription honoring Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus, the son of Emperor Philip the Arab, likely dating to the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09000274.text_edition: \n Dominae nostrae Corneliae Saloninae Augustae coniugi domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Publi Licini Gallieni Pii Felicis et invicti Augusti Dacici maximi Germanici maximi tribunicia potestate IIII consulis III imperatoris III patris patriae proconsulis provinciae Baeticae devota numini maiestatique eius COR \n
EDCS-09000274.translation: To our Lady Cornelia Salonina Augusta, wife of our Lord Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus, Pious, Fortunate, and Invincible Augustus, Dacicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the fourth time, Consul for the third time, Imperator for the third time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul of the province of Baetica, devoted to his divine majesty, [made by] COR [partially illegible name or abbreviation].
EDCS-09000274.summary: This is a dedication to Empress Cornelia Salonina, wife of Emperor Gallienus (reigned 253-268 CE), likely in the province of Baetica.

EDCS-09000275.text_edition: \n provincia Baetica devota numini maiestatique eorum dedicante Marco Aurelio Alexandro procuratore Auggustorum nnostrorum viro egregio agente vice praesidis flamonio Marci Valeri Saturnini Lacipponensis\n
EDCS-09000275.translation: In the province of Baetica, devoted to their divine majesty, dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Alexander, procurator of our Augusti, an outstanding man acting in place of the governor, by the flaminius of Marcus Valerius Saturninus of Lacippo.
EDCS-09000275.summary: This inscription records a dedication in the province of Baetica by a procurator acting as governor, likely during the reigns of emperors in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09000276.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Domitio Aureliano Pio Felici invicto Augusto res publica Astigitana devota numini maiestatique eius Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantio Pio Felici invicto Augusto tribunicia potestate imperatori \n
EDCS-09000276.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus; to the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, with tribunician power, imperator. The state of Astigi, devoted to the divine majesty of them.
EDCS-09000276.summary: This is a state dedication from Astigi to Emperors Aurelian and Constantius, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-09000278.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantio Pio Felici invicto Augusto Octavius Rufus praeses provinciae Baeticae devotus numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-09000278.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus. Octavius Rufus, governor of the province of Baetica, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-09000278.summary: This is a dedication from Octavius Rufus, governor of Baetica, to Emperor Constantius, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-09000280.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Imperatori Caesari Flavio <Val=INV>(erio) Constantino Maximo Pio Felici aeterno Augusto Quintus Aeclanius Hermias vir perfectissimus agens vices praefectorum praetorio et iudex sacrarum cognitionum numini maiestatique eius semper dicatissimus \n
EDCS-09000280.translation: To our lord the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Maximus, pious, fortunate, eternal Augustus.
