Quintus Aeclanius Hermias, a man of perfect status, acting in place of the praetorian prefect and judge of sacred cognition, always most devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-09000280.summary: This is a dedication from Quintus Aeclanius Hermias to Emperor Constantine the Great, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-09000281.text_edition: \n Domino nostro fortissimo adque indulgentissimo Constantino invicto adque aeterno Augusto Egnatius Faustinus vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Baeticae devotus numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-09000281.translation: To our lord, the most brave and most indulgent Constantine, unconquered and eternal Augustus. Egnatius Faustinus, a man of perfect status, governor of the province of Baetica, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-09000281.summary: This is a dedication from Egnatius Faustinus, governor of Baetica, to Emperor Constantine the Great, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-09000287.text_edition: \n Aeliae Vitali coniugi agentis vices praefectorum\n
EDCS-09000287.translation: To Aelia Vitalis, wife of an official acting in place of a prefect.
EDCS-09000287.summary: This is a dedication to Aelia Vitalis, wife of a high-ranking official, likely from the 3rd or 4th century CE.

EDCS-09000288.text_edition: \n In honorem memoriae Cai Annii Cai fili Lepidi Marcelli triumviri kapitalis ordo splendidissimae coloniae Cordubensium statuam equestrem poni decrevit Quintia Publi filia Galla mater honore accepto inpensam remisit\n
EDCS-09000288.translation: In honor and memory of Caius Annius Lepidus Marcellus, son of Caius, IIIvir capitalis, the council of the most splendid colony of Corduba decreed that an equestrian statue be erected. Quintia Galla, daughter of Publius, his mother, after accepting the honor, remitted the expense.
EDCS-09000288.summary: This records a decree by the city council of Corduba to erect a statue for Caius Annius Lepidus Marcellus, with his mother remitting the costs, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09000289.text_edition: \n Lucio Axio Luci filio Pollia Nasoni quaestori tribuno militum pro legato Xviro stlitibus iudicandis vicani vici Forensis\n
EDCS-09000289.translation: To Lucius Axios, son of Lucius, of the Pollia tribe, son of Nason, quaestor, military tribune, prolegatus, XVIvir for judging disputes, the villagers of the vici Forensis.
EDCS-09000289.summary: This records a dedication by the villagers of vici Forensis to Lucius Axios, listing his extensive career, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09000290.text_edition: \n Lucio Axio Luci filio Pollia Nasoni quaestori tribuno militum pro legato Xviro stlitibus iudicandis vicani vici Hispani\n
EDCS-09000290.translation: To Lucius Axios, son of Lucius, of the Pollia tribe, son of Nason, quaestor, military tribune, prolegatus, XVIvir for judging disputes, the villagers of the vici Hispani.
EDCS-09000290.summary: This records a dedication by the villagers of vici Hispani to Lucius Axios, listing his extensive career, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09000291.text_edition: \n Marco Bassaeo Rufo viri consularis progenero ex testamento Marciae Sexti filiae Lupatae heredis eius\n
EDCS-09000291.translation: To Marcus Bassaeus Rufus, son-in-law of a consular, from the will of Marcia Lupata, daughter of Sextus, his heir.
EDCS-09000291.summary: This records a dedication to Marcus Bassaeus Rufus, based on the will of Marcia Lupata, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09000292.text_edition: \n Caio Dillio Luci filio Auli nepoti Sergia Aponiano tribuno militum legionis IIII Macedonicae IIIviro capitali quaestori provinciae Siciliae tribuno plebis praetori legato Imperatoris Caesaris Vespasiani Augusti legionis III Gallicae legato pro praetore \n
EDCS-09000292.translation: To Caius Dillius, son of Lucius, grandson of Aulus, of the Sergia tribe, Aponianus, military tribune of the IV Macedonica legion, IIIvir capitalis, quaestor of the province of Sicily, tribune of the plebs, praetor, legate of Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus of the III Gallica legion, propraetor.
EDCS-09000292.summary: This records the honors and career of Caius Dillius Aponianus, likely from the Flavian period (late 1st century CE).

EDCS-09000295.text_edition: \n Marco Cassio Marci filio Agrippae procuratori Augusti Marcus Cassius Pollio fratri piissimo\n
EDCS-09000295.translation: To Marcus Cassius Agrippa, son of Marcus Cassius, procurator of the emperor. Marcus Cassius Pollio, to his most devoted brother.
EDCS-09000295.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by a brother to Marcus Cassius Agrippa, procurator Augusti, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09000297.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gai Docquiri Flacci Aquiflaviensis egregii viri patrono provinciae Hispaniae citerioris Annaeus Vernaculus cliens et Iulius Phosphorus libertus\n
EDCS-09000297.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Gaius Docquirius Flaccus of Aquiflavia, a distinguished man, patron of the province of Hispania Citerior. Annaeus Vernaculus, his client, and Iulius Phosphorus, his freedman, dedicated this.
EDCS-09000297.summary: A funerary inscription honoring Gaius Docquirius Flaccus, patron of Hispania Citerior, set up by his client and freedman, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09000299.text_edition: \n Iulio Marci filio Quinti nepoti Galeria Gallo Mummiano tribuno militum cohortis maritimae IIviro colonorum coloniae Patriciae flamini Divorum Augustorum provinciae Baeticae huic ordo colonorum coloniae Patriciae statuam equestrem decrevit Aelia Flaviana uxor honore usa impensam remisit\n
EDCS-09000299.translation: To Iulius Gallus Mummianus, son of Marcus, grandson of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, military tribune of the maritime cohort, duovir of the colonists of Colonia Patricia, and flamen of the deified emperors of the province of Baetica. The council of colonists of Colonia Patricia decreed an equestrian statue for him. Aelia Flaviana, his wife, having used the honor, remitted the expense.
EDCS-09000299.summary: An inscription recording the decree of an equestrian statue for Iulius Gallus Mummianus, a prominent local official in Colonia Patricia, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09000300.text_edition: \n Iunio Basso Miloniano IIviro praefecto fabrum coloni et incolae\n
EDCS-09000300.translation: To Iunius Bassus Milonianus, duovir, prefect of engineers, by the colonists and inhabitants.
EDCS-09000300.summary: An inscription honoring Iunius Bassus Milonianus, a duovir and prefect of engineers, erected by the colonists and inhabitants, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09000301.text_edition: \n Lucio Manlio Auli filio Auli nepoti Galeria Boccho tribuno militum legionis XV IIviro praefecto iure dicundo decreto decurionum coloniae Patriciae\n
EDCS-09000301.translation: To Lucius Manlius Bocchus, son of Aulus, grandson of Aulus, of the Galeria tribe, military tribune of the 15th legion, duovir, prefect, and administrator of justice, by decree of the decurions of Colonia Patricia.
EDCS-09000301.summary: An inscription honoring Lucius Manlius Bocchus, a military tribune and high-ranking official in Colonia Patricia, erected by decree of the decurions, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09000303.text_edition: \n Caio Rocio Rociano Valeriano tribuno militum legionis equitatae O edidit munera \n
EDCS-09000303.translation: To Gaius Rocius Valerianus, son of Rocius, military tribune of an equestrian legion. O... offered the games.
EDCS-09000303.summary: An inscription honoring Gaius Rocius Valerianus, a military tribune, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09000304.text_edition: \n Titus Acclenus Titi filius Quirina centurio Annia Titi liberta Helena uxor frugi summa qum probitate fide magna concordes indigne morbo excruciati morte obierunt Decimus Vergilius Amarantus annorum LXXX\n
EDCS-09000304.translation: Titus Acclenus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, a centurion. Annia Helena, his freedwoman wife, daughter of Titus, well-behaved, with the highest integrity and great loyalty, lived in harmony and died prematurely from illness. Decimus Vergilius Amarantus, aged 80.
EDCS-09000304.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Acclenus, a centurion, and his wife Annia Helena, who died prematurely, with a final epitaph for Decimus Vergilius Amarantus, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09000305.text_edition: \n Philocli patri matri uxori primus pilus\n
EDCS-09000305.translation: To my father Philo, to my mother, to my wife. First spear.
EDCS-09000305.summary: A funerary epitaph for a father, mother, and wife, by someone identified as 'primus pilus' (first spear/centurion), likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09000306.text_edition: \n Marcus Septicius Cai filius Papiria aquilifer sibi et Sabinae contubernali suae et Marco Septicio Marci liberto Martiali filio naturali annorum X et mensum sic VII hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis post eorum obitum hoc monimentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-09000306.translation: Marcus Septicius, son of Caius, of the Papiria tribe, an aquilifer, for himself, and for Sabina, his partner, and for Marcus Septicius Martialius, his natural son, freedman of Marcus, aged 10 years and 7 months. Here they are buried. May the earth lie lightly upon you. After their death, this monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-09000306.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Septicius, an aquilifer, his partner Sabina, and his son Martialius, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09000307.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum hianus vilicus arkarii XX hereditatium ann--- XXXVIII pius in suis hic situs est lia Docime marito posuit\n
EDCS-09000307.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulianus, vilicus of the arkarii XX, [died after] 38 years... pious to his own, is buried here. Lia Docime placed this for her husband.
EDCS-09000307.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulianus, an administrator of inheritances, dedicated by his wife Lia Docime, likely dating to the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09000308.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Titi filio Claudia Antonino huic ordo colonorum coloniae Patriciae funeris inpensam locum sepulturae statuam decrevit Speudon Augusti libertus tabularius provinciae Baeticae et Antonia Rhodoe parentes honore usi piissimo posuerunt\n
EDCS-09000308.translation: To Titus Flavius Antoninus, son of Titus Flavius Claudia, the council of the colony Patricia decreed funeral expenses, a burial place, and a statue. His parents, Speudon, freedman of Augustus and registrar of the province Baetica, and Antonia Rhodoe, honored him with this pious monument.
EDCS-09000308.summary: A funerary monument set up by parents for their son, Titus Flavius Antoninus, in the colony Patricia, with funeral honors decreed by the local council.

EDCS-09000310.text_edition: \n Caio Antonio filio Galeria Serano Iporcensi flamini divorum Augustorum provinciae Baeticae huic consummato honore flamoni Publio Cluvio Max<im>o Marco Servilio Silano consulibus consensu concili provinciae Baeticae decreti sunt honores quantos quisque maximos consecutus est cum statua cuius honore accepto inpensam remisit\n
EDCS-09000310.translation: To Gaius Antonius Seranus, son of Gaius Antonius, of Iporca, flamen of the deified Augusti of the province Baetica, honors as great as any man could achieve were decreed to him by the consensus of the provincial council, with a statue, upon receiving which he remitted the expenses.
EDCS-09000310.summary: A decree of honors and a statue were awarded to Gaius Antonius Seranus, a flamen of the deified Augusti of Baetica, by the provincial council after his death.

EDCS-09000311.text_edition: \n Clodio Gai filio Sergia Saturnino Patriciensi IIviro flamini Divorum Auggustorum provinciae Baeticae Clodius Setuleius ius filius patri pientissimo decurionum decreto\n
EDCS-09000311.translation: To Saturninus, son of Gaius Clodius, of Patricia, twice decurion, flamen of the deified Augusti of the province Baetica. Clodius Setuleius, his son, [dedicated this] to his most dutiful father by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09000311.summary: A dedication by a son to his father, Clodius Saturninus, who held the offices of duovir and flamen of the deified Augusti in Patricia, based on a decree of the decurions.

EDCS-09000312.text_edition: \n Lucio Cominio Luci filio Galeria Iuliano Ilurconensi flamini divorum Auggustorum provinciae Baeticae huic consummato honore flamoni Aproniano et Maurico consulibus consensu concili provinciae Baeticae decreti sunt honores quantos quisque maximos consecutus est cum statua cuius honore accepto inpensam remisit\n
EDCS-09000312.translation: To Lucius Cominius Iulianus, son of Lucius Cominius, of Ilurco, flamen of the deified Augusti of the province Baetica, honors as great as any man could achieve were decreed to him by the consensus of the provincial council, with a statue, upon receiving which he remitted the expenses.
EDCS-09000312.summary: A decree of honors and a statue were awarded to Lucius Cominius Iulianus, a flamen of the deified Augusti of Baetica, by the provincial council after his death.

EDCS-09000313.translation: Gaius Cosanus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-09000313.summary: A fragmentary inscription recording the name and tribal affiliation of Gaius Cosanus.

EDCS-09000314.text_edition: \n Fabio Marci filio Galeria do flamini Divorum Augustorum provinciae Baeticae huic consummato honore flamoni Cattio Sabino II Cornelio Anullino consulibus consensu concili universae provinciae Baeticae decreti sunt honores quantos quisque maximos plurimosque flamen est consecutus cum statua Marcus Fabius Basileus Celtitanus pater honore accepto impensam remisit\n
EDCS-09000314.translation: To Marcus Fabius Basileus Celtitanus, son of Fabius Marcius, of [the Galeria tribe], flamen of the deified Augusti of the province Baetica, honors as many and as great as any flamen has ever achieved were decreed to him by the consensus of the council of the entire province Baetica, with a statue, upon receiving which he remitted the expenses. His father, Marcus Fabius Basileus Celtitanus, accepted the honor and remitted the expenses.
EDCS-09000314.summary: A decree of exceptional honors and a statue for Marcus Fabius Basileus Celtitanus, flamen of the deified Augusti of Baetica, with his father also remitting expenses after accepting the honor.

EDCS-09000315.text_edition: \n Marco Helvio Marci filio Sergia Rufo Patriciensi flamini divorum Augustorum provinciae Baeticae colonia Patricia decreto splendidissimi ordinis Rufus accepto honori sic impensam remisit\n
EDCS-09000315.translation: To Marcus Helvius Rufus, son of Marcus Helvius, of Patricia, flamen of the deified Augusti of the province Baetica. Rufus, having accepted the honor, remitted the expenses, by decree of the splendid order of the colony Patricia.
EDCS-09000315.summary: Marcus Helvius Rufus, flamen of the deified Augusti of Baetica, remitted expenses after accepting honors, as decreed by the council of the colony Patricia.

EDCS-09000316.text_edition: \n Lucio Octavio Liciniano Galeria Seriensium flaminali provinciae Baeticae Octavius Licinianus patri pientissimo decurionum decreto
EDCS-09000316.translation: To Lucius Octavius Licinianus, of Seriensium, flamen of the province Baetica. Octavius Licinianus, [dedicated this] to his most dutiful father by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09000316.summary: A dedication by a son, Octavius Licinianus, to his father, Lucius Octavius Licinianus, a flamen in the province Baetica, following a decurial decree.

EDCS-09000319.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Publicius provinciae Baeticae libertus \n
EDCS-09000319.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Publicius, of the province Baetica, freedman.
EDCS-09000319.summary: A funerary dedication to Gaius Publicius, a freedman from the province Baetica.

EDCS-09000320.text_edition: \n Publius Publicius provinciae Baeticae libertus Fortunatus marmorarius signuarius verna urbicus nnorum LXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000320.translation: Publius Publicius Fortunatus, freedman of the province Baetica, a marble worker and sculptor, aged 75, pious to his own, rests here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-09000320.summary: A tombstone for Publius Publicius Fortunatus, a 75-year-old freedman, marble worker, and sculptor from the province Baetica.

EDCS-09000321.text_edition: \n Lucio Aelio Luci filio Galeria Faustino IIviro colonorum coloniae Patriciae decreto decurionum Aelia Faustina filia honore accepto inpensam remisit\n
EDCS-09000321.translation: To Lucius Aelius Faustinus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, twice-magistrate of the colonists of Colonia Patricia, upon receiving an honor, his daughter Aelia Faustina waived the expense.
EDCS-09000321.summary: This inscription records the honoring of Lucius Aelius Faustinus, a local magistrate, by the colonists of Colonia Patricia, with his daughter waiving the associated costs.

EDCS-09000323.text_edition: \n - Clodio Luci filio Serg(ia) Cinnae praefecto IIvirali potestate decreto decurionum coloniae Patriciae impensa remissa est\n
EDCS-09000323.translation: For Clodius, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, praefect with twice-magistrate power, the expense was waived by decree of the town councillors of Colonia Patricia.
EDCS-09000323.summary: This inscription records the waiving of expenses for Clodius, a former local magistrate, by the town council of Colonia Patricia.

EDCS-09000325.text_edition: \n Caius Furnius Cai filius Papiria Fortunatus decurio coloniae Augustae Firmae nnum XVIIII dierum C pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis huic ordo colonorum coloniae Augustae Firmae locum sepulturae funeris inpensam statuam decrevit\n
EDCS-09000325.translation: Gaius Furnius Fortunatus, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, a town councillor of Colonia Augusta Firma, aged nineteen years, is buried here; may the earth be light upon you; the council of colonists of Colonia Augusta Firma decreed a burial place, funeral expenses, and a statue for him.
EDCS-09000325.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gaius Furnius Fortunatus, a young town councillor of Colonia Augusta Firma, and the provisions made by the local council for his burial and monument.

EDCS-09000326.text_edition: \n ius Marci filius Sergia Heres nnum XII hic situs est huic ordo decrevit colonorum coloniae Genetivae Iuliae impensam funeris locum sepulturae lapides at sic extruendum monumentum \n
EDCS-09000326.translation: Ius, son of Marcus, of the Sergia tribe, aged twelve years, is buried here; the council of colonists of Colonia Genetiva Julia decreed funeral expenses, a burial place, stones, and the construction of a monument for him.
EDCS-09000326.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a twelve-year-old boy named Ius and the decree by the council of colonists of Colonia Genetiva Julia for his funeral expenses, burial plot, and monument construction.

EDCS-09000327.text_edition: \n Tito Iulio Titi filio aedili colonorum coloniae Claritatis Iuliae Liciniae Luci libertae Antiochini Liciniae Luci libertae Aesionae Iuliae Titi libertae Lybissae\n
EDCS-09000327.translation: For Titus Julius, son of Titus, aedile of the colonists of Colonia Claritas Julia; [dedicated by?] Licinia, freedwoman of Lucius Antiochus, and Licinia, freedwoman of Lucius Aesiona, and Julia, freedwoman of Titus Lybissae.
EDCS-09000327.summary: This inscription records the dedication by three freedwomen, Licinia, Licinia, and Julia, to Titus Julius, an aedile of the colonists of Colonia Claritas Julia.

EDCS-09000329.summary: This is a simple dedication or memorial inscription to Macrina, daughter of Iunia Augusta.

EDCS-09000330.text_edition: \n Tito Mercelloni Persino Mario aedili IIviro coloni et incolae\n
EDCS-09000330.translation: To Titus Marcellonius Persinus Marius, aedile and twice-magistrate, by the colonists and residents.
EDCS-09000330.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Titus Marcellonius Persinus Marius, who held the offices of aedile and twice-magistrate, made by the local colonists and residents.

EDCS-09000333.translation: [Dedicated to?] the twice-magistrates of the colonists of Colonia Patricia.
EDCS-09000333.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to refer to the office of twice-magistrate within the community of colonists of Colonia Patricia.

EDCS-09000335.text_edition: \n Aulo Publicio Germano sacerdoti familiae publicae colonorum coloniae Patriciae perpetuo magistro II Trophimus colonorum coloniae Patriciae servus empticius Germanianus de suo dedit\n
EDCS-09000335.translation: To Aulus Publicius Germanus, priest of the public family of the colonists of Colonia Patricia, perpetual master; Germanianus, a purchased slave of the colonists of Colonia Patricia, gifted from his own resources.
EDCS-09000335.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a purchased slave, Germanianus, to Aulus Publicius Germanus, priest and master of the colonists of Colonia Patricia.

EDCS-09000343.text_edition: \n Felix sagarius magister Larum Augustorum colonorum coloniae Patriciae sibi et aphrae patrono magistro Larum Augustorum colonorum coloniae Patriciae et \n
EDCS-09000343.translation: Felix the weaver, master of the August Lords of the colonists of Colonia Patricia, for himself and for Aphra, patron and master of the August Lords of the colonists of Colonia Patricia, and...
EDCS-09000343.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication made by Felix, a weaver and master of the August Lords of Colonia Patricia, and mentions his patron, Aphra, who also held a similar position.

EDCS-09000345.text_edition: \n Quintus Marius Quinti libertus Eumolpus Palatina Augustalis nnorum XXVIII pius in suuis sic hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Claudia Saturnina Tiberi liberta annorum LX pia in suuis sic hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000345.translation: Quintus Marius, freedman of Quintus, Augustalis of the Palatine tribe, aged 28, dutiful to his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Claudia Saturnina, freedwoman of Tiberius, aged 60, dutiful to her family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000345.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Quintus Marius and Claudia Saturnina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000346.text_edition: \n Lucius Numisius |(mulieris) libertus Cytorus VIvir nnum LXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000346.translation: Lucius Numisius Cytorus, freedman of (a woman), VIvir, aged 65, dutiful to his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000346.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucius Numisius Cytorus, a VIvir, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000347.text_edition: \n lius Paganus magister Larum Augustorum hic situs est sit tibi terra levis lia Berulla uxor et liberta hic sita est\n
EDCS-09000347.translation: Paganus, magistrate of the Lares Augusti, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. His wife and freedwoman, Ilia Berulla, lies here.
EDCS-09000347.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Paganus, a magistrate of the Lares Augusti, and his wife Ilia Berulla, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000349.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Vibius Polyanthus IIIIIIvir colonorum coloniae Patriciae annorum LXXXX et Fabia Helpis uxor nnorum LXX hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-09000349.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Vibius Polyanthus, IIIIvir of the colonists of Colonia Patricia, aged 90, and his wife Fabia Helpis, aged 70, lie here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000349.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucius Vibius Polyanthus, an official in Colonia Patricia, and his wife Fabia Helpis, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000351.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sevir.
EDCS-09000351.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning a sevir, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000352.text_edition: \n Limenio et Catullino nnulibus V Idus Apriles Iulio Caninio patrono merentissimo principatus ob splendorem domus tuae patrone honorificentissime offerimus tibi cuncti tesseram patronatus fabri subidiani quem libenti animo iubemus suscipi offerentes rectores Clodius Augendus Curiatius Innocentius Iunius Germanus\n
EDCS-09000352.translation: To Limenius and Catullinus. On the Ides of April. To our most deserving patron Iulio Caninius, for the prestige of your house, most honorable patron, we, all the guild leaders (offer this) the symbol of subidianus the craftsman, whom we joyfully order to be accepted, with the offering rectores: Clodius Augendus, Curiatius Innocentius, Iunius Germanus.
EDCS-09000352.summary: This is an inscription from the guild of craftsmen dated to April 9th, honoring their patron Iulio Caninius and acknowledging the acceptance of a new craftsman named subidianus.

EDCS-09000353.text_edition: \n Decimus Aemilius Decimi libertus Nicephorus brattiarius Aemilia Decimi liberta Quarta\n
EDCS-09000353.translation: Decimus Aemilius Nicephorus, freedman of Decimus, a brattiarius (a type of craftsman or performer), and Aemilia Quarta, freedwoman of Decimus.
EDCS-09000353.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the names of Decimus Aemilius Nicephorus and Aemilia Quarta, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000354.text_edition: \n Marcus Aerarius sociorum aerariorum libertus Telemachus medicus hic quiescit vale\n
EDCS-09000354.translation: Marcus Aerarius Telemachus, freedman of the partners of the aerarium (treasury), a doctor, rests here. Farewell.
EDCS-09000354.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Marcus Aerarius Telemachus, a doctor and freedman associated with the treasury, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000355.text_edition: \n Diocles purpurarius pius mater pia ema ex testamento fratris faciendum curavit otimus purpurarius hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-09000355.translation: Diocles, a purple-dyer, dutiful. His mother, pia Ema, had this monument made in accordance with the will of her brother. The purple-dyer Optimus will not be succeeded by an heir.
EDCS-09000355.summary: This is a funerary inscription detailing the burial arrangements for the purple-dyer Diocles, funded by his mother, and notes that the purple-dyer Optimus will not have heirs, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000356.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Domitius Isquilinus sic magister grammaticus Graecus nnum CI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000356.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Domitius Isquilinus, a Greek teacher of grammar, aged 101, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000356.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Domitius Isquilinus, a Greek teacher of grammar, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000357.text_edition: \n Aulus Etrilius Auli libertus institor armorum hic est Cosana |(mulieris) liberta Su Vabia Cai liberta T\n
EDCS-09000357.translation: Aulus Etrilius, freedman of Aulus, a merchant of weapons, is here. Cosana |(his wife), freedwoman of Su, Vabia, freedwoman of Cai, [is here].
EDCS-09000357.summary: This funerary inscription records the presence of Aulus Etrilius, a weapons merchant, and possibly his wife or another female relative, in Cosana.

EDCS-09000358.text_edition: \n Lucius Iulius Protogenes medicus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000358.translation: Lucius Iulius Protogenes, physician, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000358.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Iulius Protogenes, a physician, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000359.text_edition: \n Marcus Latinius Marci libertus? Lucius Afinius Luci libertus Ata Latinia Marci liberta T Demetrius filius Latinia Marci liberta Da sarcinatrix \n
EDCS-09000359.translation: Marcus Latinius, freedman of Marcus?, Lucius Afinius, freedman of Lucius, Ata Latinia, freedwoman of Marcus, T. Demetrius, son of Latinia, freedwoman of Marcus, Da, a seamstress.
EDCS-09000359.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, likely family members and possibly freedmen or freedwomen, possibly marking a family tomb.

EDCS-09000360.text_edition: \n Lucio Lollidio Aucto praeceptori Lollidia Luci liberta Procula hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-09000360.translation: For Lucius Lollidius Auctus, teacher. Lollidia Procula, freedwoman of Lucius, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you all.
EDCS-09000360.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Lucius Lollidius Auctus, a teacher, and Lollidia Procula, his freedwoman, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000361.text_edition: \n Octavia o mater Caius Octavius AV Felix aerarius Octavia Titi Cai liberta Modesta uxor Caius Octavius Modestae libertus Macer Octavia Modestae liberta Chloris mater Caius Octavius Titi Cai libertus Primus AA Caius Octavius Cai libertus Cuccio Caius Octavius Cai libertus Prot\n
EDCS-09000361.translation: Octavia, mother. Caius Octavius, Felix, treasurer. Octavia Modesta, freedwoman of Titus Cai, wife. Caius Octavius Macer, freedman of Modesta. Octavia Chloris, freedwoman of Modesta, mother. Caius Octavius Primus, freedman of Titus Cai, AA. Caius Octavius Cuccio, freedman of Cai. Caius Octavius Prot [....
EDCS-09000361.summary: This lengthy inscription lists members of the Octavius family, including a treasurer, wives, and freedmen, likely commemorating a family burial plot.

EDCS-09000362.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus sacrum Lucius Persius Diallus coactor argentarius hic situs est sit tibi terra levis C \n
EDCS-09000362.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Gods. Lucius Persius Diallus, a tax collector for silver, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000362.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the gods of the underworld for Lucius Persius Diallus, a silver tax collector, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000364.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Pomponia Septumilla alumna portonariorum nnorum XVI dierum XXXXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000364.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Gods. Pomponia Septumilla, foster-child of the gatekeepers, sixteen years old, forty-five days old, dutiful, is buried here in her own property. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000364.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Pomponia Septumilla, an underage foster-child of gatekeepers, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000365.text_edition: \n Titus Servius Titi libertus Clarus dissignator hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000365.translation: Titus Servius Clarus, freedman of Titus, a decorator, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000365.summary: This tombstone honors Titus Servius Clarus, a decorator and freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000366.text_edition: \n Stelenus ostiarius Petroni uxsor sic de suo dedit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000366.translation: Stelenus, doorkeeper, wife of Petronius. She dedicated this from her own resources. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000366.summary: This inscription records that Stelenus, a doorkeeper and wife of Petronius, erected this monument from her own funds, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-09000367.text_edition: \n Caius Valerius Anemption Tuccitanus caelator anaglyptarius incrementum maximum nnum XL mensium V dierum VI pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Caius Valerius Zephyrus successorem suum libertum et alumnum indulgentissimum hic consecravit\n
EDCS-09000367.translation: Caius Valerius Anemption Tuccitanus, an engraver of reliefs, forty years, five months, and six days old, dutiful, is buried here in his own property. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Caius Valerius Zephyrus consecrated this man here, his most indulgent freedman and foster-child.
EDCS-09000367.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caius Valerius Anemption Tuccitanus, a relief engraver, and notes his consecration by his freedman and foster-child, Caius Valerius Zephyrus.

EDCS-09000369.text_edition: \n Lucio Valerio Poeno Lucio Antistio Rustico IIviris ante diem III Kalendas Septembres Lucius Valerius Cai filius Kapito alvari locum occupavit\n
EDCS-09000369.translation: On the third day before the Kalends of September, during the duumvirate of Lucius Valerius Poenus and Lucius Antistius Rusticus, Lucius Valerius Capito, son of Caius, occupied the place of the alvari.
EDCS-09000369.summary: This inscription records the occupation of the alvari place by Lucius Valerius Capito during the duumvirate of Lucius Valerius Poenus and Lucius Antistius Rusticus.

EDCS-09000373.text_edition: \n Actius murmillo vicit VI annorum XXI hic situs est sit terra levis uxor viro de suo quot sic quisquis vestrum mortuo optarit mihi it sic illi di faciant semper vivo et mortuo\n
EDCS-09000373.translation: Actius the murmillo, aged 6, won. He lies here. May the earth rest lightly on you. His wife, for her husband, says: whoever wishes this for the dead man, may the gods grant him the same, both alive and dead.
EDCS-09000373.summary: This is a tombstone for Actius, a gladiator (murmillo), who died at the age of 6, with his wife leaving a curse against anyone who would wish him ill.

EDCS-09000374.text_edition: \n Alipus sic annorum XXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis posuit Amabilis de suo Amabilis annorum XXX natione Gallus hic situs est sit <t=L>(erra) levis \n
EDCS-09000374.translation: Alipus, aged 30, lies here. May the earth rest lightly on you. Amabilis, aged 30, a Gaul by nationality, placed this from his own resources. He lies here. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-09000374.summary: This tombstone records Alipus, a Gaul aged 30, with a second epitaph for Amabilis, also aged 30, placed by Amabilis himself.

EDCS-09000375.text_edition: \n Traex Amandus Neroniani XVI verna Placentinus annorum XXII murmillo Pudens liberatus \n
EDCS-09000375.translation: Traex Amandus Neroniani, aged 22, a native of Placentia. Murmillo Pudens, freedman.
EDCS-09000375.summary: This inscription lists two gladiators: Amandus, a traex, and Pudens, a murmillo, noting their age and origin.

EDCS-09000376.text_edition: \n Murmillo Ampliatus natione Syrus pugnavit XXXIII nnorum XXX traex Studiosus frater de suo posuit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000376.translation: Ampliatus the murmillo, a Syrian by nationality, fought 33 times, aged 30. Studiosus, a traex and his brother, placed this from his own resources. He lies here. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-09000376.summary: This tombstone records Ampliatus, a Syrian murmillo, and states that his brother Studiosus, a traex, erected the monument.

EDCS-09000379.text_edition: \n Murmillo Cerinthus Neroniani II natione Graecus annorum XXV Rome coniunx bene merenti de suo posuit te rogo praeteriens dicas sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000379.translation: Cerinthus the murmillo of the Neroniani, a Greek by nationality, aged 25. His wife Rome placed this from her own resources for her well-deserving husband. I ask you passing by, say: May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-09000379.summary: This tombstone is for Cerinthus, a Greek murmillo aged 25, erected by his wife Rome.

EDCS-09000380.text_edition: \n Cursor doctor retiariorum hic situs est\n
EDCS-09000380.translation: Cursor, instructor of the retiarii. He lies here.
EDCS-09000380.summary: This is a tombstone for Cursor, identified as an instructor of the retiarii gladiators.

EDCS-09000381.text_edition: \n Murmillo |(contra) rete Faustus Neroniani XII verna Alexandrinus annorum XXXV hic situs est Apollonia uxor et Hermes traex de suo posuerunt\n
EDCS-09000381.translation: Faustus the murmillo, fighting against the retiarius, of the Neroniani, aged 35, an Alexandrian by birth. Apollonia, his wife, and Hermes, a traex, placed this from their own resources. He lies here.
EDCS-09000381.summary: This tombstone records Faustus, a murmillo aged 35, with his wife Apollonia and a traex named Hermes erecting the monument.

EDCS-09000382.text_edition: \n Essedarius Ingenuus Galliciani annorum XXV palmarum XII natione Germanus familia universa de suo faciendum curavit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000382.translation: Ingenuus the essedarius, aged 25, with 12 palm honours, a German by nationality. The entire household took care to have this made from their own resources. He lies here. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-09000382.summary: This tombstone is for Ingenuus, a German essedarius gladiator aged 25, with 12 palm honours, erected by his entire household.

EDCS-09000383.text_edition: \n Murmillo |(contra) rete Probus PAVIL LXXXXIX natione Germanus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Volumnia Sperata coniu<g=C>i pio merenti Publius Volumnius Vitalis patri pio sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-09000383.translation: Probus the murmillo, fighting against the retiarius, of the PAVIL, aged 26, a German by nationality. He lies here. May the earth rest lightly on you. Volumnia Sperata for her pious, deserving husband. Publius Volumnius Vitalis for his pious father. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-09000383.summary: This tombstone honours Probus, a German murmillo aged 26, with epitaphs from his wife Volumnia Sperata and his son Publius Volumnius Vitalis.

EDCS-09000384.text_edition: \n R natione traex Sagitta M natione Hispanus hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis conservi de suo bene merentibus\n
EDCS-09000384.translation: Sagitta from Thrace and Hispanus from Hispania are buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you, fellow slaves, who are well deserving at their own expense.
EDCS-09000384.summary: This epitaph, likely from the imperial period, records the burial of two slaves, Sagitta and Hispanus, with a dedication from fellow slaves.

EDCS-09000385.text_edition: \n Satur murmillo Iuliani XIII Bassus liberatus murmillo I |(coronae) I hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis Cornelia Severa uxor de suo dedit\n
EDCS-09000385.translation: Satur, a Thracian murmillo (gladiator), and Bassus, a freedman murmillo, are buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Cornelia Severa, his wife, dedicated this at her own expense.
EDCS-09000385.summary: This epitaph, probably from the Roman imperial period, records the burial of two gladiators, Satur and Bassus, at the expense of Bassus's wife, Cornelia Severa.

EDCS-09000386.summary: This inscription, likely from the Roman imperial period, records the name and age of Triumphalis, possibly a soldier or another individual with a military-sounding designation.

EDCS-09000390.text_edition: \n Calpurniae Quinti filiae Anui Severi decreto decurionum coloniae Patriciae inpensa remissa\n
EDCS-09000390.translation: To Calpurnia, daughter of Quintus, wife of Anuius Severus, with tax payment remitted by decree of the town councilors of the colony Patricia.
EDCS-09000390.summary: This inscription from the Roman imperial period honors Calpurnia, daughter of Quintus and wife of Anuius Severus, noting a remission of taxes granted by the local council of Colonia Patricia.

EDCS-09000391.text_edition: \n Fabiae Cnaei filiae Priscae Asidonensi Fabius Seneca et Valeria Quinti filia Prisca\n
EDCS-09000391.translation: To Prisca, daughter of Cnaeus Fabius, from Asido. Fabius Seneca and Valeria Prisca, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-09000391.summary: This funerary inscription from the Roman imperial period identifies Prisca, daughter of Cnaeus Fabius, from Asido, and mentions Fabius Seneca and Valeria Prisca, daughter of Quintus, as possibly related to her burial.

EDCS-09000394.text_edition: \n Sulpiciae Luci filiae Rufinae Lucius Fulcinius Lalus H S NITIAS D D\n
EDCS-09000394.translation: To Sulpicia, daughter of Lucius, Rufina. Lucius Fulcinius Lalus set this up.
EDCS-09000394.summary: This funerary inscription from the Roman imperial period records the dedication to Sulpicia Rufina, daughter of Lucius, by Lucius Fulcinius Lalus.

EDCS-09000397.text_edition: \n Lucio Acilio Luci filio Modesto Felix dispensator de sua pecunia\n
EDCS-09000397.translation: To Lucius Acilius Modestus, son of Lucius. Felix, the administrator, from his own funds.
EDCS-09000397.summary: This inscription from the Roman imperial period records a dedication to Lucius Acilius Modestus, son of Lucius, made by Felix, an administrator, at his own expense.

EDCS-09000398.translation: To Macilioni Agilio, a slave, gives a gift.
EDCS-09000398.summary: This inscription from the Roman imperial period records a slave named Agilio making a dedication to Macilioni.

EDCS-09000399.text_edition: \n Calpurniae Tertullae Secundina et Rufina Rufini filiae dant\n
EDCS-09000399.translation: To Calpurnia Tertulla. Secundina and Rufina, daughters of Rufinus, give (this).
EDCS-09000399.summary: This funerary inscription from the Roman imperial period records a dedication to Calpurnia Tertulla by Secundina and Rufina, daughters of Rufinus.

EDCS-09000400.text_edition: \n Quinto Herennio Rufo Montanus Rufi filius contribulis dat\n
EDCS-09000400.translation: To Quintus Herennius Rufus. Montanus, son of Rufus, a fellow citizen, gives (this).
EDCS-09000400.summary: This inscription from the Roman imperial period records a dedication to Quintus Herennius Rufus by Montanus, son of Rufus, and a fellow citizen.

EDCS-09000401.text_edition: \n Lolliae Cai filiae Manlianae nostrae Aulus Fannius Speudo et Voltilia Nice donum dant\n
EDCS-09000401.translation: Aulus Fannius Speudo and Voltilia Nice give this gift to our Lollia Manliana, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-09000401.summary: This inscription is a dedication of a gift by Aulus Fannius Speudo and Voltilia Nice to Lollia Manliana.

EDCS-09000402.text_edition: \n Nicei nostrae Agilio servus donum dat\n
EDCS-09000402.translation: Agilio's slave gives this gift to our Nicei.
EDCS-09000402.summary: This inscription records a gift given by the slave of Agilio to Nicei.

EDCS-09000403.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Optata.

EDCS-09000404.text_edition: \n Pollioni Pollionis filio Apollonius li libertus \n
EDCS-09000404.translation: Apollonius, freedman of Pollio, [son of] Pollio.
EDCS-09000404.summary: This inscription likely identifies Apollonius, freedman of Pollio, son of Pollio, possibly as part of a longer text.

EDCS-09000405.text_edition: \n Lucio Stertinio Luci filio Horatia Maxumo Publius Cornelius Philoclis libertus Auctus\n
EDCS-09000405.translation: Publius Cornelius Philoclis, freedman Auctus, [to] Lucius Stertinius, son of Lucius, of the Horatia tribe, Maxumus.
EDCS-09000405.summary: This is a dedication by the freedman Auctus to Lucius Stertinius Maxumus.

EDCS-09000406.text_edition: \n Caius Colinecus Acconis filius Uxamensis annorum XXX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000406.translation: Gaius Colinecus, son of Acconis, from Uxama, aged 30, pious in his own things, is laid to rest here; may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-09000406.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Colinecus, recording his age and origin.

EDCS-09000407.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Cornelius Fannianus Obulconensis annorum LXXII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000407.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Cornelius Fannianus, from Obulco, aged 72, is laid to rest here; may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-09000407.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Cornelius Fannianus, recording his age and origin.

EDCS-09000408.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication or funerary notice to Marcus Fabius of Eporeda.

EDCS-09000409.text_edition: \n Marcus Fabius Themison Asidonensis nnum LXX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Fabia Modesta Themisonis filia nnum XX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Iunia Titi liberta Clarina nnorum LX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000409.translation: Marcus Fabius Themison, from Asido, aged 70, pious in his own things, is laid to rest here; may the earth be light for you. Fabia Modesta, daughter of Themison, aged 20, pious in her own things, is laid to rest here; may the earth be light for you. Iunia Clarina, freedwoman of Titus, aged 60, pious in her own things, is laid to rest here; may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-09000409.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burials and ages of Marcus Fabius Themison, Fabia Modesta, and Iunia Clarina.

EDCS-09000410.text_edition: \n noverat unum at et pia nutrix coepit et unus a fiant mater habebit Sum genere Macedon set sic in arvis Baeticae partus quintus post decimum revolutus fugerat annus et iam iamque viro toga se sociare parabat deficiunt fata totus labor excidit hora hic ego sum positus festus de nomine Festi\n
EDCS-09000410.translation: He had known only one, but also a pious nurse began and one from his mother will have. I am of Macedonian stock, but thus in the fields of Baetica, the fifth child born after the tenth year had revolved, and now, now the toga was preparing to join him as a husband, fate fails, all labor has fallen away. Here I am placed, Festus by name, from Festus.
EDCS-09000410.summary: This is a complex epitaph in verse for Festus, a man of Macedonian origin who died in Baetica at a young age before marriage, lamenting his unfulfilled life.

EDCS-09000411.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Lucretius Verna Patriciensis nnorum LV pius in suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000411.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Lucretius Verna, of Patrician origin, aged 55, pious towards his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000411.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Lucretius Verna, aged 55, who is buried here and remembered as pious towards his family.

EDCS-09000412.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Marcius Niger Baxonensis nnorum LX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000412.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Marcius Niger, of Baxon origin, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000412.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Marcius Niger, aged 60, from Baxon, who is buried here.

EDCS-09000413.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Persia Luci filia Secunda Patriciensis nnorum XXII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000413.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Persia Secunda, daughter of Lucius, of Patrician origin, aged 22, pious towards her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000413.summary: A funerary epitaph for Persia Secunda, aged 22, daughter of Lucius and of Patrician origin, who is buried here and remembered as pious towards her family.

EDCS-09000414.text_edition: \n Lucius Salvenus Sedatus verna Romanus annorum LXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000414.translation: Lucius Salvenus Sedatus, a Roman freedman, aged 75, pious towards his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000414.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Salvenus Sedatus, a 75-year-old Roman freedman, who is buried here and remembered as pious towards his family.

EDCS-09000415.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Valerius Avitus natione Ta gemma quod est A quod tenebr sic inter \n
EDCS-09000415.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Valerius Avitus, by nation a Tagemma, which means 'he who holds darkness', [died]...
EDCS-09000415.summary: A fragment of a funerary epitaph for Caius Valerius Avitus, whose ethnic origin 'Tagemma' is explained with a descriptive phrase.

EDCS-09000416.text_edition: \n Valeria Quinta a capite canteri hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Dis Manibus sacrum Kalva annorum V mensium VII Aphrodisia annorum II V pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000416.translation: Valeria Quinta is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Sacred to the departed spirits. Kalva, aged 5 years and 7 months, and Aphrodisia, aged 2 years and 5 months, pious towards their family, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000416.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of two young children, Kalva and Aphrodisia, who are described as pious towards their family.

EDCS-09000417.summary: An incomplete inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, for Numerius Abullius Chriestus.

EDCS-09000418.text_edition: \n Caio Sentio Saturnino consule Kalendis Sextilibus Dei Manes receperunt Abulliam Numeri libertam Nigellam\n
EDCS-09000418.translation: Under the consulship of Gaius Sentius Saturninus, on the Kalends of August, the divine shades received Abullia Nigella, freedwoman of Numerius.
EDCS-09000418.summary: A funerary dedication dated to 19 BC, recording the death of Abullia Nigella, freedwoman of Numerius, under Consul Gaius Sentius Saturninus.

EDCS-09000419.text_edition: \n Marcus Aelius Modestus hic situs est \n
EDCS-09000419.translation: Marcus Aelius Modestus is buried here.
EDCS-09000419.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Marcus Aelius Modestus.

EDCS-09000421.text_edition: \n Lucius Aemilius Luci libertus Xanthus nnorum LVIIII pius in suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000421.translation: Lucius Aemilius Xanthus, freedman of Lucius, aged 59, pious towards his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000421.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Aemilius Xanthus, a 59-year-old freedman, who is buried here and remembered as pious towards his family.

EDCS-09000422.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aemilia Ermogene nnis mensibus III diebus XVII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000422.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Hermogene, having lived for 3 months and 17 days, lies here, pious in her own family; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000422.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Aemilia Hermogene, who died at a very young age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000423.text_edition: \n Decimus Aerariuscus hic situs est --- Luclena Tertulla hic sita est Luclena Aucta hic sita est tuere monumentum inferi gaudebis qui sancte et caste vixerit honeste ho\n
EDCS-09000423.translation: Decimus Aerariuscus lies here. Luclena Tertulla lies here. Luclena Aucta lies here. Protect this monument; you will rejoice in the underworld if you have lived holy and chaste and honestly.
EDCS-09000423.summary: A tombstone for multiple individuals, Decimus Aerariuscus, Luclena Tertulla, and Luclena Aucta, with an admonition to passersby, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000425.text_edition: \n Aglaus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000425.translation: Aglaus lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000425.summary: A tombstone for Aglaus, with a common funerary wish, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000426.text_edition: \n Quintus Annedius Quinti libertus Surillio hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Luclena ||(mulierum) liberta Grata pia frugi hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000426.translation: Quintus Annedius Surillio, freedman of Quintus, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Luclena Grata, freedwoman of Luclena, pious and thrifty, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000426.summary: A tombstone recording the burials of Quintus Annedius Surillio and Luclena Grata, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000427.text_edition: \n Lucius Annaeus Luci hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Annaea Luci liberta Prima hic sita est tibi terra levis sit Psechas nnorum XX hic sita est tibi terra levis sit\n
EDCS-09000427.translation: Lucius Annaeus, son of Lucius, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Annaea Prima, freedwoman of Lucius, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Psechas lies here, aged 20; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000427.summary: A tombstone recording the burials of Lucius Annaeus, Annaea Prima, and Psechas, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000428.text_edition: \n Antestia Luci filia Iuniana nnorum XIII Iunia |(mulieris) liberta Diutera sic hic sitae sunt sit vobis terra levis Dis Manibus sacrum Asicia Facunda nnorum XXXVII pia in contubernalem suum Manius Asicius Mani libertus Pelops nnorum L pii in suis hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-09000428.translation: Antestia Iuniana, daughter of Lucius, aged 13, and Iunia Diutera, freedwoman of Iunia, are buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you both. Sacred to the departed spirits. Asicia Facunda, aged 37, pious in her partner, and Manius Asicius Pelops, freedman of Manius, aged 50, pious in their own family, are buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you both.
EDCS-09000428.summary: A tombstone for two sets of partners, Antestia Iuniana and Iunia Diutera, and Asicia Facunda and Manius Asicius Pelops, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000429.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Anthis.
EDCS-09000429.summary: A tombstone fragment dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the name Anthis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000432.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Antonius Alchimus vicsit nnis LXX pius in suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000432.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antonius Alchimus lived for 70 years, pious towards his own family, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000432.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Antonius Alchimus, who lived to 70 years old, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000433.text_edition: \n Lucius Antonius Constans carus suis annorum XXVII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Lucius Antonius Saturninus fratri bene merenti\n
EDCS-09000433.translation: Lucius Antonius Constans, dear to his family, aged 27, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius Antonius Saturninus, to his well-deserving brother.
EDCS-09000433.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Antonius Constans, with a dedication from his brother Lucius Antonius Saturninus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000435.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum ilia Anulina vixit annis XXIII Felix mensium I \n
EDCS-09000435.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ilia Anulina lived for 23 years. Felix, 1 month.
EDCS-09000435.summary: A tombstone fragment recording the deaths of Ilia Anulina, aged 23, and Felix, aged 1 month, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000437.text_edition: \n Publius Argentarius |(mulieris) libertus Faustus sibi et uxori Decentia Cai liberta Rustica hic sita est\n
EDCS-09000437.translation: Faustus, freedman of Publius Argentarius and freedwoman of his wife, is buried here with his wife Rustica, freedwoman of Caius.
EDCS-09000437.summary: A funerary epitaph for Faustus and his wife Rustica, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000438.text_edition: \n Marcus Argentarius societatis Sisaponensis libertus Philinus Aulus Argentarius societatis Sisaponensis libertus Rufus Marcus Argentarius societatis Sisaponensis libertus Succio suo testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-09000438.translation: Philinus, freedman of the company of Sisapo, Rufus, freedman of the company of Sisapo, and Succio, freedman of the company of Sisapo, Marcus Argentarius, ordered this to be made in his will.
EDCS-09000438.summary: A funerary inscription indicating that Marcus Argentarius commissioned the tomb for his freedmen Philinus, Rufus, and Succio, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000441.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum aeternae securitati Auge annorum XXXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Caius Tanonius As Lucius Marius Phoe Maria Persin Maria Luci filia Secund Caecilia Qinti filia Secund Lucius Marius Luci filius Phoe\n
EDCS-09000441.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Auge, aged 35, dutiful towards her own, is buried here for eternal peace. May the earth lie lightly on you. Caius Tanonius As, Lucius Marius Phoe, Maria Persin, Maria daughter of Luci, Secund [---], Caecilia daughter of Qintus Secund [---], Lucius Marius son of Luci Phoe [---].
EDCS-09000441.summary: A funerary epitaph for Auge, with a list of associated individuals, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000442.text_edition: \n Avitae Eucumene Avitae liberta Philargyris Philargyri liberta Iris hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000442.translation: Iris, freedwoman of Philargyris, who was freedwoman of Avita Eucumene, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000442.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iris, a freedwoman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000444.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelius Celerinianus vixit annis XVIIII mensibus VIIII diebus XVII fecit mater pia filio sit terra levis\n
EDCS-09000444.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Celerinianus lived 17 years, 9 months, and 17 days. His dutiful mother made this for her son. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000444.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Celerinianus, commissioned by his mother, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000445.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Aurelius Marcellinus nnum VIII pius in suis \n
EDCS-09000445.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aurelius Marcellinus, aged 8 years, dutiful towards his own [is buried here].
EDCS-09000445.summary: A fragment of a funerary epitaph for a young boy named Marcus Aurelius Marcellinus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000446.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Marcianae annorum XXVIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000446.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Marciana, aged 28, dutiful towards her own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000446.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Marciana, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000447.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelia Valeriana nnorum LXXII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000447.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Valeriana, aged 72, dutiful towards her own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000447.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Valeriana, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000449.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Badia nnorum LVIII pia in suis hic sita sit tibi terra levis tu qui stas et leges sic titulum meum lude iocari sic veni\n
EDCS-09000449.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Badia, aged 58, dutiful towards her own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. You who stand and read, thus mock my epitaph; thus have you come.
EDCS-09000449.summary: A funerary epitaph for Badia, including a unique rhyming curse or admonition to passers-by, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000450.summary: A very brief inscription likely recording the names of two individuals associated with a tomb or property, possibly from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000451.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Cacius Cnaei Cai libertus Firmo Cnaeus Cacius Cnaei libertus Ursus lubens merito bene merenti bene referri gratiam et tibi et tuis est sed tamen et tibi hoc veniundum sic est Cnaeus Cacius Cnaei libertus Quietus \n
EDCS-09000451.translation: Gnaeus Cacius Firmo, freedman of Gnaeus Cacius, son of Gnaeus, gladly gives his thanks to you and yours, as is deserved for your good deeds; indeed, it is fitting for you also, Gnaeus Cacius Quietus, freedman of Gnaeus Cacius, son of Gnaeus.
EDCS-09000451.summary: A dedication by a freedman named Firmo to another freedman, Gnaeus Cacius Quietus, expressing gratitude, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-09000452.text_edition: \n Caecilio Chrysantho annorum LXX Vibia Prisciana marito\n
EDCS-09000452.translation: Vibia Prisciana to her husband Caecilius Chrysanthus, aged 70 years.
EDCS-09000452.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilius Chrysanthus, dedicated by his wife Vibia Prisciana, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000453.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caecilia Tusculana cara suis vixit nnis XIX mensibus VII et diebus XX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000453.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Tusculana, dear to her family, lived 19 years, 7 months, and 20 days; she rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000453.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Tusculana, recording her age and expressing a wish for peaceful rest, typical of Roman burial inscriptions.

EDCS-09000454.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caesullus servus nnum XVIII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000454.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caesullus, a devoted slave, aged 18 years, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000454.summary: A funerary epitaph for a devoted slave named Caesullus, recording his age and expressing a wish for peaceful rest, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000455.text_edition: \n Calpurnius Urbanus Calpurni Salviani libertus manumissus ex testamento beneficio accepto nihil praeter praemium libertatis famulae suae hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-09000455.translation: Calpurnius Urbanus, freedman of Calpurnius Salvianus, manumitted by will, having received a benefit, nothing beyond the reward of freedom for his own freedwoman, rest here.
EDCS-09000455.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Calpurnius Urbanus and his freedwoman, who were granted freedom and burial by will, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000457.text_edition: \n Marcus Caninius Chilo Marcus Caninius Marci libertus Alexander Caninia Marci liberta Secunda\n
EDCS-09000457.translation: Marcus Caninius Chilo; Marcus Caninius Alexander, freedman of Marcus; Caninia Secunda, freedwoman of Marcus.
EDCS-09000457.summary: A dedication or tomb inscription listing three individuals, two of whom are freedmen of Marcus Caninius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000458.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Catia Sphendusa nnorum \n
EDCS-09000458.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Catia Sphendusa, aged [---] years.
EDCS-09000458.summary: A funerary epitaph for Catia Sphendusa, beginning a dedication to the departed, with the age illegible, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000459.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Circia Cieta sic nnorum XXXXIII kara suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000459.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Circia Cieta, dear to her family, lived 43 years; she rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000459.summary: A funerary epitaph for Circia Cieta, recording her age and status as dear to her family, with a wish for peaceful rest, typical of Roman burial inscriptions.

EDCS-09000461.text_edition: \n Cleobis Paciaecae nnum VI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000461.translation: Cleobis, aged 6 years, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000461.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Cleobis, recording his age and expressing a wish for peaceful rest, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000462.text_edition: \n Memoriae aeternae Clodiae Euporiae nnorum XXXX dies sic XXVIII castae et abstinentis bonae indolis matronae nec alieni cupida par opinionis suae sancta in coniuge sic pia quoque natae famulisque benigna obsequentissima dignis hanc omnis aetas amavit quae mutata patria casu rapta manet relictaque nata reddidit in peregre plura meruisti marito digna testimonio eius ut CV in saeculo mater Cast maritae incomparabili matri castissimae fecit
EDCS-09000462.translation: To eternal memory. For Clodia Euporia, aged 40 years and 28 days, chaste and abstinent, of good disposition, a matron, not covetous of others' property, true to her own reputation, holy to her husband, pious to her children, and kind to her slaves; she was loved by all ages; she remains in a foreign land, taken away by a change of fate, leaving behind her children. You deserved more in life. Her husband, whose testimony is worthy, made this for his incomparable wife, a most chaste mother.
EDCS-09000462.summary: A detailed funerary epitaph for Clodia Euporia, praising her virtues and lamenting her death, dedicated by her husband, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000463.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed.
EDCS-09000463.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, likely from a tomb, with no specific individuals named.

EDCS-09000464.text_edition: \n Corinthius Sexti Marii servus nnum XX quem sui maiores superaverunt pius in suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000464.translation: Corinthius, slave of Sextus Marius, lived 20 years, whom his descendants surpassed in devotion; pious to his own people, he is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000464.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Corinthius, a slave of Sextus Marius, who died at 20 years old and was known for his piety to his family.

EDCS-09000465.summary: This is a memorial inscription for Cornelia, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-09000467.text_edition: \n Lucio Cornelio Luci liberto Euhemero Cornelia Alethea uxor faciendum curavit Caecilia Quinti liberta Firma mater hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-09000467.translation: Cornelia Alethea, wife, had this made for Lucius Cornelius Euhemerus, freedman of Lucius; Firma, freedwoman of Quintus Caecilius, mother, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you both.
EDCS-09000467.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Cornelia Alethea for her husband Lucius Cornelius Euhemerus and her mother Firma, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-09000468.text_edition: \n Sacrum Dis Manes sic Cornelia Cai liberta Quarta annorum XIII hic sita est Iunia Delicata filia de suo fecit\n
EDCS-09000468.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Here lies Cornelia Quarta, freedwoman of Caius, aged 13. Iunia Delicata, her daughter, made this at her own expense.
EDCS-09000468.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Cornelia Quarta, a 13-year-old freedwoman, and was commissioned by her daughter, Iunia Delicata.

EDCS-09000469.text_edition: \n Cornelia Cai liberta Theocrite annorum XXVII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000469.translation: Cornelia Theocrite, freedwoman of Caius, aged 27, pious to her own people, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000469.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Cornelia Theocrite, a 27-year-old freedwoman, noted for her devotion to her family.

EDCS-09000471.text_edition: \n Cutia Luci liberta Lezbia Licinia Quinti liberta Rufa Annia Quinti liberta Caesia hic sitae sunt sit tibi sic terra levis\n
EDCS-09000471.translation: Cutia Lezbia, freedwoman of Lucius; Licinia Rufa, freedwoman of Quintus; Annia Caesia, freedwoman of Quintus, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you all.
EDCS-09000471.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of three women: Cutia Lezbia, Licinia Rufa, and Annia Caesia, all freedwomen, suggesting a shared burial space.

EDCS-09000472.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Dasumius Solon vixit annis XXXX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000472.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Dasumius Solon lived 40 years; pious to his own people, he is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000472.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintus Dasumius Solon, who lived 40 years and was known for his piety to his family.

EDCS-09000473.text_edition: \n Cai libertus Diaconus V tana pius in suis Cai liberta Compse pia in suis hic sita est Cai liberta Nice nnorum Cai libertus Luchoreus! a hic situs est\n
EDCS-09000473.translation: Caius Diaconus, freedman of Caius, lived [many] years; pious to his own people. Compse, freedwoman of Caius, pious to her own people, is buried here. Nice, freedwoman of Caius, aged [many] years. Luchoreus, freedman of Caius, is buried here.
EDCS-09000473.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates the burials of several freedpersons of Caius: Diaconus, Compse, Nice, and Luchoreus, highlighting their piety and lives.

EDCS-09000474.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Didia Severina nnorum XXVIII pia in <suis> hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000474.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Didia Severina, aged 28, pious to her own people, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000474.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Didia Severina, aged 28, who was known for her devotion to her family.

EDCS-09000475.text_edition: \n Egnatia Florentina hic sita est sit tibi terra levis quod parenti facere debuit filia id immature filiae fecit pater\n
EDCS-09000475.translation: Egnatia Florentina is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. What the daughter ought to have done for her parent, the father did for his immature daughter.
EDCS-09000475.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Egnatia Florentina, with a poignant note from her father about her early death.

EDCS-09000476.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription recording the name and age of Publius Fabius.

EDCS-09000477.text_edition: \n Sextus Fabius Phaeder hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Lucius Quintius Luci libertus Amphio hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Quintia Luci liberta Caletyche nnum LXII hic sita est tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000477.translation: Sextus Fabius Phaeder is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Lucius Quintius, freedman of Lucius Amphio, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Quintia, freedwoman of Lucius, is buried here, aged 62. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000477.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of three individuals: Sextus Fabius Phaeder, Lucius Quintius Amphio, and Quintia, with ages provided for Quintia.

EDCS-09000479.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fannia Placida annorum XXXXI pia suis hic sit terra levis\n
EDCS-09000479.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fannia Placida, aged 41, pious towards her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000479.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Fannia Placida, aged 41, and emphasizes her piety towards her family.

EDCS-09000480.text_edition: \n Fannia Marci liberta Rhodine hic sita est Marcus Fannius Marci libertus Rhodo Marcus Atilius Marci libertus Apelles\n
EDCS-09000480.translation: Rhodine, freedwoman of Marcus Fannius, is buried here. Marcus Fannius, freedman of Marcus, and Apelles, freedman of Marcus Atilius.
EDCS-09000480.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Rhodine, freedwoman of Marcus Fannius, and mentions two other freedmen, Marcus Fannius and Marcus Atilius Apelles.

EDCS-09000483.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely funerary, indicating that Arete, a freedwoman and wife, erected the monument.

EDCS-09000484.text_edition: \n MR nnorum XX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Flavia Parthenio annorum XXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000484.translation: MR, aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Flavia Parthenio, aged 25, pious towards her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000484.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two individuals, MR (aged 20) and Flavia Parthenio (aged 25), noting the latter's piety.

EDCS-09000487.text_edition: \n Marco Fulvio Sergia K locus pedum XII\n
EDCS-09000487.translation: To Marcus Fulvius, son of Sergius K. A plot of 12 feet.
EDCS-09000487.summary: This inscription records a plot of land of 12 feet dedicated to Marcus Fulvius, son of Sergius K.

EDCS-09000488.translation: To Rulliana, daughter of Publius Furius Philotmus.
EDCS-09000488.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Rulliana, daughter of Publius Furius Philotmus.

EDCS-09000490.text_edition: \n Gaditana cara suis annorum II hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000490.translation: Gaditana, dear to her family, aged 2, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000490.summary: This is a touching funerary epitaph for a young child named Gaditana, who died at the age of two.

EDCS-09000491.text_edition: \n Galliai Cnaei libertai Monumei ab oriente ad occidentem annorum XVIIII hic ossa bene quiescunt\n
EDCS-09000491.translation: Here lie the remains of Gallia, freedwoman of Gnaeus, from east to west; may she rest well.
EDCS-09000491.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of a freedwoman named Gallia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000492.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gallia Quirina cara suis nnorum XXVI sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000492.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gallia, Quirina tribe, dear to her family, aged 26. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000492.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gallia, a woman of the Quirina tribe, expressing the wish for a peaceful afterlife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000493.text_edition: \n Quinto Herennio Quinti liberto Phileroti et Herenniae Quinti libertae Lezbiae Quintus Herennius Quinti libertus Clarus Herennia Quinti liberta Cretica Herennia Quinti liberta Palaestra patrono de sua pecunia faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09000493.translation: Quintus Herennius Clarus, freedman of Quintus Herennius, and Herennia Cretica, freedwoman of Quintus Herennius, and Herennia Palaestra, freedwoman of Quintus Herennius, had this made at their own expense for their patron Quintus Herennius Philerotus and their patroness Herennia Lezbia.
EDCS-09000493.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by freedmen and freedwomen for their patrons, indicating a master-freedperson relationship within a family context, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000494.text_edition: \n Hirria Luci filia Pa hic sita est annorum XVII pia tibi terra levis sit Petilia Titi liberta Marta hic sita est tibi terra levis sit\n
EDCS-09000494.translation: Hirria, daughter of Lucius Pa, aged 17, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Petilia Marta, freedwoman of Titus, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000494.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of two women, Hirria and Petilia Marta, both aged 17 and wishing for a peaceful rest, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000495.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ingenua nnorum V kara suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000495.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ingenua, aged 5, dear to her family, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000495.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Ingenua, aged 5, expressing love from her family and a wish for a peaceful afterlife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000497.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai Luci Sexti libertus Athenidorus Iulia |(mulieris) Cai liberta Pusinna Iulia Cai Luci Sexti liberta Eunica\n
EDCS-09000497.translation: Gaius Julius Athenidorus, freedman of Gaius Julius, son of Sextus. Iulia Pusinna, freedwoman of Gaius, wife of Caius. Iulia Eunica, freedwoman of Gaius Julius, son of Sextus.
EDCS-09000497.summary: A funerary inscription listing three individuals: two freedmen and a freedwoman, identifying their patrons and relationships, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000498.text_edition: \n Aram portis geminis Lucius Iunius Platon et Iunia Lycias filia ob Servilios Patricium et Patriciensem et Nepontinam sic parentes votum libentes solverunt\n
EDCS-09000498.translation: Lucius Iunius Platon and his daughter Iunia Lycias, with joy fulfilled their vow to their parents Servilius Patricius and Patriciensis, and Nepotina.
EDCS-09000498.summary: A funerary altar dedicated by a son and daughter to their deceased parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000499.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iunius Ursus nnrum III pius in <suis> hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000499.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iunius Ursus, aged 3, dutiful to his family, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000499.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Iunius Ursus, aged 3, highlighting his piety towards his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000500.text_edition: \n Iunia Genice nnorum XXVII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000500.translation: Iunia Genice, aged 27, dutiful to her family, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000500.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iunia Genice, aged 27, emphasizing her devotion to her family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000501.text_edition: \n C(aius) Licinius Cai libertus Salvius Plattia |(mulieris) liberta Salvia\n
EDCS-09000501.translation: Gaius Licinius Salvius, freedman of Gaius Licinius. Salvia, freedwoman of Plattia.
EDCS-09000501.summary: A funerary inscription recording two freed individuals, Gaius Licinius Salvius and Salvia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000502.text_edition: \n Marcus Licinius Sodalis hic situs est\n
EDCS-09000502.translation: Marcus Licinius Sodalis is buried here.
EDCS-09000502.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Marcus Licinius Sodalis.

EDCS-09000505.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes: Lucius...
EDCS-09000505.summary: A funerary inscription, dedicated to the Divine Manes, beginning with the name Lucius.

EDCS-09000506.text_edition: \n Marcus Lucius Marci filius Sergia Cornelia Luci liberta Prima frugi pia Lucius Cornelius Luci filius Caranto Lucius Cornelius Primus pius frugi\n
EDCS-09000506.translation: Marcus Lucius, son of Marcus, of the Sergia tribe, Cornelia Prima, freedwoman of Lucius, virtuous and dutiful; Lucius Cornelius Caranto, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe; Lucius Cornelius Primus, pious and virtuous.
EDCS-09000506.summary: A complex funerary inscription honoring multiple individuals, likely family members, with emphasis on their virtues.

EDCS-09000507.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucrio nnorum XVIII amantissimus dominorum pius in omnibus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000507.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius, aged 18, most loving to his masters, dutiful in all things, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000507.summary: A funerary inscription for a young man named Lucius, highlighting his devotion to his masters and ending with a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-09000508.text_edition: \n Maia Publi filia Secunda mater Publius Marcius Publi filius Sergia pater Publius Marcius Publi filius Sergia Pollio filius Marcia Publi liberta Fausta hic sita est\n
EDCS-09000508.translation: Maia Secunda, daughter of Publius, mother; Publius Marcius, son of Publius, of the Sergia tribe, father; Publius Marcius Pollio, son of Publius, of the Sergia tribe; Marcia Fausta, freedwoman of Publius, is buried here.
EDCS-09000508.summary: A funerary inscription detailing a family, including parents, son, and a freedwoman, with their paternal lineage.

EDCS-09000509.text_edition: \n Locus pedum XII Mamilia Marci filia\n
EDCS-09000509.translation: A plot of 12 feet. Mamilia, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-09000509.summary: An inscription indicating a burial plot size and identifying Mamilia, daughter of Marcus, as potentially the owner or interred.
