EDCS-09000510.text_edition: \n Lucius Manilius Maniliae libertus Hilarus testamento fieri iusit sic sibi\n
EDCS-09000510.translation: Lucius Manilius Hilarus, freedman of Manilia, ordered this to be made in his will, as he wished for himself.
EDCS-09000510.summary: A funerary inscription stating that Lucius Manilius Hilarus, a freedman, commissioned his tomb through his will.

EDCS-09000511.text_edition: \n Lucio Manio Luci filio vixit annis XXIII SAHELICIA Delphis filio pientissimo posuit\n
EDCS-09000511.translation: To Lucius Manius, son of Lucius, who lived 23 years. Sahelia Delphis erected this for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-09000511.summary: A funerary monument set up by Sahelia Delphis for her son, Lucius Manius, who died at age 23.

EDCS-09000512.text_edition: \n Mansuetus Publi Minici Facundi servus annorum III hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000512.translation: Mansuetus, slave of Publius Minicius Facundus, aged 3 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000512.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young slave named Mansuetus, belonging to Publius Minicius Facundus.

EDCS-09000513.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcia Celsa Cai Marci Celsi filia [[]] [[]] [] vixit nnis XXX pi- in suos sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000513.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcia Celsa, daughter of Caius Marcius Celsus, lived 30 years... dutiful towards her family. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000513.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Marcia Celsa, daughter of Caius Marcius Celsus, who died at age 30, emphasizing her devotion to her family.

EDCS-09000514.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Maria.
EDCS-09000514.summary: A funerary inscription recording the dedication to the Manes for Maria.

EDCS-09000515.text_edition: \n Maria pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000515.translation: Maria the pious rests here among her own. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000515.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maria, emphasizing her piety and her resting place among her family.

EDCS-09000516.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Maria Cai liberta Hellas Patriciensis nnorum LXV pia in viro et in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000516.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Hellas, freedwoman of Maria, a Patrician woman, aged 65, pious to her husband and her own, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000516.summary: A funerary epitaph for Hellas, freedwoman of Maria, a Patrician woman, aged 65, who rests here among her family.

EDCS-09000517.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Maria Hellas nnorum LXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000517.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Hellas, aged 65, pious to her own, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000517.summary: A funerary epitaph for Hellas, aged 65, who rests here among her family.

EDCS-09000519.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Mascellio annorum II dulce solacium hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000519.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Mascellio, aged 2 years, a sweet comfort, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000519.summary: A funerary epitaph for Mascellio, a child aged 2, described as a sweet comfort.

EDCS-09000520.text_edition: \n Marci liberta Maxsuma pia in suis annorum \n
EDCS-09000520.translation: Maxsuma, freedwoman of Marcus, pious to her own, aged...
EDCS-09000520.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph for Maxsuma, freedwoman of Marcus, indicating her piety and age.

EDCS-09000521.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Melitine sic nnorum VIIII mensium VI dierum VIII hic sita est infans patri per saecula flenda quam raptam adsiduae sic mater maerore requirit grata blandi quales quisque sibi cupiat producere natos hanc annus X privavit munere lucis casum quisque legat fato maledicat iniquo sit tibi terra levis Carpophorus pater et Felicula mater filiae pientissimae\n
EDCS-09000521.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Melitine, aged 9 years, 6 months, and 8 days, rests here, an infant to be mourned forever by her father, whom her mother misses with constant sorrow; who would wish for children to raise as he desires? The year X deprived her of this gift of light. Let each person read of her fate and curse unjust destiny. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Carpophorus, father, and Felicula, mother, for their most devoted daughter.
EDCS-09000521.summary: A poignant funerary epitaph for Melitine, aged 9 years, 6 months, and 8 days, mourning her premature death and expressing grief from her parents, Carpophorus and Felicula.

EDCS-09000522.text_edition: \n Mussia Agele bis denis Agele florentibus annis et specie et vita femina prima fuit hunc sortita locum miserae sunt ossa puellae hic raptam matri consociavit humus sedibus aeternis Agele non laesa quiescas et bene conposita sit tibi terra levis Mussia |(mulieris) liberta Rosia hic sit tibi terra levis Lucius Postumius Barnaeus |(mulieris) libertus hic sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000522.translation: Agele Mussia, in her twentieth year, in bloom of age and beauty, was the foremost woman. She obtained this place. Miserable are the bones of the girl, the earth associated her here, taken from her mother. Agele, may you rest undisturbed in eternal abodes, and may the earth lie lightly upon you. Mussia |(woman's) freedwoman Rosia rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius Postumius Barnaeus |(woman's) freedman rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000522.summary: A funerary epitaph for Agele Mussia, a young woman aged 20, also including epitaphs for Rosia, freedwoman of Mussia, and Lucius Postumius Barnaeus, freedman of Mussia.

EDCS-09000525.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Myrt...
EDCS-09000525.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Myrt[...].

EDCS-09000526.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Myrtale nnorum XXXXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000526.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Myrtale, aged 45 years, pious to her own, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000526.summary: A funerary epitaph for Myrtale, aged 45 years, who rests here among her family.

EDCS-09000527.text_edition: \n Titus Nerius Titi libertus Antigonus Titus Nerius Titi libertus Hilarus filius Cornelia Luci liberta Danais mater Neria Titi liberta Firma filia Neria |(mulieris) et Titi liberta Daphne Titus Nerius Titi libertus Asiaticus\n
EDCS-09000527.translation: Titus Nerius Antigonus, freedman of Titus Nerius. Titus Nerius Hilarus, son of Titus Nerius, freedman. Danais, freedwoman of Lucius Cornelia, mother. Neria Firma, freedwoman of Neria (of a woman), daughter. Daphne, freedwoman of Titus Nerius, freedman.
EDCS-09000527.summary: This inscription lists members of the Nerius family, likely for a tomb or funerary monument, naming freedmen and family members.

EDCS-09000529.text_edition: \n Lucius Numisius Gaetulus nnorum L pius in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000529.translation: Lucius Numisius Gaetulus, aged L years, pious in his own [tomb]; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000529.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Numisius Gaetulus, recording his age and piety.

EDCS-09000530.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Octavius Pa vixit nnis \n
EDCS-09000530.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Octavius Pa lived [X] years.
EDCS-09000530.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits (Dis Manibus) for Octavius Pa, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-09000531.text_edition: \n Aulo Ofillio i patri Pandiaeae matri Quinto Ofillio fratri Pandiae sorori Pandiaeae sorori Ofillius Persen faciundum curavit \n
EDCS-09000531.translation: Ofillius Persen arranged for this to be made for his father Aulus Ofillius, his mother Pandiaea, his brother Quintus Ofillius, and his sister Pandiaea.
EDCS-09000531.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Ofillius Persen for his deceased family members: father, mother, brother, and sister.

EDCS-09000532.text_edition: \n Luci |(mulieris) liberta Optata dulcis coniunx quiescunt\n
EDCS-09000532.translation: Optata, freedwoman of Lucius (of a woman), his sweet wife, rest here.
EDCS-09000532.summary: A funerary epitaph for Optata, described as a freedwoman and a sweet wife.

EDCS-09000533.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum P T Trophima nnorum LXX pia in <s>(uis) hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000533.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. P T Trophima, aged LXX years, pious in her own [tomb], lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000533.summary: A funerary epitaph for P T Trophima, recording her age, piety, and burial place, with the standard wish for a light earth.

EDCS-09000534.text_edition: \n Securitati aeternae Paciae Saturninae piissimae indulgentissimae omnium suorum cum qua vixi nnis XXXII e<t=E> bilem cum ea numquam habui Iulius Baeticus maritus eius et Pacia Natalis liberta fecerunt excessit nnis XXXXVIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-09000534.translation: For eternal Security. For Saturnina Pacia, most pious and most indulgent of all her family, with whom I lived 32 years, I never had a quarrel with her. Iulius Baeticus, her husband, and Pacia Natalis, freedwoman, made this. She died aged 48 years, pious in her own [tomb], lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000534.summary: This epitaph commemorates Saturnina Pacia, a beloved wife who died at 48, and was erected by her husband Iulius Baeticus and freedwoman Pacia Natalis, praising her piety and their harmonious marriage.

EDCS-09000538.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Patricia nnorum XXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000538.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Patricia, aged 30 years, pious in her own [tomb], lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000538.summary: A funerary epitaph for Patricia, aged 30, noting her piety and burial location.

EDCS-09000539.text_edition: \n Lucius Petilius Luci libertus Priamus Petilia |(mulieris) liberta Communis nnrum XXXVI hic sita est Petilia Luci liberta Sextula\n
EDCS-09000539.translation: Lucius Petilius Priamus, freedman of Lucius Petilius. Petilia Communis, freedwoman of [the family/estate], aged 36 years, lies here. Petilia Sextula, freedwoman of Lucius Petilius.
EDCS-09000539.summary: This inscription lists members of the Petilius family and their freedmen, likely for a tomb.

EDCS-09000540.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Petronius Discoridianus nnum LV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000540.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Petronius Discoridianus, aged 55 years, pious in his own [tomb], lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000540.summary: A funerary epitaph for Petronius Discoridianus, recording his age, piety, and burial place.

EDCS-09000541.text_edition: \n Philetusa hic sita est Veneris annorum XX hic sita est Nicephora annorum XVIII hic sita est sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-09000541.translation: Philetusa, aged 20, is buried here. Nicephora, aged 18, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you both.
EDCS-09000541.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Philetusa and Nicephora, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000543.text_edition: \n Latum in fronte pedum XV longum in agro pedum XV Caius Pomponius Cai libertus Licinus hic situs est Philomusus libertus cippum dat\n
EDCS-09000543.translation: 15 feet wide at the front, 15 feet long in the field. Gaius Pomponius Licinus, freedman of Caius, called Philomusus, his freedman, gives this boundary stone.
EDCS-09000543.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land and the donation of a boundary stone by the freedman Licinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000544.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Pomponia Quinti filia Tusca Aufidi\n
EDCS-09000544.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pomponia Tusca, daughter of Quintus, wife of Aufidius.
EDCS-09000544.summary: This is a dedication to the Manes (departed spirits) for Pomponia Tusca, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000545.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Porcius Chryseros nnorum LXXXI pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000545.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Porcius Chryseros, aged 81, pious towards his own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000545.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Porcius Chryseros, aged 81, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000546.text_edition: \n Porcia Luci filia Faustina nnum LXX R \n
EDCS-09000546.translation: Porcia Faustina, daughter of Lucius, aged 70.
EDCS-09000546.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Porcia Faustina, aged 70, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000547.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Primula nnorum XXVI pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Aemilius Eupropes pater \n
EDCS-09000547.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Primula, aged 26, pious towards her own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Her father, Aemilius Eupropes.
EDCS-09000547.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Primula, aged 26, dedicated by her father, Aemilius Eupropes, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000548.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publicia Martia incomparabilis soror vixit annis LXXI pia in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000548.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publicia Martia, an incomparable sister, lived 71 years, pious towards her own. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000548.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Publicia Martia, aged 71, and is dedicated by a sibling, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000550.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintina anni I mensium V dierum X hic sita est \n
EDCS-09000550.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintina, aged 1 year, 5 months, and 10 days, is buried here.
EDCS-09000550.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of the infant Quintina, aged 1 year, 5 months, and 10 days, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000552.text_edition: \n Rubria Faustina vixit nnis XXVIII pia in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000552.translation: Rubria Faustina lived 28 years, pious towards her own. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000552.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Rubria Faustina, aged 28, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000553.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum S---ae Chrysothoriae annorum LXVII pia in suis hic sita est sit terra levis E C\n
EDCS-09000553.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. S---ae Chrysothoriae, aged 67, pious towards her own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. E.C.
EDCS-09000553.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of S---ae Chrysothoriae, aged 67, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000554.text_edition: \n Sabinus annorum XL ex familia A---ae Septuminae pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000554.translation: Sabinus, 40 years old, from the household of A---a Septumina, pious, is buried here on his own property; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000554.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Sabinus, who died at age 40.

EDCS-09000556.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Samia vixit nnis LI pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000556.translation: To the Divine Manes. Samia lived 51 years, pious, is buried here on her own property; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000556.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Samia, who died at age 51.

EDCS-09000557.text_edition: \n Saturninus nnorum XXXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Conservata nnorum XXXX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000557.translation: Saturninus, 45 years old, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Conservata, 40 years old, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000557.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the deaths of Saturninus (aged 45) and Conservata (aged 40).

EDCS-09000558.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Sempronio Donato cui et Lauricio qui vixit annis XXVII diebus pater filio pientissimo hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000558.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Sempronius Donatus, also called Lauricio, who lived 27 years and some days, his most dutiful father buried him here on his own property; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000558.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Sempronius Donatus, aged 27, dedicated by his father.

EDCS-09000559.text_edition: \n Marco Sempronio Marci liberto Principi Semp<r>oniae Luci libertae Secundae Luriae Cai libertae Faustillae annorum XXVI hic sita est sit tibi terra levis hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-09000559.translation: To Marcus Sempronius, freedman of Marcus, chief of Principi; to Sempronia Secunda, freedwoman of Lucius; to Luria Faustilla, freedwoman of Caius, 26 years old, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-09000559.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Luria Faustilla, aged 26, and specifying that the monument is non-hereditary.

EDCS-09000560.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Sempronia Thaumaste annorum LV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000560.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Sempronia Thaumaste, 55 years old, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000560.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Sempronia Thaumaste, who died at age 55.

EDCS-09000561.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Sempronia Vitalis vixit nnis XXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000561.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Sempronia Vitalis lived 35 years, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000561.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Sempronia Vitalis, who died at age 35.

EDCS-09000562.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Senius Faustinus annorum VIII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000562.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Senius Faustinus, 8 years old, pious, is buried here on his own property; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000562.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Gaius Senius Faustinus, who died at age 8.

EDCS-09000563.text_edition: \n Lucius Sentinans sic Luci libertus Septumius nnorum XXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000563.translation: Lucius Sentinans, freedman of Lucius Septumius, 35 years old, pious, is buried here on his own property; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000563.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Lucius Sentinans, freedman of Lucius Septumius, who died at age 35.

EDCS-09000564.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Sentia Mapalia pia in suis annorum XXX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000564.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Sentia Mapalia, pious, on her own property, 30 years old, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000564.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Sentia Mapalia, who died at age 30.

EDCS-09000565.text_edition: \n Quintus Servilius Quinti libertus Chryseros nnorum LVI pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000565.translation: Quintus Servilius Chryseros, freedman of Quintus, 56 years old, pious to his own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000565.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Servilius Chryseros, recording his name, manumission, age, and a common wish for the deceased, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000566.text_edition: \n Nominibus cunctis digno laudata pudore Servilia inmiti funere rapta iacet coniunx dulcis obit mater pia nata probanda cara soror veris mentis honesta bonis custos sancta domus vita miranda pudica conspicuum species quae parat ipsa decuss facta quater solito Lucinae numine mater pignoribus cecidit semper amanda probis infelix pater et genetrix sua volnera deflent deflent germani tempora maesta genis\n
EDCS-09000566.translation: Praised by all for her worthy modesty, Servilia lies snatched away by a harsh funeral; a dear wife, a pious mother, a praiseworthy daughter, a beloved sister, honest in her good qualities, a chaste guardian of the home, her life wondrous, modest, her beauty that adorns herself surpassing the usual standard. Made a mother for the fourth time by Lucina's divine favor, she fell among her cherished offspring, forever to be loved by the good. The unhappy father and her parents mourn their wounds; her brothers mourn with sad faces.
EDCS-09000566.summary: A lengthy funerary poem lamenting the death of Servilia, detailing her virtues as a wife, mother, daughter, and sister, and expressing the grief of her family, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000567.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Situleia Filumene annorum XXXVIII pia in <s>(uis) hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000567.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Situleia Filumene, 38 years old, pious to her own, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000567.summary: A funerary epitaph for Situleia Filumene, recording her name, age, piety, and a standard wish for the deceased, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000568.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Setuleia Isias nnum XXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000568.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Setuleia Isias, 30 years old, pious to her own, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000568.summary: A funerary epitaph for Setuleia Isias, recording her name, age, piety, and a standard wish for the deceased, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000572.text_edition: \n annorum? circum CVI hic sit- est sit tibi terra levis Tertiola Faustae liberta \n
EDCS-09000572.translation: Around 106 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Tertiola, freedwoman of Faustus.
EDCS-09000572.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tertiola, a freedwoman of Faustus, noting her exceptionally long age and a standard wish for the deceased, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000573.text_edition: \n anus Luci libertus epor ana Luci liberta Thais anus Luci libertus rus\n
EDCS-09000573.translation: Anus, freedman of Lucius; Epora, freedwoman of Lucius; Thais, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-09000573.summary: A funerary epitaph listing three freedpersons (Anus, Epora, Thais) and their former master, Lucius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000574.text_edition: \n Caius Traius Logismus Quartula Cantria Faustus mensum sic VII hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis quoquo versus pedes XII\n
EDCS-09000574.translation: Gaius Traius Logismus, Quartula, Cantria, Faustus, 7 months old, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you all. 12 feet in all directions.
EDCS-09000574.summary: A funerary epitaph for multiple individuals, including infants (implied by 7 months), recording their names, age for one, and the dimensions of the burial plot, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000575.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Trebia Romana nnorum LXX kara suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000575.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Trebia Romana, 70 years old, dear to her own, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000575.summary: A funerary epitaph for Trebia Romana, recording her name, age, affection for her family, and a standard wish for the deceased, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000577.text_edition: \n Caius Valerius Aestivus annorum XXXX karus suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000577.translation: Gaius Valerius Aestivus, 40 years old, dear to his own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000577.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Aestivus, recording his name, age, affection for his family, and a standard wish for the deceased, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000578.text_edition: \n Valeria hic sita est vixit annis XIX tibi terra sit levis\n
EDCS-09000578.translation: Valeria is laid here. She lived 19 years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000578.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria, recording her name, age at death, and a standard wish for the deceased, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000580.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Valeria Felicitas nnum XIX mensium V pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000580.translation: To the divine shades. Valeria Felicitas, aged 19 years and 5 months, devoted to her family, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000580.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Valeria Felicitas, who died at the age of 19 years and 5 months.

EDCS-09000581.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Venusta nnorum LIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000581.translation: To the divine shades. Venusta, aged 53 years, devoted to her family, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000581.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Venusta, who died at the age of 53 years.

EDCS-09000582.text_edition: \n Decimus Veturius Decimi filius Galeria Niger Albucia Marci liberta Prima Albucia Marci liberta Hispana\n
EDCS-09000582.translation: Decimus Veturius Niger, son of Decimus, of the Galeria tribe; Prima, freedwoman of Albucia Marci; Hispana, freedwoman of Albucia Marci.
EDCS-09000582.summary: This inscription lists individuals, likely family members or associates, with their names, patronymic, tribe, and status as freedpersons.

EDCS-09000585.text_edition: \n Publius Vibius Publi libertus Protus hic situs est et Vibia Publi liberta Phiale annorum XXIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000585.translation: Publius Vibius Protus, freedman of Publius, is buried here, and Vibia Phiale, freedwoman of Publius, aged 23 years, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000585.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Publius Vibius Protus and Vibia Phiale, who died at the age of 23 years.

EDCS-09000586.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Vibia Verecunda nnorum XXII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000586.translation: To the divine shades. Vibia Verecunda, aged 22 years, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000586.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Vibia Verecunda, who died at the age of 22 years.

EDCS-09000587.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Victor nnum XXX desiderium omnium pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000587.translation: To the divine shades. Victor, aged 30 years, the beloved of all, devoted to his family, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000587.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Victor, who died at the age of 30 years.

EDCS-09000604.text_edition: \n nnorum L pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000604.translation: Aged 50 years, devoted to his family, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000604.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of an unnamed male, who died at the age of 50 years.

EDCS-09000605.text_edition: \n nnum XXII hic sit- est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000605.translation: Aged 22 years, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000605.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of an unnamed individual, who died at the age of 22 years.

EDCS-09000607.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription that records the age of an individual as 51 years.

EDCS-09000612.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that mentions 'mother' and part of a name or word.

EDCS-09000637.summary: This inscription is likely a funerary marker for Titus M. Fortunatus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09000638.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to provide a definitive translation or summary, but it appears to be a name or abbreviation, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09000663.translation: Thalassius, who, as a hunter, [text uncertain]
EDCS-09000663.summary: This inscription mentions Thalassius, a hunter, but the rest of the text is unclear, making its exact purpose uncertain, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09000664.text_edition: \n Semuncia Caio Caesare Augusto patre patriae\n
EDCS-09000664.translation: Semuncia, to Gaius Caesar Augustus, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-09000664.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Augustus by Semuncia, likely dating to his reign (27 BC – 14 AD).

EDCS-09000670.summary: This very brief inscription records the name Adamatus and likely a young age at death.

EDCS-09000744.text_edition: \n Hic viae servitus imposita est ab societate Sisaponensi susum ad montes societatis Sisaponensis lata pedes XIV\n
EDCS-09000744.summary: This inscription, though fragmented, records the imposition of a right of way for a road by the Sisaponensian association to the mountains belonging to the same association, measuring 14 feet wide, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09000745.text_edition: \n Locus pedum XX Valeria Quinti filia Rustica hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000745.translation: A space of 20 feet. Valeria Rustica, daughter of Quintus, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000745.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Rustica, daughter of Quintus, indicating the size of her burial plot, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09000748.text_edition: \n Marcus Fusius filius Amerimnus votum solvit libens animo\n
EDCS-09000748.translation: Marcus Fusius Amerimnus, son of [name missing], has gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-09000748.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Marcus Fusius Amerimnus, son of [name missing], likely dedicated to a deity, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09000755.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Alethia mater felix nnorum LXXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000755.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Alethia, a happy mother, aged 80, pious towards her family, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000755.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Alethia, commemorating her age and piety, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09000756.text_edition: \n Luci libertus Heracleo Secunda us Luci filius Mentus Publi filius Sergia Celer\n
EDCS-09000756.translation: Heracleo, freedman of Lucius. Secunda, daughter of Lucius. Mentus, son of Publius. Sergius Celer.
EDCS-09000756.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly related to a legal document, property ownership, or a funerary monument, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09000757.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Caesius Euelpistus nnum LIIII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000757.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Caesius Euelpistus, aged 54, dutiful towards his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000757.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Caesius Euelpistus, who died at the age of 54, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000758.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gamus nnorum XXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000758.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gamus, aged 25, dutiful towards his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000758.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gamus, who died at the age of 25, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000759.text_edition: \n Hemerine nnm XVI hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000759.translation: Hemerine, aged 16, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000759.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Hemerine, who died at the age of 16, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000761.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII imperator VIII consul III designatus IIII pater patriae refecit\n
EDCS-09000761.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 4th time, imperator for the 8th time, consul designate for the 3rd time, father of his country, restored (this).
EDCS-09000761.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a building by Emperor Claudius, likely in the mid-1st century AD.

EDCS-09000762.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Celtiber Aulus Albanius Ianuarius de suo dederunt d\n
EDCS-09000762.translation: Lucius Valerius Celtiber and Aulus Albanius Ianuarius gave from their own funds.
EDCS-09000762.summary: This inscription records a donation made by Lucius Valerius Celtiber and Aulus Albanius Ianuarius, likely for a public work or building.

EDCS-09000763.text_edition: \n Helvia Tu Marco Helvio viro et patrono et Lucio Iulio Tha\n
EDCS-09000763.translation: To Helvia, his wife and patroness, and Lucius Iulius Tha[...]
EDCS-09000763.summary: This is a dedication to Helvia, wife and patroness, and an incomplete dedication to Lucius Iulius Tha[...], likely part of a tomb or honorary monument.

EDCS-09000764.text_edition: \n Manlius Myrum sic pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000764.translation: Manlius Myrum, dutiful towards his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000764.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Manlius Myrum, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000765.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Trophime nnum XX pia in <s>(uis) hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000765.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Trophime, aged 20, dutiful towards her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000765.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Trophime, who died at the age of 20, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000766.translation: Quintus Valerius D[...], S[...], the place.
EDCS-09000766.summary: This fragmented inscription likely records Quintus Valerius dedicating or allocating a place, possibly for burial or construction.

EDCS-09000767.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Manlia Victorina nnum XXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000767.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Manlia Victorina, aged 30, dutiful towards her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000767.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Manlia Victorina, who died at the age of 30, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000769.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Syntrophillus musicarius Luci Semproni C nnorum \n
EDCS-09000769.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Syntrophillus, a musician, son of Lucius Sempronius, [aged] ...
EDCS-09000769.summary: This funerary epitaph records Syntrophillus, a musician, who is commemorated by his heirs, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09000770.text_edition: \n Quintus Accius Quinti filius Masculus hic situs est\n
EDCS-09000770.translation: Quintus Accius Masculus, son of Quintus, is buried here.
EDCS-09000770.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Quintus Accius Masculus, son of Quintus, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09000771.text_edition: \n Veneri Augustae Lucius Aelius Aelianus dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000771.translation: Lucius Aelius Aelianus gave and dedicated this to Venus Augusta.
EDCS-09000771.summary: This dedication records Lucius Aelius Aelianus making an offering to the goddess Venus Augusta, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09000773.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Primigenius annorum XXXXXV pius in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000773.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Primigenius, aged 55, pious towards his own people. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000773.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Primigenius, aged 55, who is described as pious, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09000775.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Vespasiano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate V imperatori XI patri patriae consuli V designato VI censori liberisque eius pagani pagi Carbulensis\n
EDCS-09000775.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 5th time, Imperator for the 11th time, Father of the Fatherland, Consul for the 5th time designated, Censor. And to his children. By the villagers of the Carbulan pagus.
EDCS-09000775.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Vespasian and his children by the villagers of the Carbulan pagus, dating to Vespasian's reign (69-79 CE).

EDCS-09000777.text_edition: \n Quinti filius Sergia Asiaticus nnorum LXV IIvir colonorum coloniae Patriciae huic ordo decrevit locum sepulturae funeris inpensam statuam pius in suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000777.translation: Asiaticus, son of Quintus, of the Sergia tribe, aged 65, a twice-elected official of the colonists of the colony Patricia. The town council decreed a burial plot, funeral expenses, and a statue for him; he was pious towards his own people. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000777.summary: This funerary inscription honors Asiaticus, a prominent local official in the colony Patricia, who was granted public honors in death, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09000779.text_edition: \n Urso nnorum LXXXX pius in <s>(uis) hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000779.translation: Urso, aged 90, pious towards his own people. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000779.summary: This funerary epitaph records Urso, aged 90, who is described as pious, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09000780.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying Primigenius as a freedman, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09000783.text_edition: \n Clara annorum XXXX hic sita est pia in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000783.translation: Clara, aged 40, is buried here; pious towards her own people. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000783.summary: This funerary epitaph records Clara, aged 40, who is described as pious, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09000784.text_edition: \n us Mari mensium IIII e vivo et genitore recessit qui posset patrios iam bene nosse Lares s totius dare vota fuerunt non data sorte gravi tu qui perlegis hunc titulum studiose viato<r> dicas praeteriens sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000784.translation: US Mari, 4 months old, departed from life and his father, who would have already known his paternal Lares. He had vowed to give his whole life, but it was not granted by a harsh fate. You who read this inscription, zealous traveler, say as you pass by, 'May the earth lie lightly upon you.'
EDCS-09000784.summary: This poignant epitaph records the death of a baby named US Mari at four months old, lamenting the short life and expressing a wish for a peaceful afterlife, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09000785.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fabia Mirine vixit nnis XXV maritae sanctissimae Geminus memoriam posuit hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000785.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fabia Mirine, who lived 25 years, placed this memorial for her most holy wife. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000785.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life of Fabia Mirine, who died at 25, and erected by her wife.

EDCS-09000786.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Primitivus servus nnorum LXXI pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000786.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Primitivus, a devoted slave, 71 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000786.summary: A funerary epitaph for Primitivus, a slave who died at the age of 71.

EDCS-09000787.text_edition: \n Sabina annorum XVII sit tibi terra levis pia in suis\n
EDCS-09000787.translation: Sabina, 17 years old. May the earth lie lightly upon you, devoted to her family.
EDCS-09000787.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sabina, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-09000788.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Superatus nnorum X pius in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000788.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Superatus, 10 years old, devoted to his family. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000788.summary: A funerary epitaph for Superatus, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-09000789.text_edition: \n sit tibi terra levis Victor Victoris filius nnorum VIII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000789.translation: May the earth lie lightly upon you. Victor, son of Victor, 8 years old, devoted to his family. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000789.summary: A funerary epitaph for Victor, son of Victor, who died at the age of 8.

EDCS-09000792.text_edition: \n Quintusrarius Quinti Cai libertus Daesa monumentum fecit se vivo sibi heic est sepultus pontufex sic nemoris Daesa Dis Manibus bonus homo et ego\n
EDCS-09000792.translation: Quintus Varrarius, freedman of Quintus Cai, had this monument made for himself while alive; he is buried here. Pontifex Sic Nemoris Daesa. A good man, and I [buried him/this is for him].
EDCS-09000792.summary: A self-funded tomb inscription by Quintus Varrarius, a freedman and pontifex, dated to his lifetime.

EDCS-09000798.text_edition: \n Crescens annorum X Crispina soror annorum hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-09000798.translation: Crescens, 10 years old, and Crispina, his sister, [age missing]. They lie here. May the earth lie lightly upon you both.
EDCS-09000798.summary: A funerary epitaph for two siblings, Crescens and Crispina.

EDCS-09000802.translation: To Lucana, wife of Lucius Annius.
EDCS-09000802.summary: A dedication on a tomb or monument to Lucana, wife of Lucius Annius.

EDCS-09000804.text_edition: \n ------] Sextus Fanniusanus prae Modesti V SA PIIA \n
EDCS-09000804.translation: [---] Sextus Fanniusianus, lieutenant of Modestus, [---] pious.
EDCS-09000804.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning Sextus Fanniusianus, a lieutenant, possibly a military or funerary dedication.

EDCS-09000805.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning the name Quintus Fulvius, likely part of a larger dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-09000808.text_edition: \n Porcia Sertoriana annorum sex hic sita est ]\n
EDCS-09000808.translation: Porcia Sertoriana, aged six, lies here.
EDCS-09000808.summary: A tombstone recording the death of a young girl named Porcia Sertoriana.

EDCS-09000809.text_edition: \n <D>(is) <M>(anibus) <s>(acrum) IVIA Cai filia Rufina annorum XX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000809.translation: To the departed spirits. IVIA Rufina, daughter of Caius, aged twenty, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000809.summary: A tombstone for IVIA Rufina, daughter of Caius, who died at the age of twenty.

EDCS-09000810.text_edition: \n Vibia Quinti filia Gaetula annorum LXXX hic sita <e=H>(st) \n
EDCS-09000810.translation: Vibia Gaetula, daughter of Quintus, aged eighty, lies here.
EDCS-09000810.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Vibia Gaetula, daughter of Quintus, at the age of eighty.

EDCS-09000814.summary: A fragment of an inscription likely naming Arantonius Taporus, son of Cilius.

EDCS-09000815.text_edition: \n Lupus Camali Taporus nnorum XXXV hic situs est te rogo praeteriens dicas sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000815.translation: Lupus Taporus, son of Camalus, aged thirty-five, lies here. I ask you passing by, say: May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000815.summary: A tombstone for Lupus Taporus, son of Camalus, aged thirty-five, who requests passersby to wish him well.

EDCS-09000816.text_edition: \n Vedia |(mulieris) liberta Gorge annorum XVIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000816.translation: Vedia Gorge, freedwoman of Vedia, aged eighteen, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000816.summary: A tombstone for Vedia Gorge, a freedwoman, aged eighteen.

EDCS-09000817.text_edition: \n Quintus Petronius Salvius annorum XX pius in suos hic situs <e=T>(st) sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-09000817.translation: Quintus Petronius Salvius, aged twenty, dutiful to his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000817.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Petronius Salvius, aged twenty, noted for his piety towards his family.

EDCS-09000818.text_edition: \n Sempronia Quinti filia Viniopis Soliensis annorum XXXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis fili faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09000818.translation: Sempronia Viniopis, daughter of Quintus, of Solium, aged forty-five, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her sons arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09000818.summary: A tombstone for Sempronia Viniopis, daughter of Quintus, from Solium, aged forty-five, erected by her sons.

EDCS-09000819.summary: An inscription that likely records the name Gaius Pontius, son of Nianus.

EDCS-09000820.text_edition: \n Sextus Valerius Auctus annorum XX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000820.translation: Sextus Valerius Auctus, aged twenty, dutiful to his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000820.summary: A tombstone for Sextus Valerius Auctus, aged twenty, noted for his family devotion.

EDCS-09000823.text_edition: \n Me donec vivam dolor excruciabit acerbus stirps mea quod misero funere rapta mihi est strage repentina puerilis concidit aetas eripuitque dies lumine summus eam tristis et infelix percontor numina semper cur non me quaeso diripiunt brevi<ter> nunc cum conditus in tumulo sis Classice care contegat ossa et sit tibi perpetuo pia Tellus\n
EDCS-09000823.translation: Bitter grief for my child, snatched from me by a sudden slaughter, will torment me as long as I live; the flower of youth perished, and death stole her from the light. Sad and unhappy, I always question the gods: why, I beg, do they not quickly tear me apart? Now that you are buried in the tomb, Classicus, may the earth cover your bones and may the pious ground be yours forever.
EDCS-09000823.summary: This is a funerary epitaph lamenting the death of a young child named Classicus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000824.text_edition: \n Trifinium inter Sacilienses Eporenses Solienses ex sententia Iuli Proculi iudicis confirmatum ab Imperatore Caesare Hadriano Augusto\n
EDCS-09000824.translation: The boundary between the Sacilienses, Eporenses, and Solienses was confirmed by the judgment of the judge Lucius Julius Proculus, and approved by the Emperor Caesar Hadrian Augustus.
EDCS-09000824.summary: This inscription records the confirmation of a territorial boundary dispute between three peoples, ratified by Emperor Hadrian, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09000825.text_edition: \n Lucius Porcius Portianus posuit aram Iovi\n
EDCS-09000825.translation: Lucius Porcius Portianus erected an altar to Jupiter.
EDCS-09000825.summary: A dedication of an altar to Jupiter by Lucius Porcius Portianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000829.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Calpurnius Fronto annorum XXX Lucius Fulvius Badius annorum XV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000829.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Calpurnius Fronto, aged 30, and Lucius Fulvius Badius, aged 15, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000829.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Calpurnius Fronto and Lucius Fulvius Badius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000830.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum M A libens animo posuit\n
EDCS-09000830.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. M. A. dedicated this willingly.
EDCS-09000830.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by an individual identified by the initials M. A., likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000831.text_edition: \n Rufina Domitia annorum X pia in suis hic sita sit tibi terra levis IIP\n
EDCS-09000831.translation: Rufina Domitia, aged 10, pious towards her family, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you. (II.P. possibly indicates 'in pace' or similar).
EDCS-09000831.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rufina Domitia, a pious young girl, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000832.text_edition: \n Aemilius Celsi filius Reburrus Manca Cantaber Munis O \n
EDCS-09000832.translation: Aemilius, son of Celsus, Reburrus, Manca, the Cantabrian, Munis.
EDCS-09000832.summary: This appears to be a list of names, possibly representing individuals from the Cantabrian region, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000836.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription beginning with the name Gaius Sentius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000838.summary: This inscription records the name Marcus Racilius Firmus, possibly noting an increase in wealth or status, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000840.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to be a dedication or address to an Emperor Caesar, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000843.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Calpurnia Ahprodisia nnorum LV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Hilarus nnorum \n
EDCS-09000843.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Calpurnia Ahprodisia, aged 55, is buried here with her own. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Hilarus, aged [---]
EDCS-09000843.summary: A funerary epitaph for Calpurnia Ahprodisia, dated to the Roman Imperial period, recording her age and burial by Hilarus.

EDCS-09000846.text_edition: \n Fabia Pacula Marci filia annorum XXV hic sita est tibi terra levis sit t(ibi)\n
EDCS-09000846.translation: Fabia Pacula, daughter of Marcus, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. May [the earth lie lightly] upon you.
EDCS-09000846.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fabia Pacula, a woman of 25, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording her burial and a wish for a light grave.

EDCS-09000847.text_edition: \n Aquam Augustam Caius Annius Cai filius Quirina Annianus IIvir bis ponti<f=II>(ex) perpetualis mun<e=I>ris municipio suo ex HS nummorum testamento perduci iussit \n
EDCS-09000847.translation: Caius Annius Annianus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, twice perpetual duovir and pontifex, ordered the Augustan aqueduct to be brought to his municipality by his will, at a cost of [HS] nummi.
EDCS-09000847.summary: An inscription from a Roman municipality, likely dating to the early Imperial period, recording the construction of an aqueduct funded by a donation from Caius Annius Annianus.

EDCS-09000848.text_edition: \n Caio Sempronio Sperato flamini Divorum Auggustorum provinciae Baeticae Imperatore Nerva Traiano Caesare Augusto Germanico III <A>(ulo) Vic<i=E>rio <Martiali=Alariano> et Lucio Ma<e=R>cio Postumo consulibus hic provinciae Baeticae consensu flamini<s=C> munus est consequutus peracto honore flaminico e<i=T> FECIALI omnis concilii consensus statuam decrevit huic ordo Mellariensis decreverunt sepulturae locum impensam funeris laudationem statuas equestres duas Venusta uxor honore accepto impensa remissa posuit \n
EDCS-09000848.translation: To Gaius Sempronius Speratus, flamen of the Divine Emperors of the province of Baetica, when Emperor Nerva Trajan Caesar Augustus Germanicus was consul for the third time, and Albinus Victor Martialis and Lucius Maecius Postumus were consuls, here in the province of Baetica, by the consensus of the provincial council, he obtained the honour of flamen. After completing his duty as flamen and as a fetial, the consensus of the entire council decreed a statue to him. The order of the city of Mella decreed a burial place, funeral expenses, and a statue on horseback for him. Venusta his wife, having received the honour, repaid the expense and erected [this statue].
EDCS-09000848.summary: A complex inscription from Roman Baetica, dating to the consulship of A.V. Martialis and L.M. Postumus (c. 102 CE), honouring Gaius Sempronius Speratus for his service as flamen and fetial with a statue and other honors, which his wife Venusta funded.

EDCS-09000849.text_edition: \n Semproniae Varillae huic Mellarienses locum sepulturae funeris inpensam statuam laudationem decrevere Sempronia Varilla filia honore accepto inpensa remissa piissumae sic matri posuit\n
EDCS-09000849.translation: To Sempronia Varilla, the people of Mella decreed a burial place, funeral expenses, and a statue. Sempronia Varilla her daughter, having received the honour, repaid the expense and erected [this monument] to her most devoted mother.
EDCS-09000849.summary: A funerary monument from Mella, likely from the Roman Imperial period, dedicated to Sempronia Varilla by her daughter, also named Sempronia Varilla, who funded the burial and honors.

EDCS-09000851.translation: Caius Sempronius Severus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-09000851.summary: A simple funerary epitaph or ownership mark for Caius Sempronius Severus, son of Marcus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000859.text_edition: \n Coilia Quinti filia Vetla sic pia hic sita est annorum XXIIX\n
EDCS-09000859.translation: Coilia Vetla, daughter of Quintus, a devoted woman, is buried here, aged 28.
EDCS-09000859.summary: A funerary epitaph for Coilia Vetla, a 28-year-old woman, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording her burial and familial details.

EDCS-09000862.text_edition: \n Marcus Aemilius Cai filius annorum XII hic situs est\n
EDCS-09000862.translation: Marcus Aemilius, son of Caius, aged 12, is buried here.
EDCS-09000862.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aemilius, a 12-year-old boy, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording his burial.

EDCS-09000863.translation: A girl, daughter of Marcus Licinianus.
EDCS-09000863.summary: A very brief funerary epitaph or marker for an unnamed daughter of Marcus Licinianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000864.text_edition: \n Lucius Varvius Luci libertus Sarapio hic situs est V \n
EDCS-09000864.translation: Lucius Varvius Sarapio, freedman of Lucius, is buried here, aged 5.
EDCS-09000864.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Varvius Sarapio, a freedman, aged 5, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording his burial.

EDCS-09000865.text_edition: \n Rusticus Luci Cornelii Badronensis filius hic est situs\n
EDCS-09000865.translation: Rusticus, son of Lucius Cornelius Badronensis, is buried here.
EDCS-09000865.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Rusticus, son of Lucius Cornelius Badronensis.

EDCS-09000867.text_edition: \n Aulus Iunius Peregrinus uxori Amiae Sisan<n=I>ae <h=S>(ic) sita sit tibi terra levis VIV S C F \n
EDCS-09000867.translation: Aulus Iunius Peregrinus for his wife, Amia Sisanna. May the earth lie lightly upon you. VIV S C F.
EDCS-09000867.summary: A funerary inscription from Aulus Iunius Peregrinus for his wife Amia Sisanna, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09000868.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Servilia Pyletae sic annorum XXXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000868.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Servilia Pyletae, aged 45, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000868.summary: A funerary inscription for Servilia Pyletae, aged 45, dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-09000872.translation: Gaius Racilius Nestor, freedman of Gaius.
EDCS-09000872.summary: A simple epitaph identifying Gaius Racilius Nestor, a freedman.

EDCS-09000873.summary: A brief epitaph recording the name and age of Peregrinus, who died at 19.

EDCS-09000874.text_edition: \n Marcus Servilius Maurus Mellariensis annorum LXXXV hic situs sit tibi terra <l>(evis) faciendum curavit Servilia Festa patrono suo\n
EDCS-09000874.translation: Marcus Servilius Maurus of Mellaria, aged 85, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Servilia Festa had this made for her patron.
EDCS-09000874.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Servilius Maurus, aged 85, for whom his patron Servilia Festa arranged the burial.

EDCS-09000876.text_edition: \n Aemilius icius municipii Flavii Lacinimurg- annorum hic situs est\n
EDCS-09000876.translation: Aemilius Icius of the municipium of Flavia Lacinimurgia, aged [---], is buried here.
EDCS-09000876.summary: A funerary inscription for Aemilius Icius from Flavia Lacinimurgia, with his age partially missing.

EDCS-09000877.text_edition: \n Gaius Gavius Gai libertus Sergia Auctus annorum LXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000877.translation: Gaius Gavius Sergiactus, freedman of Gaius, aged 70, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000877.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Gavius Sergiactus, freedman of Gaius, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-09000878.text_edition: \n Flavia Faustina pia in suis hic est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000878.translation: Flavia Faustina the dutiful is here in her own plot. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000878.summary: A funerary inscription for Flavia Faustina, emphasizing her piety and ownership of the burial plot.

EDCS-09000879.text_edition: \n Vibius Vascorus pius patri faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09000879.translation: Vibius Vascorus the dutiful arranged this for his father.
EDCS-09000879.summary: A funerary inscription recording that Vibius Vascorus arranged a monument for his pious father.

EDCS-09000883.text_edition: \n Tuscae Marci Helvi Stapili sic filiae Patriciensis nnrum XVIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Titus Iulius Firmanus Patriciensis nnrum hic situs est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-09000883.translation: Tusca, daughter of Marcus Helvius Stapilius, 18 years old, lies here. May the earth be light on you. Titus Iulius Firmanus, 18 years old, lies here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-09000883.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Tusca and Titus Iulius Firmanus, likely a tombstone with epitaphs for two individuals.

EDCS-09000885.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quinti Senproni sic Serviliani Bedronensis nnm LXXX pius in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000885.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Senpronius Servilianus of Bedronia, 80 years old, pious, rests among his own. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-09000885.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintus Senpronius Servilianus of Bedronia.

EDCS-09000886.text_edition: \n Gaius Licinius Rufus hic situs tibi terra leve sic annorum LX\n
EDCS-09000886.translation: Gaius Licinius Rufus rests here, may the earth be light on you, 60 years old.
EDCS-09000886.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gaius Licinius Rufus, a tombstone with an epitaph.

EDCS-09000892.text_edition: \n Rosia Urhelae liberta Baedronensis annorum LXXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000892.translation: Rosia Urhelae, freedwoman of Baedronia, 85 years old, lies here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-09000892.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Rosia Urhelae, a freedwoman.

EDCS-09000893.text_edition: \n Urbanus Sagae libertus annorum LV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000893.translation: Urbanus, freedman of Saga, 55 years old, lies here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-09000893.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Urbanus, a freedman.

EDCS-09000894.text_edition: \n Publius Paperius sic Niger Quirina annorum LXXXXV Calpurnia Andrana Quirina annorum LXV Baedronenses hic siti sunt vobis sit terra levis\n
EDCS-09000894.translation: Publius Paperius Niger of the Quirina tribe, 95 years old, and Calpurnia Andrana of the Quirina tribe, 65 years old, both from Baedronia, rest here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-09000894.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two individuals, Publius Paperius Niger and Calpurnia Andrana, both belonging to the Quirina tribe and from Baedronia.

EDCS-09000895.text_edition: \n Caius Papirius Publi filius Quirina Nigrinus nnorum LXXV Caius Papirius Cai filius Quirina Niger nnorum XXXX Baedronenses his ordo Baedronensium locum sepulturae funeris inpensas laudationem decrevit hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-09000895.translation: Caius Papirius Nigrinus, son of Publius Papirius of the Quirina tribe, 75 years old, and Caius Papirius Niger, son of Caius Papirius of the Quirina tribe, 40 years old, from Baedronia. The order of Baedronians decreed a burial place, funeral expenses, and a eulogy for them. They rest here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-09000895.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caius Papirius Nigrinus and Caius Papirius Niger, honored by the Baedronian community with a burial plot, expenses, and eulogy.

EDCS-09000896.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Va<l>(eria) Tertiola annorum XXI hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000896.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Tertiola, 21 years old, lies here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-09000896.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Valeria Tertiola.

EDCS-09000898.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caesio
EDCS-09000898.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely dedicated to Caesio.

EDCS-09000901.text_edition: \n Palei dominae ex voto G C S Q posuerunt\n
EDCS-09000901.translation: From the Palei family, as vowed. G.C.S.Q. placed this.
EDCS-09000901.summary: This votive inscription records a dedication made by individuals identified by initials from the Palei family.

EDCS-09000903.text_edition: \n nepoti divi Nervae pronepoti Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio municipium Flavium Mirobrigensium IIvir egit Licinius Licinianus\n
EDCS-09000903.translation: To Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, grandson of the deified Nerva, great-grandson of Trajan, the municipality of Mirobrigenses, under the duumvir Licinius Licinianus, carried out [this work].
EDCS-09000903.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely erected by the municipality of Mirobrigenses during the reign of Antoninus Pius (AD 138-161).

EDCS-09000904.text_edition: \n Licinia Caprilla Mirobrigensis annorum LXXII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Gemina \n
EDCS-09000904.translation: Licinia Caprilla of Mirobriga, aged 72, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Gemina [erected this].
EDCS-09000904.summary: This is a tombstone for Licinia Caprilla, dated to the Roman Imperial period based on naming conventions and common funerary formula.

EDCS-09000905.text_edition: \n Pulcher Pulchri filius Mirobrigensis annorum XLVII pius in omnes suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis faciendum curavit Honesta uxor viro et patrono\n
EDCS-09000905.translation: Pulcher, son of Pulcher, of Mirobriga, aged 47, pious towards all his family, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Honesta, his wife and patroness, saw to its construction for her husband.
EDCS-09000905.summary: This is a tombstone for Pulcher, erected by his wife Honesta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000906.translation: To Marcus Vironius Semo, son of Val of Mirobriga.
EDCS-09000906.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Vironius Semo, likely a local magistrate or notable of Mirobriga, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000908.text_edition: \n Minucia Gai filia Annia annorum XV hic sita est\n
EDCS-09000908.translation: Minucia, daughter of Gaius, of the Annian tribe, aged 15, is buried here.
EDCS-09000908.summary: This is a tombstone for Minucia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000910.text_edition: \n ae uxori et Gaius OITILNVS TOVIIIVIL sorori sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000910.translation: [For his] wife and [his] sister, Gaius Oitilnius Toviivil [dedicated this]; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000910.summary: This is a tombstone fragment, likely dedicating a burial to a wife and sister, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000913.text_edition: \n Atia Pintili filia nata annis L hic sita est ALIE et Allia matri faciendum curaverunt sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000913.translation: Atia, daughter of Pintilius, born 50 years ago, is buried here. Alie and Allia had it made for their mother; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000913.summary: This is a tombstone for Atia, erected by her daughters Alie and Allia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000914.text_edition: \n Cereri Lucius Iulius Luci filius Afer vicanis dono dedit\n
EDCS-09000914.translation: Lucius Julius Afer, son of Lucius, gave this to Ceres for the villagers.
EDCS-09000914.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Ceres, likely a small shrine or offering, donated by Lucius Julius Afer for the local villagers, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000915.text_edition: \n Caius C Modestinus Deanae votum libens animo solvit\n
EDCS-09000915.translation: Gaius C. Modestinus willingly paid his vow to Deana.
EDCS-09000915.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Gaius C. Modestinus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Deana, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000916.text_edition: \n VPAISN Titanius Uccisus Suri filius votum libens animo solvit\n
EDCS-09000916.translation: Titanius Uccisus, son of Surus, willingly paid his vow to Upaisna.
EDCS-09000916.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Titanius Uccisus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Upaisna, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000917.text_edition: \n Gacalia Tritei filia hic sita est annorum LXX\n
EDCS-09000917.translation: Gacalia, daughter of Triteius, lies here, aged 70 years.
EDCS-09000917.summary: A tombstone for Gacalia, daughter of Triteius, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000918.text_edition: \n Placida Pisiri filia hic sita est annorum XX\n
EDCS-09000918.translation: Placida, daughter of Pisirus, lies here, aged 20 years.
EDCS-09000918.summary: A tombstone for Placida, daughter of Pisirus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000920.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Augustus Vespasianus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII imperator X pater patriae consul IIII designatus V terminavit inter Lacinimurgenses et Ucubitanos colonos coloniae Claritatis Iuliae\n
EDCS-09000920.translation: The Emperor Caesar Augustus Vespasianus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 4th time, emperor for the 10th time, father of the country, consul for the 4th time, designated consul for the 5th time, established the boundary between the Lacinimurgenses and the Ucubitanos, colonists of the colony of Claritas Iulia.
EDCS-09000920.summary: An imperial boundary marker set up by Emperor Vespasian, defining land ownership between two groups of colonists, dated to between 72 and 79 AD.

EDCS-09000921.text_edition: \n Imperatore Domitiano Caesare Augusto divi Augusti Vespasiani filio Augustalis terminus colonorum coloniae Claritatis Iuliae Ucubitanorum inter Augustanos Emeritenses\n
EDCS-09000921.translation: Under Emperor Domitian Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus Vespasian, the Augustan boundary stone of the colonists of the colony of Claritas Iulia, the Ucubitanos, between the Augustani and the Emeritenses.
EDCS-09000921.summary: An imperial boundary marker related to the colony of Claritas Iulia and its inhabitants, potentially referencing a dispute or definition of territory, dated to the reign of Domitian (81-96 AD).

EDCS-09000922.text_edition: \n Festus Megari f(ilius) annorum VI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000922.translation: Festus, son of Megas, aged 6 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000922.summary: A tombstone for a young child named Festus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000923.text_edition: \n Lucius Iulius Cai filius Galeria Eburancus nnorum XXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-09000923.translation: Lucius Iulius Eburancus, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 30 years, lies here.
EDCS-09000923.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Iulius Eburancus, son of Caius, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000924.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Clemens annorum LX hic situs est\n
EDCS-09000924.translation: Caius Iulius Clemens, aged 60 years, lies here.
EDCS-09000924.summary: A tombstone for Caius Iulius Clemens, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000925.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai filius Fronto annorum XL hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000925.translation: Caius Iulius Fronto, son of Caius, aged 40 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000925.summary: A tombstone for Caius Iulius Fronto, son of Caius, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000926.text_edition: \n Caius Caecilius Picus et Lucius Sempronius Pollio pagi magistri Iovi Optimo Maximo voverunt\n
EDCS-09000926.translation: Caius Caecilius Picus and Lucius Sempronius Pollio, as district magistrates, vowed to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-09000926.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by two local officials (magistri pagi), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000927.text_edition: \n Laribus Lucius Malius Callicrates aram ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-09000927.translation: Lucius Malius Callicrates set up this altar to the Lares by vow.
EDCS-09000927.summary: A votive altar dedicated to the household gods (Lares) by Lucius Malius Callicrates, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000928.text_edition: \n Lucius Aemilius Medutus Mirobrigensis nnorum LXXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000928.translation: Lucius Aemilius Medutus, from Mirobriga, eighty years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000928.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Aemilius Medutus, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-09000929.text_edition: \n Aplondus Arquiaecus Surnae filius Mirobrigensis pius suis omnibus annorum XIX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000929.translation: Aplondus, son of Arquiaecus Surnae, from Mirobriga, dutiful to all his family, nineteen years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000929.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aplondus, who died at the age of 19.

EDCS-09000930.text_edition: \n hic sit- est sit tibi terra levis Caecili AETIA CARVS coniux \n
EDCS-09000930.translation: Here lies [Caecilius] Carus, husband of Aetia. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000930.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilius Carus, husband of Aetia, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-09000932.text_edition: \n V ci fili- annorum LX hic sit- est\n
EDCS-09000932.translation: V., son of Cius, sixty years old, lies here.
EDCS-09000932.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of V., son of Cius, at the age of 60.

EDCS-09000933.translation: Gaius, of the Ariorum [people], son of [---]
EDCS-09000933.summary: This is a damaged inscription that seems to record the name and tribal affiliation of an individual named Gaius.

EDCS-09000939.text_edition: \n Druso Caesari Tiberi filio divi Augusti nepoti pontifici auguri consuli II tribunicia potestate \n
EDCS-09000939.translation: To Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius, grandson of the deified Augustus, pontiff, augur, consul twice, with tribunician power.
EDCS-09000939.summary: This is a dedication honoring Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius and grandson of Augustus, listing his distinguished titles and offices.

EDCS-09000940.text_edition: \n Divo Nervae decreto decurionum municipium Flavium V pecunia publica fecit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000940.translation: By decree of the town councilors, the Municipium Flavium [erected this] at public expense, [and] dedicated it to the deified Nerva.
EDCS-09000940.summary: This inscription records the erection and dedication of a monument by the town councilors of the Municipium Flavium to the deified Emperor Nerva at public expense.

EDCS-09000941.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano optimo Augusto Germanico Dacico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVIIII imperatori XI consuli VI patri patriae decreto decurionum municipium Flavium V pecunia publica fecit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000941.translation: By decree of the town councilors, the Municipium Flavium [erected this] at public expense, [and] dedicated it to Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Optimus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the deified Nerva, chief pontiff, with tribunician power for the 19th time, hailed as Imperator for the 11th time, consul for the 6th time, father of the country.
EDCS-09000941.summary: This is a dedication by the Municipium Flavium to Emperor Trajan, listing his numerous titles and honors, including his 19th grant of tribunician power and 6th consulship.

EDCS-09000942.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano optimo Augusto Germanico Parthico Dacico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVIIII imperatori XI consuli VI patri patriae decreto decurionum municipium Flavium V pecunia publica fecit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000942.translation: By decree of the town councilors, the Municipium Flavium [erected this] at public expense, [and] dedicated it to Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Optimus Augustus Germanicus Parthicus Dacicus, son of the deified Nerva, chief pontiff, with tribunician power for the 19th time, hailed as Imperator for the 11th time, consul for the 6th time, father of the country.
EDCS-09000942.summary: This is a dedication by the Municipium Flavium to Emperor Trajan, similar to EDCS-09000941 but also including the title 'Parthicus', listing his numerous honors and imperial powers.

EDCS-09000943.text_edition: \n Matidiae Augustae Imperatoris Caesaris divi Nervae fili Nervae Traiani optimi Augusti Germanici Dacici Parthici sororis filiae \n
EDCS-09000943.translation: To Matidia Augusta, sister of Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Optimus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus Parthicus, son of the deified Nerva.
EDCS-09000943.summary: This is a dedication to Matidia Augusta, sister of Emperor Trajan, highlighting her imperial relationship.

EDCS-09000944.text_edition: \n Matidiae Augustae Imperatoris Caesaris divi Nervae fili Nervae Traiani optimi Augusti Germanici Dacici sororis filiae decreto decurionum municipium Flavium V pecunia publica fecit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000944.translation: By decree of the decurions of the Flavian municipality, this was made and dedicated at public expense to Matidia Augusta, sister of Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Optimus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the divine Nerva.
EDCS-09000944.summary: This inscription records the public dedication of a monument to Matidia Augusta, sister of Emperor Trajan, likely from the Trajanic period.

EDCS-09000945.text_edition: \n Divae Marci<an>ae Augustae Imperatoris Caesaris divi Nervae fili Nervae Traiani optimi Augusti Germanici Dacici Parthici sorori \n
EDCS-09000945.translation: To the divine Marciana Augusta, sister of Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Optimus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus Parthicus, son of the divine Nerva.
EDCS-09000945.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the deified Marciana Augusta, sister of Emperor Trajan.

EDCS-09000946.translation: To the divine Marciana Augusta, sister of Augustus.
EDCS-09000946.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the deified Marciana Augusta, sister of Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-09000949.text_edition: \n Marco Herennio Marci filio Galeria Laetino aedili IIviro III pontifici Augusti heredes ex testamento eius\n
EDCS-09000949.translation: His heirs, according to his will, [dedicated this] to Marcus Herennius, son of Marcus, aedile, duovir, and pontiff of Augustus.
EDCS-09000949.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the heirs of Marcus Herennius to him, listing his local magistracies and religious offices.

EDCS-09000954.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Victor servus nnorum XXI mensium IIII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000954.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Victor, a slave, aged twenty-one years and four months, pious in his own [affairs], lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000954.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Victor, a slave, at the age of twenty-one years and four months, with a common epitaph wishing him peace.

EDCS-09000955.summary: This inscription appears to record the name and official position of Lucius Pomponius Frontinus, likely related to tax collection.

EDCS-09000956.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Fa Florentius aram ex voto dedit dedicavit Lucius nepos\n
EDCS-09000956.translation: Lucius Fa. Florentius gave and dedicated this altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus in fulfillment of a vow; Lucius, his grandson.
EDCS-09000956.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Jupiter by Lucius Fa. Florentius in fulfillment of a vow, with his grandson Lucius also mentioned.

EDCS-09000957.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiiano sic Augusto Germanico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate IIII consuli IIII municipium Iulipense decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09000957.translation: By decree of the decurions of the Iulipense municipality [this was dedicated] to Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus, pontiff maximus, with tribunician power for the 4th time, consul for the 4th time, son of the divine Nerva.
EDCS-09000957.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by the municipality of Iulipense to Emperor Trajan, listing his titles and honors.

EDCS-09000958.text_edition: \n lius Auli filius Pius ensis IIvir decessit nnis XXXXIII Herennia Restituta nnorum hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000958.translation: Lucius Aulus Pius, son of Aulus, a duovir, died at the age of 43. Herennia Restituta, at the age of [unknown]. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000958.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of Lucius Aulus Pius and Herennia Restituta, with Lucius having held the office of duovir.

EDCS-09000959.text_edition: \n Lucius Attius Luci filius Galeria Optatus Arsensis decessit nnis LIIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000959.translation: Lucius Attius Optatus, son of Lucius Attius, of the Galeria tribe, from Arsium, died at the age of 54. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000959.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Lucius Attius Optatus, a citizen from Arsium.

EDCS-09000960.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Titi filius Papiria Proculus annorum XX pius in suis hic situs est\n
EDCS-09000960.translation: Titus Flavius Proculus, son of Titus, of the Papirian tribe, aged 20, lies here, dutiful to his family.
EDCS-09000960.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Titus Flavius Proculus at the age of 20.

EDCS-09000961.text_edition: \n Publius Pomponius Publi filius P<a>piria Sulpicianus annorum XVII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Sulpicia mulieris lib(erta) Callirhoe mater annorum L hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
