EDCS-05200315.summary: This is a tombstone for a woman, placed by her husband, who died at the age of forty.

EDCS-05500103.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pompeia Daphine lived for 10 years.
EDCS-05500103.summary: This is a tombstone for Pompeia Daphine, who died at the age of ten.

EDCS-05500663.translation: Pareavus, son of Gemellus, aged 55 years. Modesta made this for her husband. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500663.summary: This is a tombstone for Pareavus, son of Gemellus, who died at the age of fifty-five, dedicated by his wife Modesta.

EDCS-05500961.translation: A gift for the Emperor Augustus. Gaius Sempronius Proculus Servilianus and Gaius Sempronius Servilianus, son of Gaius, gave [this]. Sempronia Anullina dedicated [it].
EDCS-05500961.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to Emperor Augustus by Gaius Sempronius Proculus Servilianus and his son, Gaius Sempronius Servilianus, and dedicated by Sempronia Anullina.

EDCS-05500962.translation: Britto, aged 45 years. Whoever you are, passerby, read this: may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500962.summary: This is a tombstone for Britto, who died at the age of forty-five, with an exhortation to passersby.

EDCS-05500963.translation: Thetis, freedwoman of Optatus, aged 28 years. She is buried here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500963.summary: This is a tombstone for Thetis, freedwoman of Optatus, who died at the age of twenty-eight.

EDCS-05500965.translation: To Antoninus. Marcus Corn[elius] vir ..., Marcus Corn[elius] vir ..., [---] of the IIvirate, to this [man] most devoted, for the honor given, the ornaments.
EDCS-05500965.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Antoninus, likely honoring a magistrate with ornaments, possibly from the time of Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-05500966.translation: To the Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul for the third time, designated consul for the fourth time. Pudens, freedman of Augustus, his procurator, erected this at his own expense.
EDCS-05500966.summary: This inscription records a statue base dedicated to Emperor Nerva by his freedman and procurator, Pudens, likely during Nerva's reign (96-98 AD).

EDCS-05500967.translation: Theodorus, deputy of Diogenes, to Firmia Epiphania, most holy lady, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05500967.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Theodorus, deputy of Diogenes, to Firmia Epiphania, likely a local dignitary, based on a decree of the town council.

EDCS-05500968.translation: Gaius Julius Marcellus, aged 35, is buried here. I ask you, as you pass by, to say: 'May the earth be light upon you'.
EDCS-05500968.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Julius Marcellus, who died at age 35, including a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-05500970.translation: Onia D Italia is buried here. Age 19. I CC T.
EDCS-05500970.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Onia D Italia, who died at age 19, with an unclear final abbreviation.

EDCS-05500971.translation: Glaucus, son of Aluquius, is buried here.
EDCS-05500971.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Glaucus, son of Aluquius.

EDCS-05500973.translation: To Iulia Agrippina, mother of Caesar Augustus Germanicus, granddaughter of Augustus, the citizenship of Arucci.
EDCS-05500973.summary: This inscription records the granting of citizenship of Arucci to Iulia Agrippina, mother of Emperor Caligula, likely in recognition of her status.

EDCS-05500974.translation: To Baebia Crinitus, daughter of Caius, of Turibriga, priestess, who dedicated the temple of Apollo and Diana with 200,000 sesterces, from which sum the twentieth part of the Roman people, 20,000, was deducted, and an additional feast was given; she ordered that temple to be built and this statue to be erected to herself.
EDCS-05500974.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple to Apollo and Diana and the erection of a statue by Baebia Crinitus, a priestess, funded by a significant sum of money and further resources.

EDCS-05500975.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Vibius Baebianus, aged 44, and Vibia Marcella made this monument for a most devoted husband, deserving of all, here he lies. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05500975.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Vibia Marcella for her husband Quintus Vibius Baebianus, who died at age 44.

EDCS-05500976.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Vibia Marcella, aged 38, made this for her parent Iulia Restituta. Here she lies. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05500976.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Vibia Marcella for her parent Iulia Restituta, who died at age 38.

EDCS-05500977.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Vibia Crispa, of Rufinus of Arabriga, aged 67, lies here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05500977.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Vibia Crispa, aged 67, identified with Rufinus of Arabriga.

EDCS-05500980.translation: Modesta, daughter of Modestus, from Pacensis, aged 12, lies here. I ask you, as you pass by, to say 'May the earth be light on you'. Her mother [placed this].
EDCS-05500980.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Modesta, indicating her age and location of burial, with a plea to passersby for a kind wish.

EDCS-05500981.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Fabia Prisca, a Roman citizen aged 20, lies here. May the earth be light on you. Caius Geminius Priscus and Fabia Cadilla, her parents, set this up.
EDCS-05500981.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia Prisca, a Roman citizen who died at age 20, placed by her parents.

EDCS-05500984.translation: Domitia Avita, daughter of Lucius, aged 41, lies here. May the earth be light on you. Lucius Iulius Festus arranged for this to be made for his wife and himself.
EDCS-05500984.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Domitia Avita, aged 41, indicating that her husband, Lucius Iulius Festus, commissioned the monument for both of them.

EDCS-05500985.translation: Memmia, daughter of Titus, arranged for this to be made from her own funds for her husband Lucius Antistius Seranus and her son Lucius Antistius Avitus, aged 22. They lie here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-05500985.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Memmia commissioned the monument from her own funds for her deceased husband and son, who are identified by name and the son's age.

EDCS-05500986.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Asinius Triarius [dedicated this] to his wife Petreia Clara, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-05500986.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Asinius Triarius to his deceased wife, Petreia Clara.

EDCS-05500987.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. [Her] daughter arranged for this to be made. Titus Ponponius Vegetus, aged 73, lies here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-05500987.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Ponponius Vegetus, aged 73, indicating that his daughter commissioned the monument.

EDCS-05500990.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Diana, initiated by Caius, with the rest of the inscription lost.

EDCS-05500992.translation: Dedicated to Victoria Augusta. Marcus Terentius Ianuarius, freedman of Marcus, gives from his own funds.
EDCS-05500992.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Victoria Augusta, made at his own expense by Marcus Terentius Ianuarius, a freedman.

EDCS-05500993.translation: Varinia Flaccina, a most distinguished woman, made this for Gaius Varinius Pietas, holder of the office of II vir for the priesthood of the province of Baetica, aged 71.
EDCS-05500993.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Varinia Flaccina commissioned a monument for her husband, Gaius Varinius Pietas, who was a prominent local magistrate in the province of Baetica.

EDCS-05500994.translation: Lucius Valerius Amandus and Lucius Valerius Lucumo arranged for this podium in the circus, 600 feet long, to be made from their own funds by decree of the decurions, in honor of their tenure as IIII viri.
EDCS-05500994.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Valerius Amandus and Lucius Valerius Lucumo funded and constructed a large podium in the circus as a public honor related to their tenure as officials.

EDCS-05500995.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, a veteran soldier of the Second Legion, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500995.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Rufus, a veteran of the Second Legion, indicating he is buried here.

EDCS-05500996.translation: XXIII A BIVS Ceretanus, B Severa Lacimurgensis, [aged] XXXX [and?] [---]. Buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500996.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of two individuals, Bivius Ceretanus and Severa Lacimurgensis, with their ages indicated.

EDCS-05500997.translation: Allia Severa from Igaeditana, aged 22, is buried here. Her mother, Aelia Modesta, saw to the making of this monument.
EDCS-05500997.summary: A tombstone erected by Aelia Modesta for her daughter Allia Severa, aged 22.

EDCS-05500998.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sperata from Segedensis, aged 45, is buried here. L. Quaternus and L. Cincinatus erected this for their wife and mother, the excellent one.
EDCS-05500998.summary: A tombstone dedicated by L. Quaternus and L. Cincinatus to their wife and mother, Sperata, aged 45.

EDCS-05500999.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Quintus Antonius Severus Vamensis, aged 47. His son, Quintus Antonius Severianus, had this made for his most dutiful father. Buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500999.summary: A tombstone commissioned by Quintus Antonius Severianus for his father, Quintus Antonius Severus, aged 47.

EDCS-05501000.translation: Aelia Rufina, aged 19, is buried here. Alvius placed this.
EDCS-05501000.summary: A tombstone erected by Alvius for Aelia Rufina, aged 19.

EDCS-05501001.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Albia Valentina lived 42 years.
EDCS-05501001.summary: An epitaph recording the life span of Albia Valentina, who lived for 42 years.

EDCS-05501002.translation: Appuleia Brocina, daughter of Marcius, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501002.summary: A tombstone for Appuleia Brocina, daughter of Marcius.

EDCS-05501003.translation: Quintus Euangelus, freedman of Marcus, fulfilled his vow willingly to his brother Appuleius.
EDCS-05501003.summary: A dedication by Quintus Euangelus, freedman of Marcus, fulfilling a vow to his brother Appuleius.

EDCS-05501004.translation: For Quintus Arrius Flavius, aged 2, he is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Flavia Secunda made this for her deserving son from her own means.
EDCS-05501004.summary: A tombstone erected by Flavia Secunda for her son Quintus Arrius Flavius, aged 2.

EDCS-05501007.translation: Quintus Pompeius Sosius Priscus and Didia Severina, as heirs, fulfilled the vows for Didia Severina, daughter of Lucius, from the will of her husband Fabius Turpio.
EDCS-05501007.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of vows by heirs for a woman named Didia Severina, related to her husband's will.

EDCS-05501009.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Helvia Modesta, daughter of Caius, aged 40, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius Blaius Calpurnianus erected this for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-05501009.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Helvia Modesta, aged 40, with a monument erected by her son Lucius Blaius Calpurnianus.

EDCS-05501010.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Helvius Rufinus, son of Marcus, aged 60, and Lucius Helvius Novatus, son of Marcus, aged 20, and Marcus Helvius Rufus, son of Marcus, aged 30, brothers, are buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Their most devoted mother Helvia Rufina made this.
EDCS-05501010.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of three brothers, Marcus Helvius Rufinus, Lucius Helvius Novatus, and Marcus Helvius Rufus, with the monument erected by their mother Helvia Rufina.

EDCS-05501011.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Iulius Avitus, aged 83, and Iulius Avitianus, aged 40, are buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Terentia Severina arranged for this monument to be erected for her son and husband.
EDCS-05501011.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Iulius Avitus and Iulius Avitianus, with Terentia Severina arranging the monument for her son and husband.

EDCS-05501013.translation: Lucius Lucretius Marinus of Emerita fulfills his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05501013.summary: This inscription records Lucius Lucretius Marinus of Emerita fulfilling a vow, likely related to a personal dedication or offering.

EDCS-05501015.translation: Maria Marcella, daughter of Marcus, aged 25, is buried here.
EDCS-05501015.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Maria Marcella, aged 25.

EDCS-05501022.summary: This very brief inscription records the name and age of Gaius Servenius Racilianus, possibly a tomb marker or a record of a person.

EDCS-05501023.translation: Terentia DC MILES EDA, aged 6, died; Severa, her mother, provided this from her own money; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501023.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Terentia DC MILES EDA, aged 6, with the monument provided by her mother Severa.

EDCS-05501024.translation: Valeria Rappa, aged 93. Caesia erected this monument for her dearest mother-in-law, willingly. She is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501024.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Valeria Rappa, aged 93, with the monument erected by her daughter-in-law Caesia.

EDCS-05501035.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Antestius Calvus and Gaius Antestius Proculus are buried here. Cornelia Tertulla arranged for this to be made from her own money for her husband and son.
EDCS-05501035.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gaius Antestius Calvus and Gaius Antestius Proculus, with Cornelia Tertulla arranging the monument for her husband and son.

EDCS-05501036.translation: Sacred to Juno Regina; Licinius Serenianus, a most distinguished man, and Varinia Flaccina, a most distinguished woman, dedicated this for the health of their daughter Varinia Serena.
EDCS-05501036.summary: A dedication to Juno Regina by a married couple for their daughter's health.

EDCS-05501037.translation: From the municipality of Contributa Iulia; Aulus Asellius Threptus of Romula, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501037.summary: An inscription recording a public honor or dedication from the municipality of Contributa Iulia.

EDCS-05501038.translation: Licinius Albanus, son of Marcius, of Emerita, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05501038.summary: A soldier or veteran, Licinius Albanus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-05501039.translation: Lucius Lucullus of Iulia and Annius Lucanus, father, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-05501039.summary: Two individuals, Lucius Lucullus and Annius Lucanus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-05501040.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes; Quintus Manlius Avitus of Galeria Contributensis, twice a duovir, aged 76, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Manlia Avita dedicated this to her most devoted father.
EDCS-05501040.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Manlius Avitus, dedicated by his daughter, who served twice as duovir in Galeria Contributensis.

EDCS-05501041.translation: From the municipality of Contributa; Titus Junius Crassidianus, son of Titus, of Galeria, father, aged 50; Terentia Pithane, mother, aged 45; Titus Junius Fundinus, son of Titus, of Galeria, aged 18.
EDCS-05501041.summary: A family record listing the ages and parentage of three individuals from the municipality of Contributa.

EDCS-05501044.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes; Petronia Materna, wife of Junius Placidus, aged 35, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501044.summary: A funerary epitaph for Petronia Materna, wife of Junius Placidus.

EDCS-05501045.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes; Didymus and Lici [---]
EDCS-05501045.summary: A funerary inscription, incomplete, likely for multiple individuals named Didymus and Lici.

EDCS-05501048.translation: To Dineas, daughter of Marcus, of Sperata; Gaius Antistius Crassus, son of Caius, of Quirina.
EDCS-05501048.summary: An inscription possibly recording a dedication or a relationship between Dineas and Gaius Antistius Crassus.

EDCS-05501049.translation: Lucius Attius Lucanus, aged 65, his associates the Marmorarii set up this monument; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501049.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Attius Lucanus, erected by his fellow stonemasons (Marmorarii).

EDCS-05501050.translation: Sacred to Holy Proserpina. Lucius Samnius Sulla gave this with a vow, having been healed from illness.
EDCS-05501050.summary: This votive inscription records Lucius Samnius Sulla dedicating a gift to Proserpina for his recovery from illness.

EDCS-05501051.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fabia Fabiana, daughter of Lucius, aged 40, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501051.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fabia Fabiana, recording her death at age 40 and expressing a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-05501052.translation: For Cornelia Clementis, daughter of Tusca, perpetual priestess, the order of the people of Iporca erected a statue in recognition of her generosity, remitting public expenses for feasts; likewise the seviri remitted the feast expenses.
EDCS-05501052.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue by the local council of Iporca to honor Cornelia Clementis, a perpetual priestess, for her munificence in covering public feast expenses.

EDCS-05501053.translation: Cornelia Prisca, sister and heir, took care to have erected a statue to Quintus Cornelius Gallus (of the Quirina tribe), whom he ordered to be erected by his will with distributions of money to the decurions, by distributions of money to the decurions and from her own funds, she arranged for a feast for the local people and the order.
EDCS-05501053.summary: This inscription records Cornelia Prisca erecting a statue and arranging a feast in honor of her deceased brother, Quintus Cornelius Gallus, fulfilling his testamentary wishes and adding her own generosity.

EDCS-05501054.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Sergius Aelius Rusticus (of the Quirina tribe), aged 73, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501054.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sergius Aelius Rusticus, recording his death at age 73 and expressing a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-05501057.translation: [...IVIN, perhaps Emperor?] [...IP...] [...II?...] Pontifex Maximus, Father of the Fatherland, the public community of Muniguensium, by decree of the decurions, gave this. Lucius Licinius Annianus dedicated it.
EDCS-05501057.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by the public community of Muniguensium, likely honoring an emperor or high-ranking official, and dedicated by Lucius Licinius Annianus.

EDCS-05501059.translation: For Marcus Aelius Gallus (of the Quirina tribe), twice chief magistrate of the municipality, the public community of Muniguensium, for his merit and as an excellent citizen, [erected this statue] for his services.
EDCS-05501059.summary: This inscription records the public community of Muniguensium honoring Marcus Aelius Gallus, a former chief magistrate, for his distinguished service and citizenship.

EDCS-05501061.translation: For Lucius Lucretius Severus, a Patrician, and a decurion of the municipality of Flavius Axatitanus by residence, for whom he ordered a statue to be erected by his will, with distributions of money to the decurions of the municipalities of Flavius Axatitanus, he provided 12 measures [of grain?].
EDCS-05501061.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue for Lucius Lucretius Severus, a Patrician and decurion of Flavius Axatitanus, as stipulated in his will, with funds provided for distributions.

EDCS-05501062.translation: Cornelia Rustica, daughter of Lucius, of Oducium, for whom the order...
EDCS-05501062.summary: This fragmented inscription begins to record something concerning Cornelia Rustica, daughter of Lucius, and her connection to the local council.

EDCS-05501064.translation: To the Genius of the Flavius Arvensis municipality. Lucius Coranius Tuscus willingly ordered this to be erected.
EDCS-05501064.summary: This inscription records Lucius Coranius Tuscus willingly dedicating a monument to the Genius of the Flavius Arvensis municipality.

EDCS-05501065.translation: Sacred to the Augustan Peace. Hermes, freedman of Lucius Licinius Crescentis, a VI vir Augustalis, gave this gift from his own money.
EDCS-05501065.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the cult of the Augustan Peace by a freedman and a member of the seviri Augustales.

EDCS-05501066.translation: Marcus Egnatius Verna, freedman of Scitus, a VI vir Augustalis, ordered this to be made by his will in the place designated by the council.
EDCS-05501066.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument ordered by will for a member of the seviri Augustales, dedicated to the Augustan Virtue.

EDCS-05501068.translation: For Quintus Fulvius Attianus, son of Quintus Fulvius, grandson of Quintus Fulvius Rusticus, patron and priest, Galeria Carisiano, on account of the merits of the century of Ores Manes Halos Erques Beres Arvabores Isines Isurgut, set this up in the place designated by the council of citizens of the municipium Flavium Arvense, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501068.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument for Quintus Fulvius Attianus by members of his century, honoring his merits and service to the municipium Flavium Arvense.

EDCS-05501069.translation: For Quintus Traius Areianus, son of Quintus Traius, a citizen of the Quirina tribe from Arventus, the council of the municipium Flavium Arvense decreed for him a burial place, tomb, and statue on account of his merits, the praise, and the expenses for his funeral; Aemilia Lucilla, his mother, and Sergius Rufinus, his brother, having used the honor, remitted the expenses.
EDCS-05501069.summary: This inscription records honors and burial arrangements decreed by the council of the municipium Flavium Arvense for Quintus Traius Areianus, with his mother and brother covering the expenses.

EDCS-05501070.translation: Marcus Egnatius Venustus, freedman of Scitus, a VI vir, the council of citizens of the municipium Flavium Arvense decreed for him a statue and the ornaments of the decurionate; Marcus Egnatius Venustus, from his own money, placed a statue and marble benches and an antefix in the place designated by the council.
EDCS-05501070.summary: This inscription records a statue and civic honors granted to Marcus Egnatius Venustus, a freedman and VI vir, who also funded additional embellishments for his designated plot.

EDCS-05501071.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Egnatia Farucia, also called Letina, aged forty-five, pious, is buried here in her own tomb. May the earth be light upon her.
EDCS-05501071.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Egnatia Farucia (Letina), providing her age and a pious wish for her repose.

EDCS-05501072.translation: Ripanus Crispini, son of Lucius Fabius Galba Severus, and Crispinus Ripani, son of Ripanus, gave from their own resources.
EDCS-05501072.summary: This inscription records a dedication or offering made by two individuals, Lucius Fabius Galba Severus Ripanus and his son Crispinus Ripani.

EDCS-05501074.translation: Fabia Rustica, mother, placed [this] for Lucius Postumius Nerva, son of Quintus.
EDCS-05501074.summary: This is a dedication of a monument by a mother, Fabia Rustica, for her son, Lucius Postumius Nerva.

EDCS-05501075.translation: From the will of Saturninus Rufus, his freedwoman and heir, Sergia Salvia, saw to it that the pool and bronze fittings were made.
EDCS-05501075.summary: This inscription records the completion of a pool and bronze fittings, funded by the will of Saturninus Rufus and executed by his freedwoman and heir, Sergia Salvia.

EDCS-05501080.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pompeius Salax, aged thirty, is buried here.
EDCS-05501080.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pompeius Salax, providing his age and indicating his burial location.

EDCS-05501082.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Laberia Felicia, aged 21, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501082.summary: A funerary epitaph for Laberia Felicia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501083.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Messia Fortunata, aged 26, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501083.summary: A funerary epitaph for Messia Fortunata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501084.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Septumina, aged 27, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501084.summary: A funerary epitaph for Septumina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501085.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tatia Lucilla, daughter of Lucius, aged 5, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501085.summary: A funerary epitaph for the child Tatia Lucilla, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501086.translation: To the Emperor Galerius Valerius Maximianus, most noble Caesar, son of the pious and fortunate Augustus.
EDCS-05501086.summary: A dedication to Emperor Galerius Valerius Maximianus, likely from the Tetrarchic period.

EDCS-05501088.summary: A dedication or funerary marker for Marcus Calpurnius.

EDCS-05501091.translation: Urchail, son of Attita, paid for the construction of the porticoes and arches with his own money.
EDCS-05501091.summary: A building inscription recording the financing of porticoes and arches by Urchail Attita, likely from a Roman province.

EDCS-05501092.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Calpurnius Lucius, a decurion, do you weep? Whoever you are, cease your tears at my misfortunes inscribed on marble. I, wretched, lost to an unfair fate, buried here, having lost a pious father devoted to the tomb, lived 26 years, 6 months, and 9 days, leaving behind a miserable father. Now my miserable mother, beating her breasts, and my unhappy sister, both accompanying [me] with griefs, [and] my dear wife, left behind with a small son, a chaste mother, [now] a widow, now survives me against her will. She honored our tomb. May the earth lie lightly upon me, buried deep in the grave. O pious parents, you who hold the realms of the world, here I lie buried and rest peacefully. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501092.summary: A complex funerary epitaph for Marcus Calpurnius Lucius, a decurion, lamenting his death at age 26 and describing his grieving family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501093.translation: To Dasumia, daughter of Lucius, wife of Turpillus: a public eulogy and expenses for funeral and burial place by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501093.summary: A record of public honors and funerary arrangements for Dasumia, wife of Turpillus, decreed by the local council, likely from a Roman municipality.

EDCS-05501096.translation: Attenia Anna, freedwoman of Lucius. Hail.
EDCS-05501096.summary: A simple funerary greeting for Attenia Anna, a freedwoman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501097.summary: This inscription identifies Lucius Auctus as being from Syria.

EDCS-05501098.translation: Calethyche, 22 years old. Stop, whoever you are, I beg you, and linger a little; you will learn that I am buried with a mournful song in the prime of life. Unhappy that I am, I am taken by death twice, at less than twenty years of age. To my ancestral spirits, my altar. And to whomever sees our funeral, may the earth be light upon your ashes. It also provides solace even to the wretched, and to those whom the happy crowd frequently crushes. Unhappy that I am, may it be light for you to see that the earth is light for me.
EDCS-05501098.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calethyche, who died at age 22, lamenting her early death and wishing light earth for those who view her tomb.

EDCS-05501100.summary: This inscription records Quintus Dasumius, possibly as a dedicator or the subject of the monument.

EDCS-05501101.summary: This is a dedication or memorial for Felicula, associated with Lucius Octavius.

EDCS-05501102.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Gelio, son of Gelio, grandson of Gelio, great-grandson. Nonius [dedicated this] with the expense for the funeral and the burial place, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501102.summary: This funerary inscription, commissioned by decree of the decurions, is dedicated to Gelio and his ancestors by Nonius, commemorating the funeral and burial expenses.

EDCS-05501103.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Maria, daughter of Quintus, wife of Marcus Aurelius Nympho. She lived 45 years. To Maria Tertulla Rectina and Marcus Aurelius Nympho, her sons, to their most dutiful mother, buried here. I beg you, passerby, say: may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-05501103.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Maria by her sons, listing her parentage, husband, and lifespan, and includes a plea for passersby to wish her light earth.

EDCS-05501104.translation: Caius Mummius Tusculanus is buried here, and Atennia Restituta is buried here.
EDCS-05501104.summary: This is a simple burial inscription marking the resting place of Caius Mummius Tusculanus and Atennia Restituta.

EDCS-05501105.translation: Aulus Publicius Turpio is buried here. May the earth be light for you. SVP.
EDCS-05501105.summary: This inscription marks the burial of Aulus Publicius Turpio and includes the common wish for light earth, followed by an abbreviation.

EDCS-05501108.translation: Son of Lucius, son of Facilis.
EDCS-05501108.summary: This inscription records a patron-client or familial relationship, indicating a son of Lucius and grandson of Facilis.

EDCS-05501112.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. Lucius Caelius Saturninus, freedman of Lucius Caelius Parthenopaeus, gave this gift during his tenure as a magistrate of the four priests, with performances of stage plays.
EDCS-05501112.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Caelius Saturninus, a freedman, to Liber Pater during his priesthood, accompanied by theatrical performances.

EDCS-05501113.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater Augustus; Aulus Cumelius Augustalis, augur of the municipality, decurion, on the day before the Kalends.
EDCS-05501113.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Liber Pater Augustus by Aulus Cumelius Augustalis, an augur and decurion, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05501114.translation: To Mascel, Marcianus, Clio, Euterpe, Terpsichore, Erato, Polyhymnia, Calliope, Urania, Melpomene, Thalia.
EDCS-05501114.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or listing of names, possibly related to the Muses, with Mascel Marcianus as the primary individual, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501115.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deities Apro and Venus, possibly by Tulia, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501116.translation: Gaius Flavius Firmus, having undertaken a vow, willingly paid it for Flavius Successus.
EDCS-05501116.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Gaius Flavius Firmus on behalf of Flavius Successus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501121.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Caro Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul; the state of Italicensium is devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-05501121.summary: This inscription is a formal dedication to Emperor Carus by the community of Italicensium, praising his titles and divine authority, dated to 282-283 CE.

EDCS-05501130.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits; of Lucius Valerius Nepos, a soldier of the VII Gemina legion, with seven years of service, 30 years old; may the earth lie lightly on you, who reads this.
EDCS-05501130.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Valerius Nepos, a soldier of the VII Gemina legion, who died at age 30, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501133.translation: To Lucius Raius, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, Plebeius, as duovir, by the will of his father Plebeius.
EDCS-05501133.summary: This inscription records a dedication or honorific notice for Lucius Raius, who served as duovir, by the will of his father Plebeius, from the Roman municipal context.

EDCS-05501134.translation: The most distinguished council of Italicensium decreed that the expenses for the funeral, a burial place, and the setting up of a statue for Aelia Quinti, daughter of Quintus, Licinia Valeriana, should be borne by the community; Aelius Priscus, her father, and Laberius Firmanus, her husband, having received the honor, erected them with their own money.
EDCS-05501134.summary: This inscription details a decree by the town council of Italicensium to provide funeral expenses and a statue for Aelia Licinia Valeriana, with her father and husband contributing to the cost, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501135.translation: Marcus Coelius Alexander donated a marble tablet as a gift to the guild of Augustan locksmiths.
EDCS-05501135.summary: This inscription records a donation of a marble tablet by Marcus Coelius Alexander to the guild of locksmiths, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501137.translation: Caius Marcius Apilus, master of the Lares Augusti and the Genius of Emperor Augustus, is buried here; 20 feet in front, 20 feet in the ground.
EDCS-05501137.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Marcius Apilus, who held religious offices related to the imperial cult, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501139.translation: Gabinius Mucro, curator of the state of the colony of Colonia Ulpia Italicensium, erected this.
EDCS-05501139.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Gabinius Mucro, an official of Colonia Ulpia Italicensium.

EDCS-05501141.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. For Marcus Acena Rufus, dearest infant, who lived 3 months and 5 days. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501141.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Acena Rufus, an infant who died at 3 months and 5 days old.

EDCS-05501142.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Titus Aelius Assatus, who lived about 25 years, pious among his own. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501142.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Aelius Assatus, who died at approximately 25 years of age.

EDCS-05501144.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Ammia Veneria, aged 60 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501144.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ammia Veneria, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-05501145.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Annius K us, pious among his own, lived [for some number of] years.
EDCS-05501145.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Annius K us, recording his piety and lifespan.

EDCS-05501147.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Claudia Maritima, who lived 7 months and 12 days. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501147.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudia Maritima, who died at 7 months and 12 days old.

EDCS-05501150.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. For Fabio Superatus, who lived 80 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501150.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fabio Superatus, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-05501151.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Titus Flavius Trophimus, who lived 9 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501151.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Trophimus, who died at the age of 9.

EDCS-05501152.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Sextus Fuficius Tertullinus, aged 36 years and 47 days. Claudia Tertulla for her son. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501152.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Claudia Tertulla for her son, Sextus Fuficius Tertullinus, who died at 36 years and 47 days old.

EDCS-05501154.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Heria Calpurnia, who lived 5 years, 5 months, and 19 days. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501154.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Heria Calpurnia, who died at 5 years, 5 months, and 19 days old.

EDCS-05501155.translation: Quintus Herius, freedman of Aulus, greetings.
EDCS-05501155.summary: This is a simple greeting from a freedman named Quintus Herius.

EDCS-05501156.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Valentina lived X months and Y years, dutiful in her piety.
EDCS-05501156.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Valentina, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-05501158.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Numisius Fortunatus lived more or less XXV years, dutiful to his family; Serapia, his freedwoman, to her dearest husband.
EDCS-05501158.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Numisius Fortunatus and is dedicated by his freedwoman, Serapia, to her husband.

EDCS-05501159.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Silvana lived XV years, pure and dutiful in her family; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501159.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Silvana, recording her age and wishing her peace.

EDCS-05501160.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Specla, a servant, XXXXI years old, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501160.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Specla, a servant, and wishes her peace.

EDCS-05501161.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Ulpius Heuretus, a most sweet infant, who lived V years and II months. Vibius Zoticus and Valeria Sponde, parents, to their most beloved son.
EDCS-05501161.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young child, Marcus Ulpius Heuretus, and is dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-05501162.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Ulpius Rusticus, freedman of Lucius, LXX years old, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501162.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Ulpius Rusticus, a freedman, and wishes him peace.

EDCS-05501164.summary: This is a fragment likely from a larger inscription, identifying someone as the son of Quintus.

EDCS-05501167.translation: Lucius Iulius dedicated a bronze statue of the Genius of Baetis, fulfilling a vow made for the public good of the college of foreigners, using his annual salary of LXII denarii, along with its base, as a gift.
EDCS-05501167.summary: This inscription records a dedication of a statue by Lucius Iulius to the Genius of Baetis for the benefit of a college of foreigners, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05501168.translation: Loricius Hilus willingly placed this for Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator and the Nymphs.
EDCS-05501168.summary: This is a votive inscription by Loricius Hilus dedicating offerings to Jupiter and the Nymphs.

EDCS-05501169.translation: Sacred to the Emperor Augustus, Lucius Licinius Adamas, freedman of Faustus, a priest of the imperial cult, gave this gift.
EDCS-05501169.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Augustus by Lucius Licinius Adamas, a priest of the imperial cult, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05501170.translation: Sacred to Empress Vesta, Marcus Iunius Quirina, a man from Segovia, ordered this to be made by his will, and the council of the Naevensians granted him a place for it by decree.
EDCS-05501170.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Vesta by Marcus Iunius Quirina, with the local council providing the space, likely from the Roman province of Hispania.

EDCS-05501172.translation: The bargemen who trade in the Tiber district, out of their own funds, gave this gift to the Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Hadrian, grandson of the divine Trajan the Parthian, great-grandson of the divine Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus, high priest, in his ninth year of tribunician power, consul for the second time, emperor for the second time, father of his country.
EDCS-05501172.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Antoninus Pius by the bargemen of Rome, likely dating to his second consulship and ninth tribunician power year (145-146 CE).

EDCS-05501173.translation: The bargemen who trade in the Tiber district, out of their own funds, gave this gift to Marcus Aurelius Verus Caesar, son of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of his country, consul for the second time.
EDCS-05501173.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Marcus Aurelius as Caesar by the bargemen of Rome, likely dating to his second consulship (145 CE).

EDCS-05501174.translation: Ianus Lycomedes, procurator of the Emperors, placed and dedicated this to the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, son of the divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, in his eighth year of tribunician power, consul for the second time, proconsul.
EDCS-05501174.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by a procurator to Emperor Caracalla, likely dating to his eighth tribunician power year and second consulship (205 CE).

EDCS-05501175.translation: The municipality of Hispalis, devoted to the divine majesty and authority of our lord Flavius Valerius Constantius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-05501175.summary: This inscription records the devotion of the city of Hispalis to the Caesar Constantius, likely dating to the Tetrarchy period (late 3rd to early 4th century CE).

EDCS-05501176.translation: To Decimus Cutilius Balbinus, Marcus Cornelius Potitus, Lucius Attius Iunianus Romulus, the four men in charge of the roads, the most dutiful son, from Balbinus father and Prisca mother.
EDCS-05501176.summary: This is a funerary dedication from parents to their son, who held the office of aedile and was one of the four men responsible for roads, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05501177.translation: To Cutius Romulus, the most dutiful son, from Balbinus father and Prisca mother.
EDCS-05501177.summary: This is a funerary dedication from parents to their son, Cutius Romulus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05501179.translation: To Gaius Aelius, son of Gaius, grandson of Gaius, from Quirinus, patron of all wagoners, the wagoners of Cananenses, Oducienses, and Naevenses.
EDCS-05501179.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Gaius Aelius Avitus, patron of wagoners, by various groups of wagoners, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-05501180.translation: To Lucius Blatius, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, from the colonists and inhabitants, as tribune of the soldiers of the V and X Gemina legions, and as aedile, and as duovir.
EDCS-05501180.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Lucius Blatius by the colonists and inhabitants, honoring his military and civil service, likely from a Roman colony.

EDCS-05501181.translation: To Gaius Caecilius Virgillianus, son of Gaius Caecilius Silvanus, an outstanding man, procurator of the Emperor on the river of the province of Baetica.
EDCS-05501181.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Caecilius Virgillianus, a procurator of Baetica, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05501182.translation: To Marcus Calpurnius Seneca Sentinatianus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, son of Seneca Fabio Turpioni, prefect of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, prefect of the Praetorian fleet at Ravenna, procurator of the province of Lusitania and Vettonia, former first spear of the I Adiutrix legion. The town council of the colony Romula, showing him honour, remitted the expense.
EDCS-05501182.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Marcus Calpurnius Seneca Sentinatianus, a prominent military and administrative official, honoring him with a remission of expenses by the town council of Colonia Romula, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05501185.translation: To Lucius Vibius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, son of Aurelius, prefect of the first cohort of the Ausetani, a most dutiful brother, by the most distinguished council of Italica.
EDCS-05501185.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Lucius Vibius Rufus, a cohort prefect, by the council of Italica, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05501186.translation: To Marcus Messius Rusticus Aemilius Verus Aelius Romulus Priscianus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, son of Rusticus.
EDCS-05501186.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Messius Rusticus Aemilius Verus Aelius Romulus Priscianus, whose full name suggests senatorial or equestrian rank, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05501187.translation: The ferrymen of Romula, stationed here, erected this at their own expense to Lucius Castricius Honoratus, son of Quintus, a good man, former first spear, for his integrity and singular justice.
EDCS-05501187.summary: This is a dedication by ferrymen to Lucius Castricius Honoratus for his justice, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05501188.translation: To Marcus Helvius Agrippa, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, son of Sergia [?], of the Sergia tribe. The most distinguished council of the Romulans decreed for him the expenses for his funeral, a statue, and a burial place. Marcus Helvius Agrippa, his father, showing honour, remitted the expense.
EDCS-05501188.summary: This is a decree by the council of Romula for the funeral and burial of Marcus Helvius Agrippa, with his father remitting the costs, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05501189.translation: To Quintus Junius Venustus, of the Quirina tribe. The council of Romula decreed for him a burial place, funeral expenses, a public eulogy, and the ornaments of the decurionate. Quintus Junius, his father, accepting the honour, remitted the expense.
EDCS-05501189.summary: This inscription records the council of Romula granting honors, including burial and public recognition, to Quintus Junius Venustus, with his father remitting costs, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05501190.translation: To Lucius, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501190.summary: This is a public dedication by decree of the decurions to Lucius, son of Marcus, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05501191.translation: To Quintus Pomponius Sabinianus, former aedile, duumvir of the colony Romula, pontiff, augur, by decree of the decurions of the colony Romula. Quintus Pomponius Clodianus, his father, and Claudia Sabina, daughter of Tiberius, his mother, accepting the honour, remitted the expense. To Quintus Pomponius Clodianus Iuventinus, by decree of the decurions of the colony Romula. Quintus Pomponius Clodianus, his grandfather, and Claudia Sabina, daughter of Tiberius, his grandmother, accepting the honour, remitted the expense.
EDCS-05501191.summary: This is a complex honorific inscription recording decrees by the decurions of Colonia Romula for two generations of Quintus Pomponius, with parents and grandparents remitting expenses, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05501192.translation: Publius Valerius Gallus, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe. The council of decurions decreed for him a burial place at public expense, amounting to 500 sesterces.
EDCS-05501192.summary: This is a decree by the decurions of a colony (likely Romula) granting a burial plot and funeral expenses to Publius Valerius Gallus, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05501193.translation: To Lucio Vivio Flavino, son of Marcus, a gladiator of the Seventh Thracian cohort, erected this in the games of the people of Hispalis.
EDCS-05501193.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument erected by a gladiator for a fellow gladiator, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05501198.translation: Quintus Ennius, freedman of the Ennii Albanians and Ennii, and Herma, as Augustales, gave this gift from their own money.
EDCS-05501198.summary: This inscription records a dedication by two freedmen who held the prestigious position of Augustales, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05501199.translation: Quintus Fabius Firmanus, for the office of VIvir.
EDCS-05501199.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Quintus Fabius Firmanus upon holding the office of a municipal magistrate (VIvir), likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05501200.translation: Pius, steward of our Emperor, in charge of the grain supply from the private estates, made this for his most holy and chaste wife, Italia.
EDCS-05501200.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Pius, an imperial official managing the grain supply, for his wife Italia, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05501201.translation: To the Divine Shades. Primitius, Clemens, Parthenius, Pamphilus, and Fortunatus made this for their deserving colleague Felix, steward of the patrimonial treasury, and his substitute; he is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05501201.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by colleagues for Felix, an official managing imperial finances, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05501203.translation: Quintus Fabius Fabianus, son of Quintus, of the Fabian tribe, from Ilurco, also a Patrician, aged 43, pious in his own affairs, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05501203.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintus Fabius Fabianus, a local man identified with both his tribal and a more prestigious classification, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05501204.translation: Lucius Licinius Cogitans, son of Lucius, of the Patrician tribe, aged 70, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you. Licinia Tyche, freedwoman of Lucius, aged 73.
EDCS-05501204.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Lucius Licinius Cogitans and Licinia Tyche, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05501205.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Sergia Salvia Salpensana, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05501205.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Sergia Salvia Salpensana, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05501206.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Publius Aelius Aemilianus, aged 29 years and 31 days, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05501206.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Publius Aelius Aemilianus, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05501208.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Aemilia Anulla, daughter of Lucius, after death. Her mother Aelia Maura made this.
EDCS-05501208.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by a mother, Aelia Maura, for her deceased daughter Aemilia Anulla, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05501209.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aemilia Honesta, aged 40, pious towards her family, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Quintus Clodius Primigenius made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-05501209.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Honesta, set up by her husband Quintus Clodius Primigenius.

EDCS-05501211.translation: For Aemilia, daughter of Marcus. Quintus Fabius [made this] for his wife and for Lucius Fabius, son of Quintus.
EDCS-05501211.summary: This funerary monument is dedicated to Aemilia and Lucius Fabius by Quintus Fabius.

EDCS-05501212.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Agria Matuna is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501212.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Agria Matuna.

EDCS-05501213.translation: Quintus Alfius Iulianus is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. BERVALIAM
EDCS-05501213.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Alfius Iulianus.

EDCS-05501214.translation: Antonia Lais, aged 1 year and 7 months. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501214.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Lais.

EDCS-05501215.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Antonia Romula lived 25 years and 30 days. Lucius Antonius Theophilus [made this] for his dearest wife.
EDCS-05501215.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Romula, set up by her husband Lucius Antonius Theophilus.

EDCS-05501216.translation: Gaius Atenius Acutus, in accordance with his will, for himself and for Optata and Tertia his natural daughter.
EDCS-05501216.summary: This inscription records the provisions made in the will of Gaius Atenius Acutus for himself and his family.

EDCS-05501217.translation: Sextus Avienus, son of Laberia, 12 years old.
EDCS-05501217.summary: This inscription records the death of Sextus Avienus, son of Laberia.

EDCS-05501218.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Venustus V Fabul made this for his most pious nephew, Lucius Avilius Successus, who lived 22 years.
EDCS-05501218.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Avilius Successus, set up by his uncle Caius Venustus V Fabul.

EDCS-05501219.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aurelius Hermes, pious father, pious towards his own, lived 85 years, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501219.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Hermes, described as a pious father.

EDCS-05501220.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aurelius, son of Lucius, lived 22 years, 5 months, and 10 days, and made this.
EDCS-05501220.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Marcus Aurelius, son of Lucius, who lived 22 years, 5 months, and 10 days.

EDCS-05501221.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Canullius Fusichio, aged 23 and a half, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501221.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Quintus Canullius Fusichio, who died at the age of 23 and a half.

EDCS-05501222.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. C. Zosa lived one year and 40 days. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501222.summary: This tombstone records the death of C. Zosa, who lived for one year and 40 days.

EDCS-05501223.translation: Caius Rufus, son of Caius, lived 55 days and 5 hours. I ask you passing by to say: 'He lies here'.
EDCS-05501223.summary: This tombstone records the short life of Caius Rufus, son of Caius, who lived 55 days and 5 hours.

EDCS-05501224.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Eutychia, 1 year and 3 months old, lies here.
EDCS-05501224.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Eutychia, who died at the age of 1 year and 3 months.

EDCS-05501225.translation: Salvia, freedwoman of Didia Titius, a mother. In my sorrow, in this tomb, I inscribed my own name while alive. This is my home with my family.
EDCS-05501225.summary: This is a tomb inscription by Salvia, freedwoman of Didia Titius, who prepared her own burial place.

EDCS-05501226.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fabia Sympherusa, wife of Germana Badiola, aged 31. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501226.summary: This tombstone records the death of Fabia Sympherusa, wife of Germana Badiola, at the age of 31.

EDCS-05501227.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Fabius Fortunatus, aged 40, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501227.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Marcus Fabius Fortunatus, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-05501228.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Fabius Melippus lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501228.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Quintus Fabius Melippus.

EDCS-05501229.translation: Fabricius Severus, aged 65, lies here. Valeria Severina, his wife, aged 50, lies here. Fabricius Festus, their son, aged 50, lies here.
EDCS-05501229.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Fabricius Severus, his wife Valeria Severina, and their son Fabricius Festus, with their respective ages.

EDCS-05501230.translation: Firmia Apate, freedwoman of Aulus, and Aulus Firmius Chius, freedman of Aulus, and Mela, freedman of Nitoris, (set this up).
EDCS-05501230.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary dedication by three freedmen, possibly setting up a tomb for themselves or a patron, with a date not precisely inferable but likely Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-05501231.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Facilix lived for [X] years and 6 months; here she lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501231.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Facilix, recording her lifespan and expressing a wish for peace in the afterlife, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-05501232.translation: Helena lies here, having lived 68 years. I ask you, passerby, as you go past, say 'May the earth lie lightly upon you'.
EDCS-05501232.summary: This epitaph for Helena records her age at death and includes a direct appeal to passersby to offer a prayer for her repose, dating to the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-05501233.translation: Iunia Elpis, dear to her family, aged 9 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501233.summary: This tombstone commemorates the young Iunia Elpis, noting her age and family affection, and is likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-05501235.translation: Decumus Lyricus lived 46 years, dutiful to his family and deserving; here he lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501235.summary: This funerary inscription honors Decumus Lyricus, highlighting his age and his devotion to his family, dating to the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-05501236.translation: To the departed spirits. Magnus, aged 66 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501236.summary: A simple funerary dedication for Magnus, stating his age and a wish for his peaceful rest, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-05501237.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marius Fortunatus, aged 31 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501237.summary: This epitaph records the death of Marius Fortunatus at the age of 31, with the standard wish for repose, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-05501238.translation: Nome lived 1 year and 8 months and 12 days; here she lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Nome was the name given at birth; the name Cusuccia stuck. I signify both names in this inscription. I lived briefly and was sweet to my parent while I lived; I am covered by this tomb. I have paid my dues. Whoever reads this inscription, understand how short my life was. I ask now that you say 'May the earth lie lightly upon you'.
EDCS-05501238.summary: This poignant epitaph for a very young child named Nome (also Cusuccia) details her short life and expresses her parents' grief and love, likely dating to the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-05501239.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pyramis, a dutiful servant, aged 45 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501239.summary: This funerary inscription is for Pyramis, a servant who lived to 45, and includes a standard wish for her peace, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-05501240.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Samnia Germana, aged 35 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501240.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Samnia Germana at the age of 35, with a wish for her soul's rest, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-05501241.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sergius Roe, aged 28, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501241.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sergius Roe, aged 28, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501242.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Servilia Melie, aged 29, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501242.summary: A funerary epitaph for Servilia Melie, aged 29, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501244.summary: A funerary epitaph possibly identifying a woman named Stlaccia, daughter of Lucius, with the cognomen Triaria, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501245.translation: Sollentina, dear to all her family, aged 4, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501245.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sollentina, aged 4, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501246.translation: Tatia, daughter of Optatus, aged 28, dear to her family, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501246.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tatia, daughter of Optatus, aged 28, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501247.summary: An epitaph possibly for Lucius Terentius Iucundus, aged 27, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501248.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Testia lived 13 years.
EDCS-05501248.summary: A funerary epitaph for Testia, who lived 13 years, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501250.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerius Laurentinus, aged 35, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Valeria Lychnis, his mother, made this.
EDCS-05501250.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Laurentinus, aged 35, erected by his mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501251.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Valerius Faustus, freedman of Lucius Valerius, for himself and for his wife Domitia Felicula, freedwoman of Sextus, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501251.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Faustus and his wife Domitia Felicula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501252.translation: Lucius Valerius Hyginus, freedman of Marcellus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05501252.summary: An inscription recording a vow fulfilled by Lucius Valerius Hyginus, a freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501255.translation: The inscription is for Publius Volumnius Stabilio, son of Aulus.
EDCS-05501255.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Publius Volumnius Stabilio, son of Aulus.

EDCS-05501256.summary: A short funerary dedication to the Divine Shades.

EDCS-05501259.translation: To Lucius Caelsius Pollio, son of Lucius, who served twice as aedile and twice as duumvir, having performed his duties well and for the public good, by his fellow townsmen.
EDCS-05501259.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Caelius Pollio for his service as aedile and duumvir, erected by his fellow townsmen, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05501260.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fortunata, a servant of Herus, aged 75, rests here, missed by her family. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501260.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fortunata, a freedwoman aged 75, set up by her loved ones, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501261.translation: Cornelia Fabulla, daughter of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, to her most devoted and excellent father, Quintus Cornelius Senex, son of Quintus, former duumvir. Having given a feast, she dedicated it.
EDCS-05501261.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Cornelia Fabulla to her father, Quintus Cornelius Senex, a former duumvir, who also provided a feast, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501263.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Aelius Caecilius, son of Gaius Aelius, aged 10, rests here.
EDCS-05501263.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Aelius Caecilius, a boy aged 10, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501264.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Silius, son of Lucius, [name missing/illegible].
EDCS-05501264.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication to the Divine Shades for Lucius Silius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-05501267.translation: Marcus Iunius Brutus Lucurgentinus, aged 23, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Iulia Paterna, his wife, made this at her own expense.
EDCS-05501267.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Iunius Brutus Lucurgentinus, aged 23, erected by his wife Iulia Paterna, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501268.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Fabius Cornelianus, aged 26, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501268.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Fabius Cornelianus, aged 26, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501270.translation: To Juno Regina. Marcus Calpurnius Seneca, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, also known as Fabius Turpio Sentinatianus, former centurion of the first cohort of the legion I Adiutrix, procurator of the provinces of Lusitania and Vettonia, prefect of the praetorian fleet at Ravenna, gave this as a gift from one hundred pounds of silver. Suconia Rustica, daughter of Caius, his wife, dedicated it, having provided a feast for both sexes.
EDCS-05501270.summary: A monumental dedication to Juno Regina by Marcus Calpurnius Seneca, a high-ranking military and administrative official, funded by a significant donation of silver and accompanied by a feast, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05501272.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Firmus lived 39 years, 2 months, 2 days. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501272.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Firmus, who lived 39 years, 2 months, and 2 days.

EDCS-05501273.translation: To Quarto, an experienced quaestor and praetor of the legion, deservedly.
EDCS-05501273.summary: This inscription honors Quarto, an experienced quaestor and praetor of a legion, likely for his merits.

EDCS-05501274.translation: To Gaius Lucius, from the RABN region of the Nisienses, a municipal official. Because of his merits in public affairs, a statue was erected for him by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501274.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue by the decurions in honor of Gaius Lucius, a municipal official, for his public service.

EDCS-05501275.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Herennia Avita is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501275.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the burial of Herennia Avita.

EDCS-05501276.translation: Elapusa, 23 years old, is buried here. I ask you passing by to say: May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501276.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Elapusa, who died at the age of 23, with a request to passers-by to wish her peace.

EDCS-05501277.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Chruse, 20 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501277.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the burial of Chruse, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-05501278.translation: To Gaius Sabius, son of Gaius.
EDCS-05501278.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription for Gaius Sabius, son of Gaius.

EDCS-05501279.translation: Dulcinia Messia, by a more unfavorable fate and destiny, has been taken from her master. I commend her to you. I ask that her birthday, which is the sixth day before the Kalends of August, be celebrated by you for twenty years with great zeal, despite the unfavorable circumstances. Because of this, every year as long as I live, I will divide among our municipality of Siarenses, specifically to the decurions present, 10 denarii each, to the seviri 10 denarii each, to the plebs of both sexes and the inhabitants, 10 denarii each; these amounts I will provide as long as I live.
EDCS-05501279.summary: This inscription is a funerary endowment from Dulcinia Messia, establishing an annual distribution of money to the decurions, seviri, and plebs of Siarenses to celebrate her birthday for twenty years.

EDCS-05501280.translation: To Maecilia Herenniana, daughter of Publius, wife most beloved to Titus Aelius Clodianus. Placed at his own expense in an accepted location by the most distinguished order of Siarenses.
EDCS-05501280.summary: This inscription records a tomb monument erected by Titus Aelius Clodianus for his wife Maecilia Herenniana, paid for by him and facilitated by the distinguished order of Siarenses.

EDCS-05501281.translation: To Maecilia Herenniana, daughter of Publius, wife most beloved to Titus Aelius Clodianus. Placed at his own expense by the most distinguished order of Siarenses.
EDCS-05501281.summary: This inscription records a tomb monument erected by Titus Aelius Clodianus for his wife Maecilia Herenniana, paid for by him and facilitated by the distinguished order of Siarenses.

EDCS-05501282.translation: To Fortuna Augusta, Lucia Celerina, daughter of Lucius, having twice before feasted publicly at her own expense, gave this gift for the honor of the priesthood, a feast costing 100 pounds of silver.
EDCS-05501282.summary: A woman named Lucia Celerina dedicated a gift to Fortuna Augusta, including a public feast, likely to celebrate her priestly office.

EDCS-05501286.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan the Parthian, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 10th time, Imperator for the second time, Consul for the fourth time, Father of the Fatherland, Marcus Cutius Priscus Messius Rusticus Aemilius Papus Arrius Proculus Iulius Celsus, his consul and governor of the province of Dalmatia, dedicated this to him, the best and dearest Prince, and to himself.
EDCS-05501286.summary: An inscription dedicating honors to Emperor Antoninus Pius by Marcus Cutius Priscus, the governor of Dalmatia, who served during Antoninus Pius' reign.

EDCS-05501287.translation: If a candidate is worthy of praise, it is fitting to remind him of the merits shown in military service. Legio XII’s record of this duty is particularly noteworthy and offers a prime example for his commendation, especially since he has been approved by his uncle, Aemilius Papus, a most distinguished and severe man, for his constant gravity and care for his own people, who seeks proper institutions. His earnest and solicitous support not only encourages us to seek favor from such a man but also gives us the best hope for the candidate, especially since he is content with the support of the municipals and the exceptional assistance of distinguished men for his merits.
EDCS-05501287.summary: This inscription seems to be a recommendation or endorsement for a candidate, highlighting his military service and the support of his uncle, Aemilius Papus, suggesting it relates to a political or social advancement within a municipality.

EDCS-05501292.translation: Lucius Marcius, son of Lucius, grandson of Lucius, great-grandson of Lucius, great-great-grandson of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, Saturninus, aged 12 years and 5 months, lies here. For him, the town council of the Flavian Salpensani municipality decreed a laudation, a burial place, funeral expenses, a bronze shield, a standing statue, and the ornaments of the decurionate; and all the honors given by the people and inhabitants were also granted to him. Lucius Marcius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, his father, received the honor and remitted the expenses.
EDCS-05501292.summary: This inscription records the posthumous honors and funeral expenses decreed by the municipality of Salpensans to Lucius Marcius Saturninus, a young man, with his father opting to remit the costs.

EDCS-05501294.translation: Aemilia, aged 16, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501294.summary: A brief epitaph for a 16-year-old girl named Aemilia.

EDCS-05501295.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Aemilia Anniola, aged 14 years, 11 months, and 13 days, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501295.summary: An epitaph for Aemilia Anniola, who died at the age of 14.

EDCS-05501296.summary: A very brief inscription likely serving as a tomb marker or identification, naming Quintus Annius, grandson of Galeria.

EDCS-05501297.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Prima, aged 22 years, 1 month, and 6 days, lies here. I ask you, passerby, as you go, to say, 'May the earth lie lightly upon you.' To her husband, Gaius Aurelius Ampliatus, well-deserving.
EDCS-05501297.summary: An epitaph for Flavia Prima, aged 22, set up by her deserving husband, Gaius Aurelius Ampliatus.

EDCS-05501298.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Fortuna lived 18 years and 16 days. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501298.summary: An epitaph for a woman named Fortuna, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-05501299.translation: Pylades, son of Novatus, lies here. This altar to youth, erected by Prima, indicates Pylades here. It consecrated a worthy example of friendship and also of a brotherhood for the future indication of his name and no less of his duty. Say, you who read, to the buried one according to the usual rite for his merits: Pylades, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501299.summary: An epitaph for Pylades, set up by Prima, commemorating his friendship and brotherhood, and describing him as a worthy example.

EDCS-05501300.translation: It was decided by the Conobariensis people that a statue be erected to Lucius Acilius Albanus, son of Quirina; Aemilia Lucilla, his mother, remitted the expense.
EDCS-05501300.summary: This inscription records a posthumous statue erected to Lucius Acilius Albanus by the people of Conobariensis, paid for by his mother.

EDCS-05501301.translation: To Lucius Cornelius Saturninus, may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05501301.summary: This is a simple tombstone inscription for Lucius Cornelius Saturninus, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05501302.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Herennia Clodiana lived 52 years and 38 days; pious to her family, she is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05501302.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Herennia Clodiana, providing her age at death and a wish for her repose.

EDCS-05501304.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Licinius Nympheros, about 50 years old, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05501304.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Licinius Nympheros, including his approximate age and a common funerary wish.

EDCS-05501305.translation: Sempronia, freedwoman of Gaius and Decimus, 27 years old, is buried here. I ask you, passing by, to say: May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05501305.summary: This inscription records the burial of Sempronia, a freedwoman of 27, who requests passers-by to offer a wish for her peace.

EDCS-05501306.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely funerary, identifying a woman named Asrauvenua as a mother, possibly a freedwoman.

EDCS-05501307.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus. Lucius Catinius Martialis, freedman of Lucius, dedicated this gift in honor of his holding the office of IIII vir for the fourth time.
EDCS-05501307.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars Augustus by Lucius Catinius Martialis, a freedman, to honor his repeated holding of a specific magistracy.

EDCS-05501308.translation: During the reign of Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 8th time, imperator for the 16th time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, censor. Terpulia, daughter of Saunus, according to the will of her husband Albanius Sunna, son of [---].
EDCS-05501308.summary: This inscription records a funerary dedication made by Terpulia, daughter of Saunus, in accordance with her husband Albanius Sunna's will, during the reign of Emperor Claudius.

EDCS-05501309.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Hercules Augustus.

EDCS-05501312.translation: To Lucius Titius, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, from Verna. By decree of the decurions, for the reason of honor and virtue, from donated money, [he served as] aedile and II vir.
EDCS-05501312.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Titius for his service as aedile and II vir, awarded by the decurions due to his merit and funded by public money.

EDCS-05501313.translation: Lucius Alpidius, son of Lucius, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501313.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Lucius Alpidius, with a common epitaph wishing him a light rest.

EDCS-05501314.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Cornelia Tryphaena, dear to her family, buried here at the age of 50; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501314.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the Manes by Cornelia Tryphaena, who records her age and burial place.

EDCS-05501315.translation: To Lucius Vecilius, son of Caius, by Sergia in her will.
EDCS-05501315.summary: This inscription records a funerary dedication to Lucius Vecilius, established through a will.

EDCS-05501318.translation: Valerius Fortunatus gladly paid his vow, as deserved.
EDCS-05501318.summary: This inscription records Valerius Fortunatus fulfilling a vow willingly and appropriately.

EDCS-05501319.translation: To Marcus Antonius, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, duovir of the municipium of Augusta Gaditana, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501319.summary: This is a public dedication honoring Marcus Antonius, who served as duovir in Augusta Gaditana, passed by the town council.

EDCS-05501320.translation: Marcus Acilius Silo, son of Manius, of the Galeria tribe, duovir and prefect of a cohort, lies here.
EDCS-05501320.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Marcus Acilius Silo, who held both civil and military offices.

EDCS-05501321.translation: To Quintus Fabius, son of Cnaeus, of the Galeria tribe, by the fellow citizens of Caesarina, as IVvir.
EDCS-05501321.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Quintus Fabius by the citizens of Caesarina, noting his role as a IVvir.

EDCS-05501322.summary: This is a simple tombstone recording the burial of Lucius Annius Charito.

EDCS-05501323.translation: To Cassia Hellas, from Marcus Cassius Pyrrhus, for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-05501323.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Cassia Hellas by her son, Marcus Cassius Pyrrhus.

EDCS-05501324.translation: To Marcus Cassius Sabinus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, from his brother Fabius Capito.
EDCS-05501324.summary: This tombstone records a dedication to Marcus Cassius Sabinus by his brother, Fabius Capito.

EDCS-05501325.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Tiberius Claudius Cilus, son of Quintus, aged 60.
