EDCS-05501325.summary: A funerary inscription for Tiberius Claudius Cilus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501327.translation: Publius Cornelius Iulius, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-05501327.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely recording a name and tribal affiliation, possibly for a tombstone or a public record, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501330.translation: To Lucius Fabio Capito, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, his best friend, Lucius Aelius Rocianus (dedicated this).
EDCS-05501330.summary: A dedication by Lucius Aelius Rocianus to his friend Lucius Fabio Capito, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501331.translation: Flavia Quintilla, aged 60, is laid here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-05501331.summary: A funerary inscription for Flavia Quintilla, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501332.translation: Iusta, freedwoman of Flavia, is laid here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-05501332.summary: A funerary inscription for Iusta, a freedwoman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501333.translation: Licinia Gallula, freedwoman of Marcus, aged 50. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-05501333.summary: A funerary inscription for Licinia Gallula, a freedwoman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501334.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Maurus, aged 13, dutiful to his family, is laid here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-05501334.summary: A funerary inscription for a young boy named Maurus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501335.summary: A very brief funerary inscription for Sempherusa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501336.translation: The public order of Obens dedicated a feast given, with Lucius Cornelius Herennius Rusticus and Marcus Sentius Restitutus, the duovirs, overseeing.
EDCS-05501336.summary: A dedication by the local government of Obens for a public feast, overseen by two magistrates, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501338.translation: Lucius Cornelius Herennius Rusticus placed this according to the will of Lucius Herennius Herennianus, his grandson. On the Nones of March, in the consulship of Sextus Quintilius Condianus and Sextus Quintilius Maximus.
EDCS-05501338.summary: A posthumous dedication erected by a grandson for Lucius Herennius Herennianus, with specific dating information provided by the consuls, dating to 209 CE.

EDCS-05501339.translation: Lucius Cornelius Herennius Rusticus set up this tomb in accordance with the will of his grandfather, Lucius Herennius Herennianus.
EDCS-05501339.summary: A funerary monument erected by Lucius Cornelius Herennius Rusticus for his grandfather, Lucius Herennius Herennianus, as stipulated in the grandfather's will.

EDCS-05501341.translation: Auctinus Clementis set this up for himself and his family, and for his mother Brita, aged 60, buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501341.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Auctinus Clementis for himself, his family, and his mother Brita, who is buried here.

EDCS-05501342.translation: The Republic of the Ocuritani, by decree of the council of decurions, gave this gift to the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, who is pontifex maximus, has held tribunician power 5 times, consul 3 times, and is father of the country.
EDCS-05501342.summary: A dedication by the town of Ocurra to Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely on the occasion of his consulship or tribunician power, as decreed by the local council.

EDCS-05501343.translation: The Republic of the Ocuritani, by decree of the council of decurions, gave this gift to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix Germanicus Sarmaticus, who is pontifex maximus, has held tribunician power 4 times, imperatorial acclamations 10 times, consul 5 times, and is father of the country.
EDCS-05501343.summary: A dedication by the town of Ocurra to Emperor Commodus, as decreed by the local council, likely on the occasion of his consulship or tribunician power.

EDCS-05501344.translation: Postumius Optatus, her father, established this for his daughter Postumia Honorata, daughter of Caius, from Barbesula, perpetual priestess of the deified Empresses, for her perpetual honor and that of their municipality, by a decree of the decurions, and likewise for a feast provided by a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501344.summary: A monument erected by Postumius Optatus in honor of his daughter Postumia Honorata, a priestess, for her merits and the municipality's honor, with provisions for a feast.

EDCS-05501347.translation: Pomponia Rosciana, daughter of Marcus, perpetual priestess of the deified gods and goddesses, to whom the most distinguished order of the municipality of Victrix Saeponensium decreed a statue in the forum, which she erected at her own expense, with a feast provided by a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501347.summary: A statue erected by Pomponia Rosciana, a priestess, at her own expense in the forum of Victrix Saeponensium, as decreed by the local council, which also provided for a feast.

EDCS-05501348.translation: To Lucius Sempronius, son of Quirinus, an Ianuarian; the order of Lacibulenses decreed for him a commendation, a burial place, funeral expenses, and a statue. Iunia Lucilla, daughter of Lucius, his wife, used the honor but remitted the expense.
EDCS-05501348.summary: A decree by the town of Lacibulenses honoring Lucius Sempronius with funeral expenses and a statue, which his wife Iunia Lucilla covered but then remitted.

EDCS-05501351.summary: A simple dedication to the goddess Victoria Augusta by Fabius Proculus.

EDCS-05501352.translation: Sacred to the Genius of the town. Marcus Servilius Asper, centurion in charge of the sacred rites, dedicated this at his own expense.
EDCS-05501352.summary: A dedication to the Genius of the town by Marcus Servilius Asper, a centurion responsible for religious matters.

EDCS-05501353.translation: The common people of Acinippum in the colony of Patricia, by decree of the decurions, decreed a statue at their own expense for Marcus Junius Terentianus Servilius Sabinus, son of Lucius, grandson of Lucius, formerly *duovir* and perpetual priest, their patron, on account of his merits. Marcus Junius Terentianus Servilius Sabinus, having received the honor, remitted the expense.
EDCS-05501353.summary: A statue was decreed by the people of Acinippum for their patron, Marcus Junius Terentianus Servilius Sabinus, in recognition of his merits, with the recipient later remitting the cost.

EDCS-05501354.translation: To Marco Mario Frontone, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, pontiff, twice duovir, patron of the plebs, for his merits, from a collection of money, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501354.summary: This is a dedication to Marco Mario Frontone by the plebs and decurions of Aciniponensis, honoring his merits and service, likely erected by public funds.

EDCS-05501355.translation: To Quintus Servilius Lupo, son of Quintus, of the Maecia tribe, pontiff, patron.
EDCS-05501355.summary: This is a dedication to Quintus Servilius Lupo, identifying him as a pontiff and patron.

EDCS-05501356.translation: Lucius Fabius Victor ordered a statue to be set up for his wife Fabia Maura by her will, and the town council of Aciniponensis decreed the place; Marcus Aemilius Dio, son of Spurius, PMPA (Prefect of the Military Post?), her heir, saw to the monument being erected.
EDCS-05501356.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument and statue for Fabia Maura, funded by her will and arranged by her husband and heir, by decree of the Aciniponensis town council.

EDCS-05501357.translation: To Flavius Galus, son of Caius, twice duovir, MVIR (?), M.N.I.O. R.M. (?), by decree of the decurions of Aciniponensis.
EDCS-05501357.summary: This is a dedication to Flavius Galus, identifying him as a duovir and honoring him by decree of the Aciniponensis decurions.

EDCS-05501359.summary: This is a simple funerary or honorary inscription for Aemilius Secundus.

EDCS-05501361.translation: Caius Appuleius Apollonius, 8 months old, is buried here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501361.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Appuleius Apollonius, who died at a very young age.

EDCS-05501362.translation: To Caius Fabius Fabianus, son, by the will of his father Caius Fabius Fabianus.
EDCS-05501362.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument erected for Caius Fabius Fabianus, funded by his father's will.

EDCS-05501363.summary: This is a simple funerary or honorary inscription for Porcilia.

EDCS-05501364.translation: Severus, freedman of Publius, paid a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Victor.
EDCS-05501364.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Severus, a freedman, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-05501365.translation: To Lucius Junius Iunianus, son of Lucius, twice duovir, who had provided in his will for a tomb to be made for himself for 200,000 denarii, and to the will of his patron; Lucius Junius Auctinus, his freedman and heir, requested by the town council of Arunda to set up statues of both Iunianus and his son Gallus in the forum, although he felt burdened by the greater expense, considered it honorable and necessary to obey by complying with the will of the council.
EDCS-05501365.summary: This inscription details the posthumous honors decreed by the Arunda town council for Lucius Junius Iunianus, including statues in the forum, funded by his heir Lucius Junius Auctinus who was fulfilling Iunianus' own will and the council's request.

EDCS-05501366.translation: To Licinius Iunius L. Cor AN for meals, from Lucius Iunius Licinianus Pater, his father, in place of a statue for his most distinguished friend, by decree of the town council, for the games.
EDCS-05501366.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue in honor of Licinius Iunius L. Cor AN for providing meals and for the games, funded by his father Lucius Iunius Licinianus Pater, by decree of the town council.

EDCS-05501367.translation: Sacred to the Genius of the town. Lucius Caecilius Attarius dedicated this gift in honor of his tenure as a duovir.
EDCS-05501367.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Caecilius Attarius to the Genius of the municipality upon completing his term as a duovir.

EDCS-05501368.translation: Terentius Herophilus, duovir Augustalis, best and most pious father, aged 60. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501368.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Terentius Herophilus, who was a duovir Augustalis, a father, and died at the age of 60.

EDCS-05501369.translation: By decree of the town council and the people. Clodia Optata, daughter of Caius, mother, having used the honor for Calpurnia Galla, daughter of Quintus, remitted the expense.
EDCS-05501369.summary: This inscription records that Clodia Optata, mother of Calpurnia Galla, was granted an honor by decree of the town council and people, but chose to remit the associated expense.

EDCS-05501370.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Calpurnia Camilla, aged 58, is laid here.
EDCS-05501370.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calpurnia Camilla, who died at the age of 58.

EDCS-05501371.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Mummus Herb, aged 32, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501371.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mummus Herb, who died at the age of 32.

EDCS-05501372.translation: To Lucius Fabius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, after death. Lucius Postumius Silo dedicated this tombstone at his own expense.
EDCS-05501372.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a tombstone by Lucius Postumius Silo at his own expense for Lucius Fabius Severus, who died posthumously.

EDCS-05501373.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying Aelia Husbatta, daughter of Mani.

EDCS-05501378.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintius Brutus Basilippensis, aged [ ] years, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501378.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintius Brutus Basilippensis, whose age is partially illegible.

EDCS-05501379.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Logias, aged 21. Gaius Julius Attisaga, father, laid here. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-05501379.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Logias, aged 21, whose father Gaius Julius Attisaga also laid him to rest.

EDCS-05501380.translation: Marcia Lucilla, daughter of Lucius, aged 31, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Her husband set up this altar for her.
EDCS-05501380.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcia Lucilla, recording her age and that her husband erected the altar.

EDCS-05501381.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Ulpius Pro [---]
EDCS-05501381.summary: A funerary inscription, likely beginning a longer dedication, for Marcus Ulpius Pro.

EDCS-05501382.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pyramus, aged 2 months, pious to his own, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501382.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pyramus, recording his very young age.

EDCS-05501383.translation: Lucius Aemilius Pudentus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, twice chief magistrate of the municipium of Flavius Muniguensis, having accepted the place, by decree of the council, gave a feast for both sexes, and dedicated this gift.
EDCS-05501383.summary: A dedication by Lucius Aemilius Pudentus, a local magistrate, for a public feast provided by him.

EDCS-05501384.translation: To Fonteius Calpurnianus, son of Caius, aedile.
EDCS-05501384.summary: A dedication or funerary marker for Fonteius Calpurnianus, who held the office of aedile.

EDCS-05501385.translation: Lucius Junius Rufus, son of Lucius, grandson of Marcus, great-grandson of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, quattuorvir, pontiff, Aug(ustalis), who held the office of quattuorvir, for the purpose of presenting public games, Roman equites by means of contributed money set up [this monument]. // Lucius Junius Rufus, son of Lucius, grandson of Marcus, great-grandson of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, quattuorvir, pontiff, Aug(ustalis), who held the office of quattuorvir, for the purpose of presenting public games.
EDCS-05501385.summary: A funerary monument erected by Roman equites for Lucius Junius Rufus, a prominent local official and priest, commemorating his service.

EDCS-05501386.translation: Antonia Saturnina, daughter of Lucius, had this monument made for her husband Geminus Abranus, a freedman, and for Antonia Primigenia, a freedwoman, and for herself, while she was alive; this monument will not pass to her heir.
EDCS-05501386.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Antonia Saturnina for herself, her husband, and a freedwoman, with provisions against inheritance.

EDCS-05501387.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Rusticius, son of Ammius, of Melusina, from Vamensis, aged 2 years, 1 month, 28 days, stop.
EDCS-05501387.summary: A funerary epitaph for Rusticius, recording his very young age.

EDCS-05501388.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucao, a slave, aged 1 year and 8 months, is laid here.
EDCS-05501388.summary: A funerary epitaph for a slave named Lucao, recording his young age.

EDCS-05501389.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcellina lived 10 years, 3 months. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501389.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcellina, recording her age.

EDCS-05501391.translation: Paezon, 33 years old, dear to his family. Quintus Iunius Paedio [and] Publicia Sophe.
EDCS-05501391.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Quintus Iunius Paedio and Publicia Sophe for Paezon, who died at the age of 33.

EDCS-05501392.translation: The bones of Papiria Phiales are placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501392.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Papiria Phiales, a common phrase in Roman burial inscriptions.

EDCS-05501394.translation: To the gods of the underworld, with the consent of Marcus, son of Cate, with the consent of Galius, son of Marcus.
EDCS-05501394.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the underworld deities, likely related to a burial or funerary cult.

EDCS-05501395.translation: Celsius made this altar to Safety for the safe return of Lucius, son of Numerius.
EDCS-05501395.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating an altar to the goddess Salus (Safety) by Celsius for the safe return of Lucius.

EDCS-05501397.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Aurelia Capitolina lived for 14 years; she is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501397.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Capitolina, who died at the age of 14.

EDCS-05501398.translation: Calpurnia Felicia Baegesis, 26 years old, dutiful to her family, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501398.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calpurnia Felicia Baegesis, who died at the age of 26.

EDCS-05501399.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Calpurnia, daughter of Gaius, from Tusculum, who lived for 72 years, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501399.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calpurnia Tusculana, who died at the age of 72.

EDCS-05501400.translation: Titus Domitius Clemens, son of Titus, of the Papiria tribe, 80 years old, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Titus Domitius Agrestis, son of Titus, of the Papiria tribe, 60 years old, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Titus Domitius Optatus, son of Titus, of the Papiria tribe, 30 years old, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501400.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burials of three individuals, Titus Domitius Clemens, Agrestis, and Optatus, likely family members.

EDCS-05501402.translation: Titus Fannius Proculus, son of Titus, of the Papiria tribe, who lived for 94 years, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501402.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Fannius Proculus, who died at the age of 94.

EDCS-05501403.translation: Firma, of Epaphroditus, 24 years old, is placed here. 'Firma, quite fortunate while my life lasted, I was dutiful to my husband. His affection and goodness were hardly equaled by any husband. With my diligence, I earned the pleased expressions and minds of my fellow freedmen. I ask you, passerby, saying 'may the earth lie lightly on you,' to always have them with you, those who follow you through the stars, and also him whom the law of servitude separates from his master, that all who are joined to you by blood may think they are with you, and he also, who is accustomed to follow you, and also he whom the law of servitude separates from his master.'
EDCS-05501403.summary: This is a complex funerary epitaph for Firma, who died at the age of 24, expressing her satisfaction with her life and her devotion to her husband and freedmen.

EDCS-05501405.translation: Acanthus, freedman, [for] Postumia Antulla, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-05501405.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a freedman for his patroness, Postumia Antulla.

EDCS-05501406.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Spatia, aged 36 years, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501406.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Spatia, recording her age and a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-05501408.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Fabius Rufinus, dutiful to his family, aged 70 years, is laid here. I ask you, passerby, as you go, to say: May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501408.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Fabius Rufinus, recording his age, his devotion to family, and a plea to passersby to wish him well.

EDCS-05501409.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Nigrinus, aged 18 years, dutiful to his family, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501409.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Nigrinus, recording his young age and his devotion to family.

EDCS-05501410.translation: Lucius Sempronius Silvinus set this up for his well-deserving father, Marcus Sempronius Optatus, aged 80 years.
EDCS-05501410.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Sempronius Silvinus for his father, Marcus Sempronius Optatus, noting his age.

EDCS-05501411.translation: Manilia Chelido, daughter of Marcus, aged 26 years, dutiful to her family, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501411.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Manilia Chelido, recording her age and her devotion to family.

EDCS-05501413.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Hermogenes, dutiful to his family, aged 8 years, 7 months, 14 days, 'I was nothing, I am nothing, and you who are alive are nothing. Drink, play, come.' He is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501413.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Hermogenes, a young child, noting his age and including a philosophical reflection on life and death.

EDCS-05501416.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Memmia Marciana, aged 26 years, dutiful to her family, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501416.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Memmia Marciana, recording her age and her devotion to family.

EDCS-05501417.translation: Publius Rutilius Syntrophus, a marble worker, built the 'theostasion' (a scene with divine figures) in the temple of Minerva with marble and at his own expense, a gift which he had promised.
EDCS-05501417.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the marble worker Publius Rutilius Syntrophus of a 'theostasion' in the temple of Minerva, fulfilling a promise.

EDCS-05501419.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, grandson of the deified Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Sarmaticus Germanicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 7th time, imperator for the 4th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, for the Republic of Gades by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501419.summary: This is a formal inscription by Emperor Commodus, listing his full titles and parentage, dedicating something to the Republic of Gades by order of its town council.

EDCS-05501420.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Mittius Maternus, son of Marcus, of the Galerian tribe, a tribune of the Twelfth Legion Fulminata, aged 70.
EDCS-05501420.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Mittius Maternus, a tribune of a Roman legion, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-05501421.translation: Lucius Antonius Antullus, son of Quintus, of the Galerian tribe, a quinquennial duovir with curuleian power, is buried here.
EDCS-05501421.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Antonius Antullus, who held a significant magistracy in his town, recording his burial.

EDCS-05501422.translation: Lucius Antonius Antullus, son of Caius, of the Galerian tribe, a priest, an excellent man, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. I ask you, passerby, as you read this, to say: May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501422.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Antonius Antullus, a priest, who was buried with a request for passersby to offer a blessing.

EDCS-05501423.translation: In memory of Quintus Antonius Rogatus, son of Caius, of the Galerian tribe, a decurion of the Augustan Gaditani.
EDCS-05501423.summary: This is a memorial inscription for Quintus Antonius Rogatus, a decurion of a military unit, likely commemorating his life or service.

EDCS-05501424.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Breccius Secundianus, with curuleian power, lived 34 years, dutiful to his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501424.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Breccius Secundianus, who held a magistracy and died at 34, emphasizing his devotion to his family.

EDCS-05501425.translation: Lucius Fabius Rufinus, son of Lucius, of the Galerian tribe, quinquennial duovir, prefect for administering justice, appointed by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501425.summary: This inscription records the career of Lucius Fabius Rufinus, detailing his appointment to judicial office by the town council.

EDCS-05501427.translation: Lucius Baebius Hermes, quinquennial Augustalis, aged 53, dear to his family, is buried here. Lucius Baebius Herma, his freedman, dedicated this to his excellent patron.
EDCS-05501427.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Lucius Baebius Hermes and notes a dedication from his freedman.

EDCS-05501428.translation: Quintus Valerius Littera, executor of a will, quinquennial, dear to his family, aged 63, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501428.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Quintus Valerius Littera, who held a quinquennial office and acted as a testamentary executor.

EDCS-05501430.translation: Aulus Baebius Rufus, son of Aulus, of the Galerian tribe. E. N.
EDCS-05501430.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a name and tribal affiliation, with an incomplete ending.

EDCS-05501431.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Albanius Artemidorus, eye doctor, aged 36, dear to his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501431.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Albanius Artemidorus, a specialized doctor, who died at the age of 36.

EDCS-05501432.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Troilus, identified as a Greek rhetorician.

EDCS-05501433.translation: Simplex, slave of Caius, a gladiator (hoplomachus) with twenty palm victories, a Bessian by nationality, aged 35, to his wife, who deserved well of her husband.
EDCS-05501433.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Simplex, a gladiator of Bessian origin, erected by his wife.

EDCS-05501434.translation: Turpa Thyce, beloved by her family, may the earth lie lightly upon you. She is buried here.
EDCS-05501434.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Turpa Thyce, a hairdresser, expressing affection from her family.

EDCS-05501435.translation: Herois, of the twentieth inheritance, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501435.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Herois, associated with a 'twentieth inheritance'.

EDCS-05501437.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Baebia Veneria Peraria, beloved by her family, to her dearest grandfather, aged 25. Baebius Veneriosus, aged 1 year and 3 months. May the earth lie lightly upon you all.
EDCS-05501437.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to the departed spirits and honors both Baebia Veneria Peraria and her infant grandson, Baebius Veneriosus.

EDCS-05501438.translation: Accia Stratonice, aged 65, beloved by her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Marcus Accius Florus, aged 75, beloved by his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-05501439.translation: Acutia Marina is buried here, aged 41. May the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-05501440.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelia Peculio lived for 81 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501440.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to the departed spirits and records the lifespan of Aelia Peculio as 81 years.

EDCS-05501441.translation: Aelia Rogata, daughter of Quinti, is buried here.
EDCS-05501441.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Aelia Rogata, daughter of Quinti.

EDCS-05501442.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Graficus, aged 33, a most dutiful son, beloved by his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501442.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to the departed spirits and commemorates Publius Aelius Graficus, a dutiful son.

EDCS-05501443.translation: To their deserving slave by the master and mistress, Lucius Aelius Rocianus.

EDCS-05501447.translation: Marcus Aemilius Optatus Longus, son of Marcus, aged [---], is buried here. Suavis gives this final gift in memory of friendship for Decimus Valerius Stabilionis.
EDCS-05501447.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Aemilius Optatus Longus, a Roman man, and records a gift made in memory of friendship.

EDCS-05501505.translation: Marcus Fabricius Laetus is laid here; may the earth lie lightly on you.

EDCS-05501508.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Felix, aged 18, dear to his own, may the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-05501509.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Fo Felix, aged 30, dear to his own, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-05501510.translation: Fortunata, dear to her own, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-05501512.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a child named Gemella, stating her age and that she is buried here.

EDCS-05501513.translation: Genetiva, dear to her own, aged 37, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-05501514.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Granius Infans, aged 61, dear to his own, may the earth lie lightly upon you; he lived well with good fortune.

EDCS-05501515.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Granius Laurentinus, aged 43, dear to his own, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-05501516.translation: Herenniola, aged 21, dear to her own, is placed here.
EDCS-05501516.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Herenniola, stating her age and her beloved status by her family.

EDCS-05501517.translation: Hail Herennia Crocine, dear to her own. I am enclosed in this tomb. Crocine, dear to her own, I lived, and before me other girls lived. Now it is enough, reader. As you leave, say: may the earth lie lightly upon Crocine. Farewell, dwellers above.
EDCS-05501517.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Herennia Crocine, written in the first person, reflecting on her life and the lives of other girls, and addressing the reader.

EDCS-05501518.translation: Caius Herennius, dear to his own, is placed here.
EDCS-05501518.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caius Herennius, indicating he is buried here and was beloved by his family.

EDCS-05501519.translation: Lucius Herennius Anteros is buried here.
EDCS-05501519.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucius Herennius Anteros.

EDCS-05501520.translation: Ianuaria, aged 17, dear to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501520.summary: A funerary inscription for Ianuaria, aged 17, including a common epitaph wishing her peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-05501521.translation: Iucunda, dear to her family, is buried here.
