EDCS-05501566.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Publicia Felicia, aged 26, who is wished a light earth.

EDCS-05501567.translation: Greetings, Publicia Fortunata, aged 36. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501567.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Publicia Fortunata, aged 36, who is stated to be buried here, with a wish for a light earth.

EDCS-05501568.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publicia Ursa, aged 75, dear to her own people, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501568.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Publicia Ursa, aged 75, who was dear to her family and is buried here, with a wish for a light earth.

EDCS-05501569.translation: Publicius Diadumenus, aged 93, dear to his own people. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501569.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Publicius Diadumenus, aged 93, who was dear to his family, with a wish for a light earth.

EDCS-05501570.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Publicius Victor Cippianus, aged 48, dear to his own people, is buried here. Flavia Optata, wife of Optatus and Victor's, aged 26, dear to her own people, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you both.
EDCS-05501570.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the burial of Marcus Publicius Victor Cippianus, aged 48, and his wife Flavia Optata, aged 26, both of whom were dear to their families.

EDCS-05501571.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Pyramus, aged 33, who is stated to be buried here.

EDCS-05501572.translation: Marcus Reburrius Philippus, dear to his own people, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501572.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Reburrius Philippus, who was dear to his family and is buried here, with a wish for a light earth.

EDCS-05501573.translation: Romana, dear to her own people, aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. I beg you, passerby, come, eat, drink, play.
EDCS-05501573.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Romana, aged 20, who was dear to her family and is buried here, inviting passersby to enjoy themselves.

EDCS-05501574.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Romula, aged 65, dear to her own people, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501574.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Romula, aged 65, who was dear to her family and is buried here, with a wish for a light earth.

EDCS-05501575.translation: Quartilla, M. Here lie (plural). May the earth lie lightly upon you all.
EDCS-05501575.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quartilla, M., indicating that multiple individuals are buried here and wishing a light earth for all.

EDCS-05501576.translation: Rufina, aged 23, dear to her family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501576.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rufina, a young woman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501577.translation: Rufina, aged 70, dear to her family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501577.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rufina, an elderly woman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501578.translation: Rustica, dear to her family, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501578.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rustica, a woman whose age is not specified, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501579.translation: Secunda, dear to her family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501579.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Secunda, a woman whose age is not specified, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501580.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph for Secundus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501581.translation: Secundus, dear to his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501581.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Secundus, a man whose age is not specified, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501582.translation: Sergia Gemella, dear to her family, aged 70, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501582.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sergia Gemella, an elderly woman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501583.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Servilius Herennius, aged 81, beloved by his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501583.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Servilius Herennius, an elderly man, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501584.translation: Successus, aged 45, beloved by his family, a good soul, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501584.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Successus, a middle-aged man, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501585.translation: Syntrophus, beloved by his family, aged 8, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501585.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Syntrophus, a child, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501586.translation: Caius Turus ChrHestes, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501586.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Turus ChrHestes, with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05501587.translation: Sextus Turius Suavis, aged 74, cherished by his family, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501587.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Turius Suavis, who died at the age of 74 and was loved by his family.

EDCS-05501589.translation: Valeria Fructilla, aged 73, cherished by her family, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501589.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Fructilla, who died at the age of 73 and was loved by her family.

EDCS-05501590.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valeria Gemina, aged 7, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501590.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Gemina, who died at the young age of 7.

EDCS-05501591.translation: Vale Lacce, aged 70, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501591.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vale Lacce, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-05501592.translation: Valeria Martilla, cherished by her family, is laid here.
EDCS-05501592.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Martilla, who was loved by her family.

EDCS-05501593.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph for Valeria Pia, mentioning her name and the beginning of her age.

EDCS-05501594.translation: Valeria Saturnina, wife, aged 22, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501594.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Saturnina, the wife of someone, who died at the young age of 22.

EDCS-05501595.translation: Valeria Urbana, cherished by her family, aged 100, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501595.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Urbana, who lived to be 100 years old and was loved by her family.

EDCS-05501596.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valerius Gallius, freedman, aged 61, dutiful to his family, is laid here.
EDCS-05501596.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valerius Gallius, a freedman who died at the age of 61 and was devoted to his family.

EDCS-05501597.translation: Aulus Valerius Felix, aged sixty years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501597.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Valerius Felix, aged 60, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501598.translation: Marcus Valerius Fortunatus, dear to his family, aged nine years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501598.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Fortunatus, aged 9, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501599.translation: Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501599.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501600.translation: Gaius Valerius Montanus, dear to his family, aged forty-five years, is buried here.
EDCS-05501600.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Montanus, aged 45, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501601.translation: Lucius Valerius Philargurus is buried here.
EDCS-05501601.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Philargurus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501602.translation: Marcus Valerius Quartio is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501602.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Quartio, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501603.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Valerius. [---]
EDCS-05501603.summary: This inscription likely begins a funerary dedication for Lucius Valerius, with the rest of the text lost or illegible.

EDCS-05501604.translation: Quintus Valerius Saturninus, dear to his family, aged twenty years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501604.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Valerius Saturninus, aged 20, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501605.translation: Marcus Valerius Senex, dear to his family, aged sixty-five years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501605.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Senex, aged 65, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501606.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Veneria, aged twenty-three years, dear to her family. May the earth lie lightly on you. She is buried here.
EDCS-05501606.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Veneria, aged 23, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501607.translation: Verania Albula, aged 50, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501607.summary: A funerary epitaph for Verania Albula, aged 50, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05501608.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Vibia Gallula, aged 37, dear to her own people, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501608.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Divine Manes for Vibia Gallula, aged 37, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05501609.translation: Quintus Vilius, aged 21, dear to his own people, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501609.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Vilius, aged 21, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05501610.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vinicia Chelido, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05501611.translation: Vinicia Thallusa, freedwoman of Lucius, is placed here.
EDCS-05501611.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vinicia Thallusa, freedwoman of Lucius, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05501612.translation: Urso, dear to his own people, aged 3, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501612.summary: A funerary epitaph for Urso, aged 3, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05501613.translation: Canius Epaprus, dear to his own people, aged 25, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501613.summary: A funerary epitaph for Canius Epaprus, aged 25, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05501614.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aged 35. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501614.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Divine Manes for an individual aged 35, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05501615.summary: A funerary epitaph, possibly incomplete, for an individual named FEL us F, aged 20, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05501616.translation: us isius, aged 115, dear to his own people, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501616.summary: A funerary epitaph for us isius, aged 115, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05501617.translation: Carus, aged 37 years, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501617.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Carus, dated by its formulaic phrasing to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501621.translation: Fabia Fabiana ordered this to be erected by her will, and Fabius Montanus, her brother and heir, took care of its erection, having accepted the site from the most distinguished council of the Iptucitani, and dedicated it according to custom.
EDCS-05501621.summary: A funerary monument erected by Fabius Montanus for his sister Fabia Fabiana, with details about the land acquisition and dedication to the local council.

EDCS-05501622.translation: To Marcus Valerius Romulus Timotheus, Vinuleius Gallus, a good man, a most dutiful son.
EDCS-05501622.summary: An epitaph honoring Marcus Valerius Romulus Timotheus, likely a personal dedication by Vinuleius Gallus.

EDCS-05501623.translation: Lucius Statorius Iucundillus, one of the IIII viri, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501623.summary: A tombstone inscription for Lucius Statorius Iucundillus, a member of the college of IIII viri, dated by its formulaic phrasing.

EDCS-05501624.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Clodius Seranus, aged 30 years, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501624.summary: A tombstone inscription for Marcus Clodius Seranus, dated by its formulaic phrasing to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501625.summary: A very short inscription, possibly a dedicatory or protective charm invoking Hercules.

EDCS-05501627.translation: To Quintus Cornelius Gallus Senecio Annianus, consul, proconsul of Pontus and Bithynia, curator of the Appian Way, legate of the Legio VII Gemina Felix, curator of the Latin Way, praetor, tribune of the plebs, urban quaestor, priest of Hercules.
EDCS-05501627.summary: A lengthy dedication listing the extensive career and honors of Quintus Cornelius Gallus Senecio Annianus, from consul to priest.

EDCS-05501628.translation: To Publius Tillius Quintio, son of Titus.
EDCS-05501628.summary: A dedication honoring Publius Tillius Quintio, son of Titus, likely an epitaph or a votive offering.

EDCS-05501629.translation: To the departed spirits. Valeria Modesta, aged 27 years, is buried here.
EDCS-05501629.summary: A tombstone inscription for Valeria Modesta, dated by its formulaic phrasing to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501630.summary: An inscription identifying Varia Marcella as a granddaughter, possibly an epitaph or a familial dedication.

EDCS-05501631.translation: Sacred to the underworld gods [dedication by] Num...
EDCS-05501631.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a dedication to the Dis Manibus by someone named Num...

EDCS-05501633.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta. Gaius Marcius December, in return for the honor of the sevirate, gives from his own money a donation of 500 denarii, from 750 denarii remitted to him by the order.
EDCS-05501633.summary: This is a dedication to Fortuna Augusta made by Gaius Marcius December from his own funds to commemorate his sevirate, with a portion of his expenses remitted by the local council.

EDCS-05501634.translation: To Youth and Augustus. Gaius Marcius Niger, because of the honor of the flamenship, [erected] a base and altar. He gladly gave.
EDCS-05501634.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Youth and Augustus by Gaius Marcius Niger, likely to commemorate his appointment as a flamen, and appears to be a votive offering.

EDCS-05501635.translation: The state, having decreed that a monument be erected with 10,000 denarii that he had guaranteed, received from the heirs of Gaius Marcius Cephalon, on account of the honor of the flamenship, with Valerius Titianus, Numisius Valerianus Flavus, and Rusticus, the two chief magistrates, overseeing.
EDCS-05501635.summary: This inscription records the posthumous erection of a monument to Gaius Marcius Cephalon by the state, funded by money he had guaranteed for his flamenship, overseen by local magistrates.

EDCS-05501637.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus. Lucius Vibius Persinus gave a gift from his own money.
EDCS-05501637.summary: This is a dedication to Mars Augustus by Lucius Vibius Persinus, who donated a gift from his personal funds.

EDCS-05501639.translation: The most distinguished council decreed a statue for Gaius Cervius Quintianus, twice chief magistrate; his father, Cervius Honoratus Ostorianus Rufus, erected it from his own money, having received the honor, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501639.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue by a father in honor of his son, Gaius Cervius Quintianus, who held the office of II vir, based on a decree from the town council.

EDCS-05501640.translation: Fabia Fabiana, daughter of Gaius, and Fulvia Honorata, daughter of Sextus, his heirs, by his will, erected [this monument] and provided a feast for Lucius Fabius Caesianus, twice chief magistrate and perpetual flamen of the municipal town of Barbesula.
EDCS-05501640.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument and the provision of a feast by the heirs of Lucius Fabius Caesianus, a prominent local official, in accordance with his will.

EDCS-05501641.translation: Cervia Maura, the mother, having received [permission] from the council, gave from her own money a gift in honor of her most dutiful son, Gaius Cretius Maurus.
EDCS-05501641.summary: This is a dedication made by a mother, Cervia Maura, from her own funds, to honor her son Gaius Cretius Maurus, likely related to his public service or death.

EDCS-05501643.translation: Sacred to Neptune Augustus. Lucius Iunius Puteolanus, one of the six priests of the Imperial cult in the municipality of Suelita, honored with all the offices that freedmen could hold, having received the honor, gave a gift from his own money, providing a feast, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501643.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Neptune Augustus by Lucius Iunius Puteolanus, a high-ranking freedman and local official in Suelita, who funded a feast and donation from his own money.

EDCS-05501644.translation: Lucius Munnius Novatus and Lucius Munnius Aurelianus, having obtained Roman citizenship through the honor of the office of II vir, gave a gift from their own money to Emperor Domitian Caesar Augustus Germanicus.
EDCS-05501644.summary: This inscription records a joint dedication by Lucius Munnius Novatus and Lucius Munnius Aurelianus to Emperor Domitian, commemorating their acquisition of Roman citizenship through their service as II viri.

EDCS-05501646.translation: Fabia Firma, the heiress, dedicated the statue which Gaius Fabius Vibianus, duovir, ordered to be made in his will for his mother, Vibiae Lucanae.
EDCS-05501646.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue ordered by a duovir for his mother, and dedicated by his heiress.

EDCS-05501647.translation: Lucius Porcius Quirina Victor of Cartimitanus ordered this statue to Mars Augustus to be set up in his will; his heir did not deduct the 1/20th from this gift, but gave it as a feast.
EDCS-05501647.summary: This inscription records a statue dedicated to Mars Augustus by Lucius Porcius Quirina Victor, with his heir honoring his will by providing a feast instead of deducting an inheritance tax.

EDCS-05501649.translation: Lucius Porcius Quirina Victor of Cartimitanus ordered this statue to Venus Augusta to be set up in his will, in his own name and that of his wife Scribonia Marciana; their heir did not deduct the 1/20th from this gift, but gave it as a feast.
EDCS-05501649.summary: This inscription records a statue dedicated to Venus Augusta by Lucius Porcius Quirina Victor and his wife, with their heir providing a feast as stipulated in the will.

EDCS-05501650.translation: Vibia Rusticana, daughter of Lucius, of Cartimitanus, ordered this statue to Venus Augusta to be set up in her will; her heirs did not deduct the 1/20th from this gift, but gave it as a feast and dedicated it.
EDCS-05501650.summary: This inscription records a statue dedicated to Venus Augusta by Vibia Rusticana, with her heirs providing a feast and completing the dedication as per her will.

EDCS-05501651.translation: Vestinus, son of Rusticus, and his son Rusticus, from their own funds, gave a gift to Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 13th time, imperator for the 27th time, consul for the 5th time, father of his country, censor. Since its base had been corrupted by age, Vibia Rusticana, his daughter-in-law, restored a new one in its place.
EDCS-05501651.summary: This inscription records a donation and the restoration of a monument base for Emperor Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus, initiated by Vestinus and his son, and completed by Vibia Rusticana.

EDCS-05501652.translation: The council of Cartimitanus decreed that a statue be erected to Marcus Decimius Quirina Proculus, perpetual pontifex, who, after accepting the honor, remitted the expense.
EDCS-05501652.summary: This inscription records the council of Cartimitanus decreeing a statue for Marcus Decimius Quirina Proculus, who declined personal costs for the honor.

EDCS-05501653.translation: The mother of Decimus Junius Galeria Melinus, a Roman knight from the citizenry of Cartimitanus, had arranged for friends to have a statue made for him while alive, and after Melinus's death, she ensured it was erected, remitting the expense.
EDCS-05501653.summary: This inscription records a statue commissioned by friends for Decimus Junius Melinus and later erected by his mother, who covered the costs after his death.

EDCS-05501654.translation: Iunia Rustica, daughter of Decimus, perpetual priestess and the first in the municipality of Cartimitanus, repaired the public porticoes, which had been corrupted by age, gave the ground for the bath, reclaimed public revenues, and placed a bronze statue of Mars in the forum. She dedicated the porticoes to the bath, along with a pool and a statue of Cupid, from her own funds, after providing a feast and staging public games. She also dedicated from her own funds statues of herself and her son Gaius Fabius Iunianus, which had been decreed by the council of Cartimitanus and for which she remitted the expense, and similarly a statue of her husband Gaius Fabius Fabianus.
EDCS-05501654.summary: This inscription details extensive public works and dedications by Iunia Rustica, including the repair of porticoes, the donation of land for a bath, the erection of statues, and the funding of games and feasts.

EDCS-05501655.translation: Friends arranged for the statue of Lucius Porcius Quirina Saturninus, pontifex, who gave 20,000 sesterces for the honor, so that he might clear the debts of the state of Cartimitanus; Lucius Porcius Quirina Saturninus, after accepting the honor, remitted the expense.
EDCS-05501655.summary: This inscription records a statue commissioned by friends for Lucius Porcius Quirina Saturninus, who provided funds to clear public debt and subsequently waived the cost of his own honor.

EDCS-05501656.translation: The council of Cartimitanus decreed that a statue be erected to Vibia Turrina, daughter of Lucius, perpetual priestess, who, after accepting the honor, remitted the expense.
EDCS-05501656.summary: This inscription records the council of Cartimitanus decreeing a statue for Vibia Turrina, a perpetual priestess, who declined personal costs for the honor.

EDCS-05501657.translation: Decimia Procula, daughter of Decimus, mother of Rusticus.
EDCS-05501657.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Decimia Procula, identifying her parentage and her son.

EDCS-05501658.translation: To Pompeia, daughter of Marcus, aged 30, his wife. Victorinus for himself.
EDCS-05501658.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pompeia, set up by her husband Victorinus.

EDCS-05501660.translation: Lucius Vibius Rusticus, of the Quirina tribe, made this.
EDCS-05501660.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Vibius Rusticus made a monument or object.

EDCS-05501663.translation: To Jupiter, Marcus Lucretius Cyrus fulfilled a vow seen in a vision and dedicated the temple.
EDCS-05501663.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Marcus Lucretius Cyrus dedicating a temple to Jupiter after a vision.

EDCS-05501664.translation: Lucius Servilius Superatus placed this gift willingly for his unconquered lord, an altar for one deserving.
EDCS-05501664.summary: This inscription records Lucius Servilius Superatus dedicating an altar to an unconquered lord.

EDCS-05501665.translation: Lucius Octavius Rusticus, son of Lucius, and Lucius Granius Balbus, son of Marcus, quaestors, dedicate this sacred offering to Victoria Augusta from their own funds.
EDCS-05501665.summary: This is a dedication by two quaestors, Lucius Octavius Rusticus and Lucius Granius Balbus, to Victoria Augusta.

EDCS-05501666.translation: Lucius Granius gave and donated the lake, made at his expense.
EDCS-05501666.summary: This inscription records Lucius Granius dedicating a lake he had constructed.

EDCS-05501667.translation: The city council of Malaca gives a gift to the son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Parthicus Arabicus Adiabenicus, the pacifier of the world and founder of the Roman Empire.
EDCS-05501667.summary: This inscription records the city of Malaca dedicating a gift to Caracalla, son of Septimius Severus.

EDCS-05501668.translation: To Lucius Valerius Proculus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, prefect of the IV Cohort of Thracians from Syria, military tribune of Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, prefect of the Alexandrian fleet and of the river watch, imperial procurator of the Maritime Alps, imperial elector, procurator of the province of Baetica in Hispania, procurator of the province of Cappadocia, procurator of the province of Asia, procurator of the three provinces of Gaul, procurator of the imperial treasury, prefect of the grain supply, prefect of Egypt; the city council of Malaca, on the decree of its decurions, [honored him] as patron.
EDCS-05501668.summary: This is a lengthy honorary inscription set up by the city of Malaca for Lucius Valerius Proculus, detailing his numerous high-ranking military and administrative posts.

EDCS-05501669.translation: Valeria Lucilla, daughter of Gaius, and Lucius Valerius Proculus, prefect of Egypt, were set up by citizens of Malaca contributing from the public fund, by decree of the decurions. Publius Clodius, having received honor, returned the contribution.
EDCS-05501669.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by citizens of Malaca, honoring Valeria Lucilla and Lucius Valerius Proculus, with a mention of Publius Clodius's contribution.

EDCS-05501670.translation: For the merits of all his virtues, which he provided through the administration of the entire province and through his own governance, and for his praiseworthy goodness, singular prudence, exceptional eloquence, and outstanding elevation, the city of Malaca, with the unanimous consent of all the provincial consuls, erected a marble equestrian statue to its most innocent and most devoted patron.
EDCS-05501670.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue to an unnamed patron by the city of Malaca, likely in the Imperial period, in recognition of his administrative and personal virtues.

EDCS-05501671.translation: Valeria Macrina, wife of Lucius Caecilius Bassus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, in accordance with the decree of the decurions of the municipality of Malaca, released the expenses, content with the honor.
EDCS-05501671.summary: This inscription records that Valeria Macrina, wife of Lucius Caecilius Bassus, waived the costs for his honors decreed by the town council of Malaca, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501672.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Fortunatianus, best and most holy father, son of Quintus, who lived 37 years and 20 days; he lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501672.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Quintus Caecilius Fortunatianus, honoring his father and stating his age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501673.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-05501673.summary: This is a dedicatory or funerary inscription for Marcus Cornelius Severus, son of Lucius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501674.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Porcius Gaetulus, aged 25, dutiful in his college, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501674.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Porcius Gaetulus, who died at age 25 and was a member of a college, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501675.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Of Gaius Valerius Crescens, aged one year and 46 days.
EDCS-05501675.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Gaius Valerius Crescens, who died in infancy, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501679.translation: Auctus the teacher gave as a gift to our Gaius Annianus.
EDCS-05501679.summary: This is a short dedicatory inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording a gift from a teacher to his student.

EDCS-05501681.summary: This appears to be a dedication or tomb inscription, possibly indicating Lucius Aemilius was honored or buried by Cornelia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501683.translation: Anniola; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501683.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Anniola, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501684.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Attia Galla the dutiful, in her own [tomb].
EDCS-05501684.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Attia Galla, who is described as dutiful and buried in her own property, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501685.translation: Concessa Reburri is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Varus, son of Gallus, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501685.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two individuals, Concessa Reburri and Varus, son of Gallus.

EDCS-05501687.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcia Stratonice, daughter of Marcus Callistus, aged 30, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501687.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcia Stratonice, daughter of Marcus Callistus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-05501688.translation: Martialis, son of Martialis, aged 22. Potita, daughter of Martialis, aged 21. They are buried here.
EDCS-05501688.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two individuals, Martialis and Potita, who were siblings and children of Martialis.

EDCS-05501690.translation: Mummia Saturnina, wife of Quintus, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Quintus Caecilius Macer is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501690.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Mummia Saturnina, wife of Quintus, and Quintus Caecilius Macer.

EDCS-05501696.translation: Modestus [died?] aged [---] 22? May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501696.summary: This fragment of a funerary inscription records the death of an individual named Modestus and includes the standard wish for the earth to lie lightly upon them.

EDCS-05501700.translation: Vanitas, [this is?] the inheritance from the departed. Here lies the ashes, may you rest here and farewell.
EDCS-05501700.summary: This funerary inscription, though partially damaged and difficult to fully reconstruct, records a burial and includes a farewell wish to the deceased.

EDCS-05501701.translation: To Fabia Anu, daughter of Lucius Fabius Pulverinus, [buried] in the region of Minicianus.
EDCS-05501701.summary: This inscription seems to record the burial of Fabia Anu, daughter of Lucius Fabius Pulverinus, in the Minicianus area, possibly within a columbarium.

EDCS-05501702.translation: Publius Iulius Primus is buried here with his family; may the earth lie lightly on you. I own 6 columbaria, to the right and left.
EDCS-05501702.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Publius Iulius Primus, who is buried with his family and also states his ownership of six columbaria.

EDCS-05501707.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcia Antiochis, aged 70, pious, is buried here in her own tomb; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501707.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcia Antiochis, aged 70, in her own family tomb.

EDCS-05501712.translation: Aticus, freedman of Gaius Fabius Niger, and Firmo, freedman of Bithynis, freedman of the Augustales, dedicated this gift to the Augustae Victoriae.
EDCS-05501712.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by two freedmen, Aticus and Firmo, to the goddesses Victoriae Augustae.

EDCS-05501714.translation: Aelia Optata, daughter of Quintus, ordered this to be set up in memory of Quintus Aelius Optatus, son of Quintus, by her will; Gaius Appius Superstes Caninius Montanus, the heir, saw to its erection.
EDCS-05501714.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a daughter for her father, with the heir responsible for construction.

EDCS-05501715.translation: Calpurnia Sabina, daughter of Lucius and mother of Quintus, ordered this to be set up in memory of Quintus Fulvius Lupus, son of Quintus, by her will; Gaius Appius Superstes Caninius Montanus, the heir, saw to its erection.
EDCS-05501715.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a mother for her son, with the heir responsible for construction.

EDCS-05501716.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely the ownership or dedication of a tomb belonging to Quintus Aelius Zeno.

EDCS-05501718.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lurius Fortunio lived 61 years, dutiful to his family. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501718.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lurius Fortunio, recording his age and piety.

EDCS-05501719.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcia Antiocis, 70 years old, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501719.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcia Antiocis, recording her age and piety.

EDCS-05501720.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Marius Optatus, alas, a youth. Such is the man lying in this tomb who used to catch fish with a javelin, and pursued hunting with a reed pipe, aside from his studies.
EDCS-05501720.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Marius Optatus, lamenting his youth and listing his pastimes.

EDCS-05501721.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vibia, a slave woman, 65 years old, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501721.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibia, a slave woman, recording her age and piety.

EDCS-05502598.translation: Annia Phoebas, 7 years and 9 months old, is buried here. I ask you, passerby, as you go, say: May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502598.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child, Annia Phoebas, requesting a passing remark from passersby.

EDCS-05502768.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. The order of MENTESAN decreed an expenditure for the funeral rites of Calpurnia Scantilla, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-05502768.summary: This inscription records the decree of a collegium or organization named MENTESAN to fund the funeral of Calpurnia Scantilla.

EDCS-05600063.translation: To our lord Decentius, our most pious, most flourishing Caesar, the Emperor.
EDCS-05600063.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to the Emperor Magnentius (Decentius was his Caesar), likely set up during his reign in the mid-4th century AD.

EDCS-05600390.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Fulvius Attennius Glaucus, aged 14 and a half, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600390.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Marcus Fulvius Attennius Glaucus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600391.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Maritima lived 10 years and 3 months. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600391.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Maritima, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording her age at death.

EDCS-05600392.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Superata lived 27 years. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600392.summary: A funerary epitaph for a woman named Superata, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording her age at death.

EDCS-05600393.translation: Lucius Aemilius, son of Lucius, the imperator, decreed that the slaves of the Hastenses who lived in the Turris Lascutana should be free, and that they should also possess and hold the land and town which they held at that time, for as long as the Roman people and Senate wished. Acted in the camp, on the 12th day before the Kalends of February.
EDCS-05600393.summary: A senatorial or imperial decree concerning the freedom and land rights of slaves from Hastenses settled in Turris Lascutana, dated to the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05600394.translation: Dama, slave of Lucius Titus, received in a mancipation the Baian fund, which is in the territory called Veneriensis, in the Olbensis district, as the best and greatest, for 1 sestertius and the slave Midas for 1 sestertius, for the sake of trust and confidence, from Lucius Baianius, having declared the boundaries in the presence of witnesses. Lucius Baianius said to Lucius Titus, Gaius Seius, and the people, and to whomever else it is fitting to speak: An agreement has been made between Dama, slave of Lucius Titus, and Lucius Baianius. Whatever money Lucius [Titus] gave, will give, entrusted, or will entrust to Lucius Baianius, or whatever he has spent or will spend on his behalf, or promised or will promise, or by what oath he willed his property to be his, up to that point the said fund and slaves, or whatever slaves from them Lucius Titus, or his heir, wishes, he may sell in the presence of the money, whenever and wherever he wishes, for not less than 1 sestertius against his will, nor provide security according to mancipation, nor make a promise in the terms which are usually made in security according to mancipation, neither single nor double.
EDCS-05600394.summary: A legal document detailing a mancipation (transfer of ownership) of a rural property (fundus) and slaves, specifying conditions of trust and repayment, dating to the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05600395.translation: [---] aged 9 [---] from life which above the heavens
EDCS-05600395.summary: A fragmented epitaph, likely funerary, for a child aged 9, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600428.summary: A very brief dedication, possibly a votive offering, with incomplete context, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600430.translation: The duoviri, Tamius Natalis and Sempronius AI, for H I S P, Mar Alphius.
EDCS-05600430.summary: An inscription recording the names of duoviri (magistrates) and possibly another individual, likely from a Roman municipal context.

EDCS-05600433.translation: Of Quintus Vibius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-05600433.summary: A fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or a tombstone, mentioning Quintus Vibius, son of Lucius, from the Roman Republican or Imperial period.

EDCS-05600439.translation: Rustica, dear to her family, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600439.summary: A funerary epitaph for Rustica, expressing affection from her family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600441.translation: Postumia Prisca, daughter of Quintus, wife of Lucius Servilius Pollio, son of Lucius, who held the office of IIII vir of the townspeople of the municipality twice, prefect of the cavalry under Gaius Caesar, and held the quadrumviral power, and was pontifex of public and municipal sacred rites, and pontifex of the deified Augustus, dedicated this.
EDCS-05600441.summary: This is a dedication by a wife to her husband, who held several important municipal offices, likely on a public building or monument.

EDCS-05600442.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iunia D[...], to her dear ones. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600442.summary: A funerary dedication by Iunia to her loved ones, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-05600443.translation: To the two Concordias, joined in marriage for 13 years. Marcus, son of Germanus, of the Galeria tribe, made this.
EDCS-05600443.summary: This is a dedication to two goddesses named Concordia, celebrating a marriage of 13 years and made by Marcus.

EDCS-05600639.translation: Reburrus, son of Vacisius, of the Berenis castellum, a Limigines, lies here. Apus made this for his brother.
EDCS-05600639.summary: A tombstone for Reburrus, set up by his brother Apus, indicating Reburrus was a soldier or someone associated with a border fortification (castellum).

EDCS-05600645.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Annia Marciana made this for her most devoted father, Annio Tessalo, who lived 40 years. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600645.summary: A funerary dedication by Annia Marciana to her father Annio Tessalo, likely on his tomb.

EDCS-05600646.translation: His mother Lucius R. Papirius [made this]. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600646.summary: A funerary dedication, likely by a mother for her son Lucius R. Papirius, possibly on a tomb.

EDCS-05600656.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Cornelia Coete lived 80 years. Cornelius Theseus made this for his well-deserving wife. As you pass by, I ask you to say, 'May the earth rest lightly upon you.'
EDCS-05600656.summary: A funerary dedication by Cornelius Theseus for his wife Cornelia Coete, including a plea to passersby to offer a standard funerary wish.

EDCS-05600657.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Herennia, pious in her own [family], lived 50 years. She lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600657.summary: A funerary dedication for Herennia, described as pious, likely on her tomb.

EDCS-05600658.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Licinia Licinilla lived 35 years, 4 months, and 9 days. She lies here. As you pass by, I ask you to say, 'May the earth rest lightly upon you.'
EDCS-05600658.summary: A funerary dedication for Licinia Licinilla, with specific age details and a plea to passersby, likely on her tomb.

EDCS-05600659.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lituccia Primilla, pious in her own [family], lived 15 years, 11 months, and 5 days. She lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600659.summary: A funerary dedication for Lituccia Primilla, with specific age details, likely on her tomb.

EDCS-05600660.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Publius, 5 years old, pious in his own home, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600660.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a 5-year-old boy named Publius, dated by its formulaic invocation to the underworld.

EDCS-05600661.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Satria Dolice, a freedwoman, identified by her patron's name.

EDCS-05600662.translation: Agathemerus, freedman, sets this up for Valeria Procula, daughter of Marcus, our own.
EDCS-05600662.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by a freedman for Valeria Procula, likely his patron or wife.

EDCS-05600663.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius C. Felix.
EDCS-05600663.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the underworld spirits for Lucius C. Felix.

EDCS-05600668.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Ianuarius, slave of Domitius Percas, a doctor, 31 years old, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Caius Irrius December arranged for this to be made for his brother from his own means.
EDCS-05600668.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ianuarius, a 31-year-old slave doctor, sponsored by his brother Caius Irrius December.

EDCS-05600671.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Dasumia Procne, 40 years old, pious in her own home, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600671.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Dasumia Procne, a 40-year-old woman, dated by its formulaic invocation to the underworld.

EDCS-05600672.translation: In memory of Fundaniana, whom her father, mother, and husband Leucadius erected; she lived 20 years.
EDCS-05600672.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by the parents and husband for Fundaniana, who died at age 20.

EDCS-05600674.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Publius Mar, 5 years old, pious in his own home, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600674.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a 5-year-old boy named Publius Mar, dated by its formulaic invocation to the underworld.

EDCS-05600675.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Naevius Africanus lived 75 years, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600675.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Naevius Africanus, who died at age 75, dated by its formulaic invocation to the underworld.

EDCS-05600676.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Vibia Vitalis, 30 years old, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600676.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibia Vitalis, a 30-year-old woman, dated by its formulaic invocation to the underworld.

EDCS-05600681.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Hermetius lived for 3 years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600681.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Hermetius at the age of three, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600686.translation: Gaius Cassius Pylades is buried here.
EDCS-05600686.summary: A simple tomb inscription identifying the burial place of Gaius Cassius Pylades, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600688.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia lived for 7 years. Mascutios the father made this for his well-deserving daughter. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Barbara. Flavius Abascantus the husband for his well-deserving wife. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600688.summary: A funerary inscription with two separate dedications, one for Aemilia by her father and another for Flavia Barbara by her husband, both likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600689.translation: To the Aufanian Mothers, Marcus Iulius Gratus.
EDCS-05600689.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the Matres Aufaniae by Marcus Iulius Gratus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600690.summary: An inscription possibly related to land measurement or property boundaries, specifying a width of five feet, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600691.summary: A dedication or memorial inscription for Publius Cavius Seneca, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600692.summary: A dedication or memorial inscription for Cornelius Silvanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600694.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fructus lived 45 years. Dutiful among his own. He is buried here. Stop, traveler.
EDCS-05600694.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Fructus at the age of 45, with a typical exhortation to passersby, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600695.translation: Graphe, the slave girl of Gaius Iulius Iullus, lived 17 years. She is buried here. I ask you, as you pass by, say 'May the earth lie lightly on you.'
EDCS-05600695.summary: A funerary inscription for Graphe, the slave girl of Gaius Iulius Iullus, detailing her age and including a plea to passersby, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600697.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Panthea lived 10 years. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600697.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Panthea at the age of ten, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600698.translation: Quintus Postumius Hyginus and his wife Postumia Cypare.
EDCS-05600698.summary: This inscription likely records the names of a couple, possibly for a funerary monument.

EDCS-05600699.translation: The bones of Preputsa, daughter of Eunus, aged 25.
EDCS-05600699.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age and parentage of the deceased.

EDCS-05600700.translation: Pylades, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600700.summary: This is a funerary epitaph invoking a blessing for the deceased named Pylades.

EDCS-05600701.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades of Rufinus.
EDCS-05600701.summary: This funerary inscription dedicates the monument to the departed spirit of Rufinus.

EDCS-05600702.translation: To the Divine Shades of Sulpicia Nevia.
EDCS-05600702.summary: This funerary inscription dedicates the monument to the departed spirit of Sulpicia Nevia.

EDCS-05600708.translation: Lucius, freedman of S---. I [set this up?].
EDCS-05600708.summary: This fragmented inscription names a freedman and likely indicates he erected the monument.

EDCS-05600712.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age of the deceased.

EDCS-05600717.translation: Quintus Memmius Lupus gives and dedicates this exedra as a gift.
EDCS-05600717.summary: This inscription records a donation, specifically an exedra, by Quintus Memmius Lupus.

EDCS-05600719.translation: Lucius Cornelius Drosus, aged one year.
EDCS-05600719.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and very young age of the deceased.

EDCS-05600720.translation: This tomb covers two dear pledges of their parents; the inscription also indicates their names. Fate's first lot fell upon the boy, but a sad day renews healthy wounds. And she who was recently a daughter is now ashes. Festiva, aged 11. Sodalis, a young child. They lie here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Rogatus gives [this].
EDCS-05600720.summary: This complex funerary inscription records the deaths of two young children, a daughter named Festiva and another child named Sodalis, and invokes blessings for them.

EDCS-05600721.translation: Salvia, dear to her family, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600721.summary: A tombstone inscription for Salvia, stating she is buried here and wishing her peace.

EDCS-05600722.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Valerius Sabinianus, aged 35, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600722.summary: A tombstone inscription for Lucius Valerius Sabinianus, stating his age and that he is buried here.

EDCS-05600723.translation: Valeria Logas, aged 100, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600723.summary: A tombstone inscription for Valeria Logas, stating her remarkable age and that she is buried here.

EDCS-05600727.translation: Lucius Catinius Restitutus willingly gives this to Sila, daughter of Lucius Catinius.
EDCS-05600727.summary: An inscription recording a gift from Lucius Catinius Restitutus to Sila, his daughter, likely a dedication or funerary marker.

EDCS-05600728.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name and age of Aemilia Romana.

EDCS-05600731.translation: Valeria Situllina, daughter of Caius, perpetual priestess, appointed by decree of the town councillors of the municipality of Cartimitanus, made this at her own expense on her own land and dedicated it after providing a feast.
EDCS-05600731.summary: An inscription detailing the construction and dedication of a public building or monument by Valeria Situllina, a priestess in Cartimitanus.

EDCS-05600735.translation: Marcus Aemilius Marcianus, son of Marcus, aged 43, dutiful to his family, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600735.summary: A tombstone inscription for Marcus Aemilius Marcianus, stating his age and that he is buried here.

EDCS-05600736.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. L. Valerianus lived 31 years, dutiful to his family, lies here.
EDCS-05600736.summary: A tombstone inscription for L. Valerianus, stating his age and that he is buried here.

EDCS-05600737.translation: Successus, a slave, aged 30, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600737.summary: A tombstone inscription for Successus, a slave, stating his age and that he is buried here.

EDCS-05600738.translation: Chrestos, 6 months old, dutiful to his family, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600738.summary: A tombstone inscription for Chrestos, an infant, stating his age and that he is buried here.

EDCS-05601335.translation: Sacred to Diana. Quintus Antonius Crescens Severianus gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05601335.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Diana by Quintus Antonius Crescens Severianus.

EDCS-05601336.translation: Sacred to the Spring. Flavia Severa, according to her vow.
EDCS-05601336.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Spring by Flavia Severa.

EDCS-05601338.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Ianuaria, aged 10, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Avaia Maxsuma, her most devoted mother, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05601338.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Ianuaria, aged 10, set up by her mother, Avaia Maxsuma.

EDCS-05601341.translation: Lucius Ferronius, freedman of Lucius, greetings.
EDCS-05601341.summary: This is a short inscription, possibly a greeting or an epitaph, for Lucius Ferronius.

EDCS-05601342.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Flavius Callistus, aged 70, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05601342.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Callistus, aged 70.

EDCS-05601344.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucretia Optatina lived for 30 years and six months, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05601344.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucretia Optatina, who lived for 30 years and six months.

EDCS-05601346.translation: Lucentina, aged 10, dear to her family, lies here.
EDCS-05601346.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucentina, aged 10.

EDCS-05601348.translation: Titus Stlaccius Sodalis, aged 18, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Titus Stlaccius Secundus, aged 24. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05601348.summary: This inscription contains two funerary epitaphs, one for Titus Stlaccius Sodalis (aged 18) and another for Titus Stlaccius Secundus (aged 24).

EDCS-05601351.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Servando, a pallbearer, aged 25.
EDCS-05601351.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Servando, a pallbearer, aged 25.

EDCS-05601392.translation: Aurelius Pacatianus, son of Manus, owner of leopards, flayer for the gymnasium, arranged for this to be paid off.
EDCS-05601392.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a debt by Aurelius Pacatianus, who owned leopards and worked as a flayer for a gymnasium.

EDCS-05601409.translation: To Augusta Salus, Lucius Vibius Secundus gives from his own property.
EDCS-05601409.summary: An inscription recording a private dedication to Augusta Salus by Lucius Vibius Secundus.

EDCS-05601410.translation: OSS donated a gilded equestrian statue to Pompeius with his own money.
EDCS-05601410.summary: An inscription recording the donation of a gilded equestrian statue to Pompeius by OSS.

EDCS-05900030.translation: Lucius Annius Rufinius, son of Marcius, lies here.
EDCS-05900030.summary: A tomb inscription for Lucius Annius Rufinius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05900031.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caesia Agrippina lived 56 years. Titus Iulius and Galeria Eumices erected this for their wife. She lies here.
EDCS-05900031.summary: A funerary inscription for Caesia Agrippina, set up by her husband and possibly a relative.

EDCS-05900032.translation: Iulia Camira lived 10 years. Iulius Clarus and Aelia Felicissima erected this for their dearest daughter according to her will. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05900032.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Camira, erected by her parents in accordance with her will.

EDCS-05900034.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Iuliana, freedwoman of Nasonis, aged 6 years. For Iulia Levisa, aged 22 years.
EDCS-05900034.summary: A funerary inscription for two individuals, Iuliana and Iulia Levisa, of different ages and possibly different social statuses.

EDCS-05900041.translation: Valerius Flavin C. PEL lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05900041.summary: A tomb inscription for Valerius Flavin C. PEL, with a common epitaph.

EDCS-05900043.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sura erected this tombstone for Aemilius, her freedman, and saw to the creation of this monument as a memorial.
EDCS-05900043.summary: A funerary inscription where Sura commemorates her freedman Aemilius with a tombstone and monument.

EDCS-05900045.translation: Cloutius, son of Micius, willingly fulfilled his vow to Iuppiter Anca ius.
EDCS-05900045.summary: An inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to the god Iuppiter by Cloutius.

EDCS-05900046.translation: Iulius Felix [dedicated this] to Domina Sancta.
EDCS-05900046.summary: A dedication by Iulius Felix to a deity or person named Domina Sancta.

EDCS-05900056.summary: This inscription is a simple name, likely identifying a person, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-06100351.translation: Lucius Domitius EI CTEN made this offering according to his vow.

EDCS-07000601.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Galato lived [for] years and 2 months. I beg you, traveler, as you pass by, say 'May the earth lie lightly upon you'.
EDCS-07000601.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Galato, who lived for an unspecified number of years and 2 months, and includes a common plea for the deceased.

EDCS-07500001.translation: Curtia Pelagia, freedwoman of Sextus, is buried here.
EDCS-07500001.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of Curtia Pelagia, a freedwoman of Sextus.

EDCS-07500012.translation: Cultronia Prima, aged 6 years, 16 feet in front, 16 feet in front.
EDCS-07500012.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Cultronia Prima and possibly the dimensions of her burial plot.

EDCS-07500013.translation: Fabius I lived piously in his own [property/household].
EDCS-07500013.summary: This inscription appears to be a personal epitaph for Fabius I, suggesting a pious life within his own domain.

EDCS-07500016.translation: Nunnia Communis is buried here, dear to her family. I beg you, traveler, as you pass by, say 'May the earth lie lightly upon you'.
EDCS-07500016.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nunnia Communis, expressing affection from her family and including a common plea for the deceased.

EDCS-07500019.summary: This inscription records the age of Anatoli, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-07500023.translation: Lucius Valerius Lucillus, aged 40 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-07500023.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Lucillus, aged 40, including a common plea for the deceased.

EDCS-07500031.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Calpurnius Honoratus, aged 37 years, is buried here.
EDCS-07500031.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Calpurnius Honoratus, aged 37, dedicated to the spirits of the departed.

EDCS-07500033.translation: To Rufo, who died at sixty years of age. May the earth lie light upon you, Urbana.
EDCS-07500033.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rufo, likely set up by Urbana, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500041.summary: This is a very brief dedication, likely funerary or votive, to Eumeneus, son of Aquila, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500056.translation: To Gaius Curvius, son of Gaius, of the Sergia tribe, Rusticus, for the second time a *quattuorvir*. Tertius, his freedman.
EDCS-07500056.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Curvius Rusticus, a local magistrate, likely erected by his freedman Tertius in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500057.translation: Gaius Annius Senecio, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, for himself and his family.
EDCS-07500057.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Gaius Annius Senecio for himself and his family, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500061.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. A. Felicia made this in memory of her well-deserving husband, Germanus. He lived for 60 years, 5 months, and 7 days. May the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-07500061.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Germanus, who lived 60 years, 5 months, and 7 days, and was dedicated by his wife A. Felicia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500063.translation: Marcus Petrucidius, son of Marcus, governor, to Hercules of Carteia.
EDCS-07500063.summary: This is a votive dedication made by Marcus Petrucidius, a Roman governor, to the god Hercules at Carteia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500064.translation: To Lucius Scribonius Libo, son of Lucius, former quaestor, governor, in his absence.
EDCS-07500064.summary: This is a dedication, possibly honorary or funerary, to Lucius Scribonius Libo, who held the offices of quaestor and governor, dating to the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-07500066.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valeria Pallace, aged 27 years, 6 months. May she be placed here. May the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-07500066.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Pallace, who died at the age of 27 years and 6 months, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500067.translation: Licinius Gratus, freedman of Gaius. May the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-07500067.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Licinius Gratus, a freedman, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500070.translation: Marcus Baebius Hilarus, son of Marcus, and Baebia Cosmetia, daughter of Marcus. 15 feet in front, 10 feet in front.
EDCS-07500070.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land, possibly a tomb or garden, belonging to Marcus Baebius Hilarus and Baebia Cosmetia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500071.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Voluptas is buried here. I ask you, as you pass by, to say: 'May the earth lie lightly upon you.'
EDCS-07500071.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Voluptas, likely from the Roman Imperial period, expressing a common wish for the deceased's repose.

EDCS-07500077.translation: Lucius Memmius Rusticus is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-07500094.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caecilius Elpistus, aged 33, beloved by his own. May you fare well.

EDCS-07500095.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Lacertaria, aged 51, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500096.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Servinius Restutus lived 27 years, beloved by his own. May the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-07500097.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ianuari Restitutus, aged 31. May the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-07500098.translation: Septumilla, aged 7, beloved by her father Symmacchus and her own. May she lie here, and may the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-07500182.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Baebia Nicostratis, aged 45, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-07500198.translation: To the divine Manes. Herennia, 21 years old, dear to her family; may the earth lie lightly on you.

EDCS-07500199.translation: Martialis, 1 year and 4 months old, dear to his family, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.

EDCS-07500200.translation: Prepusa, dear to her family, 26 years old, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.

EDCS-07500201.translation: Suavis, 8 years old, and Apolonia, 1 year old, dear to their family, lie here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07500201.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Suavis (8 years old) and Apolonia (1 year old).

EDCS-07500202.translation: Faustilla Gaditana, 5 months old, dear to her family, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07500202.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Faustilla Gaditana, who died at the age of 5 months.

EDCS-07500203.translation: Crispus, pious among his own people, [dedicated this].
EDCS-07500203.summary: This is a dedication by Crispus, likely on a monument.

EDCS-07500246.translation: In the consulship of Publius Memmius Regulus and Lucius Fulcinius Trio, the senate and people of Iptuccita established a guest-friendship with the colonists of the colony of Claritas Iulia Ucubis, and their children and descendants, for themselves and their children and descendants; Caius Trebecius Lucanus, Caius Attius Severus, and Lucius Catinius Optatus acted as officials.
EDCS-07500246.summary: This inscription records the establishment of a guest-friendship agreement between the people of Iptuccita and the colonists of Ucubis, likely dating to the consulship mentioned.

EDCS-07500250.translation: By decree of the decurions, [this was dedicated] to Quintus Calpurnius Quadratus.
EDCS-07500250.summary: This inscription indicates an honorific decree by the town council for Quintus Calpurnius Quadratus.

EDCS-07500251.translation: Of Sinni Nicare, of Sinni Nicare.
EDCS-07500251.summary: This inscription is a dedication or possessive marker for Sinni Nicare.

EDCS-07500253.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cornelia Marcina, daughter of Publius, lived for 45 years. Lucius Agri Marcellus and Severus [erected this] to their most loving mother.
EDCS-07500253.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Marcina, set up by her sons Lucius Agri Marcellus and Severus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07500255.translation: To Diana Augusta, Fabia, daughter of Caius Fabiana, with the ornaments listed below, having given a feast, gives and dedicates: a casket with 7 cylinders, armlets with 20 cylinders, frontlets with 13 cylinders, a sash with 18 cylinders, and 2 gemmed rings.
EDCS-07500255.summary: This is a dedication of offerings to Diana Augusta by Fabia Fabiana, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07500256.translation: To Martialis, slave of Lucius Cornelius Pusionis.
EDCS-07500256.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Martialis, the slave of Lucius Cornelius Pusionis.

EDCS-07800004.translation: Lucius Iulius, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-07800004.summary: This is a votive dedication by Lucius Iulius.

EDCS-07800009.summary: The meaning of this fragmented inscription is unclear, possibly relating to arrival or offerings.

EDCS-07800034.translation: Adherbal, 50 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you, I ask.
EDCS-07800034.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Adherbal, including a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-07800035.translation: Rogata, aged 20, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-07800035.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Rogata, who died at age 20.

EDCS-07800040.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Phiale, aged 20, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. To the Divine Spirits. Phiale, aged 25, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-07800040.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Phiale, recorded twice with different ages, suggesting a possible error or a later addition.

EDCS-07800049.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the name (partially abbreviated) and age of the deceased.

EDCS-07800077.translation: To Eumeneus, freedman of I. Aquila.
EDCS-07800077.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or a funerary monument for Eumeneus, a freedman.

EDCS-07800099.translation: A. C. I. III A. Noctes, aged 50.
EDCS-07800099.summary: This is a tombstone inscription stating the name (partially abbreviated) and age of the deceased.

EDCS-07800102.summary: This inscription is enigmatic but might be a personal motto or dedication related to Milo and Rome.

EDCS-07800103.translation: Punic apples, lupines. R. All will be...
EDCS-07800103.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment of a list of goods or an epitaph with unclear meaning.

EDCS-07800107.translation: IGI. CIAII ONIS. May the earth lie lightly upon you in the bath.
EDCS-07800107.summary: This is a tombstone inscription, possibly for someone named IGI CIAII ONIS, with a wish for them to rest lightly, perhaps near a bath.

EDCS-07800112.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to provide a clear translation or summary, but it may relate to a person named Lacunarrius.

EDCS-07800134.translation: Sabina, an honorable woman, lived more or less 20 years.
EDCS-07800134.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Sabina, an honorable woman who died around the age of 20.

EDCS-08300403.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Trebia Aphrodite, aged 26 years, 11 months, and 11 days, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, Petronius Epagatianus, for his most pious wife.
EDCS-08300403.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Trebia Aphrodite, dedicated by her husband Petronius Epagatianus.

EDCS-08700007.translation: To Gaius Grattius, son of Lucius, quinquennalis, Marcus Sassius, son of Lucius, duumvir, and their freedman Delphus, from the order of the people of Mentesa.
EDCS-08700007.summary: This inscription honors local magistrates (quinquennalis and duumvir) and a freedman, likely from the town of Mentesa.

EDCS-08700009.summary: This very short inscription likely records a dedication or tomb for Fabia, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-08700037.translation: I, Orsvs, a resident and native of Aurgitanus, a vigintivir, and my future wife Stina, are made by fate. Now I gladly live, and as much as I drink, so much I pour out for myself.
EDCS-08700037.summary: This funerary inscription is a self-epitaph by Orsvs, a vigintivir from Aurgitanus, who prepared for his own death and that of his wife Stina.

EDCS-08700062.translation: To Chioni, son of Lucius Chionius.
EDCS-08700062.summary: This brief inscription records a dedication or tomb for Chioni, son of Lucius Chionius.

EDCS-08700063.translation: To Chioni, son of Lucius Chionius.
EDCS-08700063.summary: This brief inscription records a dedication or tomb for Chioni, son of Lucius Chionius.

EDCS-08700064.summary: This very short inscription may record a dedication or tomb for a person or persons named Sollemnis.

EDCS-08700065.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaetula, aged 80, pious, rests here in her own place. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700065.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gaetula, an elderly woman.

EDCS-08700078.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the virtue of Pietas (Piety) by Iulius.

EDCS-08700102.translation: As aedile, Lucius Scipio, by his will.
EDCS-08700102.summary: This inscription records an action or dedication made by Lucius Scipio as aedile, according to his will.

EDCS-08700117.translation: Marcus Atilius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, [dedicated this] for himself and his wife Iulia Dionissia.
EDCS-08700117.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Atilius Severus for himself and his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700128.translation: Caius Iulius Noster, son of Caius, of the Sergia tribe.
EDCS-08700128.summary: A personal identification, likely part of a larger inscription or funerary monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700132.summary: A simple patronymic, likely part of a funerary inscription, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700147.summary: A personal identification, likely part of a funerary inscription, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700164.translation: Built by the order of Bishop Ceprianus.
EDCS-08700164.summary: A dedication for a building project, indicating construction under the authority of Bishop Ceprianus, likely from the Late Roman or Early Christian period.

EDCS-08700174.translation: I have sinned, my Lord, and I acknowledge my sin. Amen.
EDCS-08700174.summary: A penitential statement, likely from a Christian context, expressing personal sin and acknowledgment, possibly from a private devotional object or inscription.

EDCS-08700177.summary: A fragment of an inscription, likely referring to saints, possibly part of a dedication to martyrs or a church, dating to the Early Christian period.

EDCS-08700178.translation: Trutila offers [this] to Saint Iusta Rovina.
EDCS-08700178.summary: A votive or funerary offering by Trutila to Saint Iusta Rovina, dating to the Early Christian period.

EDCS-08700179.summary: A votive or funerary offering by Ebantius Constantinus, dating to the Early Christian period.

EDCS-08700181.translation: Iabasta offers [this] to Saint Iusta and Rofina. Amen.
EDCS-08700181.summary: A votive or funerary offering by Iabasta to Saints Iusta and Rofina, dating to the Early Christian period.

EDCS-08700185.summary: This inscription is a fragment of an offering made to a holy entity.

EDCS-08700197.summary: This inscription refers to holy women, likely within a religious context.

EDCS-08700204.translation: Campanus, from the family of Musclus
EDCS-08700204.summary: This inscription likely records the name of an individual, Campanus, possibly with a family affiliation.

EDCS-08700222.summary: This inscription identifies Clodia Hospita, a freedwoman of Appius, likely on a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-08700230.translation: To Quintus Lepidius, son of Quintus Lepidius, of the Fabia tribe, an epulo (feast or public meal) given by the order.
EDCS-08700230.summary: This inscription records a public feast or meal provided by an order, dedicated to Quintus Lepidius.

EDCS-08700232.summary: This inscription identifies Fortunata as a Roman woman, possibly on a funerary monument.

EDCS-08700275.summary: This inscription is a dedication or dedication to a lady named Iohanna.

EDCS-08700277.translation: Potentius, servant of Christ, lived for 53 years, departed in peace.
EDCS-08700277.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Potentius, a Christian servant who lived 53 years.

EDCS-08700311.translation: This basilica was consecrated to holy Mary on the second day before the Kalends of June in the year 668 (of the Spanish era, i.e., 630 AD). The lord Bacauda, bishop, dedicated this church. The Most High founded it through Eulalia and her son Paul the monk. Holy altar of the Lord.
EDCS-08700311.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a basilica to the Holy Mary in 630 AD by Bishop Bacauda, with its foundation attributed to Eulalia and her son Paul.

EDCS-08700312.translation: Eulalia lived for 94 years, she will rest in the peace of God on the Kalends of September, in the sixth indiction, in the year 700 (of the Spanish era, i.e., 662 AD).
EDCS-08700312.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Eulalia, who lived 94 years and died in 662 AD.

EDCS-08700350.translation: What life formerly brought you joyful and bright, fate has now taken away; and here lies the mortal, bound by an imperial command, a divine decree, Euresis, having completed thirty-eight years of life, crushed by dust, snatched by the fate of death.
EDCS-08700350.summary: This epitaph records the death of Euresis, who lived thirty-eight years, and speaks of fate taking her life.

EDCS-08700374.translation: Abel departed in peace. VVIL [name] had [lived] XXXVII (?) years.
EDCS-08700374.summary: This inscription records the departure in peace of Abel and provides a name and age of the deceased, likely from a tomb.

EDCS-08700376.translation: The flowers of childhood he took from us with deep sorrow and grief. LA [name] lived 10 years, received with repentance, he rests in peace on the eleventh day before the Kalends of July, in the year 700 of the era.
EDCS-08700376.summary: This epitaph laments the death of a child named LA who lived 10 years, died in the year 664 AD, and rests in peace.

EDCS-08700377.translation: Felix, may you live! M A [age] LX [years]
EDCS-08700377.summary: This is a short funerary inscription for Felix, stating his age as 60 years.

EDCS-08700384.translation: Pious one, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700384.summary: This is a standard funerary wish for a pious individual, asking for the earth to rest lightly upon them.

EDCS-08700398.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Mother Sentia Domestica lived 76 years; may the earth lie lightly on her; her son placed this.
EDCS-08700398.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the Divine Manes, records that Sentia Domestica lived 76 years and was placed here by her son.

EDCS-08700399.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Clodia Lasciva, 36 years old.
EDCS-08700399.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the Divine Manes, records the age of Clodia Lasciva as 36 years.

EDCS-08700400.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Eutychia, 3 years old, pious in her family, rests here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700400.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the Divine Manes, records that Eutychia, a pious child of 3 years, rests here.

EDCS-08700401.translation: II Fusca, [age] years old, pious in her family, rests here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700401.summary: This funerary inscription records that Fusca, a pious individual, rests here and requests the earth lie lightly upon her.

EDCS-08700402.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. T Inv, 18 years old, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700402.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the Divine Manes, records that T Inv was 18 years old and requests the earth lie lightly upon them.

EDCS-08700404.summary: This is a personal name, likely from a funerary monument, with the gentilicium and cognomen of a free man.

EDCS-08700412.summary: This is a personal name, likely from a funerary monument, possibly indicating a freedman or a slave with the same name as his master.

EDCS-08700414.translation: Sacred to Jupiter. Valerius Flavius, one of the five priests, gave it.
EDCS-08700414.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by a priest, possibly from a public or private shrine.

EDCS-08700423.translation: John the sinner cries out: Have mercy on me, God, have mercy on me. Died on the day.
EDCS-08700423.summary: This is a Christian inscription, a personal prayer for mercy, likely from a tombstone, with a reference to the date of death.

EDCS-08700446.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary dedication, mentioning a father and son, with an unclear abbreviation.

EDCS-08700484.translation: L. Chionius, son of L. Chionius, of L. Chionius.
EDCS-08700484.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary monument, indicating a relationship within the Chionius family.

EDCS-08700487.translation: Son of L. Chionius, of L. Chionius.
EDCS-08700487.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary monument, indicating a relationship within the Chionius family.

EDCS-08700495.translation: Salvo Ausentio, may you live. Fortunio.
EDCS-08700495.summary: This is a greeting or wish for good health, possibly from a casual inscription or a dedication.

EDCS-08700496.translation: Salvo Ausentio, may you live. Fortunio.
EDCS-08700496.summary: This is a greeting or wish for good health, possibly from a casual inscription or a dedication.

EDCS-08700500.translation: Salvo Ausentio, may you live. Fortunio.
EDCS-08700500.summary: This is a greeting or wish for good health, possibly from a casual inscription or a dedication.

EDCS-08700510.translation: Salvius Ausentius, may you live prosperously, Fortunius.
EDCS-08700510.summary: A short, possibly funerary, dedication or wish.
