EDCS-08700511.summary: A fragmentary inscription, likely a name or epitaph.

EDCS-08700514.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, possibly the beginning of a longer text.

EDCS-08700527.summary: A repetitive inscription, possibly a name inscribed multiple times for emphasis or a magical formula.

EDCS-08700528.translation: In this tomb rests the body of Belesarius, servant of Christ, founder of this basilica, who lived in this age more or less, departed in peace on the day of the year 700.
EDCS-08700528.summary: A funerary inscription for Belesarius, a servant of Christ and founder of a basilica, dated to 700 AD.

EDCS-08700530.summary: A repetitive inscription, likely a name or a descriptive term used for emphasis.

EDCS-08700555.translation: Frigitto, infant, lived 7 months and days.
EDCS-08700555.summary: A very brief epitaph for an infant named Frigitto.

EDCS-08700597.summary: A funerary inscription, likely a tombstone marking the burial place of Asella Flavia.

EDCS-08700607.translation: I, Peregrinus, am happy, received into peace. Achilles, all you who are sojourners, it will be well with you.
EDCS-08700607.summary: A funerary inscription that seems to welcome other travelers or sojourners into peace, possibly from a tomb.

EDCS-08700608.summary: An inscription with repetitive names or epithets, possibly wishing happiness or recording a joyful event.

EDCS-08700612.summary: This inscription repeats the name Nicare twice, possibly a dedication or a marker.

EDCS-08700613.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragmented repetition of names or titles, possibly related to Nicare.

EDCS-08700614.summary: This inscription presents two names or titles, Nicare and Sollemnis, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-08700615.summary: This inscription consists of a repeated word, likely 'solemn' or a name/title, possibly for ritualistic or commemorative purposes.

EDCS-08700678.translation: Lucius Junius Faustus, son of Lucius, Mamius Faustinus, obtained Roman citizenship by honor and by the favor of Emperor Caesar Augustus Vespasian.
EDCS-08700678.summary: This inscription records the granting of Roman citizenship by Emperor Vespasian to Lucius Junius Faustus and Mamius Faustinus, likely in the Flavian period (69-96 AD).

EDCS-08700716.translation: Here rested Ann, who lived six years and five months, in peace.
EDCS-08700716.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a person named Ann, who died at the age of six years and five months.

EDCS-08700734.translation: Horme, freedwoman of Arria, aged 10, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700734.summary: This is a tombstone for Horme, a freedwoman of Arria, who died at the age of 10, with a common Latin epitaph wishing her peace.

EDCS-08700738.translation: Of the lord Ervig the king.
EDCS-08700738.summary: This inscription is a short dedication or possessive statement pertaining to King Ervig.

EDCS-08700762.translation: In the name of the lord, Nocidius, priest, departed in peace on the twelfth day before the Kalends of May in the first year of our lord Stephen the bishop.
EDCS-08700762.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nocidius, a priest, dated by the regnal year of Bishop Stephen, likely from the early medieval period.

EDCS-08700777.translation: Postumia Aciliana, daughter of Marcus, from Baxonia, ordered a statue to be placed for herself in her will, for 8,000,000 sesterces; also ornaments of 42 northern pearls, and a line of 22 cylinders, and a band of 63 cylinders with 100 pearls; also a silver line with 12 pearls; Lucius Fabius Superstes, her son, dedicated it, with silver spatulas set with gems placed above it, in addition to the sum written above, and a ring worth 7,000,000 sesterces with a jasper.
EDCS-08700777.summary: This is a dedication inscription by Lucius Fabius Superstes for a statue and ornaments commissioned in the will of his mother, Postumia Aciliana, detailing significant expenditures and precious materials.

EDCS-08700802.translation: Dovs the son of Ovdarpil, a perpetual Augustan official, gave a small shrine on public land as a gift.
EDCS-08700802.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a small shrine by a perpetual Augustan official, likely in the Roman period.

EDCS-08700810.translation: Marcus Cornelius Optatus, restored from a double illness, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08700810.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Cornelius Optatus, who dedicated a gift upon recovering from an illness, probably in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700833.translation: In the name of the Lord, this church of Saint Peter was founded by Sixerius and Wisenda.
EDCS-08700833.summary: This inscription records the founding of the church of Saint Peter by Sixerius and Wisenda, likely dating to the Early Christian period.

EDCS-08700900.translation: Postumius Castrensis gave a gift to Apollo and Aesculapius, the Augustan gods.
EDCS-08700900.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Apollo and Aesculapius by Postumius Castrensis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700922.translation: Lucius Fabius Galeria Fabianus [erected this] to his mother, Nummia Rustica, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-08700922.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Lucius Fabius Galeria Fabianus for his mother, Nummia Rustica, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700927.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for a slave named Felix, who died at the age of 20, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700933.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Secundina, freedwoman of M. Marcius, aged 33, pious, is placed here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08700933.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Secundina, a freedwoman, including a standard Latin epitaph phrase, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700969.translation: Salvius to Imerius, with good fortune.
EDCS-08700969.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or greeting from Salvius to Imerius, with an indication of good fortune, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08700971.summary: This inscription seems to be a greeting or dedication from Felix Optata to Isidore, wishing them well, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08700981.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a gift given by someone from Astigi, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08700986.translation: May Amazon live! May Amazon live!
EDCS-08700986.summary: This is a short funerary or devotional text wishing life to someone named Amazon.

EDCS-08700987.translation: May Chio live! May Chio live! May Chio live!
EDCS-08700987.summary: This is a short funerary or devotional text wishing life to someone named Chio, repeated for emphasis.

EDCS-08701021.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Forensia, a devoted servant, lived 33 years in her own home. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701021.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Forensia, a woman who died at age 33, dedicated to the Divine Spirits.

EDCS-08701044.translation: May Chio live! Chio, Chio, may Chio live!
EDCS-08701044.summary: This is a short funerary or devotional text wishing life to someone named Chio, with repeated invocations.

EDCS-08701067.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly referring to a legal or customary practice.

EDCS-08701068.summary: This inscription appears to be a collection of names, possibly a funerary or commemorative text, including Latin and possibly Greek names.

EDCS-08701076.translation: May Chio live! E C M
EDCS-08701076.summary: This is a short funerary or devotional text wishing life to someone named Chio, with the abbreviation E C M.

EDCS-08701116.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription for Quintus Fabius Macer Tispitanus.

EDCS-08701117.translation: Acilia, daughter of Publius, freedwoman METE AS ELEIA CAAESRO
EDCS-08701117.summary: This inscription records Acilia, a freedwoman of Publius, possibly indicating her family or status, with additional Greek names or words.

EDCS-08701163.translation: SHF made XX NSXI V from his own property.
EDCS-08701163.summary: This inscription records a construction or donation made by SHF, an individual identified by an abbreviation, from his own resources.

EDCS-08701185.summary: A brief greeting addressed to Lucius Pompeius, likely from a tombstone or a dedication.

EDCS-08701186.translation: He departed in faith with the god Quaicus.
EDCS-08701186.summary: This inscription records a person's death, attributing their departure to faith in a deity named Quaicus.

EDCS-08701187.summary: This phrase likely refers to a person named Marcianus in relation to Bishop Salvius, possibly a dedication or a record of presence.

EDCS-08701192.translation: She departed in peace on the day of Mars, which was the Ides of February, in the year 746 of the era, so that we may earn entry into the gate of paradise through your promised offering and token; whom afterwards her husband Adotius, in the name of Christ, buried her in this tomb, where she rested in peace, having lived for fourteen years; she was buried in the tomb. Amen.
EDCS-08701192.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Quistricia in 746 of the era, mentioning her husband Adotius and specifying her age and burial location.

EDCS-08701351.translation: Dominica, servant of God, in her twentieth year, on the day of the Sun, the day before the Nones of June.
EDCS-08701351.summary: This inscription records the death of Dominica, a servant of God, before the age of twenty, on a specific date in June, likely from the early Christian period.

EDCS-08701353.translation: Bracari, may you live with your people.
EDCS-08701353.summary: A short inscription wishing Bracari and his family a long life, possibly a funerary or a votive text.

EDCS-08701360.translation: Leontius, servant of God, lived for more or less 70 years, departed in peace on the 3rd day before the Kalends of April in the year 634 of the era.
EDCS-08701360.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Leontius, a servant of God, at approximately 70 years of age, on a specific date in the year 634 of the era.

EDCS-08701402.summary: This inscription simply identifies 'The people of Celtitana,' possibly as a collective dedication or a marker of territory.

EDCS-08701424.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Romulensia, 45 years and 6 months old. She is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701424.summary: This funerary inscription dedicates the burial of Romulensia, who died at the age of 45 years and 6 months, to the underworld spirits.

EDCS-08701427.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Rutilianus lived 22 years, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius Rutilianus, father, to his most devoted son.
EDCS-08701427.summary: This funerary inscription, set up by a father to his son, records the death of Rutilianus at the age of 22 and dedicates his burial to the underworld spirits.

EDCS-08701429.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription referring to the emperor Gnaeus Magnus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701430.translation: Chioni, may you live; Chioni, may you live
EDCS-08701430.summary: This inscription consists of a repeated wish for Chioni to live, possibly a dedication or a personal message.

EDCS-08701431.translation: Chioni, may you live; Chioni, may you live
EDCS-08701431.summary: This inscription consists of a repeated wish for Chioni to live, possibly a dedication or a personal message.

EDCS-08701432.translation: Chioni, may you live; Chioni, may you live
EDCS-08701432.summary: This inscription consists of a repeated wish for Chioni to live, possibly a dedication or a personal message.

EDCS-08901007.translation: To Faustina Augusta, daughter of Aurelius Caesar Antoninus Augustus Pius, the public assembly
EDCS-08901007.summary: This inscription dedicates something to Faustina Augusta, daughter of Antoninus Pius, by the public assembly, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08901008.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Casinius Senicio arranged for this altar to be set up for his mother, Cassinia Hermiona, after her death.
EDCS-08901008.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publius Casinius Senicio for his mother, Cassinia Hermiona, after her death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08901012.translation: Alexandria, most distinguished woman, lived about 25 years, departed in peace on the tenth day before the Kalends of January in the year 583 (AD 325). Her son Probus lived two years, ten months, departed in peace.
EDCS-08901012.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death dates of Alexandria and her young son Probus, noting their peaceful departure in AD 325.

EDCS-08901013.translation: To Diana Augusta, in memory of Fabia Prisca, daughter of Marcus. Having received the place from a decree of the council, Priscilla, her mother, took care to have this monument erected at her own expense.
EDCS-08901013.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diana Augusta by Priscilla in memory of her daughter Fabia Prisca, funded by the mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000057.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Octavia Lucana, aged 3 years, 5 months, 5 days, and 7 hours. Octavia Facundina, a devoted mother, placed this altar in her own property. Here she lies. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-09000057.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child, Octavia Lucana, set up by her mother, Octavia Facundina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000095.summary: This is a brief inscription identifying an object as belonging to or made by Restitutus the potter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000098.translation: Here lies Vitalis, servant of God.
EDCS-09000098.summary: A funerary inscription identifying the deceased as Vitalis, a servant of God, likely from the early Christian period.

EDCS-09000102.summary: An inscription dedicating an altar to the Matres Veteres, a group of deities associated with fertility and protection, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000113.summary: A dedication by Hyllus, a freedman, to his former master Gnaeus, typical of Roman funerary or commemorative inscriptions.

EDCS-09000132.translation: Reinterred in the farm called Valles, in the suburb of Obolconense, in the chapel of Saint Mary.
EDCS-09000132.summary: An inscription recording a reburial within a specific rural property and chapel, indicating a practice of relocation for burials.

EDCS-09000145.translation: Maria, faithful servant of Christ, in her lifetime cherished this place, and there her body was buried; she lived for more than forty years and withdrew in penitence in peace, on the 7th day before the Ides of March, in the second year of the reign of King Reccisvinth with the prince his father.
EDCS-09000145.summary: A complex funerary inscription for Maria, detailing her devotion, lifespan, burial, and the date of her death within a specific regnal period in Visigothic Spain.

EDCS-09000171.summary: A dedication or memorial inscription indicating Lucius Volcidius honoring Calpurnia Pulinna, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000180.translation: Reccisvinthus, deacon and servant of Christ, lived for approximately 45 years, departed in peace on the day before the Ides of July, in the era 681.
EDCS-09000180.summary: A funerary inscription for Reccisvinthus, a deacon, stating his age and date of death according to the Visigothic era.

EDCS-09000181.translation: V viliulfus, illustrious man and servant of God, lived for approximately seventy years, departed in peace on the day before the Ides of June, in the era 600.
EDCS-09000181.summary: A funerary inscription for V viliulfus, an illustrious man and servant of God, stating his approximate age and date of death according to the Visigothic era.

EDCS-09000183.translation: Servant of Christ, lived for approximately 25 years, departed in peace.
EDCS-09000183.summary: A brief funerary inscription for an unnamed servant of Christ, stating their age and peaceful passing.

EDCS-09000185.summary: An incomplete inscription, possibly a dedication or a funerary marker, mentioning Gaius At[---] and Pollio.

EDCS-09000197.translation: Under Emperor Philippus Augustus and Caesar Philippus, the guild of the Patrician Cordubensian workshop builders enrolled Bellus Licinianus and his family as patrons; later, Bellus Licinianus received the guild of the Patrician Cordubensian workshop builders into his family and patronage.
EDCS-09000197.summary: This inscription records the adoption of Bellus Licinianus as patron by a guild of builders in Corduba during the consulship of Philippus and Philippus, likely in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09000208.translation: Marciana, may you live in Christ.
EDCS-09000208.summary: This is a short funerary or dedicatory inscription wishing a long life in Christ to a woman named Marciana, likely from the Christian period.

EDCS-09000279.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, the Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus, invested with tribunician power.
EDCS-09000279.summary: This is a formal titulary inscription dedicating honors to the Roman Emperor Constantine, likely dating to his reign in the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-09000282.translation: To the most valiant and most indulgent prince, our lord Constantius, the perpetual victorious Augustus, Decimius Germanianus, most illustrious man, governor of the province of Baetica, dedicated, devoted to his divine power and majesty.
EDCS-09000282.summary: This inscription records the devotion of Decimius Germanianus, governor of Baetica, to Emperor Constantius, likely Constantius II, highlighting his loyalty and the emperor's power in the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-09000293.translation: The council of the Tipasenses offered a tablet of patronage to Flavius Hyginus, most distinguished man, count, governor of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, for his just merits, after his administration was completed.
EDCS-09000293.summary: This inscription records the granting of a tablet of patronage to Flavius Hyginus by the council of Tipasenses in Mauretania Caesariensis for his just administration, likely in the late 4th or early 5th century CE.

EDCS-09000439.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Asicia Facunda, aged 37, dutiful to her companion Manius Asicius Pelops, freedman of Manius, aged 50, are buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000439.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Asicia Facunda and Manius Asicius Pelops, a couple buried together, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000456.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Kalva, aged 5 years and 7 months, and Aphrodisia, aged 2 years and 5 months, are buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000456.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two young children, Kalva and Aphrodisia, who died at very young ages, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000523.translation: Mussia, freedwoman of a woman, Rosia, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000523.summary: This is a short funerary inscription for Mussia, identified as the freedwoman of a woman named Rosia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000524.translation: Lucius Postumius Barnaeus, freedman of a woman, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000524.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Postumius Barnaeus, identified as a freedman of a woman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000551.translation: Rast, freedman of Lucius, Rast, freedman.
EDCS-09000551.summary: This inscription appears to record two individuals named Rast, both identified as freedmen, one of Lucius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000570.translation: Caius Tanonius As, son of Lucius Marius Phoe; Maria Persini, daughter of Maria Luci; Secun[da] Caecilia, daughter of Quinti Secu; Lucius Marius, son of Lucius Phoe.
EDCS-09000570.summary: This inscription appears to be a family funerary monument, likely Roman Republican or early Imperial, listing several individuals and their parentage.

EDCS-09000666.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Marcus Iunius Ultimus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000677.translation: Of the holy martyrs of Christ Jesus, Faustus, January, and Martial, Zoilus and Acisclus...
EDCS-09000677.summary: This inscription is a dedication or invocation related to Christian martyrs, likely from the late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-09000680.summary: This inscription is a simple mention of two important Christian saints, possibly part of a larger devotional context.

EDCS-09000681.translation: Sesuldus offers the gift of Saint Vincent the deacon.
EDCS-09000681.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Sesuldus to Saint Vincent the deacon, likely from the early medieval period.

EDCS-09000682.translation: Lampadius the bishop lived in religious life for 60 years, in the episcopate for 17 years and five months, he was received into peace on the 13th day before the Kalends of October in the era 587.
EDCS-09000682.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Bishop Lampadius, recording his age, length of episcopate, and date of death in the era 587 (likely AD 581).

EDCS-09000683.summary: This is a simple epitaph or identification for Samson the bishop, likely from the early medieval period.

EDCS-09000685.translation: Acantia, an honorable woman, servant of God, lived 23 and a half years, departed in peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of March in the era 634.
EDCS-09000685.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Acantia, a servant of God, recording her age and date of death in the era 634 (likely AD 630).

EDCS-09000686.translation: Calamarius, servant of God, lived fifty years, departed in peace on the 3rd day before the Kalends of October [?], in the era 643. Acantia, servant of God, lived twenty-three and a half years, departed in peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of March, in the era 634.
EDCS-09000686.summary: This inscription contains two funerary epitaphs: one for Calamarius (died era 643) and one for Acantia (died era 634), both servants of God.

EDCS-09000689.translation: Anerius, servant of Christ, lived fifty-three years more or less, departed from this world on the third day before the Ides of September in the 720th year of the current era.
EDCS-09000689.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Anerius, a servant of Christ, recording his approximate age and date of death in the era 720 (likely AD 716).

EDCS-09000693.translation: Felix, servant of Christ, lived 43 years more or less, departed in peace on the Kalends of December in the era 666.
EDCS-09000693.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Felix, a servant of Christ, dating to the era 666.

EDCS-09000694.translation: Fortuna, an honorable woman, lived 40 years and 6 months, departed in peace on the Februarias.
EDCS-09000694.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fortuna, an honorable woman, with an incomplete era date.

EDCS-09000695.translation: Leovigildus, servant of God, lived 46 years.
EDCS-09000695.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Leovigildus, a servant of God, dating to the 5th or 6th century.

EDCS-09000696.translation: Porpuria, servant of God, lived 40 years and 8 months, departed in peace on the 12th day before the Kalends of May in the era 563.
EDCS-09000696.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Porpuria, a servant of God, dating to the era 563.

EDCS-09000697.translation: Samon, a citizen of Toulouse, departed in peace on the eighth day before the Kalends of November, lived 53 years.
EDCS-09000697.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Samon, a citizen of Toulouse, dating to the 5th or 6th century.

EDCS-09000699.translation: Teudosius, servant of Christ, lived 43 years, departed in peace on the 12th day before the Kalends of May in the era 601.
EDCS-09000699.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Teudosius, a servant of Christ, dating to the era 601.

EDCS-09000700.translation: To Victoria of good memory, who lived 36 years, his most sweet wife, Aurelius Felix received her into peace.
EDCS-09000700.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Felix to his wife Victoria, dating to the 5th or 6th century.

EDCS-09000711.translation: servant of God, lived 20 years, departed in peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of May in the era 604.
EDCS-09000711.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed servant of God, dating to the era 604.

EDCS-09000712.translation: servant of God, lived 10 years, departed in peace.
EDCS-09000712.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed servant of God, dating to the 5th or 6th century.

EDCS-09000720.translation: died from an inguinal wound in the era 647.
EDCS-09000720.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a death from an inguinal wound in the era 647.

EDCS-09000721.translation: He lived 21 years, departed in peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of October in the era 610. Whoever disturbs this tomb shall have a share with Judas.
EDCS-09000721.summary: A funerary inscription dating to the era 610 (likely 351/352 CE), warning against tomb disturbance.

EDCS-09000742.translation: From the workshop of Leontius. From the workshop of Leontius. From the workshop of Leontius. From the workshop of Leontius. From the workshop of Leontius. From the workshop of Leontius. From the workshop of Leontius. From the workshop of Leontius. From the workshop of Leontius. From the workshop.
EDCS-09000742.summary: A repetitive inscription identifying the maker or origin of a product as 'Leontius', possibly from a workshop.

EDCS-09000743.summary: A very brief inscription, possibly a name or a title ('Solemnis') associated with 'Nicare'.

EDCS-09000750.translation: Asper, servant of Christ, lived about 75 years, departed in peace on the 5th day before the Nones of May in the era 670.
EDCS-09000750.summary: A funerary inscription dating to the era 670 (likely 611/612 CE), recording the death of Asper, a servant of Christ.

EDCS-09000752.translation: Eustadia, a virgin and servant of Christ, lived about 36 years in this world, preserving the purity of her body, joyfully ascending the threshold of heaven, rested in peace on the 11th day before the Kalends of December in the running era 687.
EDCS-09000752.summary: A funerary inscription dating to the era 687 (likely 627/628 CE), commemorating Eustadia, a virgin and servant of Christ.

EDCS-09000753.translation: Ugnericus, servant of Christ, lived about 40 years, departed in peace on the 6th day before the Ides of April in the era 653.
EDCS-09000753.summary: A funerary inscription dating to the era 653 (likely 593/594 CE), recording the death of Ugnericus, a servant of Christ.

EDCS-09000760.translation: These hollow rocks contain the limbs of Oppilanus, of a notable lineage by birth, conspicuous in conduct and appearance, powerful in wealth and skilled in the use of his limbs. He was ordered to be conveyed to the fray and destined as a prey for Bacchic rites, he was killed by the help of his companions, and brought back lifeless to his home by his devoted retainers. He is buried by his household. His wife mourns with weeping children, the family wails aloud. He lived for thirty-four years, four times three. Killed by the Vascones, he was punished on the 6th day before the Ides of October, in the year 180 of the era. Remember this event. Buried on the 6th day before the Ides of October.
EDCS-09000760.summary: A lengthy funerary inscription dating to the era 180 (likely 120/121 CE, or 680 for a later era), recounting the violent death and burial of Oppilanus.

EDCS-09000781.translation: Sanctus, servant of God, lived 70 years, departed in peace on the 7th day before the Ides of February in the era...
EDCS-09000781.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription recording the death of Sanctus, a servant of God, with an incomplete date.

EDCS-09000795.summary: A dedication or memorial inscription for Caius Valerius Leona.

EDCS-09000796.translation: Iusta, servant of Christ, lived about 67 years. Whoever reads this inscription, pour forth frequent tears. Here lies one illustrious in piety, who safely seeks the Elysian shades, received into peace on the Ides of November in the era 201.
EDCS-09000796.summary: A funerary inscription dating to the era 201 (likely 141/142 CE), for Iusta, a servant of Christ, encouraging mourning.

EDCS-09000799.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Quintus Julius Rutilianus, likely from a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-09000800.translation: In the name of the Lord, the servant of God Fla[---] lived.
EDCS-09000800.summary: This inscription begins a funerary record for a servant of God named Fla[---], likely dating from the Christian era.

EDCS-09000827.translation: Basilia, servant of INVI (likely God), in the bond of marriage lived 30 years, departed in peace on the 2nd day before the Nones of November, in the era 688.
EDCS-09000827.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the life and death of Basilia, who lived for 30 years in marriage and died in the year 688 of the era.

EDCS-09000828.translation: Ilpericus, servant of Christ, lived more or less 50 years, departed in peace on the sixth day before the Kalends of July, in the era 703.
EDCS-09000828.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Ilpericus, a servant of Christ, who died in the year 703 of the era.

EDCS-09000839.translation: Sempliciola, servant of Christ, lived 75 years, rested in peace in the era 651.
EDCS-09000839.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Sempliciola, a servant of Christ, who died in the year 651 of the era.

EDCS-09000890.summary: This short inscription likely served as a epitaph or a marker for John, a servant of Christ.

EDCS-09000953.translation: Julius, may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-09000953.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julius, invoking the common wish for a light earth burial.

EDCS-09001068.translation: EXBINIANS made [this]. Eusebius, servant of God, lived 42 years, rested in peace on the Kalends of March in the third year of the reign of our Lord V[---].
EDCS-09001068.summary: This inscription records that EXBINIANS made a monument for Eusebius, a servant of God, who died on the Kalends of March in the third regnal year of an unspecified Lord.

EDCS-09001423.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Grattius Nativus, aged 17, dutiful to his own, here lies. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-09001423.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Grattius Nativus, a 17-year-old who is described as dutiful, with the traditional wish for a light earth burial.

EDCS-09001424.translation: of Quintia, daughter of Publius, from her own [property/resources]
EDCS-09001424.summary: This inscription fragment likely records a dedication or donation made by Quintia, daughter of Publius, using her own resources.

EDCS-09001425.translation: Chrestus, aged 22 years and 8 months, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09001425.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Chrestus, detailing his age at death and expressing a wish for peace.

EDCS-09001427.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iunia Polytima, aged 29 years, is buried in this place. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09001427.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the Divine Manes for Iunia Polytima, stating her age and burial location.

EDCS-09001428.translation: Gaius Cornelius Quadratus, most dear to his family, aged 23, is buried here.
EDCS-09001428.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Gaius Cornelius Quadratus, noting his age and his cherished status within his family.

EDCS-09001431.translation: Julius Euander, freedman in the service of Julius Maximus, lived for 26 years.
EDCS-09001431.summary: This inscription records the life span of Julius Euander, a freedman associated with Julius Maximus.

EDCS-09200316.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph stating the burial location of Antonia Hellas.

EDCS-09400451.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes of Quintus Valerius and Galeria Vari(a?).
EDCS-09400451.summary: This inscription is dedicated to the Divine Manes and likely records the deceased or the patrons of a tomb belonging to Quintus Valerius and Galeria Vari(a?).

EDCS-09400452.translation: Lucius Annius Chius, aged 50, dear to his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09400452.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Lucius Annius Chius, stating his age, his affection from his family, and wishing him peace.

EDCS-09700615.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Publicius Navalis, aged 40 years, cherished, is here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09700615.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to the Divine Manes for Publicius Navalis, mentioning his age and his beloved status.

EDCS-09700617.translation: Of Marcus Tuccius, son of Lucius, of the Tromentina tribe, Galleonis.
EDCS-09700617.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary or commemorative marker identifying Marcus Tuccius, son of Lucius, and possibly his tribal affiliation and a cognomen or epithet.

EDCS-09700975.translation: Domigratia, servant of God, rests here in peace, on the Nones of November, aged three years and about six months, in the year 533 of the era.
EDCS-09700975.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Domigratia, a 'servant of God', detailing her age at death (3 years and 6 months), the date of her passing (Nones of November), and the year (533 of the era, likely AUC).

EDCS-10001230.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered, Augustus, holder of tribunician power
EDCS-10001230.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Aurelian with several honorific titles, likely part of a larger dedication dating to his reign.

EDCS-10001233.translation: To Lucius Horatius Victor, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, twice duovir, most deserving citizen, for his most abundant generosity towards his fatherland and the people, the people [erected this]
EDCS-10001233.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the people to Lucius Horatius Victor, honoring him for his generosity as a former magistrate, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-10001234.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aelia Aeliana, aged 20 years and 4 months, pious towards her family, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Oppia Firmana, mother.
EDCS-10001234.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Aelia Aeliana, set up by her mother, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001237.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Fabius Priscus, aged 71 years. Polytimus and Thyrsus, his freedmen, erected this.
EDCS-10001237.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Fabius Priscus, set up by his freedmen, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001239.translation: Peregrina is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001239.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for a woman named Peregrina, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001242.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Aelius Proculus Maecilius Marcellus Volcasius, lived 52 years and [some] days.
EDCS-10001242.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespan of Lucius Aelius Proculus Maecilius Marcellus Volcasius, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001243.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Aemilius Maximus. Aemilius Donatus and his daughter Iulia Maxima made this.
EDCS-10001243.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caius Aemilius Maximus, set up by his son and daughter, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001244.translation: Alexandra is placed here. Lucius Rubellius Philodamus, her son, saw to its construction at his own expense.
EDCS-10001244.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Alexandra, commissioned by her son, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001245.translation: To Lucius Iulius Antonius Tertullus and [Lucius Iulius Antonius] Severianus.
EDCS-10001245.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a dedication or dedication to two individuals named Lucius Iulius Antonius Tertullus and Severianus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001246.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aufustius Fortunatus lived 60 years.
EDCS-10001246.summary: This tombstone inscription records the lifespan of Aufustius Fortunatus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001248.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clarus, aged 17 years and 10 months, is buried here. I ask you, passerby, to say, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10001248.summary: A funerary epitaph for Clarus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001249.translation: Clodia Paula, daughter of Quintus, in front 12 feet, in the land 10 feet.
EDCS-10001249.summary: An epitaph recording land dimensions belonging to Clodia Paula, likely for funerary or property demarcation purposes.

EDCS-10001250.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Rana, freedwoman of Cornelius Lucius, aged 82 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10001250.summary: A funerary epitaph for Rana, freedwoman of Cornelius Lucius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001254.translation: aged 10 years, for his merits, Gaius Lucretius M. to his [---]
EDCS-10001254.summary: A funerary inscription fragment, likely commemorating a young individual and honoring their merits, possibly by Gaius Lucretius M.

EDCS-10001255.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Octavia Pebas lived 20 years. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-10001255.summary: A funerary epitaph for Octavia Pebas, who died at the age of 20, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001258.translation: aged 81 years, pious, is laid here among her own. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10001258.summary: A funerary epitaph for a pious woman aged 81, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001264.translation: aged 50 years, father to his son.
EDCS-10001264.summary: A fragmentary inscription indicating a father's memorialization of his son, aged 50, likely a funerary context.

EDCS-10001266.summary: A funerary inscription fragment dedicated to the departed spirits, possibly naming a person ending in '-lia'.

EDCS-10001268.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. [---] is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10001268.summary: A funerary epitaph fragment dedicated to the departed spirits, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001269.translation: Aurelia Proba, most distinguished lady, lived more or less 65 years, departed in peace on the 3rd day before the Ides of May.
EDCS-10001269.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Proba, a woman of high status, recording her lifespan and date of death in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001270.translation: Cervella, a most distinguished woman, servant of Christ, lived for more or less 35 years, departed in peace on the third day before the Kalends of February in the year 600.
EDCS-10001270.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Cervella, a servant of Christ, who died in 600.

EDCS-10001271.translation: Cypriana, an innocent virgin, servant of Christ, lived for more or less 23 years, departed in peace on the fourth day before the Nones of January in the year 582.
EDCS-10001271.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Cypriana, a servant of Christ and virgin, who died in 582.

EDCS-10001272.translation: Lucinus, servant of God, lived for more or less 12 years, departed in peace on the third day before the Ides of April in the year 643.
EDCS-10001272.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucinus, a servant of God, who died in 643.

EDCS-10001273.translation: Macarius, servant of God, lived for 52 years, departed in peace on the tenth day before the Kalends of January in the year 558.
EDCS-10001273.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Macarius, a servant of God, who died in 558.

EDCS-10001275.translation: Octavius, servant of God, lived for more or less 51 years, departed in peace on the fourteenth day before the Kalends of June in the year 570.
EDCS-10001275.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Octavius, a servant of God, who died in 570.

EDCS-10001276.translation: Paula, a most distinguished woman, servant of Christ, lived for 24 years and two months, departed in peace on the sixteenth day before the Kalends of February in the year 582.
EDCS-10001276.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Paula, a servant of Christ, who died in 582.

EDCS-10001277.translation: Saturninus, priest, servant of God, lived for more or less 53 years, departed in peace around the second day before the Ides of November in the year 657.
EDCS-10001277.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Saturninus, a priest and servant of God, who died in 657.

EDCS-10001286.summary: This is a dedication or maker's inscription.

EDCS-10001287.summary: This is a dedication inscription to bishop Marciano.

EDCS-10001291.translation: Apollonius, aged 33, pious in his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001291.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Apollonius, who died at age 33.

EDCS-10001292.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fabia Merope, freedwoman of Atimetus, aged 75, dutiful to her own people, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. If fortune had granted as much as piety could achieve, she would have had this inscription written in golden letters.
EDCS-10001292.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fabia Merope, a freedwoman, dating from the Roman Imperial period, lamenting that her life's circumstances did not match her devotion.

EDCS-10001293.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Bruttia Victorina Celtitana, aged 35, dutiful to her own people, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001293.summary: A funerary epitaph for Bruttia Victorina Celtitana, a dutiful woman, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001294.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caesia Annula, aged 25, dutiful to her own people, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius Licinius Gallus, aged 65, dutiful to his own people, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001294.summary: A funerary epitaph for two individuals, Caesia Annula and Lucius Licinius Gallus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001295.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Cornelius, a freedman, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001299.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maurula, a slave woman, aged 43, dutiful to her own people, is placed here.
EDCS-10001299.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maurula, a slave woman, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001300.translation: Myris, freedman of Rustica, aged 52, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Cinismus, freedman of Rustica, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001300.summary: A funerary epitaph for two freedmen, Myris and Cinismus, both associated with Rustica, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001301.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Optatinus, a slave, aged 18, dutiful to his own people, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001301.summary: A funerary epitaph for Optatinus, a young slave, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001302.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pietati Baeticae, aged 21, dutiful to her own people, is placed here. I beg you, passerby, as you go, say 'May the earth lie lightly upon you.' Barathes placed this for his most loving wife.
EDCS-10001302.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pietati Baeticae, set up by her husband Barathes, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001303.translation: Romula, aged 16, dutiful to her own people, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Sacred to the departed spirits.
EDCS-10001303.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Romula, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001304.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph for Sempronia Prixsilla, indicating her name and the start of her age, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001305.translation: Sempronia Superata, daughter of Gaius Licinius Capito, aged 21, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you, Sempronia Peregrina.
EDCS-10001305.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sempronia Superata, stating her age and parentage.

EDCS-10001308.translation: [...] aged 9, aged 41, [...] aged 20, 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10001308.summary: This fragmented funerary epitaph records the ages of the deceased.

EDCS-10001312.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Papiria Calitiche lived 26 years and 2 days. Dutifully, she is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10001312.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name, lifespan, and burial of Papiria Calitiche.

EDCS-10001316.summary: This inscription is a dedication or ownership marker by Gaius Licinius Propinquus.

EDCS-10001318.translation: Lucius Attius Quirina Vetto, twice flamen of the citizens of the municipality of Flavius Canamiensis, dedicated by his own resources, with his own expenses, marble-covered stone porticoes, built with stone and decorated with marble, stage plays, and a given feast, in his own name and in the name of his son Lucius Attius Vindex, and his daughter Attia Autumnina, and his granddaughter Antonia Procula.
EDCS-10001318.summary: This is a dedication of public works (porticoes, stage plays, feast) by Lucius Attius Quirina Vetto, a local magistrate, for the municipality of Flavius Canamiensis.

EDCS-10001319.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary personal name, possibly part of a larger dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-10001321.translation: Marcus Claudius Patriciensis, flamen of the Augusti of the province of Baetica.
EDCS-10001321.summary: This inscription records the name and religious office (flamen of the Augusti) of Marcus Claudius Patriciensis in the province of Baetica.

EDCS-10001322.translation: The town council of the municipality of Flavius Canamiensis decreed a burial place and funeral expenses and a pedestrian statue for Lucius Thacius Lupus, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina. His father Lucius Thacius Lupus and his mother Cornelia Secunda, having received the honor, waived the expenses.
EDCS-10001322.summary: This inscription records a posthumous honor decreed by the municipality of Flavius Canamiensis for Lucius Thacius Lupus, with his parents choosing to forgo the associated expenses.

EDCS-10001323.translation: To Marcianus, aged 2 years and 2 [days/months]. His mother [dedicated this]. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10001323.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Marcianus, dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-10001326.translation: Iunia Tuscilla placed this by her will for her husband Gaius Manilius Flaccinus.
EDCS-10001326.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument placed by Iunia Tuscilla for her husband Gaius Manilius Flaccinus.

EDCS-10001327.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aelia Apollonia, aged 18, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001327.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Apollonia, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-10001329.translation: Lucius Caesius, son of Lucius, from Bedule, is buried here; 15 feet wide and 24 feet long.
EDCS-10001329.summary: This inscription records the burial plot dimensions for Lucius Caesius, son of Lucius, from Bedule.

EDCS-10001330.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sulpicia Euresis, aged 46 years, 9 months, and 10 days, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001330.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sulpicia Euresis, detailing her age at death.

EDCS-10001333.translation: Aged 13, pious, buried in her own [tomb]. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001333.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a pious girl aged 13.

EDCS-10001335.translation: Aged 20. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001335.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an individual who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-10001336.summary: This is a personal name, likely a fragment of a larger inscription, possibly a dedication or funerary notice.

EDCS-10001338.translation: Of Aelius Saxus, theirs, most fortunate and legitimate.
EDCS-10001338.summary: This text likely refers to children or heirs of Aelius Saxus, indicating legitimacy and good fortune.

EDCS-10001339.summary: This is a genitive case, likely a dedication or ownership marker for Marcus Antonius Atticus.

EDCS-10001340.summary: This is a genitive case, likely a dedication or ownership marker for Aulus Suettius Pulcher.

EDCS-10001341.translation: Gulfinus, servant of God, lived more or less 70 years, departed in peace on the 3rd day before the Kalends of August in the year 600 (of the era).
EDCS-10001341.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Gulfinus, a servant of God, who died at approximately 70 years of age in 564 CE.

EDCS-10001342.translation: Servant of God, lived about 25 years, departed in peace on the 7th day before the Kalends.
EDCS-10001342.summary: This inscription records the death of a Christian individual identified as a "servant of God" who died around the age of 25.

EDCS-10001345.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aurelius Cemerus lived 43 years, 5 months, and 7 days. Pious to his own, he is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001345.summary: This funerary inscription records the life span of Marcus Aurelius Cemerus, who died at the age of 43.

EDCS-10001346.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Trebonia Iunia Carphima, aged 29, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Caecilius Priscus to his most deserving wife.
EDCS-10001346.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Trebonia Iunia Carphima, aged 29, and is dedicated by her husband, Caecilius Priscus.

EDCS-10001359.translation: Lucius Aemilius Moderatus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-10001359.summary: This inscription records Lucius Aemilius Moderatus fulfilling a vow, likely an offering or dedication.

EDCS-10001361.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Acutius Italicus lived 6 years, 9 months. He is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001361.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Publius Acutius Italicus, who died at the age of 6 years and 9 months.

EDCS-10001364.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Messia Laetina, aged 12 years, 6 months, and 26 days, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001364.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Messia Laetina, who died at the age of 12 years, 6 months, and 26 days.

EDCS-10001366.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Valerius Crispinus, aged 50, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001366.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Valerius Crispinus, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-10001369.translation: Lucius Appius, son of LTO, aged 2 years and 1 day. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001369.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Appius, who died at the age of 2 years and 1 day.

EDCS-10001371.translation: Marcus Petrucidius, son of Marcus, legate pro praetore of Marcus Licinius.
EDCS-10001371.summary: This inscription identifies Marcus Petrucidius as a legate pro praetore serving Marcus Licinius.

EDCS-10001372.translation: In this tomb lies Gregory, servant of God, who lived about 60 years, departed in peace on the Nones of February, in the year 582 of the era.
EDCS-10001372.summary: This inscription records the death of a Christian named Gregory, who lived about 60 years and died in the year 582 of the era.

EDCS-10001374.translation: Acer received in peace on the Nones of January in the year 582 (AD 524).
EDCS-10001374.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Acer in AD 524.

EDCS-10001376.translation: Stefanus offers to the church of Segis, in Ianisi.
EDCS-10001376.summary: This is a dedication by Stefanus to the church of Segis, likely a medieval inscription.

EDCS-10100004.translation: Marcus Antistius from the province of Baetica, in honor of the Genius of the colony, with his son Marcus Antistius Lucanus, for Jupiter.
EDCS-10100004.summary: This dedication to Jupiter was made by Marcus Antistius, from Baetica, and his son, honoring the Genius of the colony.

EDCS-10100005.translation: Lucius Bruttius Bargathes Firmus, Augustalis priest, gave as a gift to Mercury Augustus.
EDCS-10100005.summary: This is a votive offering to Mercury Augustus by Lucius Bruttius Bargathes Firmus, an Augustalis priest.

EDCS-10100010.translation: Publius Caesius Romulus, according to his vow, gave a gift and markings.
EDCS-10100010.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Publius Caesius Romulus.

EDCS-10100013.translation: Viciria fulfilled her vow to Augusta Nemesis.
EDCS-10100013.summary: This inscription records Viciria fulfilling a vow made to the goddess Nemesis.

EDCS-10100016.summary: This brief inscription indicates a votive offering made by Maruleius.

EDCS-10100017.translation: By the command of the god, I, Lucius T B, fulfill my vow.
EDCS-10100017.summary: This inscription records Lucius T B fulfilling a vow made at the god's command.

EDCS-10100018.translation: Mistress Fons, I entrust to you two tasks concerning my sandals: whoever takes my sandals and shoes, you, goddess, I ask you to pursue them; if a girl, a woman, or any person has stolen them, pursue them.
EDCS-10100018.summary: This is a magical or religious appeal to the goddess Fons to recover stolen sandals and shoes.

EDCS-10100019.translation: The duumvir willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100019.summary: This inscription records a duumvir fulfilling a vow, likely in an official capacity.

EDCS-10100020.summary: This inscription is a titulary fragment for Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-10100023.translation: For the safety of Hadrian Augustus and Sabina Augusta, to Silvanus Pantheon. Autarces, freedman of Sabina Augusta, from a vow.
EDCS-10100023.summary: A freedman named Autarces dedicates a vow to Silvanus Pantheon for the health of Emperor Hadrian and Empress Sabina.

EDCS-10100025.translation: To Marcus Lucretius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, Julianus, eques publicus, procurator of the Augusti of the province of Baetica, also procurator of the Vegetian Calendar, also procurator of the XX (20%) tax on inheritances in the provinces of Baetica and Lusitania, curator of the public affairs of Italicum; the most distinguished public body, by decree of the decurions, for his merits.
EDCS-10100025.summary: An honorific inscription for Marcus Lucretius Julianus, detailing his significant procuratorial and curatorial appointments, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-10100029.summary: This is a dedication to a priestess named Galla.

EDCS-10100031.translation: Marcus Cocceius Julianus with his son Quirinus and wife Junia Africana, having presented two Carian marble columns, an epistyle with bronze screens, and an altar, as a gift from a vow, with games presented. Junia Africana, Cocceius Quirinus, Marcus Cocceius Julianus.
EDCS-10100031.summary: Marcus Cocceius Julianus and his family donate architectural elements and an altar to the public body of Italicum, likely commemorating public games, probably in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-10100036.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the familial relationship of a woman, possibly a daughter and sister.

EDCS-10100042.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Tiberius Claudius Festus, aged 24, of the VII Legion Gemina Felix.
EDCS-10100042.summary: A tomb inscription for Tiberius Claudius Festus, a 24-year-old soldier of the VII Legion Gemina Felix.

EDCS-10100045.translation: To the religious observance of Caius T.
EDCS-10100045.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription related to a religious dedication or person.

EDCS-10100046.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aelius Herennius lived for more or less 65 years, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-10100046.summary: A tombstone inscription for Aelius Herennius, recording his age and a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-10100048.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Aelius Heros lived for more or less 50 years and 3 months.
EDCS-10100048.summary: A tombstone inscription for Caius Aelius Heros, recording his age at death.

EDCS-10100049.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aelius Marcianus lived 41 years, 2 months, and 28 days. Pious in his ways, may the earth lie lightly upon him here.
EDCS-10100049.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Marcianus, recording his lifespan and piety.

EDCS-10100050.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aelia Veneria lived 80 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-10100050.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Veneria, recording her lifespan and wishing her peace.

EDCS-10100051.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Agrippina lived 12 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-10100051.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Agrippina, recording her young age at death.

EDCS-10100052.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Antonia Herennia lived 28 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-10100052.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Herennia, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-10100053.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Afrodisia lived 25 years, 11 months, and 4 days. Aurelius Myrismus made this for his well-deserving companion. May the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-10100053.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aurelius Myrismus for his companion Aurelia Afrodisia, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-10100054.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Aurelia Salutaris, my dearest sister, who lived 21 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10100054.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Salutaris, dedicated by a sibling and recording her lifespan.

EDCS-10100055.translation: Caecilia Catura lived 4 years, 3 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-10100055.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilia Catura, recording her very young age at death.

EDCS-10100057.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Chresumus lived 28 years. Licinius Chresumus, his father, made this for his most dutiful son. May the earth lie lightly upon him here.
EDCS-10100057.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Licinius Chresumus for his son Chresumus, recording the son's lifespan.

EDCS-10100058.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Claudius Crispinus lived approximately 75 years. Pious in his ways, may the earth lie lightly upon him here.
EDCS-10100058.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Crispinus, recording his approximate lifespan and piety.

EDCS-10100059.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Claudia Fortunata lived 27 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-10100059.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Fortunata, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-10100060.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Clodius Panarius, aged 36, dutiful to his own, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100060.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Clodius Panarius, who died at the age of 36.

EDCS-10100062.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Exorata lived 25 years, 3 months, and 15 days. For Exorata, his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-10100062.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Exorata, who lived for 25 years, 3 months, and 15 days.

EDCS-10100063.translation: Fabia Avita, aged 12, lies here happily. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100063.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Fabia Avita, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-10100065.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Germanus, a slave, lived 26 years.
EDCS-10100065.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Germanus, a slave, who lived for 26 years.

EDCS-10100066.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Helpideophorus, by Iulianus, in memory of him.
EDCS-10100066.summary: This inscription records a memorial set up by Iulianus for Helpideophorus.

EDCS-10100067.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Iulianus, aged 60. The college, from the funeral fund which remained, made this beer barrel of 12 feet. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100067.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Iulianus, aged 60, with a monument funded by his college from their funeral fund.

EDCS-10100068.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Felix lived 8 years, 5 months. Dutiful to his own. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100068.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Iulius Felix, who died at the age of 8 years and 5 months.

EDCS-10100069.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Vincentius lived 6 years.
EDCS-10100069.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulius Vincentius, who lived for 6 years.

EDCS-10100070.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Liberina, aged 10 years, 3 months, and 21 days, lies here. I ask you, passerby, as you go, to say: May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100070.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Liberina, who died at the age of 10 years, 3 months, and 21 days, and includes a plea to passersby.

EDCS-10100072.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Magnus lived 75 years. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100072.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Marcus Magnus, who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-10100073.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Marius Letus of Salona lived 53 years. His wife placed him here, a husband well-deserving. I ask you, passerby, say 'May the earth lie lightly on you.'
EDCS-10100073.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Marcus Marius Letus, who lived 53 years, and was placed here by his wife.

EDCS-10100074.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maruleius Fortunatus lived about 11 years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100074.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Maruleius Fortunatus, who lived about 11 years.

EDCS-10100075.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Mattius Sabinus lived 45 years. His devoted brother made this. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100075.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Mattius Sabinus, who lived 45 years, and was commemorated by his brother.

EDCS-10100076.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maurus, 13 years old, devoted, lies here among his own. May the earth lie sweetly on you.
EDCS-10100076.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Maurus, who was 13 years old.

EDCS-10100077.translation: Memmia Italicilla, daughter of Lucius, 4 years and 8 months old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100077.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Memmia Italicilla, who was 4 years and 8 months old.

EDCS-10100078.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Messius Genialis lived 50 years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100078.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Messius Genialis, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-10100079.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Octavia Restituta lived 45 years and 2 months and 18 days. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100079.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Octavia Restituta, who lived 45 years, 2 months, and 18 days.

EDCS-10100080.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Perpena Valeriana lived 40 years. Aelius Thallus made this for his well-deserving wife. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100080.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Perpena Valeriana, who lived 40 years, and was commemorated by her husband Aelius Thallus.

EDCS-10100081.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Primus, 3 years old, lies here. I ask you, passerby, say 'May the earth lie lightly on you.'
EDCS-10100081.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Primus, who was 3 years old.

EDCS-10100082.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Restutus, a slave, 31 years old, devoted, among his own.
EDCS-10100082.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Restutus, a devoted slave, who was 31 years old.

EDCS-10100083.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Rubria Iulia, inhabitant of Italicensis, of Seriensis origin, lived 60 years and 20 days. She is buried here.
EDCS-10100083.summary: A funerary inscription for Rubria Iulia, recording her lifespan and place of burial.

EDCS-10100085.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Severina lived 35 years. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10100085.summary: A funerary inscription for Severina, stating her age and burial, with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-10100086.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Severina lived about 60 years. Her most devoted daughter placed this for her mother. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10100086.summary: A funerary inscription for Severina, indicating her approximate age and that the monument was erected by her devoted daughter.

EDCS-10100087.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sutera lived 40 years. Felicianus lived 30 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you both.
EDCS-10100087.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating two individuals, Sutera and Felicianus, with their ages and a wish for their rest.

EDCS-10100088.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Terentia Callirhoe.
EDCS-10100088.summary: A funerary dedication to the spirits of the departed for Terentia Callirhoe.

EDCS-10100089.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Ulpius Primio lived about 35 years.
EDCS-10100089.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Ulpius Primio, recording his approximate lifespan.

EDCS-10100090.translation: Ulpia Filete lived about 63 years. Fortuna, her freedwoman, [erected this] for her most deserving patroness.
EDCS-10100090.summary: A funerary inscription for Ulpia Filete, noting her age and erected by her freedwoman, Fortuna.

EDCS-10100091.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Iulius lived 36 years and 6 months. Good among friends. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10100091.summary: A funerary inscription for Valerius Iulius, detailing his lifespan, his character as a friend, and his burial.

EDCS-10100092.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Valerius Ti [lived] 70 years. Dutiful towards his family. He is buried here. I ask you, as you pass by, to say: May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10100092.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Ti, stating his age, his familial devotion, and requesting passersby to wish him well.

EDCS-10100093.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Valerius Titianus Iunior lived [X] years, X months, X days. Dutiful towards his family.
EDCS-10100093.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Valerius Titianus Iunior, recording his familial devotion and partial lifespan details.

EDCS-10100094.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valeria lived for 13 years. Tryphonas, to his incomparable husband, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10100094.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria, recording her age and dedicating the tomb to her husband, Tryphonas.

EDCS-10100095.summary: This is a simple sepulchral inscription identifying the deceased as Gaius Vettius Aegantus.

EDCS-10100097.translation: He lived 51 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10100097.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of an individual (VS likely indicating 'vixit' or 'vixit annis') and includes a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-10100098.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a tombstone, identifying the deceased as Quintus Numisius Lepidus.

EDCS-10100099.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelius Restitutus [made this].
EDCS-10100099.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the departed spirits and names Cornelius Restitutus as the deceased.

EDCS-10100119.summary: This is a brief inscription, possibly an epitaph or a dedication, addressing Volferna.

EDCS-10100127.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iael Lepidina lived 30 years...
EDCS-10100127.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iael Lepidina and is dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-10100155.translation: For the sake of Health, Secunda, for the health of Helvius Severianus, placed this altar, by a willing spirit, fulfilling a vow.
EDCS-10100155.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to the goddess Salus (Health) by Secunda for the well-being of Helvius Severianus.

EDCS-10100156.translation: Blattia Procla, daughter of Caius, priestess, aged 63.
EDCS-10100156.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Blattia Procla, identifying her as the daughter of Caius, a priestess, and stating her age at death.

EDCS-10100158.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Rocia Iovina.
EDCS-10100158.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits and naming the deceased as Rocia Iovina.

EDCS-10100159.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cruse, aged 20, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100159.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Cruse, aged 20.

EDCS-10100163.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Manilius Rarus, aged 41, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100163.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Lucius Manilius Rarus, aged 41.

EDCS-10100167.translation: Honoratus, the high priest, founded this holy and venerable temple of Christ, and by whose name it flourishes forever and is celebrated in deeds. He places the altar in the middle of the sanctuary, enclosing the sacred table. He retains three holy brothers whom Corduba suffered. He then rightfully dedicated the temple of the three saints, leaving the ages beneath in verses, for ages returning to ages, 670.
EDCS-10100167.summary: An inscription detailing the founding and dedication of a Christian temple by Honoratus, likely dating to 670 AD (possibly 1670 AUC).

EDCS-10100171.translation: A bedroom and crypt in the property of Q. Opulinius.
EDCS-10100171.summary: An inscription indicating ownership or construction related to a bedroom and crypt belonging to Q. Opulinius.

EDCS-10100172.translation: Galeria, aged 80, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100172.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Galeria, aged 80.

EDCS-10100174.summary: A funerary inscription for Paula, identified as a servant of God.

EDCS-10100175.translation: I believe that my redeemer lives, and that he will raise my skin on the last day, and with my flesh I will see the Lord. The righteous servant of God lived for approximately 50 years, departed in peace on the 8th day before the Ides of February.
EDCS-10100175.summary: A Christian funerary inscription expressing belief in the resurrection, for a servant of God who lived about 50 years and died on February 6th.

EDCS-10100176.summary: An inscription dedicating something to the goddess Nemesis, identified as Augusta.

EDCS-10100180.translation: Nedume, daughter of Crescentius Faustinus, aged 22. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100180.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Nedume, daughter of Crescentius Faustinus, aged 22.

EDCS-10100181.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Quietus, aged 23. The members of the association, as his heirs by his will, [erected] an altar.
EDCS-10100181.summary: A funerary inscription for Quietus, aged 23, with an altar erected by his association members as heirs.

EDCS-10100183.translation: Servilia, daughter of Lucius, mother of Publius Marius, dedicated [this].
EDCS-10100183.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Servilia, mother of Publius Marius, likely to a deceased relative.

EDCS-10100184.translation: Tituria, daughter of Ser... , 10 years old. Tituria.
EDCS-10100184.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for a young girl named Tituria, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-10100186.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for a girl named Pompeia, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-10100190.summary: This inscription, though abbreviated, likely records the name and parentage of an individual, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-10100192.summary: This inscription records the name and parentage of Sergia, daughter of Marcus, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-10100197.translation: Leontius, servant of God, lived more or less 50 years, departed in peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of October in the year 613.
EDCS-10100197.summary: This Christian funerary inscription records the lifespan and death date of Leontius, a 'servant of God', in the year 613.

EDCS-10100202.translation: To the departed spirits. [---] lived ES years.
EDCS-10100202.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the 'Dis Manibus' (departed spirits), recording the incomplete lifespan of an individual.

EDCS-10100203.translation: Leontius, servant of God, who lived more or less 82 years, rests in peace, died in the year 614.
EDCS-10100203.summary: This Christian funerary inscription records the lifespan and death date of Leontius, a 'servant of God', in the year 614.

EDCS-10100206.translation: To Mercury Augustus, Caius Marcius Restitutus, a member of the Basilippensian college of *seviri*, placed this altar in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-10100206.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Mercury Augustus by Caius Marcius Restitutus, a *sevirus* from Basilippe, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10100207.translation: Hilduarens, servant of God, lived more or less 38 years, departed in peace on the Ides of March in the year 542.
EDCS-10100207.summary: This Christian funerary inscription records the lifespan and death date of Hilduarens, a 'servant of God', in the year 542.

EDCS-10100211.translation: Publius Lucius Cosconianus erected this at his own expense in a publicly accepted place to his most devoted mother.
EDCS-10100211.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Publius Lucius Cosconianus to his mother.

EDCS-10100213.translation: Sacred to the shades below. Hemerita lived 3 years, 4 months, and 5 days. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100213.summary: A tombstone recording the short life of Hemerita and offering a standard funerary wish.

EDCS-10100227.translation: Marcus Aelius Viator, son of Quintus, aged 75, dutiful to his family. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100227.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Aelius Viator, noting his age and piety.

EDCS-10100229.translation: Cornelia Aditsa, daughter of Publius, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100229.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Cornelia Aditsa.

EDCS-10100232.summary: An inscription with only a name, likely a tombstone or ownership marker.

EDCS-10100235.translation: Marcus Quintius Rufus was the first to donate and dedicate the spectator areas, built from the ground up with stone, with his own money.
EDCS-10100235.summary: This inscription records Marcus Quintius Rufus donating and dedicating a newly constructed seating area for spectacles, funded by himself.

EDCS-10100238.translation: Marcus Petrucidius, son of Marcus, governor with praetorian power, and Gnaeus Tarquitius.
EDCS-10100238.summary: An inscription likely commemorating two individuals, one of whom, Marcus Petrucidius, held a high provincial office.

EDCS-10100239.summary: An inscription bearing only a name, possibly a tombstone or a dedication.

EDCS-10100243.translation: In the name of the Lord, here are the relics of the saints, John the Baptist, Eulalia, Justa, Rufina, and Felix, martyrs. This basilica was dedicated by the bishop Pimenius on the eighth day before the Kalends of June in the year 686 (of the Spanish Era, i.e., 648 AD).
EDCS-10100243.summary: This Christian inscription records the dedication of a basilica containing the relics of several martyrs, consecrated by Bishop Pimenius in 648 AD.

EDCS-10100244.translation: Istorna, servant of God, lived more or less 60 years. She departed in peace on the eleventh day before the Kalends of December in the year 678 (of the Spanish Era, i.e., 640 AD).
EDCS-10100244.summary: A Christian tombstone recording the death of Istorna, a 'servant of God', in 640 AD.

EDCS-10100247.translation: By decree of the town councilors, while Calpurnius Rusticus was in charge, for the colony of Romula Hispalis.
EDCS-10100247.summary: This is a public dedication or record from the colony of Romula Hispalis, noting a decree of its council and the official responsible.

EDCS-10100248.translation: V. Regis Fabianus and Senecio, the two highest magistrates, saw to its construction.
EDCS-10100248.summary: This inscription records the construction of a public work, commissioned by two magistrates of the town, likely from the 1st century BCE given the name 'V. Regis'.

EDCS-10100254.translation: Eugamia, servant of God, lived for about 26 years and departed in peace on the Kalends of November in the year 81 of the current era.
EDCS-10100254.summary: This is a Christian epitaph recording the death of Eugamia, a female servant of God, who died around 26 years old in 81 CE.

EDCS-10100255.translation: Eburinus, servant of God, lived for about 40 years and departed in peace on the day...
EDCS-10100255.summary: This is a Christian epitaph recording the death of Eburinus, a male servant of God, who died around 40 years old.

EDCS-10100259.translation: Sollers and Ingenuus, freedmen, give this to their patroness Pedania.
EDCS-10100259.summary: This is a dedication by freedmen, Sollers and Ingenuus, to their patroness Pedania.

EDCS-10100261.translation: Cornelia Adsi. Iunius Sisenna, aged 35, rests here; may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-10100261.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Iunius Sisenna, aged 35, mentioning Cornelia Adsi, perhaps his wife or mother, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-10100265.translation: Marcus Aurelius Castor lived 16 years, 5 months, and 12 days. His father and mother placed him here, their dearest son, a dutiful man among his own; may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-10100265.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Marcus Aurelius Castor, who died at 16 years, 5 months, and 12 days, erected by his parents.

EDCS-10100266.summary: This is a simple tomb inscription identifying the deceased as Lucius Calpurnius Afrinus.

EDCS-10100267.translation: Cornelia, aged 7, rests here; may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-10100267.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Cornelia, a child aged 7, with a standard epitaph wish.
