EDCS-10100268.translation: Cornelia Lucilla, daughter of Lucius, aged 30 years and 3 months, rests here; may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-10100268.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Cornelia Lucilla, daughter of Lucius, who died at the age of 30 years and 3 months.

EDCS-10100270.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Licinia Procula lived 4 years, 7 months, and 11 days; to my most dutiful granddaughter, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100270.summary: A tombstone for Licinia Procula, a young girl, dated by her age and epitaph.

EDCS-10100271.translation: Marcus V. Chrestus, aged 55, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10100271.summary: A tombstone for Marcus V. Chrestus, an adult male, indicating his burial and age.

EDCS-10100273.translation: Antonina, servant of Christ, lived 44 years; she rested here in peace. [---] XI Kalendas [---] of the era.
EDCS-10100273.summary: An epitaph for Antonina, a Christian woman, recording her age and her rest in peace, with an incomplete date.

EDCS-10100274.translation: Lucinus, servant of Christ Jesus, lived more or less 29 years; he was received into peace on the 17th day before the Kalends of February in the era 520.
EDCS-10100274.summary: An epitaph for Lucinus, a Christian man, recording his approximate age, date of death, and reception into peace.

EDCS-10102125.translation: I made this for you, my sweetest wife. Indeed, untimely death conquered your envious life. Oh, sweet wife, and your memorable name, you who were pleasant to your servants, modest to your family, and a most honorable woman. Eulalia lived [---] years; she departed in peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of [---] in the era 523.
EDCS-10102125.summary: A tombstone erected by a husband for his wife Eulalia, praising her virtues and recording her death in the era 523.

EDCS-10102127.translation: Ubitilda, servant of God, on the Ides of May in the era 50.
EDCS-10102127.summary: An inscription recording the death of Ubitilda, a servant of God, on a specific date in the era 50.

EDCS-10102129.translation: Cefalius, servant of God, lived more or less 50 years; he departed in peace on the Kalends of December in the era 572.
EDCS-10102129.summary: An epitaph for Cefalius, a servant of God, noting his approximate age and date of death.

EDCS-10102130.translation: Cornelius, son of S., of Fulvia.
EDCS-10102130.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely identifying Cornelius, son of S., and mentioning Fulvia, possibly his mother or patron.

EDCS-10102131.translation: Susanna, servant of God, lived more or less 41 years; she departed in peace on the 14th day before the Kalends of May in the era 559.
EDCS-10102131.summary: An epitaph for Susanna, a servant of God, stating her approximate age and date of departure.

EDCS-10102132.translation: Anna Gaudiosa, also called Africana, lived 7 years and 8 days, in the era 600, on the Ides of August.
EDCS-10102132.summary: A tombstone for Anna Gaudiosa (Africana), a young girl, recording her age and date of death in the era 600.

EDCS-10102133.translation: Valeria Ulpiana lived for 27 years and 20 days. She departed in peace on the Kalends of January.
EDCS-10102133.summary: This epitaph records the death of Valeria Ulpiana at the age of 27 years and 20 days.

EDCS-10102134.translation: The body of Optata, a dutiful daughter, is laid here; her bones are placed here.
EDCS-10102134.summary: This inscription indicates the burial of Optata, identified as a dutiful daughter.

EDCS-10102135.translation: Eusebia, a most distinguished woman, lived for 32 years and 9 months. She departed in peace on the 13th day before the Kalends of June in the year 559.
EDCS-10102135.summary: This epitaph commemorates Eusebia, a distinguished woman, who died in the year 559.

EDCS-10102137.translation: Marciana, a pious and beloved name to us, appeared suddenly, but a truer other life is returned for the better by the fate of another life in heaven. You lived 16 years and twice four years (28 years), and died on the eleventh day before the Kalends of August in the year 686.
EDCS-10102137.summary: This epitaph records the death of Marciana, who died at the age of 28 in the year 686, emphasizing a belief in an afterlife.

EDCS-10102140.translation: To the Divine Manes. Calidius, aged 10, is buried here.
EDCS-10102140.summary: This is a short epitaph for Calidius, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-10102145.translation: Herbulius B, a most distinguished man, aged about 60, departed in peace on the 7th day before the Ides of [month] in the year 400. At this tomb, Leontius CRO FAT LIA D.
EDCS-10102145.summary: This epitaph records the death of Herbulius B, aged about 60, in the year 400.

EDCS-10102148.translation: Licinia, sweet soul, servant of Christ, lived for 11 years and 6 months. Received in peace on the 4th day before the Ides of January in the year 523.
EDCS-10102148.summary: This epitaph marks the death of Licinia, a servant of Christ, who died at the age of 11 in the year 523.

EDCS-10102149.translation: Servandus, servant of Christ, lived for 4 years and 7 months. Received in peace on the seventh day before the Kalends of June in the year 527.
EDCS-10102149.summary: This epitaph records the death of Servandus, a servant of Christ, who died at the age of 4 in the year 527.

EDCS-10102152.translation: To Good Fortune, Augustus. Lucius Valerius Aelius Severus, freedman of Lucius Valerius Celerinus and Aelia Thallusa, gave this gift from his own funds, having accepted the place, in honor of his sevirate, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-10102152.summary: This dedication records a gift by Lucius Valerius Aelius Severus, a freedman, to Good Fortune in honor of his sevirate, funded by the town council.

EDCS-10102153.translation: To Ceres Augusta. In honor and memory of Quintia Flaccina, daughter of Marcus, Muniguensis, priestess of the Augustas and most splendid provincial priestess of Baetica. Quintus Aelius Vernaclus, Muniguensis, friend and heir, having accepted the place from the most splendid town council of the municipium Flavium Muniguense, gave a gift of a feast and distribution to be made to both sexes.
EDCS-10102153.summary: This dedication honors Quintia Flaccina, a priestess, and records a gift of a public feast and distribution by her friend and heir, Quintus Aelius Vernaclus, funded by the town council of Muniguense.

EDCS-10102154.translation: To the divine power of Dis Pater, Lucius Aelius of the tribe Quirina, son of Fronto, dedicated a horse and a stable.
EDCS-10102154.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a horse and stable to Dis Pater by Lucius Aelius.

EDCS-10102155.translation: To Fortuna Crescens Augusta, from the will of Fabia Ursina, Fabius the freedman, along with Mamercus Marius Salvius Tertius and Psyche, had it made at the cost of a pound of silver.
EDCS-10102155.summary: This inscription records the commissioning of a work in silver to Fortuna Crescens Augusta, funded by the will of Fabia Ursina and executed by her freedman Fabius and others.

EDCS-10102158.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Lucius Quintius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, gave this gift at his own expense.
EDCS-10102158.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Hercules Augustus by Lucius Quintius Rufus.

EDCS-10102160.translation: To Jupiter Pantheus Augustus, Titus Flavius Baeticus, freedman of the state of the Muniguenses, having accepted a place, gave this gift by decree of the council.
EDCS-10102160.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a gift to Jupiter Pantheus Augustus by Titus Flavius Baeticus, a freedman of the Muniguenses, following a decree of the town council.

EDCS-10102161.translation: A freedman of Ferronia placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-10102161.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by a freedman of Ferronia.

EDCS-10102162.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, father of the fatherland, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 16th time, consul for the 3rd time, commander for the 16th time, to the Best and Greatest Prince, the state of the Muniguenses.
EDCS-10102162.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the state of Muniguenses to Emperor Hadrian, listing his titles and honors, likely from his 16th tribunician power year (c. 132 AD).

EDCS-10102163.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, father of the fatherland, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 16th time, consul.
EDCS-10102163.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Hadrian, listing his titles and honors, including his 16th tribunician power year (c. 132 AD).

EDCS-10102165.translation: Lucius Aemilius Pudens, his son, having accepted a place, gave this gift by decree of the council, to his excellent father Lucius Aemilius Pudentus, twice chief magistrate and twice municipal magistrate of the Flavius Muniguensis municipality.
EDCS-10102165.summary: This inscription records a son's dedication to his deceased father, Lucius Aemilius Pudentus, a prominent local official of Muniguensis.

EDCS-10102166.translation: Lucius Quintius Rufus, his son, having accepted a place, placed this by decree of the council of the Flavius Muniguensis municipality, to his father Lucius Quintius Rufinus, twice chief magistrate and twice municipal magistrate of the Flavius Muniguensis municipality.
EDCS-10102166.summary: This inscription records a son's dedication to his deceased father, Lucius Quintius Rufinus, a prominent local official of Muniguensis.

EDCS-10102167.translation: Having accepted a place, he placed this by decree of the council, to Lucius Quintius Rufus, twice chief magistrate and twice municipal magistrate of the Flavius Muniguensis municipality.
EDCS-10102167.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Lucius Quintius Rufus, a prominent local official of Muniguensis, likely by a descendant or admirer.

EDCS-10102171.translation: To Aelia Procula, daughter of Lucius, wife of Gaius Licinius Victor Annianus, most longed-for. For her funeral expenses and burial place, the most distinguished council of the people of Muniguensium decreed a statue. Gaius Licinius Victor Annianus, her husband, repaid the expenses to the council of Muniguensium.
EDCS-10102171.summary: A public monument dedicated to Aelia Procula by her husband and the council of Muniguensium, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10102173.translation: To Fulvia, his most dutiful mother. Lucius Aemilius Pudens, her son, gave this gift, having received the place by decree of the council.
EDCS-10102173.summary: A dedication to Fulvia by her son, Lucius Aemilius Pudens, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10102176.translation: To Lucius Antonius, son of Lucius Nervas. Ocellia, freedwoman of Antonia, arranged for this to be made according to the will.
EDCS-10102176.summary: A funerary inscription stating that Ocellia, a freedwoman, fulfilled the will of Lucius Antonius, son of Lucius Nervas, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10102177.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Numeria Festa, aged 22, lies here. May the earth be light upon you. Annius Callistus placed this for one who deserved well.
EDCS-10102177.summary: A tombstone for Numeria Festa, erected by Annius Callistus, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10102178.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Stertinia Rhodine, aged 32 years, 1 month, and 3 days, lies here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-10102178.summary: A tombstone for Stertinia Rhodine, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10102211.translation: To Quintus Sempronius Secundinus of Baeges. His mother, Sempronia Rustica, daughter of Caius, ordered this to be erected in her will. His father, Sempronius Sempronianus Cembricinus, his heir, arranged for it to be erected.
EDCS-10102211.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Sempronius Secundinus of Baeges, erected by his parents according to his will, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10102214.translation: Marcus Helvius Anthus Lucurgentinus, one of the six priests of Augustus, after presenting a public show for four days of theatrical games, and providing the gymnasium free of charge to women for the same days, the most distinguished council of the people of Lucurgentum, at the request of the people, decreed him the honors of the decurionate. Helvius Anthus, on account of the honor, gave a statue of Janus the Father, with its base, as a gift at his own expense, and had it set up.
EDCS-10102214.summary: An inscription honoring Marcus Helvius Anthus for his public benefactions, including games and free access to the gymnasium for women, for which he was awarded honors and erected a statue of Janus Patris, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10102216.translation: Optata, slave of Quintus Postumius Calvinius, aged 39. I beg you, as you pass by, say: May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-10102216.summary: A tombstone for Optata, a slave of Quintus Postumius Calvinius, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10102217.translation: Arminius, servant of God, lived about 55 years, departed in peace on March 18th, in the year 663 AUC.
EDCS-10102217.summary: A Christian funerary inscription for Arminius, dated to 663 AUC (late 110 CE).

EDCS-10102219.translation: Trebtes, servant of Christ, lived about 12 years and departed in peace on the Ides of March, in the year 504 AUC.
EDCS-10102219.summary: A Christian funerary inscription for Trebtes, dated to 504 AUC (late 140 CE).

EDCS-10102220.summary: This inscription likely refers to a church official and might be found on a building or a tomb.

EDCS-10102222.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Lucía Aviricia Aciliana, daughter of Publius, most devoted mother. For her, the town council of Italicensis, and the people of Romulenses Hispalenses, Caesarini Asidonenses, Fortunales Siarenses, and Aeneanici Callenses decreed the expenses for her funeral and statues. Marcus Aemilius Afer Acilianus, her son, using his honor, remitted the expenses.
EDCS-10102222.summary: This funerary inscription records the posthumous honors granted to Lucía Aviricia Aciliana by various communities and her son, who ultimately paid for the funeral expenses and statues.

EDCS-10102223.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Hermeros lived 8 years, 5 months, and 20 days. We beg you passing by, say: May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-10102223.summary: This is a child's tombstone recording the age of the deceased and including a standard epitaph requesting passersby to wish them a light burial.

EDCS-10102224.translation: For Lucius Aelius Aegitanus and Aelia Attis.
EDCS-10102224.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial for Lucius Aelius Aegitanus and Aelia Attis, possibly on a tomb or a shared monument.

EDCS-10102232.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Here lies the mother. May the earth be light upon you. Vinius Rnus, freedman of Publius, 18 years old, lies here. We beg you passing by, say: May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-10102232.summary: This tombstone records the burial of a mother and her son, Vinius Rnus, including standard epitaphs for both.

EDCS-10200123.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Marciana, 60 years old, devout, lies here in her own [tomb]. May the earth be light upon you. LIR SE VARII gave and dedicated [this] to the well-deserving.
EDCS-10200123.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marciana, who died at 60, and mentions a dedication made by LIR SE VARII, suggesting a patron or relative.

EDCS-10200124.translation: [She] lived 85 years, devout in her own [tomb], here she is placed. May the earth be light upon you. res dominio
EDCS-10200124.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of a woman who lived 85 years and includes a standard epitaph for a light burial.

EDCS-10200125.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Sempronius Vetulus, 21 years old, lies here. May the earth be light upon you. His associates gave and dedicated [this].
EDCS-10200125.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Sempronius Vetulus at the age of 21 and notes that his colleagues provided the monument.

EDCS-10200127.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Seno Felix dedicated [this] at his own expense for his daughter.
EDCS-10200127.summary: This inscription records Lucius Seno Felix dedicating a monument at his own expense for his daughter.

EDCS-10200128.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Here lie the freedmen of Surius. Lirillia, his heir, placed a monument for them during her lifetime. Auctus lies here. May the earth be light upon you all.
EDCS-10200128.summary: This inscription honors the freedmen of Surius, with a monument placed by his heir Lirillia, and specifically mentions Auctus.

EDCS-10200144.translation: Publius Clodius Secundus, son of Publius, to the Second Man (IIvir), at the decree of the town councillors of the municipium Vergiliensis, having received an honour, remitted the expense.
EDCS-10200144.summary: This is a public dedication from the town councillors of the municipium Vergiliensis, honoring Publius Clodius Secundus.

EDCS-10200148.translation: Gaius Iulius Rusticus willingly fulfilled his vow to Hercules.
EDCS-10200148.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Iulius Rusticus to the god Hercules.

EDCS-10200149.translation: This is the tomb of Teudesinda; if anyone disturbs the tomb, may he depart with Judas to the fire.
EDCS-10200149.summary: This is a tomb inscription warning against disturbing the grave of Teudesinda, with a curse invoking Judas and hell.

EDCS-10200159.translation: In these tombs rests in peace the servant of God Telemachius, a priest; he lived 49 years, served in the diaconate for six years and as priest for eighteen.
EDCS-10200159.summary: This is a Christian tomb inscription for Telemachius, a priest, detailing his age and length of service in church offices.

EDCS-10200164.translation: To the nephew of Maximianus, the perpetual Augustus, born a good citizen for the republic.
EDCS-10200164.summary: This is a dedication to the nephew of Emperor Maximianus, celebrating his birth and potential contribution to the state.

EDCS-10200165.translation: Venerius, aged 20 years and 51 days, ordered this monument to be made for two people.
EDCS-10200165.summary: This is a tomb inscription set up by Venerius, aged 20 years and 51 days, for himself and one other person.

EDCS-11701041.summary: This inscription lists four names, possibly of mythical figures or individuals, without further context.

EDCS-11701057.translation: The more skilled one is known, who is Alexander.
EDCS-11701057.summary: This inscription suggests a comparison of skill, identifying 'Alexander' as the more proficient one.

EDCS-11701058.summary: This inscription lists two names, Arethusa and Amymone, likely figures from Greek mythology.

EDCS-11701144.translation: To the Lares of the Augusti, and Aurelius Faustus.
EDCS-11701144.summary: This is a dedication to the household gods (Lares) of the emperors, made by Aurelius Faustus.

EDCS-11701148.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Lusius to Titus Antonius, his great-uncle.
EDCS-11701148.summary: A funerary dedication by Gaius Lusius to his great-uncle Titus Antonius.

EDCS-11701151.translation: Iulia Modesta, freedwoman of Gaius, mother, to Gaius Aufustius, son of Gaius, Modestus of Seria.
EDCS-11701151.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulia Modesta, freedwoman of Gaius, to Gaius Aufustius Modestus of Seria.

EDCS-11701153.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caesia Agrippina lived for 56 years. Her son Titus placed this for his wife.
EDCS-11701153.summary: A funerary dedication by Titus for his wife Caesia Agrippina, who lived 56 years.

EDCS-11701156.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Flavius Lupercianus, aged 60, his devoted husband Claudia Ianuaria placed this. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11701156.summary: A funerary dedication by Claudia Ianuaria for her husband Marcus Flavius Lupercianus, who died at 60.

EDCS-11701157.translation: Lucius Helvius Euphrasius, aged 19, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11701157.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Helvius Euphrasius, aged 19.

EDCS-11701159.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Felicissima, most innocent soul, aged 20 years and 2 months. Her most loving husband Julius Vicentius ordered this monument to be made for his most deserving wife while she was alive, and he made it at his own expense.
EDCS-11701159.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Julius Vicentius for his wife Iulia Felicissima, who died at 20 years and 2 months.

EDCS-11701162.translation: Sextus Iulius Lupus, son of Medugenus, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-11701162.summary: A fragment of an inscription identifying Sextus Iulius Lupus, son of Medugenus, and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-11701164.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. G. Triphaena, slave of Caius, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11701164.summary: A funerary epitaph for G. Triphaena, a slave of Caius, who died at 50.

EDCS-11701165.summary: A very brief inscription mentioning Iunius and his horses, possibly related to a monument or event.

EDCS-11701170.translation: Teodemirus, servant of God, lived in this world 76 years and died in peace after receiving penance, on the 17th day before the Kalends of November, in the year 700 of the era.
EDCS-11701170.summary: An epitaph for Teodemirus, a servant of God, who died in 700 AE after 76 years of life and receiving penance.

EDCS-11701173.translation: Marcus C. Ae. is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11701173.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus C. Ae., a simple burial marker from the Roman period.

EDCS-11701177.translation: Octavia Maxuma gladly and deservedly paid her vow for a palm from eight pounds of silver to the Genius of the municipality of Concordia Iulia Nertobrigensis.
EDCS-11701177.summary: This is a votive inscription from Concordia Iulia Nertobrigensis, dedicated by Octavia Maxuma to the Genius of the municipality.

EDCS-11701178.summary: This is a very brief inscription that likely served as a property marker or tombstone, naming Maria Sanchez and her heirs.

EDCS-11701179.translation: Claudia Secundina, by the command of Venus.
EDCS-11701179.summary: This is a votive inscription possibly related to the cult of Venus, commissioned by Claudia Secundina.

EDCS-11701181.translation: [Unknown] Pollio / [Unknown] Suriacus / caused (this) to be made at his own expense.
EDCS-11701181.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument by Pollio and Suriacus, likely as a private commission.

EDCS-11701184.translation: Fructuosa lived 12 years; she is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you, Cosia's countrywoman.
EDCS-11701184.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fructuosa, recording her age and burial location, likely from a provincial context.

EDCS-11701186.translation: Exuperantius, servant of God, lived 78 years more or less, rested in peace on the 6th day before the Kalends of June, in the year 616 (of the Seleucid Era).
EDCS-11701186.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Exuperantius, a Christian convert, dating to 616 SE (304/305 CE).

EDCS-11701187.translation: In the name of the Lord, this tomb of the blessed Honorius, abbot, who holds the heavenly kingdom forever and ever, amen, you look upon a narrow tomb cut from a sheer rock.
EDCS-11701187.summary: This is a medieval epitaph for Honorius, an abbot, describing his tomb and posthumous status.

EDCS-11701190.translation: M. A. Zetonius gladly paid his vow to Dis Pater, the Father.
EDCS-11701190.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Dis Pater by M. A. Zetonius.

EDCS-11701197.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Aelius Exoratus, 60 years of age, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11701197.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Aelius Exoratus, recording his age and burial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701199.translation: Here lies Lucius Helvius Rufinus, aged 60, and Lucius Helvius Novatus, aged 41, and Marcus Helvius Rufus, aged 40. Helvia Severa, daughter of Lucius, and Marcus Helvius Rufinus, son of Rufus, had this made; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11701199.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Helvius Rufinus and two other family members, erected by Helvia Severa and Marcus Helvius Rufinus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11701200.translation: Iulia Rufina, mother, had this made for Lucius Marcius Quintinus, aged 33.
EDCS-11701200.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Marcius Quintinus, commissioned by his mother, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11701201.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. His pious son placed this for Memmia, aged 70, in her own [tomb], for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-11701201.summary: A funerary dedication to Memmia, erected by her son, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11701202.translation: Quartus, slave of Marcus Rasticius Rufianus, aged 18, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. His father had this made.
EDCS-11701202.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quartus, a slave, commissioned by his father, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11701203.translation: Valeria Paeta, daughter of Publius, aged 12.
EDCS-11701203.summary: A funerary marker for Valeria Paeta, indicating her age at death, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11701205.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For the most dutiful son, aged 32, [dedicated by] Titus's son, Galeria, to his mother.
EDCS-11701205.summary: A funerary dedication to an unnamed mother and her son, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11701223.translation: Dedicated to Salus (Health) in fulfillment of a vow by Marcus Marius Caesianus.
EDCS-11701223.summary: A votive dedication to the goddess Salus, made by Marcus Marius Caesianus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11701226.translation: Here lies Lucius Fabius, son of Lucius, aged 50. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Atilia Restituta, daughter of Marcus, and Atilia Honorata, daughter of Marcus, aged 39, and Sempronia Exorata, daughter of Lucius, aged 26, had this made.
EDCS-11701226.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Fabius, commissioned by Atilia Restituta and other women, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11701227.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iunia Calirhoe, aged 100, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Chrestus placed [this] for his mother.
EDCS-11701227.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iunia Calirhoe, erected by her son Chrestus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11701228.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Manlius Avitus.
EDCS-11701228.summary: A funerary dedication to the spirits of the underworld for Quintus Manlius Avitus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11701229.translation: To Melpomene, aged 65. Iulia Talia, freedwoman, saw to its construction according to the terms of her will.
EDCS-11701229.summary: This epitaph for Melpomene, aged 65, was commissioned by her freedwoman Iulia Talia.

EDCS-11701232.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Tullius H, aged 21, is buried here. His mother, Tullia Alv, made this for her most devoted son according to his will.
EDCS-11701232.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Quintus Tullius H, aged 21, commissioned by his mother Tullia Alv.

EDCS-11701236.translation: Iunius, servant of God, lived 81 years and rested in peace on the 3rd day before the Kalends of February in the year 554 (of the era).
EDCS-11701236.summary: This epitaph records the death of Iunius, a servant of God, who died in 554 (AD 500) and specifies his burial date.

EDCS-11701238.translation: Granniola, servant of God, who lived with her husband for 20 years and rested in peace in the year 553 (of the era).
EDCS-11701238.summary: This epitaph records the death of Granniola, a servant of God, who died in 553 (AD 499) after 20 years of marriage.

EDCS-11701241.translation: Marcus Baebius Optatus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, from Lagimurgis, aged 50, is buried here. IOA Baebia Galla, freedwoman of Marcus, saw to its construction from her own funds. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-11701241.summary: This epitaph for Marcus Baebius Optatus, aged 50, was commissioned by his freedwoman Baebia Galla.

EDCS-11701243.translation: Valerius Flavinus C PEL, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-11701243.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Valerius Flavinus C PEL.

EDCS-11701244.summary: This fragment likely refers to a monument dedicated to Quintus Sertorius, who held the office of proconsul.

EDCS-11701248.translation: Herbulius B, a most distinguished man, aged more or less 60, departed in peace on the 7th day before the Ides of [Month] in the year 400 (of the era). This tomb for Leontius, aged 51, in the year 500 (of the era).
EDCS-11701248.summary: This epitaph records the death of Herbulius B, aged around 60, and also mentions a tomb for Leontius, aged 51, with differing era dates.

EDCS-11701252.translation: Freedman and procurator of Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus.
EDCS-11701252.summary: This inscription identifies an imperial freedman who served as a procurator under Emperor Nerva.

EDCS-11701255.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Hostilius, son of Publius, named Tianus, aged 20, may the earth be light upon you. Sertoria VI, daughter of Titus, his mother.
EDCS-11701255.summary: This epitaph for Quintus Hostilius Tianus, aged 20, was commissioned by his mother Sertoria VI.

EDCS-11701257.translation: Publius Plotius Reburrus, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, from the fort of Callaeco, aged 21, is buried here; this eternal resting place, a work of brotherly affection, which he dedicated to his brother Vegetus from death, he paid his just due of love with his sepulchre and his own devotion.
EDCS-11701257.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Publius Plotius Reburrus, who died at age 21, and his devotion to his brother Vegetus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11701259.translation: Maturo, of Turibriga, aged 60, and Lupercus, aged 27, Maxima made this for her husband and sons at her own expense.
EDCS-11701259.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Maturo and Lupercus, with Maxima commissioning the monument at her own expense, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11701260.translation: Flavia Severa, his mother, arranged for this to be made for Siusnus Turibrigensis, aged 32. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11701260.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Siusnus Turibrigensis, aged 32, with his mother Flavia Severa commissioning the monument, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11701265.translation: Marcus I A erected this in fulfillment of a vow. He gladly paid his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-11701265.summary: This dedication records Marcus I A fulfilling a vow to the god Mercury, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11701268.translation: Her husband erects this [monument] for her, who lived 40 years. May she lie in peace.
EDCS-11701268.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a woman who lived 40 years, with her husband commissioning the monument, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11701285.translation: Apolausis, the beloved, aged 48 days, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11701285.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a very young child named Apolausis, aged 48 days, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11701287.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caia Valeria Amma, daughter of Caius, aged 51, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11701287.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caia Valeria Amma, aged 51, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11701288.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Valerius Valerianus lived 80 years, and his sister Me Gala; they are buried here.
EDCS-11701288.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Valerius Valerianus, who lived 80 years, and his sister Me Gala, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11701292.translation: To the god Liber Pater, in fulfillment of a vow. Plotia Severa and Valerius Crescanius fulfilled their vow with willing hearts on the third day before the Kalends of March.
EDCS-11701292.summary: This dedication records Plotia Severa and Valerius Crescanius fulfilling a vow to the god Liber Pater on March 1st, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11701296.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavia, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11701296.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Flavia, aged 30, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11701297.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gallus, aged 30, had this made by his brothers. May the earth lie lightly on you here.
EDCS-11701297.summary: A tombstone for Gallus, aged 30, set up by his brothers, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701300.translation: Da, daughter of Arconis; Optatus, son of Saelgi; Iulina, daughter of Saelgi; Amoena, Silvanus, Agricola, sons of Senecae. May the earth lie lightly on you here. They had this made in accordance with the will for their grandmother and uncle, and for their father.
EDCS-11701300.summary: A tombstone for several individuals, including Da, Optatus, Iulina, Amoena, Silvanus, and Agricola, established by will for their relatives, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701301.translation: Lucius Tancinus set this up. His miserable mother. May the earth lie lightly on you here.
EDCS-11701301.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Tancinus, set up by his mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701304.summary: An inscription likely from a workshop or indicating ownership by Vincentius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800611.translation: Caius Aurelius Galeria Crito and Lucius V gave this from their own money.
EDCS-11800611.summary: A dedication of a monument or object by Caius Aurelius Galeria Crito and Lucius V, funded by themselves, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800614.translation: In the consulship of Sextus [---] Publius [---].
EDCS-11800614.summary: An inscription marking a date by the consuls Sextus [---] and Publius [---], dating to the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-11800832.translation: Lucius Helvius Lupus from Emerita, 8 months old. May the earth lie lightly on you here. Helvia Secundilla (mother) and Probus (father) had this made.
EDCS-11800832.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Helvius Lupus, aged 8 months, set up by his parents Helvia Secundilla and Probus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800833.translation: Germanus, slave of Marinus, aged 15. May the earth lie lightly on you here.
EDCS-11800833.summary: A tombstone for Germanus, a slave of Marinus, aged 15, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800834.translation: Materna, daughter of Licinia Paterni, from Novaugusta, aged 30. May the earth lie lightly on you here.
EDCS-11800834.summary: A tombstone for Materna, daughter of Licinia Paterni, aged 30, from Novaugusta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800836.translation: The college of health [or 'well-being'] to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-11800836.summary: A dedication by a collegium (association) to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely for the well-being of its members, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800880.translation: To Apollo Augustus, Cornelius Marti, from his own funds.
EDCS-11800880.summary: An offering to the god Apollo Augustus by Cornelius Marti.

EDCS-11901708.translation: Placidina erected this in fulfillment of a vow to the Lares Viales.
EDCS-11901708.summary: A dedication to the household gods of the street (Lares Viales) by Placidina.

EDCS-13700421.translation: Procula, daughter of Rufus, willingly fulfilled her vow and dedicated this gift to the goddess Medica.
EDCS-13700421.summary: A votive offering to the goddess Medica by Procula.

EDCS-13900414.summary: A tombstone or epitaph for Servandus, a scout.

EDCS-14700034.translation: Titus Rufonius Quintianus, son of Titus Rufonius Brocchinus, a merchant of ironmongery, a resident of Romulea, aged 10 years, 9 months, and 11 days, dutiful to his family. May the earth be light upon you, innocent little soul.
EDCS-14700034.summary: An epitaph for a young boy named Titus Rufonius Quintianus, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-14700039.translation: To Proculus, O spirit of Proculus.
EDCS-14700039.summary: A funerary inscription likely addressed to the spirit of the deceased Proculus.

EDCS-14700304.translation: Fronto Modo Limicus is buried here.
EDCS-14700304.summary: A tombstone marking the burial place of Fronto Modo Limicus.

EDCS-16201532.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Lucius Valerius Fecula, member of the college of duoviri, aged 80. May the earth be light upon you. Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Acicia Antuca, aged 102. She is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-16201532.summary: A double epitaph for Lucius Valerius Fecula and Acicia Antuca, one a duovir and the other very old.

EDCS-16400428.translation: Lucius M. B., decurion of Concordiensis, erected this monument to the Genius of the municipality, dedicating and fulfilling his vow with his money and will.
EDCS-16400428.summary: A monument dedicated to the Genius of the municipality by Lucius M. B., a decurion.

EDCS-16400450.summary: An inscription identifying Lucius Attius Forn as a flamen (priest).

EDCS-16400455.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Cocceius Martensis, a good man, lived 22 years. Tiberius Flavius Felicio, his father, made this for his most devoted son. If the spirits are wise, the father has [done well]. Or, [do you understand]? (The meaning of the last two phrases is debated.)
EDCS-16400455.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cocceius Martensis, set up by his father, Tiberius Flavius Felicio, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16400456.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Elia Eliodora lived 30 years. Elia Lucana, her most unhappy mother, made this. She is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-16400456.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Elia Eliodora, erected by her mother, Elia Lucana, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16900174.translation: To Lucius Vibius Amantus, 13 and a half pounds.
EDCS-16900174.summary: This inscription records a weight of 13.5 pounds possibly related to goods or a dedication to Lucius Vibius Amantus.

EDCS-16900194.translation: Macona, a devoted servant of God, lived 52 years and rested in peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of March in the year 52 (AD 16/17 or 552 AUC).
EDCS-16900194.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Macona, a servant of God, noting her age and date of death, likely from the early Imperial period or the era of Augustus.

EDCS-16900195.summary: This is a dedication to water springs by someone named Cornelius, the date is not specified.

EDCS-16900198.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Licinius Tancinus, Licinius Ianus. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-16900198.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Licinius Tancinus, possibly mentioning another individual named Licinius Ianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16900199.translation: To the August Mothers, Julius Amabilis and Gaius Marius Cursor, the first Augustales, gave from their own funds.
EDCS-16900199.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the 'August Mothers' by Julius Amabilis and Gaius Marius Cursor, who were the first Augustales, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16900218.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying Boutia as the daughter of Alucius, likely a funerary or ownership marker.

EDCS-16900224.translation: Dannius Rusticus, son of Gaius, 8 years old.
EDCS-16900224.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Dannius Rusticus, son of Gaius, likely from a funerary context.

EDCS-16900227.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Mamilius Maximus, from Galeria Contributensis, 65 years old.
EDCS-16900227.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mamilius Maximus, who was 65 years old and possibly from the Galeria tribe, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16900229.translation: Iuliana, servant of God, who lived 20 years and with her husband 1 year, rested in peace in the year 553 (AD 295).
EDCS-16900229.summary: This epitaph records the death of Iuliana, a Christian woman, in 553.

EDCS-16900300.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely a funerary marker, for Quintus Flavius Decimus Vaccius.

EDCS-16900301.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely a funerary marker, for Publius Vibius Taiedossius.

EDCS-16900302.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely a funerary marker, for Eburinus Tyche Aulus Bassaeus Adlenteus.

EDCS-16900303.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely a funerary marker, for Adlenteus Unibele Hispanus LE.

EDCS-16900304.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely a funerary marker, for ENDAIDY Atausus Peicani Atausus.

EDCS-16900306.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely a funerary marker, for NIV Decimus Indus.

EDCS-16900307.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely a funerary marker, for Abloonius PCVH Aulus DOND.

EDCS-16900308.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely a funerary marker, for Quintus Decimus Vivius.

EDCS-16900311.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely a funerary marker, for Quintus Volusius Lucius Vivius Volusius.

EDCS-18100312.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Comarius erected this memorial for my wife Silvina, who lived with me for 25 years. May the earth be light upon you. She is buried here.
EDCS-18100312.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Comarius for his deceased wife Silvina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18100318.translation: Here lie I, son of La Dautaionis, and [my wife] Avito, daughter of Avitonis, for herself and her family.
EDCS-18100318.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial place of an individual and their spouse, intended for them and their descendants.

EDCS-18100321.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maximus, 33 years old, son of Ili, erected this for his most devoted son. He is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-18100321.summary: A funerary inscription from a mother to her deceased son, Maximus, who died at age 33, with a wish for his afterlife.

EDCS-18100323.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Mustia of Utica lived for 28 years. Lucius Firmidius Peregrinus took care to have this made for his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-18100323.summary: A funerary inscription honoring Caecilia Mustia of Utica, set up by her husband Lucius Firmidius Peregrinus.

EDCS-18800123.summary: An inscription likely belonging to a monument or dedication honouring Gnaeus Magnus, identified as a general.

EDCS-18800125.translation: The townspeople and residents gave this to Gaius Iuventius Albinus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, as aedile, twice chief magistrate, a most generous chief magistrate, and patron of the fatherland, for his merits.
EDCS-18800125.summary: A dedication by the townspeople and residents to Gaius Iuventius Albinus for his distinguished service as aedile and chief magistrate.

EDCS-19100692.translation: Nicius, 7 years old, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-19100692.summary: A tombstone for a young child named Nicius, who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-19100699.summary: An inscription likely marking the dimensions of a burial plot or tomb belonging to Caecilia Calliope.

EDCS-19100700.translation: Rocius is buried here. May the earth be light upon you. 12 feet in front, 10 feet in the field. Calus, his son, generously placed [this] for his father.
EDCS-19100700.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Calus for his father Rocius, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-19100705.translation: T. Bassius, son of Publius, is buried here.
EDCS-19100705.summary: A tombstone identifying the burial of T. Bassius, son of Publius.

EDCS-19100706.translation: Maela, freedwoman and villager, is placed here, a space of 12 feet.
EDCS-19100706.summary: This inscription is a funerary marker for Maela, specifying the dimensions of her burial plot.

EDCS-19100707.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Politice, aged 33. Cattia Ornata.
EDCS-19100707.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Politice, likely set up by Cattia Ornata.

EDCS-19100788.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Dorssuaria, aged 31, pious, is placed here in her own [burial place]. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-19100788.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dorssuaria, a pious woman who is buried in her own plot.

EDCS-19100789.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Myrinus, aged 45, pious, is placed here in his own [burial place].
EDCS-19100789.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Myrinus, a pious man buried in his own plot.

EDCS-19100791.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Manlius.
EDCS-19100791.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication for Manlius.

EDCS-19100796.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a name and filiation, possibly funerary or commemorative.

EDCS-19100838.translation: Acilia Plecusa [dedicated this] to a friend.
EDCS-19100838.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Acilia Plecusa to a friend.

EDCS-20000093.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. L. Minicius Actus Crispus, who died at Ruse, aged 25, is buried here.
EDCS-20000093.summary: This is a funerary inscription for L. Minicius Actus Crispus, specifying his age and place of death and burial.

EDCS-20100286.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-20301593.translation: Of Marcus Naevius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-20301593.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Marcus Naevius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-20301602.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Camillus Melissa.

EDCS-20301603.translation: Of the Second Cohort of Juniors, Melissaeus and Melissa.
EDCS-20301603.summary: This is a funerary dedication likely by or for Melissaeus and Melissa from the Second Cohort of Juniors.

EDCS-20301616.summary: This inscription likely records the name and office of Quintus Coelius, possibly with a dedication or a name 'Iuso'.

EDCS-20301618.summary: This inscription appears to list the names of Aulus Atilius and Lucius Aemilius, possibly on a monument.

EDCS-20301619.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Sergius Rex, possibly as a dedication or on a tomb.

EDCS-20301663.summary: This inscription states that the Consul was responsible for the construction of the monument.

EDCS-20301774.translation: Of Potitus, son of Caius Valerius.
EDCS-20301774.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Potitus, son of Caius Valerius.

EDCS-20301790.summary: This inscription indicates ownership of a workshop by Patricius.

EDCS-20301791.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Caius Vibienus Varus.

EDCS-20301792.summary: This inscription indicates ownership of a workshop by Patricia.

EDCS-20400049.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Octavia Fortunata lived 24 years. Aurelius Lupus placed this for his deserving wife. She rests here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20400049.summary: A funerary epitaph for Octavia Fortunata, erected by her husband Aurelius Lupus.

EDCS-20400199.summary: An inscription dedicating something to Gaius Memmius, identified as a son and an imperator.

EDCS-20400205.translation: To our Gaius. Princeps, the steward.
EDCS-20400205.summary: A dedication to someone named Gaius by Princeps, who is identified as a steward.

EDCS-20400344.translation: Leo, son of Verucis, to his wife Anui.
EDCS-20400344.summary: A dedication by Leo, son of Verucis, to his wife Anui.

EDCS-20400361.translation: Marcia Voluptas freely paid her vow to the Lady Isis, according to her vow and command.
EDCS-20400361.summary: A votive inscription by Marcia Voluptas to the goddess Isis.

EDCS-20400362.translation: Soter freely paid his vow to Queen Isis.
EDCS-20400362.summary: A votive inscription by Soter to the goddess Isis.

EDCS-20400363.translation: Iunia Cerasa freely paid her vow to the little lady Bubastis.
EDCS-20400363.summary: A votive inscription by Iunia Cerasa to the goddess Bubastis.

EDCS-20400364.translation: Ulpia Privata gave and dedicated this at the command of Juno.
EDCS-20400364.summary: A dedication by Ulpia Privata to an unspecified recipient, motivated by the command of Juno.

EDCS-20400365.summary: A fragmented inscription mentioning neighbours and a personal name, possibly a boundary marker or a record of a dispute.

EDCS-20400529.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Livius [restored name] lived 50 years, 10 months, and 4 days. He rests here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20400529.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Livius [name restored], providing his age at death.

EDCS-20400530.translation: To the underworld spirits, Gaius, son of Atius Ner, aged 4 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-20400530.summary: A tombstone for a young child named Gaius, dated by its style and formula to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401596.translation: To Usulenius Prosper, former consular governor of the province of Baetica, a man of consular rank, an equestrian statue.
EDCS-20401596.summary: An inscription recording the dedication of an equestrian statue to a high-ranking official, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401598.translation: Sentia Sura, freedwoman of Paullus, from Metella, aged 36 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-20401598.summary: A tombstone for a freedwoman named Sentia Sura, indicating her age and place of origin, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401600.translation: With mournful grief, the mother adds solace to the spirits and buries her daughter in a small tomb; her age, which had not yet reached two years, she had not kept for the years; behold why life is subject to envy, she was allotted this place. Miserable are the bones of the girl, may you rest undisturbed in eternal abodes, daughter. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-20401600.summary: A poignant epitaph from a mother mourning her daughter who died before the age of two, emphasizing the fragility of life.

EDCS-21700217.translation: Alexander, bravest soldier, servant of God, lived 74 years, more or less. He departed in peace on the Kalends of March, in the era 639.
EDCS-21700217.summary: An epitaph for a soldier named Alexander, indicating his age, date of death, and religious affiliation, likely from the late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-21700220.translation: To Lucius Caecilius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Scaptia tribe, as governor with the rank of propraetor.
EDCS-21700220.summary: An inscription honoring Lucius Caecilius Rufus, a Roman official who served as a propraetorian legate, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700234.translation: Pascentius, lover and faithful worshipper of God, departed from this life, aged 28 years. As soon as he perceived the voice of Christ in his ears, he renounced the world and its fleeting pomps and the deadly life and drunken cups of Bacchus, so that his sober soul might behold the heavenly realms. For as he fought bravely in this contest, it pleased God to take him to Himself before His tribunal, to give him a palm, a robe, and a crown. You who read these words and are delighted by hope, learn justice, having been warned, and do not despise God.
EDCS-21700234.summary: A Christian epitaph for Pascentius, detailing his conversion and virtuous life, and urging readers to learn justice and not to despise God, dated to the late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-21700239.translation: Romania, daughter of Vitalis, is buried here.
EDCS-21700239.summary: A simple tomb inscription for Romania, daughter of Vitalis.

EDCS-21700347.translation: Dedicated to the underworld spirits. Licinia Damale, aged 35 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700347.summary: A tombstone for a woman named Licinia Damale, indicating her age and a common funerary formula, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700396.translation: Dedicated to the underworld spirits. Anniolena Trophime, freedwoman of Quintus Anniolenus Lusius, from Rome, aged 102 years, pious, is buried in her own [tomb]. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700396.summary: An epitaph for a very elderly freedwoman named Anniolena Trophime, mentioning her age, origin, and patron, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700453.translation: Iunia, daughter of Marcus, 14 feet in front, 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-21700453.summary: This inscription appears to record land measurements, possibly for a property boundary or a grave plot, belonging to Iunia, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-21700457.summary: This very short inscription likely records the age of Flavius Aelius, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-21700470.translation: Fabia Sempronia Aciliana Celtitana, daughter of Marcus, 15 years, 8 months, and 12 days old.
EDCS-21700470.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Fabia Sempronia Aciliana Celtitana, likely from a funerary monument.

EDCS-21700485.translation: Let there be law and authority for the decrees and constitutions of the deified Augustus, Tiberius Julius Caesar Augustus, Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus, Emperor Galba Caesar Augustus, Emperor Vespasian Caesar Augustus, Emperor Titus Caesar Vespasian Augustus, Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus, chief priest, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-21700485.summary: This inscription is a legal text, likely from an official monument, affirming the authority of imperial decrees and constitutions from Augustus through Domitian.

EDCS-21700528.translation: For Fabia Vieta, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-21700528.summary: This is a dedication, likely for a tombstone, honoring Fabia Vieta, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-21700630.translation: On the day before the Kalends of August, Sextus Marius, son of Sextus, established a guest-friendship with.
EDCS-21700630.summary: This inscription records the establishment of a guest-friendship (hospitium) by Sextus Marius on a specific date, likely related to a legal or social agreement.

EDCS-21900136.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Julius Epaphroditus, freedman of Caius Julius Acilianus, is buried here.
EDCS-21900136.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Julius Epaphroditus, a freedman, identifying his former master and stating he is buried here.

EDCS-21900138.translation: Iunius Restitutus, son of Tiberius, of the Pupinia tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-21900138.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iunius Restitutus, son of Tiberius, identifying his tribal affiliation and stating he is buried here.

EDCS-21900142.translation: Caius Cabius Atius of Olisipo is buried here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-21900142.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Cabius Atius, identifying his place of origin and including a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-21900143.translation: Marcus Iunius, son of Marcus, is buried here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-21900143.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iunius, son of Marcus, identifying his father and including a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-21900146.translation: Cilius Cularus, son of Ovatus, is buried here.
EDCS-21900146.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cilius Cularus, son of Ovatus.

EDCS-21900147.translation: Antonius Caladunus, son of Marcus, of the castle, aged 20 years, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-21900147.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonius Caladunus, aged 20, who served in a castle.

EDCS-21900148.translation: To the deified Emperor Augustus, Restituta Iulia Segida [dedicated this].
EDCS-21900148.summary: This is a dedication to the deified Emperor Augustus by Restituta Iulia Segida.

EDCS-21900149.translation: Of Lucius Aurunculeius Atticus, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-21900149.summary: This is likely a funerary inscription or a dedication mentioning Lucius Aurunculeius Atticus.

EDCS-21900152.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name on a monument or grave.

EDCS-21900155.translation: [She] is buried here, aged [---], from Olisipo. May the earth be light upon you. [She] had this made.
EDCS-21900155.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman from Olisipo, with the costs of burial covered by someone else.

EDCS-21900157.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Calpurnia Afra, 25.
EDCS-21900157.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calpurnia Afra, aged 25.

EDCS-21900160.translation: Celer Limicus, son of Erbutus, a Borean from Cantiberis, gave the tessera of the munus in the year when Marcus Licinius was consul.
EDCS-21900160.summary: This inscription records the giving of a 'munus' tessera (likely a token for a public feast or duty) by Celer Limicus in the year of the consulship of Marcus Licinius.

EDCS-21900162.translation: [She] is buried here, aged 32 years. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-21900162.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed woman, aged 32.

EDCS-21900164.translation: Camalus, son of Tacitus, is buried here.
EDCS-21900164.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Camalus, son of Tacitus.

EDCS-21900165.translation: SM, son of Olisipo, is buried here.
EDCS-21900165.summary: This inscription records the burial of SM, son of Olisipo, in Lisbon.

EDCS-21900247.translation: Marcus Petrucidius, son of Marcus, legate with praetorian power of Gnaeus Tarquinius.
EDCS-21900247.summary: This inscription names Marcus Petrucidius, son of Marcus, as a legate under Gnaeus Tarquinius, likely a proconsul or governor, indicating a senatorial career.

EDCS-21900276.translation: Agrius Cuicit, Pontius Cuicit, to Aphrodisia.
EDCS-21900276.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or offering made by Agrius Cuicit and Pontius Cuicit to the goddess Aphrodisia.

EDCS-21900277.translation: Nata, daughter of M., lies here with Dolona Banda.
EDCS-21900277.summary: This inscription records the burial of Nata, daughter of M., and Dolona Banda.

EDCS-22200060.translation: Mistress Attaegina, having gathered her worshippers from Turubrige, the masters fulfilled their vow willingly.
EDCS-22200060.summary: This is a votive inscription by the masters, members of the Turubrige community, dedicated to their patron deity, Mistress Attaegina.

EDCS-22200063.translation: Galeria, daughter of Lucius, aged 59, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22200063.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Galeria, daughter of Lucius, who died at the age of 59.

EDCS-22200075.translation: Sacred to Salus (Health). Lucius Tutilius Valentinus willingly set this up.
EDCS-22200075.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Salus (Health) by Lucius Tutilius Valentinus.

EDCS-22200151.translation: To the departed spirits, mius Albu s took care to have this made.
EDCS-22200151.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Dis Manibus (departed spirits), commissioned by mius Albu s.

EDCS-22200189.translation: Priscus Attalus, son of Priscus, augur, to Invicta Roma Augusta.
EDCS-22200189.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Priscus Attalus, son of Priscus, who held the religious office of augur, to the deified Augusta Roma.

EDCS-22200190.translation: At the request of Quintus Pomponius Artigiensis, by order of the municipality of Lacibis, and at the petition of the people, Lucius Domitius Fabius took care to have this made at his own expense, and he also dedicated it by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-22200190.summary: This is a public works inscription recording that Lucius Domitius Fabius funded and dedicated a project at the request of Quintus Pomponius Artigiensis and the local council of Lacibis.

EDCS-22200270.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Baccarus lived 35 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-22200270.summary: A funerary epitaph for Baccarus, recording his age and expressing a wish for his repose.

EDCS-22200271.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Capella lived 11 years and 18 days. She is buried here among her own people. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-22200271.summary: A funerary epitaph for Capella, detailing her age at death and her burial location.

EDCS-22200275.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Firmus lived 15 years. His mother Britta had this made.
EDCS-22200275.summary: A funerary epitaph for Firmus, indicating his age and that his mother commissioned the monument.

EDCS-22400342.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Cornelia Veneriosa lived 81 years. She is buried here among her own people. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-22400342.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Veneriosa, stating her age and burial location.

EDCS-22400357.translation: elia Quieta lived 65 years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-22400357.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of 'elia Quieta' and a wish for her repose.

EDCS-22400358.translation: Cae lived [---] years. She is buried here among her own people.
EDCS-22400358.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for 'Cae', noting her burial location and familial association.

EDCS-22400393.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Pompeia Hasenna Carculensis lived 38 years. She is buried here among her own people. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-22400393.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pompeia Hasenna Carculensis, recording her age and burial location.

EDCS-22400394.translation: Maurus lived 45 years. He is buried here among his own people. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-22400394.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maurus, stating his age and burial location.

EDCS-22400398.translation: He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Plecusa.
EDCS-22400398.summary: A funerary epitaph for Plecusa, indicating her burial and expressing a wish for her repose.

EDCS-22400399.translation: The parents made this for their son Caius Iulius Cornelius, who lived 1 year. His bones are buried here. Farewell, farewell, farewell, farewell. May you rest here, my daughter.
EDCS-22400399.summary: A funerary epitaph from parents to their infant son Caius Iulius Cornelius, recording his age and the parents' grief.

EDCS-22400459.translation: To Julia Vrica, Salvitia, and Julia Vrica.
EDCS-22400459.summary: This is a dedication to three women named Julia Vrica, Salvitia, and Julia Vrica, likely from a tomb or a public monument.

EDCS-22400460.translation: To Bishop Marcianus, for his well-being.
EDCS-22400460.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Bishop Marcianus, possibly for his protection or honor.

EDCS-22500739.translation: Natalis made 35 bricks for the decurion of the town of Kaustas.
EDCS-22500739.summary: This is a record of production by Natalis of 35 bricks for a decurion of the town of Kaustas, likely a building inscription.

EDCS-22500740.translation: Clarus, witness to the roofs, and Martinus, whose limbs this hall contains, who served Christ and sought monastic life, having lived regularly, ruled the Astigitan church in the citadel for the heroes; he was laid here indeed, sculpted in marble. The year 1060, the third day before the Ides of May. Reader, commend him to God with pious prayer.
EDCS-22500740.summary: This epitaph commemorates Clarus and Martinus, the latter a monk and bishop who ruled the church in Astigi, buried in the year 1060 (likely A.D. 1022) on May 13th.

EDCS-22500742.translation: Died Matheus, servant of God, a little child. Year T.
EDCS-22500742.summary: This is a very brief epitaph recording the death of a young child named Matheus, a servant of God, with an incomplete date.

EDCS-22500743.translation: Here Speciosa rests, buried together with her daughter Tranquilla, a sacred virgin, who entered the grave in the year 1005, and then her mother passed away on the last day of the year 1004.
EDCS-22500743.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Speciosa and her daughter Tranquilla, a virgin, whose deaths occurred in consecutive years, 1005 and 1004 (likely A.D. 967 and 966 respectively).

EDCS-22500744.translation: The bishop asks his exalted Lord and the forgiveness of Christ, the weeping, the glorious one, whom the holy and illustrious master Leo will bury with all due honor; for what he has benefited for hope, therefore, reader, continually asking the Lord that forgiveness may remain, may he live eternal life, the bishop.
EDCS-22500744.summary: This inscription is a prayer for forgiveness and eternal life for a bishop, requesting the reader to pray for him, and mentioning a master named Leo who will bury him.

EDCS-22500745.translation: Here the dry limbs of Teudefred lie at rest, whose brilliant origin briefly shone forth, renowned; he entered the grave a hundred times over.
EDCS-22500745.summary: This epitaph commemorates Teudefred, whose brief but glorious life is described, and who died at a very advanced age, indicated by 'a hundred times over'.

EDCS-22600034.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Bebius Marcianus, aged 10 years, 8 months, 28 days, pious among his own. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22600034.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caius Bebius Marcianus, who died at the age of 10 years, 8 months, and 28 days.

EDCS-22600040.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that likely records the name of a person or people, possibly related to a tomb or dedication.

EDCS-22600056.translation: The place of memory for Valeria, daughter of Penius, aged thirty years.
EDCS-22600056.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial place and age of Valeria.

EDCS-22600057.translation: Recosindus the abbot lies here; a small urn came out. He departed from his lifetime and sleeps with his fatherland, his body shining like Jupiter, excellent, noble, comely, into eternal security. He sent [this] on Jupiter's day, the 1st of the Kalends of ____.
EDCS-22600057.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Recosindus, an abbot, describing his virtues and death date.

EDCS-22600058.translation: Ciprianus now lies on the celestial fields, full of grace. He was noble in the world, severe, and peaceful, sweet to his parents, sprinkled with the dew of heaven, cleansed by the streams of Christ. For on Jupiter's day he put his body in the earth, and in the days of Janus, in the said month. For he lived in the world for forty-one thousand years, thirty years and eight years.
EDCS-22600058.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ciprianus, praising his noble character and listing his death date and lifespan.

EDCS-22600059.translation: In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, the handmaiden of God Maria died in the month of August in the year 158.
EDCS-22600059.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Maria, a handmaiden of God, in August of the year 158.

EDCS-22600061.translation: The handmaiden of God Florite died a divine death in the year 89 of the indiction, in March.
EDCS-22600061.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Florite, a handmaiden of God, in March of the year 89.

EDCS-22600063.translation: Floresindus the deacon made this inscription for King Mohammet, king of the Saracens.
EDCS-22600063.summary: This inscription records that Floresindus the deacon commissioned this monument for King Mohammet.

EDCS-22600066.translation: Bracari, live well with your people.
EDCS-22600066.summary: This is a brief wish for well-being, possibly a greeting or a funerary blessing.

EDCS-22600067.summary: This is a simple dedication or self-identification by Petrus, a servant of Christ.

EDCS-22700533.translation: Valerius, aged 12 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22700533.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valerius, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-22700538.translation: Here lies the daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-22700538.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that the daughter of Lucius is buried here.

EDCS-22700556.summary: This is a personal name, likely from a funerary monument.

EDCS-22700603.summary: This is a patronymic, indicating a filial relationship, likely part of a longer inscription.

EDCS-22700604.summary: This is a Roman personal name including praenomen, nomen, and filiation, likely from a funerary or public inscription.

EDCS-22700608.summary: This inscription fragment names a male individual and indicates he is buried here, likely a funerary epitaph.

EDCS-22700610.translation: Valeria Saturnina, dear to her family, aged...
EDCS-22700610.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Valeria Saturnina, indicating her affection from her family and her age.

EDCS-22700615.translation: ... dear to his family, is laid here
EDCS-22700615.summary: This inscription fragment honors an individual and states they are buried here, likely a funerary epitaph.

EDCS-22700620.translation: Protogenes, aged 36. I beg you, passerby, as you go, say: may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-22700620.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Protogenes, giving his age and including a standard Greek funerary wish.

EDCS-22800138.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibia Pyra, stating her age.

EDCS-22800148.summary: This inscription fragment appears to be part of a legal or official record mentioning a name, Avitus.

EDCS-22800155.translation: Acurius Cilo, son of Sextus, is buried here.
EDCS-22800155.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying the deceased, Acurius Cilo, son of Sextus, and stating his burial location.

EDCS-22800156.translation: Baebia Ingenua, most dutiful, 24 years old, dear to her family, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-22800156.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Baebia Ingenua, recording her age (24) and expressing a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-22800331.translation: Marcus Aelius Viator, son of Quintus, 75 years old, dutiful to his family, may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-22800331.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aelius Viator, stating his age (75) and indicating his devotion to his family.

EDCS-23002047.translation: Quadratus, a slave, willingly paid his vow to the goddess Ataecina, Proserpina of Turibrigensis.
EDCS-23002047.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a slave named Quadratus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Ataecina, Proserpina of Turibrigensis.

EDCS-23002202.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Annia.
EDCS-23002202.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the 'Dis Manibus' (Spirits of the Dead) by Annia.

EDCS-23002232.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely identifying an individual named Marcus Aelius Valerius, possibly on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-23002242.translation: Marcus, son of Quirinus, a dear father to his family, may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-23002242.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus, son of Quirinus, highlighting his role as a beloved father.

EDCS-23002244.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating that Hilara is buried at this location.

EDCS-23002250.translation: Clodius Pius, shaven, dear to his family, 18 years old, is buried here, dear to his family.
EDCS-23002250.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clodius Pius, detailing his age (18) and his affection within his family.

EDCS-23002252.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Primnius and his age, though the specific age is incomplete.

EDCS-23002253.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from Rome, Italy, recording the burial of Scribonia Pergamis.

EDCS-23002257.translation: In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, the relics of the martyrs Stephen, Servandus, Germanus, Justa, and Rufina are deposited here, on the 18th day before the Kalends of February, in the 7th year of Bishop Theoderacis.
EDCS-23002257.summary: This is a Christian inscription from Rome, Italy, recording the deposition of relics of several martyrs under Bishop Theoderacis.

EDCS-23002307.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cornelius Fortunatus, [son of] Sergia, aged 41, pious towards his own, is buried here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-23002307.summary: This is a Roman tombstone inscription from Rome, Italy, recording the burial of Cornelius Fortunatus, with a dedication to the Manes and a wish for light earth.

EDCS-23002404.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication inscription from Rome, Italy, possibly for Lucius Licinius Marcianus.

EDCS-23002406.translation: Go forth, go forth, go forth! Greetings!
EDCS-23002406.summary: This is a short, repetitive inscription from Rome, Italy, possibly a magical charm or a farewell.

EDCS-23101967.translation: Maria Severa lived 12 years, 1 month, [and some days].
EDCS-23101967.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from Ostia, Italy, recording the age of Maria Severa at her death.

EDCS-23900236.translation: Murensis, servant of God, lived 55 years, departed in peace.
EDCS-23900236.summary: This is a Christian tombstone inscription from an unknown location, recording the life and peaceful departure of Murensis, a servant of God.

EDCS-23900237.translation: Vincomalos, bishop and servant of Christ, lived 85 years, of which he lived 43 years in the priesthood, departed in peace on the 4th day before the Nones of February, in the era 547.
EDCS-23900237.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription from an unknown location, recording the lifespan and priestly service of Bishop Vincomalos in the era 547 (AD 509).

EDCS-23900242.translation: In the name of the Lord, felicitously in the second year of the reign of our Lord King Ermenigild, whom his father King Liuvigild is persecuting, brought to the city of Hispalis by [some official].
EDCS-23900242.summary: This is a historical inscription from an unknown location, likely related to the conflict between King Ermenigild and his father King Liuvigild, mentioning the city of Hispalis.

EDCS-23900249.translation: To Lucius Caninius, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, from Pomptino, centurion of the II Legion Pansa, prefect of the cohort, augur, duovir. Arria, his wife [dedicates this].
EDCS-23900249.summary: This is a dedication inscription from Tarraco, Hispania, honoring Lucius Caninius for his military and civic career, dedicated by his wife Arria.

EDCS-24400052.summary: This is a title referring to a very important person, likely from a later Roman period.

EDCS-24400073.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Julius Severus Tuccitanus, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-24500001.translation: Publius Quintus, resident of Portus Sexitani
EDCS-24500001.summary: This inscription likely indicates a person named Publius Quintus who was a resident of Portus Sexitani.

EDCS-24500024.translation: The republic of Malaca, devoted to the divine will and majesty of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the second time, consul, father of his country, proconsul, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-24500024.summary: This is a dedication by the city of Malaca to Emperor Carinus, likely from the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-24500844.translation: Adoris, aged 70, cherished by her family, is buried here.
EDCS-24500844.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Adoris, who died at the age of 70 and was loved by her family.

EDCS-24600670.translation: For the honor of Lucius Septimius Severus
EDCS-24600670.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or commemoration made in honor of Lucius Septimius Severus.

EDCS-24600671.translation: Aelia Marcellina, daughter of Aelius Marcius, gives this gift after death to her daughter Flaccina, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-24600671.summary: This is a posthumous dedication from Aelia Marcellina to her daughter Flaccina.

EDCS-24600672.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Bruttius Primigenius, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-24600673.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed Bruttius, for his own family.
EDCS-24600673.summary: This is a dedication to the household gods or spirits of the departed, likely by a member of the Bruttius family.

EDCS-24600674.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Julius Suriacus, aged 67, dutiful to his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-24600674.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Suriacus, who died at the age of 67 and was dutiful to his family.

EDCS-24600675.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Messia Laeta, daughter of Lucius, of Patriciensis, aged 45, pious towards her own, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-24600675.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Messia Laeta, recording her name, parentage, origin, age, and a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-24600677.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for a woman named Faustina, indicating her piety and placement.

EDCS-24600679.translation: Pisus, freedman of Caius, aged 25, pious, in his own place.
EDCS-24600679.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pisus, a freedman, stating his name, age, and piety.

EDCS-24600680.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely refers to a slave belonging to Mascutius Aulus.

EDCS-24600685.translation: Pious, in his own place here.
EDCS-24600685.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely part of a tombstone, noting the deceased's piety and location.

EDCS-24600686.translation: Pious, he is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-24600686.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed male, emphasizing his piety and including a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-24600699.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to be a greeting or dedication to Marcus Aemilius Licinius.

EDCS-24600700.translation: He ordered to be set up by his will for Quintus Fulvius Rusticus Celtitanus, son of Quintus, the second pontiff and duumvir.
EDCS-24600700.summary: This inscription records a posthumous dedication ordered by will for Quintus Fulvius Rusticus Celtitanus, noting his offices.

EDCS-24600701.translation: Pratilia, freedwoman of Caius, lived 55 years, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-24600701.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pratilia, a freedwoman, recording her name, age at death, and a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-24600702.translation: Quintus Fulvius Licinius Celtitanus, aged 48, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-24600702.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Fulvius Licinius Celtitanus, recording his name, age, and a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-24600709.translation: Sacred to the god Hercules. Aurelius Zenonis Ianuarius, one of the Six, gladly paid his vow.
