EDCS-59500358.summary: A gentile or senatorial pedigree inscription naming Quintus Vibius and his male ancestors.

EDCS-59500360.translation: Marcus L. G. Caius Kacius Philargyrus, son of Quintus Bigellus Veteris.
EDCS-59500360.summary: This inscription names Marcus L. G. Caius Kacius Philargyrus and identifies his father, Quintus Bigellus Veteris.

EDCS-59500361.translation: Marcus L. G. Caius Kacius Philargyrus, son of Quintus Bigellus Veteris.
EDCS-59500361.summary: This inscription names Marcus L. G. Caius Kacius Philargyrus and identifies his father, Quintus Bigellus Veteris.

EDCS-60300120.summary: This inscription likely refers to the belonging of an object or person to the 'Firm Legion'.

EDCS-60300124.summary: This inscription is a title for 'Cnaeus Magnus', identifying him as Emperor.

EDCS-60300125.summary: This inscription is a title for 'Cnaeus Magnus', identifying him as Emperor.

EDCS-60300129.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Trophimus, aged 70, pious in his own home, rests here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-60300129.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Trophimus, including his age, status as 'pius', and the 'sit tibi terra levis' formula.

EDCS-60300132.translation: Quintus Aletinus, aged [---], pious in his own home.
EDCS-60300132.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription identifies Quintus Aletinus, notes his age is missing, and describes him as 'pius in suis'.

EDCS-60300135.summary: This inscription is a fragment mentioning a slave of Marcus Hemtius, likely part of a tombstone.

EDCS-60300183.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Annia Caesia lived 72 years. Acilius Senex made this for his most pious mother, who is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-60300183.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Acilius Senex for his pious mother, Annia Caesia.

EDCS-60300194.translation: Quintus L. Optatus and Quintus C. Optatus, the two duovirs, erected this from the many bakers.
EDCS-60300194.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by two duovirs, likely representing a guild of bakers.

EDCS-60300243.translation: Hygia, a slave, aged 15, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-60300243.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young slave girl named Hygia.

EDCS-60300280.translation: Lucius latro Balbus, Lucius latro Ba(et)icus.
EDCS-60300280.summary: This inscription appears to record the names of two individuals, possibly slaves or freedmen, identified as Lucius latro Balbus and Lucius latro Baeticus.

EDCS-60300292.translation: In all directions, 20 feet. Marcus Iulius Gallus, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, and Iulia Apicula, freedwoman of Marcus, aged 26 years and [X] days, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you both.
EDCS-60300292.summary: This funerary inscription marks a burial plot of 20 feet in extent, belonging to Marcus Iulius Gallus and Iulia Apicula, who died at different ages.

EDCS-60300449.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Quintus Satrius Fuscus, governor of the province of Lusitania, aged 75, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Quintus Satrius Maxumus for his grandfather, out of duty.
EDCS-60300449.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintus Satrius Fuscus, a governor of Lusitania, set up by his grandson Quintus Satrius Maxumus.

EDCS-61200041.summary: This brief inscription likely refers to the presence or actions of Caesar in the Roman province of Baetica.

EDCS-62800020.translation: A statue, from the will of Caius Septimius G[---].
EDCS-62800020.summary: This inscription indicates the dedication of a statue, funded by the will of Caius Septimius G[---].

EDCS-63202249.translation: Lucius Licinius Sura took care to have this made for the people of Italicum.
EDCS-63202249.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Licinius Sura arranged for a public work or monument to be made for the inhabitants of Italicum.

EDCS-64000109.translation: May all those most distinguished consular men have it cultivated and dedicated, under the care of Aurelius Florentius, curator of the colony of Astigi.
EDCS-64000109.summary: This inscription records the cultivation and dedication of something by distinguished consular men, overseen by a local curator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64500573.summary: This inscription identifies a freedman, identifying his former patron as 'Ri', likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600130.translation: May you use it happily. Made by P.
EDCS-64600130.summary: This is a brief inscription, possibly on an object, wishing the user happiness and identifying the maker as 'P', likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600139.summary: This inscription is the beginning of a dedication or ownership marking by Marcus Valerius Philom[---], likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600143.summary: This inscription names Diona and her children, possibly as part of a family monument or dedication, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600333.summary: This inscription names Cornelia and the letters 'LTA', the meaning of which is unclear but likely relates to a family or status, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600337.translation: Gaius Annius Nicephor, aged 45. Restitutus, aged 20, beloved by his family, is laid here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-64600337.summary: This is a Latin epitaph recording the death of two individuals, Gaius Annius Nicephor and Restitutus, with a common wish for a peaceful afterlife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600347.summary: This inscription begins the name of Quintus Antonius Hel[---], likely part of a funerary or dedicatory text from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600349.translation: To Pune, from Publius Annius Pusinnus.
EDCS-64600349.summary: This is a dedication, likely to a deity named Pune, by Publius Annius Pusinnus.

EDCS-64600432.summary: This inscription lists three personal names, possibly indicating owners or individuals associated with a property or object.

EDCS-64600433.translation: To Quintus Vibius, son of Quintus, grandson of Marcus, great-grandson of Gnaeus.
EDCS-64600433.summary: This is a funerary inscription naming a Roman citizen and detailing his genealogy.

EDCS-64600435.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Terentius Onesimus, likely as the deceased or the dedicator.

EDCS-65000096.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Fortuna Crescens.

EDCS-65000128.translation: From the pottery works of Lucius Fabius Cilo, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-65000128.summary: This inscription identifies the owner of the Scalensian pottery works as Lucius Fabius Cilo.

EDCS-66300086.translation: Cornelius, son of Crescens, and Cassia, daughter of Caius, wife of Arn. Here they lie; may the earth be light upon you. Lucius Cornelius Fundanus, at his own expense.
EDCS-66300086.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelius and Cassia, paid for by Lucius Cornelius Fundanus.

EDCS-67200129.summary: This inscription lists three names, possibly referring to winds or mythological figures, with Greek origins.

EDCS-67400286.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucius Manilius Primigenius, likely indicating ownership or commemoration.

EDCS-67400290.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the departed spirits (manes) by Polytimus.

EDCS-68600144.translation: A plot of land, 12 feet, from Corduba.
EDCS-68600144.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a plot of land of 12 feet, likely in Corduba.

EDCS-68600147.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly the beginning of a name and a gentilician name, Publius Opilius.

EDCS-68700053.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mercury, possibly in his imperial aspect as Augustus.

EDCS-69000001.translation: Tana, Anus, Rustica, Optate, Aucta, Fabulla, Julia, Chelido, Prime, Traxe, Dio, Polio, Nicolaus, Stabilio, Diocare, Thalame, Sum, Niasia, Aris, Soce, G, Princeps, Antronica, Frontaca, Pusellio, Cimisex, Felix, Rustica, Storge, Charis, Philonia, Mena, Helene.
EDCS-69000001.summary: This inscription is a list of many female names, possibly indicating a group of freedwomen or members of a collegium.

EDCS-69100263.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Hedone, wife of Caius Marcius Crescentius, a slave, cared for this to be done. He is buried here.
EDCS-69100263.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Crescentius, a slave, and the provision made by his wife Hedone.

EDCS-69200319.translation: Of the Grex Antoniani. Veneta and Russea, the chariot of Lascivus Verus, the chariot of Lascivus Vetius, the chariot of Margarita, the chariot of Margarita, the chariot of Gelotis, the chariot of Urbicus, the chariot of Hilarius, the chariot of Helena, the chariot of Basiliscus, the chariot of Nomantinus, the chariot of Barbarion, the chariot of Callidromus, the chariot of Lupus, drivers. Piramus, driver. And the chariots of Antoniani, Patricius, Martialis, Successus, Atiaronius, Vaicus, Narcissus, Atsertor. The whole troupe, Antoniani.
EDCS-69200319.summary: This inscription lists chariots and drivers, likely associated with a circus faction named Grex Antoniani.

EDCS-69500001.translation: A plot of land, 12 feet, Marcus Cornelius Catullus, son of Lucius, from Anticaria.
EDCS-69500001.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a plot of land of 12 feet, belonging to Marcus Cornelius Catullus from Anticaria.

EDCS-69900173.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fabia Probina, 8 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-69900173.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Fabia Probina, who died at the age of 8.

EDCS-70100016.translation: Cordyla Portensis, Lixitana, the old one, excellent, of the highest [qualities], 3 years old.
EDCS-70100016.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Cordyla, described as old and excellent, and 3 years old.

EDCS-70100017.translation: The old, excellent Saxitani, 3 years old. Of Marcus Valerius Euplus, MI.
EDCS-70100017.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the 'old, excellent Saxitani', who were 3 years old, likely referring to young children buried together.

EDCS-70100018.summary: This inscription is likely a label for a product, possibly wine or a sauce, indicating high quality.

EDCS-70100019.summary: This inscription is likely a label for a product, possibly wine or a sauce, indicating high quality.

EDCS-70100020.translation: Garum, the flower of mackerel, excellent, by Marcus Memmius Agathemerus, freedman.
EDCS-70100020.summary: This inscription is a label for a high-quality fish sauce (garum) produced or sold by Marcus Memmius Agathemerus.

EDCS-70700062.summary: This inscription is a personal name, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication by Annios Sidonius.

EDCS-70700071.translation: The relics of the saints of Jerusalem, John the Baptist, Eulalia, Justa, Rufina, and Felix the martyrs, this basilica was dedicated by Pimenius the bishop on the eighth day before the Kalends of June, in the era 680.
EDCS-70700071.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a basilica to various saints and martyrs by Bishop Pimenius in 680 AD (or equivalent era).

EDCS-70700072.translation: Appuleius and Briseis are buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-70700072.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Appuleius and Briseis, expressing a wish for their peaceful rest.

EDCS-70700079.translation: Decimus Baebius, son of Decimus, one of the IIII viri.
EDCS-70700079.summary: This inscription records the name and office of Decimus Baebius, who was a member of the IIII viri, a municipal magistracy.

EDCS-70700081.summary: This inscription is a dedication of a sacred object or place to the god Hercules.

EDCS-70700090.translation: The colony of Hasta Regia erected (this) for Marcus Popilius, son of Marcus.
EDCS-70700090.summary: This inscription records a public monument erected by the colony of Hasta Regia in honor of Marcus Popilius.

EDCS-70700110.translation: [...], son of Quintus, a soldier of the [X]th legion, buried with ten feet in front and [X] feet in the field, is buried here.
EDCS-70700110.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier, specifying his military affiliation and the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-70700161.summary: This inscription, though fragmentary, likely records the name and occupation of Quintus Postumius Rusticus as a salt inspector, possibly dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71200029.translation: Lucius, son of Quintus, of the Attenia gens, belonging to Cytonis.
EDCS-71200029.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius, son of Quintus, from the Attenia gens, and possibly a freedman connection to Cytonis.

EDCS-71300081.translation: A marvelous temple erected to Christ in September.
EDCS-71300081.summary: This inscription commemorates the dedication of a Christian temple in the month of September.

EDCS-71300335.translation: Cornelius Crescens, daughter of Cassia, mother, of this place. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Lucius Cornelius Fundanus, from his own funds.
EDCS-71300335.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Cornelius Crescens, mother and daughter of Cassia, with a wish for a light earth, and notes that Lucius Cornelius Fundanus funded it.

EDCS-71300336.translation: Here lies Riva, of good memory, and Desvndes. She lived about 20 and a half years and departed with her husband Aquilanus, having lived 7 months, receiving penance, in peace, on the day before the Ides of December in the 80th year of the era.
EDCS-71300336.summary: This epitaph commemorates Riva and Desvndes, detailing Riva's age, her departure with her husband Aquilanus, and her peaceful death after receiving penance, dated to the 80th year of an era, likely the Spanish era.

EDCS-71300339.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly containing a name or a place reference.

EDCS-71300341.translation: To Marcus Fabius, son of Gnaeus, from the [---]ilio family, Antonius Avitus.
EDCS-71300341.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication or funerary inscription to Marcus Fabius, son of Gnaeus, identified as belonging to the [---]ilio family and connected to Antonius Avitus.

EDCS-71400087.translation: Valeria lies here. May she contend with the heroes, like Paris. She terrified the Trojans with the immense spoils she brought, a woman fed by civil blood, hostile to everyone. She offered herself so often to plunder. Born in this same place, she fell. Indeed, no one can imitate her beauty; such a face needs no artist. Among equals, she was counted among fortunate girls. She took nothing from any young man, and fate preserved her old virtues. She lived so chastely that no story was heard of her. As soon as a young man turned his eyes towards her, or saw her in the choir of virgins, he would believe she had fallen from there. She lived through so many years, full of modesty and virtue. May you conquer the ages by enduring. Like pumice stone, keep the love of your brother. As you pass by, say: May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-71400087.summary: This lengthy epitaph praises Valeria, a virtuous and beautiful young woman, highlighting her chastity, noble character, and tragic death, with a final wish for her soul to rest lightly.

EDCS-72600011.translation: Torius Bilosfe, pious, of his family, aged 25, is buried here. Young man, may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-72600011.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Torius Bilosfe, a 25-year-old man, described as pious, with a wish for a light earth.

EDCS-72800136.translation: Arboria, servant of God, lived 45 years, rested in peace on the third day before the Ides of February in the year 633.
EDCS-72800136.summary: This epitaph records the death of Arboria, a 'servant of God', who lived 45 years and died peacefully in the year 633 of an era, likely the Spanish era.

EDCS-73400375.translation: Lucius Popillius Acastus is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-73400375.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Popillius Acastus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-73400376.translation: Faustilia Gaditana, 5 months old, is buried here among her own; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-73400376.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Faustilia Gaditana, an infant, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-73400377.translation: Antonia Quieta, freedwoman of Quintus, aged 31, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you, a freedwoman well-deserving of her patrons.
EDCS-73400377.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Quieta, a freedwoman, detailing her age and relationship to patrons, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-73400378.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornelia Secundina, aged 21, is buried here among her own; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-73400378.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Secundina, detailing her age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-73900028.translation: Valeria Parthenope, aged 45, to Didius Eudaemon, her most deserving wife, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-73900028.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from Didius Eudaemon for his wife Valeria Parthenope, detailing her age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74100012.translation: To Castor and Pollux, the Great Gods. Sulpicia, daughter of Quintus Sulpicius, made a vow for her son restored to health.
EDCS-74100012.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Dioscuri (Castor and Pollux) by Sulpicia for her son's recovery, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74100013.translation: Garum, an excellent sauce, of Lucius Aurelius Gemellus.
EDCS-74100013.summary: This is a dedication or ownership marker for a product named 'Liquamen' or 'Garum', belonging to Lucius Aurelius Gemellus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74100014.translation: Nisus, freedman of Severus, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-74100014.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nisus, a freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74100015.translation: To Marcus Pedanius Moschion and his daughter Carchedonia, aged 24.
EDCS-74100015.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial for Marcus Pedanius Moschion and his daughter Carchedonia, including the daughter's age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74100016.translation: Aritus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, made this.
EDCS-74100016.summary: This is a dedication or construction inscription by Aritus, son of Publius, identifying his tribal affiliation (Quirina), likely from the Roman Republican or Imperial period.

EDCS-74200229.translation: To Manius Acilius Frontonius Singiliensis Barbensis, Prefect of Engineers, by Acilia Plecusa, as patron and wife, by decree of the town councillors.
EDCS-74200229.summary: A dedication honouring Manius Acilius Frontonius, Prefect of Engineers, by Acilia Plecusa, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-74200365.translation: Petronius Priscus placed this for his parents, having lived 20 years and some days, from his own funds.
EDCS-74200365.summary: A funerary dedication by Petronius Priscus for his parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200414.summary: A commercial inscription advertising filtered lamps sold by Assene, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200420.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Veneria, freedwoman of Augustus, made this for her husband Marcus Ulpius Reginensis, aged 33. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-74200420.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Reginensis, dedicated by his wife Veneria, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200450.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Lucius Aemilius Saturius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200451.translation: Marcus Marcellus placed this for his father Sacerdos, Rufina for her husband Marcellus.
EDCS-74200451.summary: A funerary epitaph indicating familial relationships and likely dedicating a monument, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200621.translation: Eutychia, aged 50, pious, is laid here among her own. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-74200621.summary: A funerary epitaph for Eutychia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74300006.translation: Pulcheria, freedwoman of Marcus Junius Terentianus, aged 36, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-74300006.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pulcheria, freedwoman of Marcus Junius Terentianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74300007.translation: The fellow citizens to Divus Julius.
EDCS-74300007.summary: A dedication to Divus Julius (Julius Caesar, deified) by the fellow citizens of a municipality, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-74600050.translation: Sacred to Jupiter. Montanus freely and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-74600050.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter by Montanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74600089.translation: Pomponia Nice, aged 5, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-74600089.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Pomponia Nice, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-74600168.translation: Sertorius Eros, freedman of Titus Sertorius Priscus; Cornelius Hiber, freedman of Marcus Aurelius Marcelinus; Cornelius Cantaber, freedman of Marcus; Domitius Faustus, freedman of Titus, fulfilled their vow to Fortuna.
EDCS-74600168.summary: This inscription records freedmen fulfilling a vow to the goddess Fortuna, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-74600240.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Quintus Antonius Hel, possibly a name or part of a longer text.

EDCS-74600241.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Quintus Antonius Hel, possibly a name or part of a longer text.

EDCS-74600242.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Quintus Antonius Hel, possibly a name or part of a longer text.

EDCS-74600243.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Quintus Antonius M, possibly a name or part of a longer text.

EDCS-74600244.summary: This inscription simply records the name Quintus Antonius, likely a personal name.

EDCS-74800073.translation: He fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-74800073.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-74800159.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Asellus, aged 3, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-74800159.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy named Asellus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-74800160.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. [---]us is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. [---]cula saw to its construction.
EDCS-74800160.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely dating to the Imperial period, mentioning someone being buried and a person who oversaw the construction.

EDCS-75300080.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely naming an individual, Publius Vinicius Decumus, potentially from a tomb or a dedication.

EDCS-75300089.summary: This brief inscription records the death of Marcus Licinius.

EDCS-75300092.translation: From the workshop of Restitutus the potter.
EDCS-75300092.summary: This inscription indicates the origin of a pottery item, stating it was made in the workshop of Restitutus.

EDCS-75300099.summary: This inscription likely identifies an individual named Parthenis and their status as a Vernaclus (born in the household).

EDCS-75300100.translation: Caius Cacius Philo, freedman of Caius; Creta[nus(?)]. Acilia Secunda, freedwoman of Aulus.
EDCS-75300100.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary epitaph or a record of ownership, naming two individuals, Caius Cacius Philo and Acilia Secunda, and possibly their origins.

EDCS-75300101.summary: This repetitive inscription likely commemorates an individual named Nicare Sollemnis, possibly with an emphasis on their solemnity or importance.

EDCS-75300102.translation: EP gives to Publicius Fortunatus Gallus and Publicius Nicephorus SA.
EDCS-75300102.summary: This inscription records a dedication or gift made by 'EP' to Publicius Fortunatus Gallus and Publicius Nicephorus, the meaning of 'SA' is unclear.

EDCS-75900070.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragmented dedication or naming, possibly referring to an individual or a place named Asido Caesarina.

EDCS-75900113.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelia Severa, daughter of Publius, aged fifteen, most longed-for wife, is buried here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-75900113.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Severa, a fifteen-year-old girl who was a wife, expressing grief and a wish for peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-75900197.translation: Isidore, may you live! Optata, may you live!
EDCS-75900197.summary: This inscription is a hopeful or celebratory message, wishing life to individuals named Isidore and Optata.

EDCS-75900220.translation: The highly distinguished consular men cultivated and dedicated these [structures], with Aurelius Florentius, curator of the colony of Astigi, overseeing the work.
EDCS-75900220.summary: This inscription records the cultivation and dedication of structures by distinguished men, overseen by a local official, likely in the Roman province of Baetica.

EDCS-75900223.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Herennia Apra, from Emerita, aged 37, made this; she rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-75900223.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Herennia Apra, a woman from Emerita, detailing her age and expressing a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-75900226.summary: A very brief dedication to Marcia Tertinia, possibly on a tomb or dedication stone.

EDCS-76000013.summary: This inscription likely records the name of a person, Saturninus, and his father, also Saturninus.

EDCS-76200211.summary: This inscription appears to be a personal name, Lucius Lucretius Prin T, possibly the beginning of a longer inscription or a name on a tomb.

EDCS-76600237.translation: Sentina, beloved daughter of Caius Lucanus, aged 16. Her parents took care to erect this monument. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-76600237.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by parents for their deceased daughter, Sentina, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-76700082.translation: The chest, damaged by age, Vibius Facetus and Manlius Picens, as duumvirs, restored it, bringing water which had been missing from that place for a long time, further extending it from a reservoir.
EDCS-76700082.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a damaged chest and the restoration of a water supply by two duumvirs, likely in a municipal context.

EDCS-76900116.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, Iunia, daughter of Marcus, Ruf[a...]
EDCS-76900116.summary: This is the beginning of a funerary dedication to Iunia Rufa, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-77600003.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Pollicius Fuscinus, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-79300042.translation: Hieronimus Iterans, having taken care for his sins, lived piously in his time. He is asked that the power of God may deliver him from the second [punishment], safe, without doubt regarding the grace of Christ who fashioned him from dust. He humbly asks for pardon because he knows his sins. He lived 35 years in this world. On the 18th day before the Kalends of May, failing with age, he departed in the year 700 [AUC].
EDCS-79300042.summary: This epitaph records the life and death of Hieronimus Iterans, a man who lived 35 years, died on April 14th in the year 700 AUC (68 AD), and expresses Christian sentiments regarding salvation and sin.

EDCS-80100137.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely honoring a praetor named Valentius, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-80200089.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely honoring a praetor named Valentius, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-80300079.translation: To Lucius Fabius Pollio, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, from Caius Mamilius Primigenius.
EDCS-80300079.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Caius Mamilius Primigenius to Lucius Fabius Pollio, likely a prominent citizen, possibly in the early imperial period.

EDCS-80300080.translation: To Lucius Fabius Pollio, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, aedile and duovir, from Caius Poblicius Priscus, son of Caius, and Marcus Fuficius Festus.
EDCS-80300080.summary: This inscription records a dedication by two individuals to Lucius Fabius Pollio, who held the offices of aedile and duovir, likely in a Roman colony during the imperial period.

EDCS-80400008.translation: To Marcus Lucretius Julianus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, a public equestrian, procurator of the Augusti of the province of Baetica, also procurator of the Vegetian Calendar, also procurator of the Twentieth of inheritances of the provinces of Baetica and Lusitania, curator of the public affairs of Italicum; the most splendid republic, by decree of the decurions, for his merits.
EDCS-80400008.summary: This is a rescript honoring Marcus Lucretius Julianus for his distinguished service in various financial and administrative roles across Roman provinces, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-80400015.translation: To Baetis and Janus Augustus at Oceanus, by order of the Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the divine, consul for the 13th time, with tribunician power for the 21st time, pontifex maximus, on the 77th milestone.
EDCS-80400015.summary: This milestone inscription indicates a dedication or marking related to the road system and the deified emperor Augustus, probably in Hispania Baetica during the early imperial period.

EDCS-80400016.translation: By order of the Emperor Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the divine Augustus, grandson of the divine Julius, pontifex maximus, consul for the 5th time, imperator for the 8th time, with tribunician power for the 37th time, from Janus Augustus, who is at Baetis, as far as Oceanus, on the 77th milestone.
EDCS-80400016.summary: This milestone inscription marks a section of road related to the cult of Janus Augustus and the deified emperor Augustus, likely in Hispania Baetica during Tiberius' reign.

EDCS-80400023.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Augustus and a bridge, possibly a milestone or dedication related to infrastructure, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-80900083.translation: Criscentius, servant of God, lived 46 years, rested in peace on the third day before the Kalends of October, in the year 567 of the era [of the Spanish].
EDCS-80900083.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription commemorating Criscentius and providing his age at death and date of burial according to the Spanish Era, likely from late Roman or early Visigothic Hispania.

EDCS-80900150.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary epitaph, identifying an individual as Lucius, son of Quintus, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-80900256.translation: Aemilius Gemellus, freedman of Marcus, and his wife Sila, aged 40, and Fusca, granddaughter, aged 4, and Gemellus, grandson, aged 5.
EDCS-80900256.summary: This inscription is a tombstone commemorating several members of a family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-81100014.translation: Marcus Pompeius, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, first of the two men, from the Pompeius family.
EDCS-81100014.summary: This inscription likely records a prominent citizen named Marcus Pompeius who held a high office, possibly in municipal administration, dating to the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-81200160.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Aemiliana Suelitana, aged 12, dutiful to her own, is placed here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-81200160.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Aemilia Aemiliana Suelitana, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-81200164.translation: Petronius C. Sergianus [dedicated this] to his parents. He lived 20 years. Donius Priscus to his [---].
EDCS-81200164.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Petronius Sergianus to his parents and also mentions another individual, Donius Priscus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-81400110.translation: Salagini: For the safety of Burdo, Iuncus Varrenus Vowed.
EDCS-81400110.summary: This is a votive inscription where Iuncus Varrenus made a vow for the well-being of someone named Burdo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-81600023.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Faustus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, augur, and holder of the office of II vir [chief magistrate], from the fellow townsmen and inhabitants.
EDCS-81600023.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Cornelius Faustus, a man of significant public standing who held religious and administrative positions in his community, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-81800090.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a name, possibly from a tombstone or public monument, date uncertain.
